  \neg (P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow \neg Q)
}{pm2.52}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\neg (P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{conax1k}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Allgemeiner Fall von Theorem \(*2.521\)]{%
  \neg (P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)
}{pm2.521g}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\neg (P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{conax1}}
  \proofstep{}{\neg (P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.21d}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Allgemeiner Fall von Theorem \(*2.521\)]{%
  \neg (P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (R \rightarrow P)
}{pm2.521g2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\neg (P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simplim}}
  \proofstep{}{\neg (P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (R \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1d}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem \(*2.521\)]{%
  \neg (P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow P)
}{pm2.521}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\neg (P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.521g}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Exportation in geschlossener Form]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &(\neg (P \rightarrow \neg Q) \rightarrow R)\rightarrow{}\\
    &\quad (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R))
  \end{aligned}
}{expt}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow \neg (P \rightarrow \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm3.2im}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow ((\neg (P \rightarrow \neg Q) \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imim1d}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg (P \rightarrow \neg Q) \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{com12}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Importation in geschlossener Form]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &(P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R))\rightarrow{}\\
    &\quad (\neg (P \rightarrow \neg Q) \rightarrow R)
  \end{aligned}
}{impt}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\neg (P \rightarrow \neg Q) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simprim}}
  \proofstep{}{\neg (P \rightarrow \neg Q) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simplim}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    \begin{aligned}[t]
      &(P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R))\rightarrow{}\\
      &\quad (\neg (P \rightarrow \neg Q) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R))
    \end{aligned}
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{imim1i}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    \begin{aligned}[t]
      &(P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R))\rightarrow{}\\
      &\quad (\neg (P \rightarrow \neg Q) \rightarrow R)
    \end{aligned}
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{mpdi}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
Die Sätze \FormulaRefAuto{impi}, \FormulaRefAuto{expi}, \FormulaRefAuto{expt} und \FormulaRefAuto{impt} zeigen, dass die verschachtelte Implikationsform \(P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)\) und die Form \(\neg (P \rightarrow \neg Q) \rightarrow R\) im klassischen Kalkül wechselseitig ineinander überführt werden können.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Elimination eines Antezedens]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \dsep P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow R) \vdash P \rightarrow R
}{pm2.61d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (\neg R \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con1d}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (\neg R \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syld}{3,1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.18d}{4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Elimination eines Antezedens]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \dsep \neg Q \rightarrow R \vdash P \rightarrow R
}{pm2.61d1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\neg Q \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61d}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Elimination eines Antezedens]{%
  P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow R) \dsep Q \rightarrow R \vdash P \rightarrow R
}{pm2.61d2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61d}{3,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Elimination eines Antezedens]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep \neg P \rightarrow Q \vdash Q
}{pm2.61i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\neg P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\neg Q \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{nsyl4}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.18i}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Elimination zweier Antezedenzfälle]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &\neg P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow R)\dsep
    P \rightarrow R\dsep
    Q \rightarrow R \vdash{}\\
    &\quad R
  \end{aligned}
}{pm2.61ii}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{Q \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,3}{\neg P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61d2}{1,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{R}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61i}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Elimination zweier Antezedenzfälle]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)\dsep
    \neg P \rightarrow R\dsep
    \neg Q \rightarrow R \vdash{}\\
    &\quad R
  \end{aligned}
}{pm2.61nii}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\neg P \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{\neg Q \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,3}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61d1}{1,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{R}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61i}{4,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Elimination dreier Antezedenzfälle]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &\neg P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow (\neg R \rightarrow S))\dsep
    P \rightarrow S\dsep
    Q \rightarrow S\dsep
    R \rightarrow S \vdash{}\\
    &\quad S
  \end{aligned}
}{pm2.61iii}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow (\neg R \rightarrow S))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (\neg R \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1d}{2}}
  \proofstep{3}{Q \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{Q \rightarrow (\neg R \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1d}{4}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{\neg R \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61ii}{1,3,5}}
  \proofstep{4}{R \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2,3,4}{S}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61i}{7,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Vereinigung zweier Antezedenzfälle]{%
  \neg P \rightarrow R \dsep Q \rightarrow R \vdash (P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow R
}{ja}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{imim2i}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg P \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61d1}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktionsform von \FormulaRefAuto{ja}]{%
  P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow S) \dsep P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S) \vdash P \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow R) \rightarrow S)
}{jad}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{com12}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow (P \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{com12}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(Q \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ja}{2,4}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow R) \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{com12}{5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Schwaches Clavius-Gesetz]{%
  ((P \rightarrow \neg P) \rightarrow \neg P)
}{pm2.01}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\neg P \rightarrow \neg P}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow \neg P) \rightarrow \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ja}{1,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
Im Unterschied zu \FormulaRefAuto{pm2.18} liefert \FormulaRefAuto{pm2.01} aus \(P \rightarrow \neg P\) nicht \(P\), sondern direkt \(\neg P\). Der Satz ist daher die schwächere, aber oft sehr handliche Form eines Widerspruchsschlusses.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Inferenz zum schwachen Clavius-Gesetz]{%
  (P \rightarrow \neg P) \vdash \neg P
}{pm2.01i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow \neg P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow \neg P) \rightarrow \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.01}}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg P}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Reductio ad absurdum als Deduktion]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow \neg Q) \vdash P \rightarrow \neg Q
}{pm2.01d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow \neg Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{\neg Q \rightarrow \neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow \neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61d1}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem \(*2.6\)]{%
  ((\neg P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow Q))
}{pm2.6}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{idd}}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jad}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem \(*2.61\)]{%
  ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((\neg P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow Q))
}{pm2.61}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.6}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((\neg P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{com12}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61} ist die klassische Fallunterscheidung nach \(P\) und \(\neg P\): wenn beide Fälle zu \(Q\) führen, folgt \(Q\) ohne weitere Voraussetzung.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Widerspruchsbeweis]{%
  ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow \neg Q) \rightarrow \neg P))
}{pm2.65}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow \neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{idd}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow \neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{con3}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow \neg Q) \rightarrow \neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jad}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Widerspruchsbeweis als Inferenz]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep P \rightarrow \neg Q \vdash \neg P
}{pm2.65i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow \neg Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \rightarrow \neg P}{\FormulaRefAuto{con2i}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg Q \rightarrow \neg P}{\FormulaRefAuto{con3i}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\neg P}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61i}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Aus einem Widerspruch folgt Beliebiges]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep P \rightarrow \neg Q \vdash P \rightarrow R
}{pm2.21dd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow \neg Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\neg P}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.65i}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.21i}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Widerspruchsbeweis als Deduktion]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \dsep P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow \neg R) \vdash P \rightarrow \neg Q
}{pm2.65d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow \neg R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nsyld}{2,1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow \neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.01d}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.65}, \FormulaRefAuto{pm2.65i} und \FormulaRefAuto{pm2.65d} sind Grundformen des Widerspruchsbeweises: aus zwei entgegengesetzten Konsequenzen unter derselben Voraussetzung wird die Voraussetzung negiert.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Modus tollens]{%
  \neg Q \dsep P \rightarrow Q \vdash \neg P
}{mto}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow \neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\neg P}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.65i}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Modus tollens als Deduktion]{%
  P \rightarrow \neg R \dsep P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \vdash P \rightarrow \neg Q
}{mtod}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow \neg R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow \neg R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1d}{1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow \neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.65d}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Modus tollens als Inferenz]{%
  \neg R \dsep P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \vdash P \rightarrow \neg Q
}{mtoi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow \neg R}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow \neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{mtod}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Variante von Modus tollens]{%
  Q \dsep P \rightarrow \neg Q \vdash \neg P
}{mt2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow \neg Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\neg P}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.65i}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{mto} ist die übliche Form des Modus tollens. \FormulaRefAuto{mt2} ist die dazu passende Variante, in der anstelle von \(\neg Q\) bereits \(Q\) mit einer negierten Konsequenzannahme \(P \rightarrow \neg Q\) kombiniert wird.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[A rule similar to modus tollens]{%
  \neg Q \dsep \neg P \rightarrow Q \vdash P
}{mt3}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\neg P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\neg\neg P}{\FormulaRefAuto{mto}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P}{\FormulaRefAuto{notnotri}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Peirce's axiom]{%
  (((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow P) \rightarrow P)
}{peirce}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simplim}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow P) \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ja}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{peirce} ist eine der klassischen, aber nicht intuitionistisch allgemein gültigen Implikationsformeln. In der reinen Implikationslogik markiert sie genau den Übergang vom intuitionistischen zum klassischen Kalkül.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[The Inversion Axiom of the infinite-valued sentential logic of Lukasiewicz]{%
  (((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow P) \rightarrow P))
}{looinv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{%
    (((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow P) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow P)))
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{imim1}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow P) \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{peirce}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow P) \rightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl6}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A self-implication does not imply its own negation]{%
  \neg\bigl((P \rightarrow P) \rightarrow \neg(P \rightarrow P)\bigr)
}{bijust0}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    \bigl(((P \rightarrow P) \rightarrow \neg(P \rightarrow P)) \rightarrow \neg(P \rightarrow P)\bigr)
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.01}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    \neg\bigl((P \rightarrow P) \rightarrow \neg(P \rightarrow P)\bigr)
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{mt2}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem used to justify the definition of the biconditional]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &\neg\Bigl(\bigl(\neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P))
    \rightarrow \neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P))\bigr)\\
    &\qquad\rightarrow
    \neg\bigl(\neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P))
    \rightarrow \neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P))\bigr)\Bigr)
  \end{aligned}
}{bijust}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{%
    \begin{aligned}[t]
      &\neg\Bigl(\bigl(\neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P))
      \rightarrow \neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P))\bigr)\\
      &\qquad\rightarrow
      \neg\bigl(\neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P))
      \rightarrow \neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P))\bigr)\Bigr)
    \end{aligned}
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{bijust0}}
\end{tabproof}

\section{Logische Äquivalenz}

\FormulaDef[Biconditional as a formation rule]{%
  \text{If } P \text{ and } Q \text{ are wffs, then } (P \leftrightarrow Q) \text{ is a wff.}%
}{wb}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}

\FormulaDef[Definition of the biconditional]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &\neg\Bigl(\\
    &\quad \bigl((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P))\bigr)\\
    &\quad \rightarrow\\
    &\quad \neg\bigl(\neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P)) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)\bigr)\\
    &\Bigr)
  \end{aligned}
}{df-bi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{df-bi} ist in Metamath bewusst nicht als gewöhnliche Definition der Form
\[
  (P \leftrightarrow Q)\coloneqq \neg\bigl((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P)\bigr)
\]
formuliert. Der inhaltliche Sinn ist zwar genau dieser, aber das Symbol \(\leftrightarrow\) wird an dieser Stelle ja erst eingeführt und kann daher noch nicht selbst zur Formulierung seiner Definition verwendet werden.

Stattdessen benutzt Metamath hier eine allgemeinere Definitionsform: Das neue Symbol \(P \leftrightarrow Q\) wird so eingeführt, dass es durch
\[
  \neg\bigl((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P)\bigr)
\]
eliminiert werden kann, ohne dass dadurch neue Sätze in der alten Sprache beweisbar werden. Ersetzt man in \FormulaRefAuto{df-bi} das Definiendum \(P \leftrightarrow Q\) mechanisch durch dieses Definiens, so erhält man gerade den zuvor bewiesenen Rechtfertigungssatz \FormulaRefAuto{bijust}. Genau darin liegt die inhaltliche Absicherung dieser Definition.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Property of the biconditional connective]{%
  ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow P) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)))
}{impbi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{%
    \begin{aligned}[t]
      &\neg\Bigl(
        \bigl((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P))\bigr)\\
      &\qquad\rightarrow
        \neg\bigl(\neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P)) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)\bigr)
      \Bigr)
    \end{aligned}
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{df-bi}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    \begin{aligned}[t]
      &\neg\Bigl(
        \bigl((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P))\bigr)\\
      &\qquad\rightarrow
        \neg\bigl(\neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P)) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)\bigr)
      \Bigr)\\
      &\qquad\rightarrow
        \bigl(\neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P)) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)\bigr)
    \end{aligned}
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{simprim}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    \neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P)) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow P) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)))
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{expi}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Infer an equivalence from an implication and its converse]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep Q \rightarrow P \vdash P \leftrightarrow Q
}{impbii}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \rightarrow P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow P) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbi}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{mp2}{1,2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Double deduction associated with impbi]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)) \dsep
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (S \rightarrow R))
  \vdash
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S))
}{impbidd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (S \rightarrow R))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((R \rightarrow S) \rightarrow ((S \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbi}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl6c}{1,2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduce an equivalence from two implications]{%
  Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S) \dsep
  P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow R)
  \vdash
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S))
}{impbid21d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((R \rightarrow S) \rightarrow ((S \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbi}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl2imc}{1,2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduce an equivalence from two implications]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \dsep
  P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow Q)
  \vdash
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)
}{impbid}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid21d}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.43i}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Relate the biconditional connective to primitive connectives]{%
  ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P)))
}{dfbi1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{%
    \begin{aligned}[t]
      &\neg\Bigl(((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P)))\\
      &\qquad\rightarrow \neg(\neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P)) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q))\Bigr)
    \end{aligned}
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{df-bi}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    \begin{aligned}[t]
      &((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P)))\\
      &\qquad\rightarrow
      ((\neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P)) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q))\\
      &\qquad\qquad\rightarrow ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P))))
    \end{aligned}
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{impbi}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    \begin{aligned}[t]
      &\neg\bigl((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P))\bigr)\\
      &\qquad\rightarrow
      \Bigl(((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P)))\\
      &\qquad\qquad\rightarrow \neg(\neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P)) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q))\Bigr)
    \end{aligned}
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{con3rr3}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P)))
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{mt3}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Alternate proof of dfbi1]{%
  ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P)))
}{dfbi1ALT}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{dfbi1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Property of the biconditional connective]{%
  ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))
}{biimp}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{%
    \begin{aligned}[t]
      &\neg\Bigl(((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P)))\\
      &\qquad\rightarrow \neg(\neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P)) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q))\Bigr)
    \end{aligned}
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{df-bi}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    \begin{aligned}[t]
      &\neg\Bigl(((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P)))\\
      &\qquad\rightarrow \neg(\neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P)) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q))\Bigr)\\
      &\qquad\rightarrow ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P)))
    \end{aligned}
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{simplim}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{simplim}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Infer an implication from a logical equivalence]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q \vdash P \rightarrow Q
}{biimpi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimp}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A mixed syllogism inference from a biconditional and an implication]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q \dsep Q \rightarrow R \vdash P \rightarrow R
}{sylbi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{3,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A mixed syllogism inference from an implication and a biconditional]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep Q \leftrightarrow R \vdash P \rightarrow R
}{sylib}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \leftrightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A mixed syllogism inference from two biconditionals]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q \dsep Q \leftrightarrow R \vdash P \rightarrow R
}{sylbb}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \leftrightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Property of the biconditional connective]{%
  ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow P))
}{biimpr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{dfbi1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P)) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{simprim}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylbi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Commutative law for the biconditional]{%
  ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow P))
}{bicom1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpr}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimp}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Commutative law for the biconditional]{%
  ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow P))
}{bicom}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicom1}}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \leftrightarrow P) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicom1}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{bicom} zeigt, dass die beiden Seiten einer Äquivalenz vollständig austauschbar sind. Dieser einfache Symmetriesatz wird später ständig zur Umorientierung von Definitionen gebraucht.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Commute two sides of a biconditional in a deduction]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R) \vdash P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow Q)
}{bicomd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \leftrightarrow R) \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicom1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inference from commutative law for logical equivalence]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q \vdash Q \leftrightarrow P
}{bicomi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicom1}}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \leftrightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Infer an equivalence from two implications]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \dsep R \rightarrow Q \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)
}{impbid1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Infer an equivalence from two implications]{%
  Q \rightarrow R \dsep P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow Q) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)
}{impbid2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A variation on impbid with contraposition]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \dsep P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow \neg R) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)
}{impcon4bid}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow \neg R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con4d}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Infer a converse implication from a logical equivalence]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q \vdash Q \rightarrow P
}{biimpri}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpr}}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduce an implication from a logical equivalence]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)
}{biimpd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \leftrightarrow R) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimp}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[An inference from a biconditional, related to modus ponens]{%
  P \dsep P \leftrightarrow Q \vdash Q
}{mpbi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[An inference from a biconditional, related to modus ponens]{%
  Q \dsep P \leftrightarrow Q \vdash P
}{mpbir}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A deduction from a biconditional, related to modus ponens]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R) \vdash P \rightarrow R
}{mpbid}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpd}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[An inference from a nested biconditional, related to modus ponens]{%
  Q \dsep P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R) \vdash P \rightarrow R
}{mpbii}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbid}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A mixed syllogism inference from an implication and a biconditional]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep R \leftrightarrow Q \vdash P \rightarrow R
}{sylibr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A mixed syllogism inference from a biconditional and an implication]{%
  Q \leftrightarrow P \dsep Q \rightarrow R \vdash P \rightarrow R
}{sylbir}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \leftrightarrow P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpr}{1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{3,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A mixed syllogism inference from two biconditionals]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q \dsep Q \leftrightarrow R \vdash R \rightarrow P
}{sylbbr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \leftrightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{R \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{4,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A mixed syllogism inference from two biconditionals]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q \dsep P \leftrightarrow R \vdash Q \rightarrow R
}{sylbb1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \leftrightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A mixed syllogism inference from two biconditionals]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q \dsep R \leftrightarrow Q \vdash P \rightarrow R
}{sylbb2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism deduction]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \dsep P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)
}{sylibd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syld}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism deduction]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R) \dsep P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)
}{sylbid}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpd}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syld}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A deduction from a biconditional, related to modus ponens]{%
  T \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q) \dsep P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R) \vdash T \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)
}{mpbidi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{T \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{T \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylcom}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A mixed syllogism inference from a nested implication and a biconditional]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q \dsep R \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S) \vdash R \rightarrow (P \rightarrow S)
}{biimtrid}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{R \rightarrow (P \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl5}{2,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A mixed syllogism inference from a nested implication and a biconditional]{%
  Q \leftrightarrow P \dsep R \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S) \vdash R \rightarrow (P \rightarrow S)
}{biimtrrid}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \leftrightarrow P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{R \rightarrow (P \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl5}{2,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A mixed syllogism inference]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep R \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S) \vdash R \rightarrow (P \rightarrow S)
}{imbitrid}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{R \rightarrow (P \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl5}{2,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A mixed syllogism inference]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep R \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S) \vdash P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)
}{syl5ibcom}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{R \rightarrow (P \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbitrid}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{com12}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A mixed syllogism inference]{%
  P \rightarrow S \dsep R \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S) \vdash R \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)
}{imbitrrid}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{R \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbitrid}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A mixed syllogism inference]{%
  P \rightarrow S \dsep R \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S) \vdash P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow Q)
}{syl5ibrcom}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{R \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbitrrid}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{com12}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduce a converse implication from a logical equivalence]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R) \vdash P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow Q)
}{biimprd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \leftrightarrow R) \rightarrow (R \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpr}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduce a commuted implication from a logical equivalence]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R) \vdash Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)
}{biimpcd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpd}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{com12}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduce a converse commuted implication from a logical equivalence]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R) \vdash R \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)
}{biimprcd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimprd}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{R \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{com12}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A mixed syllogism inference from a nested implication and a biconditional]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \dsep R \leftrightarrow S \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)
}{imbitrdi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \leftrightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl6}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A mixed syllogism inference from a nested implication and a biconditional]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \dsep S \leftrightarrow R \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)
}{imbitrrdi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{S \leftrightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl6}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A mixed syllogism inference]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R) \dsep R \rightarrow S \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)
}{biimtrdi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpd}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl6}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A mixed syllogism inference]{%
  P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow Q) \dsep R \rightarrow S \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)
}{biimtrrdi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimprd}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl6}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A mixed syllogism inference from a doubly nested implication and a biconditional]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q \dsep R \rightarrow (S \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow T)) \vdash R \rightarrow (S \rightarrow (P \rightarrow T))
}{syl7bi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow (S \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow T))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{R \rightarrow (S \rightarrow (P \rightarrow T))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl7}{2,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism rule of inference]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)) \dsep S \leftrightarrow T \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow T))
}{syl8ib}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{S \leftrightarrow T}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{S \rightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow T))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl8}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A deduction from a biconditional, related to modus ponens]{%
  P \rightarrow R \dsep P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R) \vdash P \rightarrow Q
}{mpbird}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimprd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpd}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[An inference from a nested biconditional, related to modus ponens]{%
  R \dsep P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R) \vdash P \rightarrow Q
}{mpbiri}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbird}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism deduction]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \dsep P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow R) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)
}{sylibrd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimprd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syld}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism deduction]{%
  P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow Q) \dsep P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)
}{sylbird}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimprd}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syld}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Principle of identity for logical equivalence]{%
  (P \leftrightarrow P)
}{biid}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \leftrightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{1,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Principle of identity with antecedent]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow Q)
}{biidd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biid}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Two propositions are equivalent if they are both true]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q))
}{pm5.1im}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-1}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-1}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid21d}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Two truths are equivalent]{%
  P \dsep Q \vdash P \leftrightarrow Q
}{2th}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.1im}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{mp2}{1,2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Two truths are equivalent]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep P \rightarrow R \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)
}{2thd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.1im}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylc}{1,2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Two self-implications are equivalent]{%
  ((P \rightarrow P) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow Q))
}{monothetic}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow P) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{2th}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inference that converts a biconditional implied by one of its arguments, into an implication]{%
  P \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q) \vdash P \rightarrow Q
}{ibi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbid}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inference that converts a biconditional implied by one of its arguments, into an implication]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow P) \vdash P \rightarrow Q
}{ibir}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow P)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomd}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{ibi}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction that converts a biconditional implied by one of its arguments, into an implication]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)
}{ibd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \leftrightarrow R) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimp}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syli}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Distribution of implication over biconditional]{%
  \bigl((P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow R))\bigr)
}{pm5.74}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \leftrightarrow R) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimp}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imim3i}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \leftrightarrow R) \rightarrow (R \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpr}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imim3i}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid}{2,4}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimp}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.86d}{6}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpr}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.86d}{8}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbidd}{7,9}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    \bigl((P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow R))\bigr)
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{5,10}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74} ist einer der zentralen Umformungssätze dieses Abschnitts. Er erlaubt, eine Äquivalenz unter festem Antezedens in eine Äquivalenz zwischen zwei Implikationen umzuschreiben und später wieder zurück.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Distribution of implication over biconditional (inference form)]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R) \vdash (P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow R)
}{pm5.74i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{%
    \bigl((P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow R))\bigr)
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Distribution of implication over biconditional (reverse inference form)]{%
  (P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow R) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)
}{pm5.74ri}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{%
    \bigl((P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow R))\bigr)
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbir}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Distribution of implication over biconditional (deduction form)]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)) \vdash P \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow S))
}{pm5.74d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{%
    \bigl((Q \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)) \leftrightarrow ((Q \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow S))\bigr)
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylib}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Distribution of implication over biconditional (deduction form)]{%
  P \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S))
}{pm5.74rd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow S))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{%
    \bigl((Q \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)) \leftrightarrow ((Q \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow S))\bigr)
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylibr}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[An inference from transitive law for logical equivalence]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q \dsep Q \leftrightarrow R \vdash P \leftrightarrow R
}{bitri}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \leftrightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylbb}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{R \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylbbr}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \leftrightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[An inference from transitive law for logical equivalence]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q \dsep Q \leftrightarrow R \vdash R \leftrightarrow P
}{bitr2i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \leftrightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \leftrightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{R \leftrightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[An inference from transitive law for logical equivalence]{%
  Q \leftrightarrow P \dsep Q \leftrightarrow R \vdash P \leftrightarrow R
}{bitr3i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \leftrightarrow P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \leftrightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \leftrightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[An inference from transitive law for logical equivalence]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q \dsep R \leftrightarrow Q \vdash P \leftrightarrow R
}{bitr4i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \leftrightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \leftrightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction form of bitri]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R) \dsep P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)
}{bitrd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74i}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74i}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{2,4}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74ri}{5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction form of bitr2i]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R) \dsep P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S) \vdash P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow Q)
}{bitr2d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitrd}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomd}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction form of bitr3i]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R) \dsep P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S) \vdash P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)
}{bitr3d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomd}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitrd}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction form of bitr4i]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R) \dsep P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow R) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)
}{bitr4d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitrd}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism inference from two biconditionals]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q \dsep R \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S) \vdash R \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow S)
}{bitrid}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{R \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{R \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitrd}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism inference from two biconditionals]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q \dsep R \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S) \vdash R \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow P)
}{bitr2id}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{R \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitrid}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{R \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomd}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism inference from two biconditionals]{%
  Q \leftrightarrow P \dsep R \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S) \vdash R \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow S)
}{bitr3id}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \leftrightarrow P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{R \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitrid}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism inference from two biconditionals]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R) \dsep Q \leftrightarrow S \vdash P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)
}{bitr3di}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \leftrightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{S \leftrightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr2id}{3,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism inference from two biconditionals]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R) \dsep R \leftrightarrow S \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)
}{bitrdi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \leftrightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitrd}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism inference from two biconditionals]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R) \dsep R \leftrightarrow S \vdash P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow Q)
}{bitr2di}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \leftrightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitrdi}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomd}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism inference from two biconditionals]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R) \dsep S \leftrightarrow R \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)
}{bitr4di}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{S \leftrightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \leftrightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitrdi}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism inference from two biconditionals]{%
  Q \leftrightarrow R \dsep P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow R) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)
}{bitr4id}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \leftrightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitrid}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A mixed syllogism inference, useful for removing a definition from both sides of an implication]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep P \leftrightarrow R \dsep Q \leftrightarrow S \vdash R \rightarrow S
}{3imtr3i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \leftrightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{R \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{3,1}}
  \proofstep{3}{Q \leftrightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{Q \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{R \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{4,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A mixed syllogism inference, useful for applying a definition to both sides of an implication]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep R \leftrightarrow P \dsep S \leftrightarrow Q \vdash R \rightarrow S
}{3imtr4i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \leftrightarrow P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{R \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{3,1}}
  \proofstep{3}{S \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{Q \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{R \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{4,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[More general version of 3imtr3i]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \dsep
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S) \dsep
  P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow T)
  \vdash
  P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow T)
}{3imtr3d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimprd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syld}{3,1}}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow T)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpd}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syld}{4,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[More general version of 3imtr4i]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \dsep
  P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow Q) \dsep
  P \rightarrow (T \leftrightarrow R)
  \vdash
  P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow T)
}{3imtr4d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syld}{3,1}}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow (T \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimprd}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syld}{4,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[More general version of 3imtr3i]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \dsep Q \leftrightarrow S \dsep R \leftrightarrow T \vdash P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow T)
}{3imtr3g}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \leftrightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{S \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl5}{3,1}}
  \proofstep{3}{R \leftrightarrow T}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{R \rightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syld}{4,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[More general version of 3imtr4i]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \dsep S \leftrightarrow Q \dsep T \leftrightarrow R \vdash P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow T)
}{3imtr4g}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{S \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{S \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl5}{3,1}}
  \proofstep{3}{T \leftrightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{R \rightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syld}{4,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
Die Sätze \FormulaRefAuto{3imtr3i} bis \FormulaRefAuto{3imtr4g} sind besonders nützlich für definitorische Umschreibungen innerhalb komplexerer Formeln. Sie bilden gewissermaßen den Werkzeugkasten, um Äquivalenzen kontrolliert durch Implikationszusammenhänge hindurchzuschieben.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[A chained inference from transitive law for logical equivalence]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q \dsep Q \leftrightarrow R \dsep R \leftrightarrow S \vdash P \leftrightarrow S
}{3bitri}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \leftrightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{R \leftrightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2,3}{Q \leftrightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \leftrightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{1,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A chained inference from transitive law for logical equivalence]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q \dsep Q \leftrightarrow R \dsep R \leftrightarrow S \vdash S \leftrightarrow P
}{3bitrri}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{3}{R \leftrightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \leftrightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{R \leftrightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr2i}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{S \leftrightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr3i}{3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A chained inference from transitive law for logical equivalence]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q \dsep R \leftrightarrow Q \dsep R \leftrightarrow S \vdash P \leftrightarrow S
}{3bitr2i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \leftrightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr4i}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{3}{R \leftrightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \leftrightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A chained inference from transitive law for logical equivalence]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q \dsep R \leftrightarrow Q \dsep R \leftrightarrow S \vdash S \leftrightarrow P
}{3bitr2ri}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \leftrightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr4i}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{3}{R \leftrightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{S \leftrightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr2i}{3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A chained inference from transitive law for logical equivalence]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &(P \leftrightarrow Q)\dsep (P \leftrightarrow R)\dsep (Q \leftrightarrow S)\vdash{}\\
    &\quad R \leftrightarrow S
  \end{aligned}
}{3bitr3i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \leftrightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{Q \leftrightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{R \leftrightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr3i}{2,1}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{R \leftrightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{4,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A chained inference from transitive law for logical equivalence]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &(P \leftrightarrow Q)\dsep (P \leftrightarrow R)\dsep (Q \leftrightarrow S)\vdash{}\\
    &\quad S \leftrightarrow R
  \end{aligned}
}{3bitr3ri}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \leftrightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{Q \leftrightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{R \leftrightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr3i}{1,2,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{S \leftrightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A chained inference from transitive law for logical equivalence]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &(P \leftrightarrow Q)\dsep (R \leftrightarrow P)\dsep (S \leftrightarrow Q)\vdash{}\\
    &\quad R \leftrightarrow S
  \end{aligned}
}{3bitr4i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \leftrightarrow P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{S \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,3}{P \leftrightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr4i}{1,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{R \leftrightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A chained inference from transitive law for logical equivalence]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &(P \leftrightarrow Q)\dsep (R \leftrightarrow P)\dsep (S \leftrightarrow Q)\vdash{}\\
    &\quad S \leftrightarrow R
  \end{aligned}
}{3bitr4ri}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \leftrightarrow P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{S \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{R \leftrightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4i}{1,2,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{S \leftrightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction from transitivity of biconditional]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\dsep
    P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)\dsep
    P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow T)\vdash{}\\
    &\quad P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow T)
  \end{aligned}
}{3bitrd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow T)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitrd}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitrd}{4,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction from transitivity of biconditional]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\dsep
    P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)\dsep
    P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow T)\vdash{}\\
    &\quad P \rightarrow (T \leftrightarrow Q)
  \end{aligned}
}{3bitrrd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow T)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitrd}{1,2,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow (T \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomd}{4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction from transitivity of biconditional]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\dsep
    P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow R)\dsep
    P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow T)\vdash{}\\
    &\quad P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow T)
  \end{aligned}
}{3bitr2d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow T)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitrd}{1,4}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitrd}{5,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction from transitivity of biconditional]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\dsep
    P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow R)\dsep
    P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow T)\vdash{}\\
    &\quad P \rightarrow (T \leftrightarrow Q)
  \end{aligned}
}{3bitr2rd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow T)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr2d}{1,2,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow (T \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomd}{4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction from transitivity of biconditional]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\dsep
    P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)\dsep
    P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow T)\vdash{}\\
    &\quad P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow T)
  \end{aligned}
}{3bitr3d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow T)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr3d}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitrd}{4,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction from transitivity of biconditional]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\dsep
    P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)\dsep
    P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow T)\vdash{}\\
    &\quad P \rightarrow (T \leftrightarrow S)
  \end{aligned}
}{3bitr3rd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow T)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr3d}{1,2,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow (T \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomd}{4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction from transitivity of biconditional]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\dsep
    P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow Q)\dsep
    P \rightarrow (T \leftrightarrow R)\vdash{}\\
    &\quad P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow T)
  \end{aligned}
}{3bitr4d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow (T \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr4d}{1,2}}
