  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomd}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitrd}{4,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction from transitivity of biconditional]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\dsep
    P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow Q)\dsep
    P \rightarrow (T \leftrightarrow R)\vdash{}\\
    &\quad P \rightarrow (T \leftrightarrow S)
  \end{aligned}
}{3bitr4rd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow (T \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4d}{1,2,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow (T \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomd}{4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[More general version of 3bitr3i]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\dsep (Q \leftrightarrow S)\dsep (R \leftrightarrow T)\vdash{}\\
    &\quad P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow T)
  \end{aligned}
}{3bitr3g}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \leftrightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{R \leftrightarrow T}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{S \leftrightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{2}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{4}}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4d}{1,5,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[More general version of 3bitr4i]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\dsep (S \leftrightarrow Q)\dsep (T \leftrightarrow R)\vdash{}\\
    &\quad P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow T)
  \end{aligned}
}{3bitr4g}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{S \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{T \leftrightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{R \leftrightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,4}{P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr3g}{1,2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
Die Familie \FormulaRefAuto{3bitr3i} bis \FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4g} ist besonders nützlich, wenn Definitionsäquivalenzen schrittweise in größere Formeln eingesetzt werden sollen.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Double negation]{%
  (P \leftrightarrow \neg \neg P)
}{notnotb}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow \neg \neg P}{\FormulaRefAuto{notnot}}
  \proofstep{}{\neg \neg P \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{notnotr}}
  \proofstep{}{P \leftrightarrow \neg \neg P}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A biconditional form of contraposition]{%
  ((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow \neg P))
}{con34b}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg Q \rightarrow \neg P) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con4}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow \neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A contraposition deduction]{%
  P \rightarrow (\neg Q \leftrightarrow \neg R)\vdash P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)
}{con4bid}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\neg Q \leftrightarrow \neg R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\neg R \rightarrow \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimprd}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con4d}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow \neg R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpd}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impcon4bid}{3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction negating both sides of a logical equivalence]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\vdash P \rightarrow (\neg Q \leftrightarrow \neg R)
}{notbid}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{Q \leftrightarrow \neg \neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{notnotb}}
  \proofstep{}{R \leftrightarrow \neg \neg R}{\FormulaRefAuto{notnotb}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\neg \neg Q \leftrightarrow \neg \neg R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr3g}{1,2,3}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\neg Q \leftrightarrow \neg R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con4bid}{4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Contraposition]{%
  ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \leftrightarrow \neg Q))
}{notbi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\neg P \leftrightarrow \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{notbid}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \leftrightarrow \neg Q) \rightarrow (\neg P \leftrightarrow \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \leftrightarrow \neg Q) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con4bid}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \leftrightarrow \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Negate both sides of a logical equivalence]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q\vdash \neg P \leftrightarrow \neg Q
}{notbii}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \leftrightarrow \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{notbi}}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg P \leftrightarrow \neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A contraposition inference]{%
  \neg P \leftrightarrow \neg Q\vdash P \leftrightarrow Q
}{con4bii}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg P \leftrightarrow \neg Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \leftrightarrow \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{notbi}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbir}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
Mit \FormulaRefAuto{notbid}, \FormulaRefAuto{notbi}, \FormulaRefAuto{notbii} und \FormulaRefAuto{con4bii} steht nun ein kleiner Standardapparat bereit, um Äquivalenzen unter Negation hin- und herzutransportieren.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[An inference from a biconditional, related to modus tollens]{%
  \neg P\dsep P \leftrightarrow Q\vdash \neg Q
}{mtbi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{mto}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[An inference from a biconditional, related to modus tollens]{%
  \neg Q\dsep P \leftrightarrow Q\vdash \neg P
}{mtbir}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \leftrightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\neg P}{\FormulaRefAuto{mtbi}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A deduction from a biconditional, similar to modus tollens]{%
  P \rightarrow \neg Q\dsep P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\vdash P \rightarrow \neg R
}{mtbid}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow \neg Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimprd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow \neg R}{\FormulaRefAuto{mtod}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A deduction from a biconditional, similar to modus tollens]{%
  P \rightarrow \neg R\dsep P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\vdash P \rightarrow \neg Q
}{mtbird}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow \neg R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow \neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{mtod}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[An inference from a biconditional, similar to modus tollens]{%
  \neg Q\dsep P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\vdash P \rightarrow \neg R
}{mtbii}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimprd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow \neg R}{\FormulaRefAuto{mtoi}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[An inference from a biconditional, similar to modus tollens]{%
  \neg R\dsep P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\vdash P \rightarrow \neg Q
}{mtbiri}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow \neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{mtoi}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A mixed syllogism inference from an implication and a biconditional]{%
  P \rightarrow \neg Q\dsep Q \leftrightarrow R\vdash P \rightarrow \neg R
}{sylnib}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow \neg Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \leftrightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow \neg R}{\FormulaRefAuto{nsyl}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A mixed syllogism inference from an implication and a biconditional]{%
  P \rightarrow \neg Q\dsep R \leftrightarrow Q\vdash P \rightarrow \neg R
}{sylnibr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow \neg Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \leftrightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow \neg R}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylnib}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A mixed syllogism inference from a biconditional and an implication]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q\dsep \neg Q \rightarrow R\vdash \neg P \rightarrow R
}{sylnbi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg P \leftrightarrow \neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{notbii}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{\neg Q \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\neg P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylbi}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A mixed syllogism inference from a biconditional and an implication]{%
  Q \leftrightarrow P\dsep \neg Q \rightarrow R\vdash \neg P \rightarrow R
}{sylnbir}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \leftrightarrow P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{\neg Q \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\neg P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylnbi}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Replacement of a subexpression by an equivalent one]{%
  \neg P \leftrightarrow Q\dsep P \leftrightarrow R\vdash \neg R \leftrightarrow Q
}{xchnxbi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{2}{P \leftrightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\neg P \leftrightarrow \neg R}{\FormulaRefAuto{notbii}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\neg R \leftrightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr3i}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Replacement of a subexpression by an equivalent one]{%
  \neg P \leftrightarrow Q\dsep R \leftrightarrow P\vdash \neg R \leftrightarrow Q
}{xchnxbir}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \leftrightarrow P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \leftrightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\neg R \leftrightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{xchnxbi}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Replacement of a subexpression by an equivalent one]{%
  P \leftrightarrow \neg Q\dsep Q \leftrightarrow R\vdash P \leftrightarrow \neg R
}{xchbinx}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow \neg Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \leftrightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\neg Q \leftrightarrow \neg R}{\FormulaRefAuto{notbii}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \leftrightarrow \neg R}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Replacement of a subexpression by an equivalent one]{%
  P \leftrightarrow \neg Q\dsep R \leftrightarrow Q\vdash P \leftrightarrow \neg R
}{xchbinxr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow \neg Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \leftrightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \leftrightarrow \neg R}{\FormulaRefAuto{xchbinx}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Introduce an antecedent to both sides of a logical equivalence]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q\vdash (R \rightarrow P) \leftrightarrow (R \rightarrow Q)
}{imbi2i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{R \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(R \rightarrow P) \leftrightarrow (R \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74i}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inference adding a biconditional to the left in an equivalence]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q\vdash (R \leftrightarrow P) \leftrightarrow (R \leftrightarrow Q)
}{bibi2i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(R \leftrightarrow P) \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(R \leftrightarrow P) \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitrdi}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(R \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{1}{(R \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr4di}{4,2}}
  \proofstep{1}{(R \leftrightarrow P) \leftrightarrow (R \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{3,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inference adding a biconditional to the right in an equivalence]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q\vdash (P \leftrightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)
}{bibi1i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \leftrightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (R \leftrightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicom}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(R \leftrightarrow P) \leftrightarrow (R \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bibi2i}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(R \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicom}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \leftrightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitri}{1,3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[The equivalence of two equivalences]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q\dsep R \leftrightarrow S\vdash (P \leftrightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)
}{bibi12i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{2}{R \leftrightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \leftrightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bibi2i}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \leftrightarrow S) \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bibi1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \leftrightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction adding an antecedent to both sides of a logical equivalence]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\vdash P \rightarrow ((S \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (S \rightarrow R))
}{imbi2d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1d}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((S \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (S \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74d}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction adding a consequent to both sides of a logical equivalence]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\vdash P \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow S) \leftrightarrow (R \rightarrow S))
}{imbi1d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimprd}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow S) \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imim1d}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpd}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((R \rightarrow S) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imim1d}{4}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow S) \leftrightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid}{3,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction adding a biconditional to the left in an equivalence]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\vdash P \rightarrow ((S \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (S \leftrightarrow R))
}{bibi2d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74i}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \rightarrow S) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)) \leftrightarrow ((P \rightarrow S) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bibi2i}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow Q)) \leftrightarrow ((P \rightarrow S) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \rightarrow S) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow Q)) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4i}{4,3,5}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((S \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (S \leftrightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74ri}{6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction adding a biconditional to the right in an equivalence]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\vdash P \rightarrow ((Q \leftrightarrow S) \leftrightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S))
}{bibi1d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((S \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (S \leftrightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bibi2d}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \leftrightarrow S) \leftrightarrow (S \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicom}}
  \proofstep{}{(R \leftrightarrow S) \leftrightarrow (S \leftrightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicom}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((Q \leftrightarrow S) \leftrightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4g}{2,3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction joining two equivalences to form equivalence of implications]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\dsep P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow T)\vdash{}\\
    &\quad P \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow S) \leftrightarrow (R \rightarrow T))
  \end{aligned}
}{imbi12d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow S) \leftrightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi1d}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow T)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow ((R \rightarrow S) \leftrightarrow (R \rightarrow T))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi2d}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow S) \leftrightarrow (R \rightarrow T))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitrd}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction joining two equivalences to form equivalence of biconditionals]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\dsep P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow T)\vdash{}\\
    &\quad P \rightarrow ((Q \leftrightarrow S) \leftrightarrow (R \leftrightarrow T))
  \end{aligned}
}{bibi12d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((Q \leftrightarrow S) \leftrightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bibi1d}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow T)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow ((R \leftrightarrow S) \leftrightarrow (R \leftrightarrow T))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bibi2d}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow ((Q \leftrightarrow S) \leftrightarrow (R \leftrightarrow T))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitrd}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Closed form of imbi12i]{%
  ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((R \leftrightarrow S) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow S))))
}{imbi12}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{%
    \neg((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(R \leftrightarrow S)) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{simplim}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    \neg((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(R \leftrightarrow S)) \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{simprim}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    \neg((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(R \leftrightarrow S)) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow S))
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi12d}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((R \leftrightarrow S) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow S))))
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{expi}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *4.84 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)))
}{imbi1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi1d}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *4.85 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((R \rightarrow P) \leftrightarrow (R \rightarrow Q)))
}{imbi2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((R \rightarrow P) \leftrightarrow (R \rightarrow Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi2d}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Introduce a consequent to both sides of a logical equivalence]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q\vdash (P \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)
}{imbi1i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Join two logical equivalences to form equivalence of implications]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q\dsep R \leftrightarrow S\vdash (P \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)
}{imbi12i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \leftrightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((R \leftrightarrow S) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow S))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi12}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mp2}{1,2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *4.86 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \leftrightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)))
}{bibi1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \leftrightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bibi1d}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Closed nested implication form of bitr3i]{%
  ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \leftrightarrow R) \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)))
}{bitr3}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \leftrightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bibi1}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \leftrightarrow R) \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpd}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
Die Regeln \FormulaRefAuto{imbi2i} bis \FormulaRefAuto{bibi12d} sind genau die Werkzeuge, mit denen man eine bereits bekannte Äquivalenz systematisch in größere Implikations- und Äquivalenzterme hineinträgt.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Contraposition]{%
  ((P \leftrightarrow \neg Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow \neg P))
}{con2bi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow \neg Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \leftrightarrow \neg \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{notbi}}
  \proofstep{}{Q \leftrightarrow \neg \neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{notnotb}}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \leftrightarrow \neg \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bibi2i}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow \neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicom}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow \neg Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow \neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr2i}{1,3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A contraposition deduction]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow \neg R)\vdash P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow \neg Q)
}{con2bid}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow \neg R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((R \leftrightarrow \neg Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow \neg R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{con2bi}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylibr}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A contraposition deduction]{%
  P \rightarrow (\neg Q \leftrightarrow R)\vdash P \rightarrow (\neg R \leftrightarrow Q)
}{con1bid}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\neg Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomd}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow \neg R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con2bid}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\neg R \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomd}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A contraposition inference]{%
  \neg P \leftrightarrow Q\vdash \neg Q \leftrightarrow P
}{con1bii}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{P \leftrightarrow \neg \neg P}{\FormulaRefAuto{notnotb}}
  \proofstep{2}{\neg P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \leftrightarrow \neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{xchbinx}{2,1}}
  \proofstep{2}{\neg Q \leftrightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A contraposition inference]{%
  P \leftrightarrow \neg Q\vdash Q \leftrightarrow \neg P
}{con2bii}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{Q \leftrightarrow \neg \neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{notnotb}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \leftrightarrow \neg Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \leftrightarrow \neg P}{\FormulaRefAuto{xchbinxr}{2,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Contraposition. Bidirectional version of con1]{%
  ((\neg P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow P))
}{con1b}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg Q \rightarrow P) \rightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con1}}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Contraposition. Bidirectional version of con2]{%
  ((P \rightarrow \neg Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow \neg P))
}{con2b}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow \neg Q) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con2}}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \rightarrow \neg P) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con2}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow \neg Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow \neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A wff is equivalent to itself with true antecedent]{%
  (P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)))
}{biimt}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-1}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.27}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid2}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *5.5 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  (P \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow Q))
}{pm5.5}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimt}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomd}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inference introducing a theorem as an antecedent]{%
  P\vdash Q \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)
}{a1bi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimt}}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A false consequent falsifies an antecedent]{%
  P\vdash \neg Q \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow \neg P)
}{mt2bi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg Q \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1bi}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow \neg Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow \neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{con2b}}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg Q \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Modus-tollens-like theorem]{%
  (\neg P \rightarrow (\neg Q \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow P)))
}{mtt}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \rightarrow (\neg Q \leftrightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow \neg Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimt}}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \rightarrow P) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{con34b}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \rightarrow (\neg Q \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr4di}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[If a proposition is false, then implying it is equivalent to being false]{%
  (\neg Q \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \neg P))
}{imnot}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg Q \rightarrow (\neg P \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{mtt}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg Q \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomd}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *5.501 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  (P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)))
}{pm5.501}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.1im}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimp}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{com12}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Implication in terms of implication and biconditional]{%
  ((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)))
}{ibib}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.501}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74i}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Implication in terms of implication and biconditional]{%
  ((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow P)))
}{ibibr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.501}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicom}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitrdi}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74i}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A wff is equivalent to its equivalence with a truth]{%
  P\vdash Q \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow P)
}{tbt}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ibibr}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74ri}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[The negation of a wff is equivalent to the wff's equivalence to falsehood]{%
  (\neg P \rightarrow (\neg Q \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)))
}{nbn2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \rightarrow (\neg Q \leftrightarrow (\neg P \leftrightarrow \neg Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.501}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \leftrightarrow \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{notbi}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \rightarrow (\neg Q \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr4di}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Transfer negation via an equivalence]{%
  (\neg Q \rightarrow ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \neg P))
}{bibif}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg Q \rightarrow (\neg P \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nbn2}}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \leftrightarrow P) \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicom}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg Q \rightarrow ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr2di}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[The negation of a wff is equivalent to the wff's equivalence to falsehood]{%
  \neg P\vdash \neg Q \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow P)
}{nbn}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \rightarrow ((Q \leftrightarrow P) \leftrightarrow \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bibif}}
  \proofstep{1}{(Q \leftrightarrow P) \leftrightarrow \neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg Q \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Transfer falsehood via equivalence]{%
  P\vdash \neg Q \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow \neg P)
}{nbn3}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg \neg P}{\FormulaRefAuto{notnoti}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg Q \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nbn}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Two propositions are equivalent if they are both false]{%
  (\neg P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)))
}{pm5.21im}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \rightarrow (\neg Q \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nbn2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpd}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Two falsehoods are equivalent]{%
  \neg P\dsep \neg Q\vdash P \leftrightarrow Q
}{2false}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\neg Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\neg P \leftrightarrow \neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{2th}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{con4bii}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Two falsehoods are equivalent (deduction form)]{%
  P \rightarrow \neg Q\dsep P \rightarrow \neg R\vdash P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)
}{2falsed}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow \neg Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow \neg R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (\neg Q \leftrightarrow \neg R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{2thd}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con4bid}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Two propositions implying a false one are equivalent]{%
  P \rightarrow Q\dsep R \rightarrow Q\vdash \neg Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R)
}{pm5.21ni}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg Q \rightarrow \neg P}{\FormulaRefAuto{con3i}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\neg Q \rightarrow \neg R}{\FormulaRefAuto{con3i}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\neg Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{2falsed}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Eliminate an antecedent implied by each side of a biconditional]{%
  P \rightarrow Q\dsep R \rightarrow Q\dsep Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R)\vdash P \leftrightarrow R
}{pm5.21nii}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{3}{Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\neg Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.21ni}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \leftrightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61i}{3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Eliminate an antecedent implied by each side of a biconditional, deduction version]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)\dsep
    P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow R)\dsep
    P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S))\vdash{}\\
    &\quad P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)
  \end{aligned}
}{pm5.21ndd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\neg R \rightarrow \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con3d}{2}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (\neg R \rightarrow \neg S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con3d}{4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg Q \rightarrow (\neg S \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.21im}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (\neg R \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl6c}{3,5,6}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61d}{1,7}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Combine antecedents into a single biconditional]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)\dsep \neg P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow R)\vdash{}\\
    &\quad (P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow R
  \end{aligned}
}{bija}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpr}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syli}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimp}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con3d}{4}}
  \proofstep{2}{\neg P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syli}{5,6}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61d}{3,7}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *5.18 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(P \leftrightarrow \neg Q))
}{pm5.18}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (\neg Q \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.501}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (\neg(P \leftrightarrow \neg Q) \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con1bid}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.501}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(P \leftrightarrow \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr2d}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{}{\neg P \rightarrow (\neg \neg Q \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nbn2}}
  \proofstep{}{\neg P \rightarrow (\neg(P \leftrightarrow \neg Q) \leftrightarrow \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con1bid}{5}}
  \proofstep{}{\neg P \rightarrow (\neg Q \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nbn2}}
  \proofstep{}{\neg P \rightarrow ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(P \leftrightarrow \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr2d}{6,7}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(P \leftrightarrow \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61i}{4,8}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Two ways to express exclusive or]{%
  (\neg(P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow \neg Q))
}{xor3}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(P \leftrightarrow \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.18}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow \neg Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(P \leftrightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{con2bii}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Move negation outside of biconditional]{%
  ((\neg P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(P \leftrightarrow Q))
}{nbbn}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{xor3}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow \neg Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow \neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{con2bi}}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \leftrightarrow \neg P) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \leftrightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicom}}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(P \leftrightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitrri}{1,2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Associative law for the biconditional]{%
  (((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)))
}{biass}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.501}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow ((Q \leftrightarrow R) \leftrightarrow ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bibi1d}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow ((Q \leftrightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.501}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr3d}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg Q \leftrightarrow R) \leftrightarrow \neg(Q \leftrightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nbbn}}
  \proofstep{}{\neg P \rightarrow (\neg Q \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nbn2}}
  \proofstep{}{\neg P \rightarrow ((\neg Q \leftrightarrow R) \leftrightarrow ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bibi1d}{6}}
  \proofstep{}{\neg P \rightarrow (\neg(Q \leftrightarrow R) \leftrightarrow ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr3id}{5,7}}
  \proofstep{}{\neg P \rightarrow (\neg(Q \leftrightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nbn2}}
  \proofstep{}{\neg P \rightarrow (((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr3d}{8,9}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61i}{4,10}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Lukasiewicz's shortest axiom for equivalential calculus]{%
  ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow ((R \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R)))
}{biluk}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicom}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow R) \leftrightarrow ((Q \leftrightarrow P) \leftrightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bibi1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(((Q \leftrightarrow P) \leftrightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biass}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{}{((((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R))) \leftrightarrow ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (R \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R))))) }{\FormulaRefAuto{biass}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (R \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbi}{4,5}}
  \proofstep{}{(((R \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow (R \leftrightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biass}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow ((R \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr4i}{6,7}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *5.19 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  \neg(P \leftrightarrow \neg P)
}{pm5.19}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{P \leftrightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{biid}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow P) \leftrightarrow \neg(P \leftrightarrow \neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.18}}
  \proofstep{}{\neg(P \leftrightarrow \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Logical equivalence of commuted antecedents]{%
  ((P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)))
}{bi2.04}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.04}}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.04}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Antecedent absorption implication]{%
  ((P \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))
}{pm5.4}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.5}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74i}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Distributive law for implication]{%
  ((P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)))
}{imdi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-2}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.86}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *5.41 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  (((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)))
}{pm5.41}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imdi}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[The antecedent of one side of a biconditional can be moved out of the biconditional]{%
  (((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow R) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)))
}{imbibi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.4}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow R) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi2}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow R) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr3id}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow R) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74rd}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *4.8 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  ((P \rightarrow \neg P) \leftrightarrow \neg P)
}{pm4.8}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow \neg P) \rightarrow \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.01}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \rightarrow (P \rightarrow \neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-1}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow \neg P) \leftrightarrow \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A formula is equivalent to its negation implying it]{%
  ((\neg P \rightarrow P) \leftrightarrow P)
}{pm4.81}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg P \rightarrow P) \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.18}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.24}}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg P \rightarrow P) \leftrightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Simplify an implication between two implications]{%
  ((Q \rightarrow P) \rightarrow (((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))))
}{imim21b}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow S)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bi2.04}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow S) \leftrightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.5}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow S)) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi1d}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \rightarrow P) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow S) \leftrightarrow (R \rightarrow S)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imim2i}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \rightarrow P) \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow S)) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74d}{4}}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \rightarrow P) \rightarrow (((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitrid}{1,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
Mit \FormulaRefAuto{pm5.18}, \FormulaRefAuto{xor3}, \FormulaRefAuto{nbbn} und \FormulaRefAuto{biass} ist der Äquivalenzkalkül nun so weit ausgebaut, dass auch exklusives Oder, Negationsverschiebungen und Assoziativität direkt verfügbar sind.
\end{remark}

\section{Logische Konjunktion}

\FormulaDef[Conjunction as a formation rule]{%
  \text{If } P \text{ and } Q \text{ are wffs, then } (P \land Q) \text{ is a wff.}%
}{wa}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}

\FormulaDef[Define conjunction (logical "and")]{%
  ((P \land Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(P \rightarrow \neg Q))
}{df-an}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{df-an} ist die erste Definition in diesem Aufbau, die die Äquivalenz selbst als Definitionsform benutzt. Das ist hier besonders praktisch, weil sich die Definition anschließend unmittelbar in Beweisen verwenden lässt.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *4.63 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  (\neg(P \rightarrow \neg Q) \leftrightarrow (P \land Q))
}{pm4.63}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(P \rightarrow \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-an}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(P \rightarrow \neg Q) \leftrightarrow (P \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *4.67 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  (\neg(\neg P \rightarrow \neg Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \land Q))
}{pm4.67}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(\neg P \rightarrow \neg Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm4.63}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Express an implication in terms of a negated conjunction]{%
  ((P \rightarrow \neg Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(P \land Q))
}{imnan}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(P \rightarrow \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-an}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow \neg Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(P \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{con2bii}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Infer an implication from a negated conjunction]{%
  \neg(P \land Q)\vdash P \rightarrow \neg Q
}{imnani}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg(P \land Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow \neg Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(P \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imnan}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow \neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbir}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Implikation in Termen von Konjunktion und Negation]{%
  ((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(P \land \neg Q))
}{iman}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{Q \leftrightarrow \neg\neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{notnotb}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow \neg\neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi2i}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow \neg\neg Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(P \land \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{imnan}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(P \land \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Gesetz der Nicht-Widersprüchlichkeit]{%
  \neg(P \land \neg P)
}{pm3.24}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow P) \leftrightarrow \neg(P \land \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{iman}}
  \proofstep{}{\neg(P \land \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{pm3.24} ist die klassische Form des Widerspruchsprinzips: eine Aussage kann nicht zugleich wahr und falsch sein.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Drücke eine Konjunktion als negierte Implikation aus]{%
  ((P \land \neg Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(P \rightarrow Q))
}{annim}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(P \land \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{iman}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land \neg Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con2bii}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*4.61$}{*4.61} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  (\neg(P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \land \neg Q))
}{pm4.61}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land \neg Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{annim}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \land \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*4.65$}{*4.65} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  (\neg(\neg P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \land \neg Q))
}{pm4.65}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(\neg P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \land \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm4.61}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Importations-Inferenz]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)\vdash (P \land Q) \rightarrow R
}{imp}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(P \rightarrow \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-an}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\neg(P \rightarrow \neg Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{impi}{2}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylbi}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Importations-Inferenz mit vertauschten Antezedenzien]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)\vdash (Q \land P) \rightarrow R
}{impcom}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{com12}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(Q \land P) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Variante von \FormulaRefAuto{con3d} mit Importation]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)\vdash (P \land \neg R) \rightarrow \neg Q
}{con3dimp}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\neg R \rightarrow \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con3d}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land \neg R) \rightarrow \neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Eliminiere den rechten Konjunkt aus einer negierten Konjunktion unter einer Implikation]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep P \rightarrow \neg(Q \land R)\vdash P \rightarrow \neg R
}{mpnanrd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow \neg(Q \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \rightarrow \neg R) \leftrightarrow \neg(Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{imnan}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow \neg R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylibr}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow \neg R}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpd}{1,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Importations-Deduktion]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))\vdash P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S)
}{impd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow (P \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{com3l}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{com12}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Importations-Deduktion mit vertauschten Antezedenzien]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))\vdash P \rightarrow ((R \land Q) \rightarrow S)
}{impcomd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{com23}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((R \land Q) \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impd}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Exportations-Inferenz]{%
  (P \land Q) \rightarrow R\vdash P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)
}{ex}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(P \rightarrow \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-an}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\neg(P \rightarrow \neg Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylbir}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{expi}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{imp} und \FormulaRefAuto{ex} sind die beiden elementaren Richtungen des Import-/Export-Prinzips. Sie bilden den technischen Kern fast aller folgenden Umklammerungs- und Vertauschungssätze für Konjunktionen im Antezedens.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Exportations-Inferenz mit vertauschten Antezedenzien]{%
  (P \land Q) \rightarrow R\vdash Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)
}{expcom}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{com12}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Vertauschte Form von \FormulaRefAuto{expd}]{%
  P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S)\vdash Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow (P \rightarrow S))
}{expdcom}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{com12}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow (P \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Exportations-Deduktion]{%
  P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S)\vdash P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))
}{expd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow (P \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{expdcom}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{com3r}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktive Form von \FormulaRefAuto{expcom}]{%
  P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S)\vdash P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S))
}{expcomd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{expd}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{com23}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Eine Importations-Inferenz]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))\vdash{}\\
    &\quad ((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S
  \end{aligned}
}{imp31}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Eine Importations-Inferenz]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))\vdash{}\\
    &\quad (P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S
  \end{aligned}
}{imp32}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\rA}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*3.3$}{*3.3} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  (((P \land Q) \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)))
}{pm3.3}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \rightarrow R) \rightarrow ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \land Q) \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{expd}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*3.31$}{*3.31} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  ((P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R))
}{pm3.31}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impd}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Import-Export-Satz]{%
  (((P \land Q) \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)))
}{impexp}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm3.3}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm3.31}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \land Q) \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{impexp} identifiziert die beiden Sichtweisen auf einen zweistelligen Vordersatz: entweder als Konjunktion im Antezedens oder als verschachtelte Implikation. Dieser Satz ist einer der wichtigsten organisatorischen Knotenpunkte des ganzen Abschnitts.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Gemischte Importations-/Vertauschungs-Inferenz]{%
  ((P \land Q) \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))\vdash ((P \land R) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S))
}{impancom}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land Q) \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{com23}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land R) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktive Exportation gefolgt von Importation]{%
  P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S)\vdash (P \land Q) \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)
}{expdimp}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{expd}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Exportation gefolgt von deduktiver Importation]{%
  ((P \land Q) \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))\vdash P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S)
}{expimpd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land Q) \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impd}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Importiere eine Aussage in den rechten Konjunkt]{%
  ((P \land Q) \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))\vdash (P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S
}{impr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land Q) \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp32}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Exportiere eine Aussage aus dem linken Konjunkt]{%
  P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S)\vdash ((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S
}{impl}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{expd}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp31}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Exportiere eine Aussage aus dem rechten Konjunkt]{%
  (P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S\vdash (P \land Q) \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)
}{expr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exp32}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Exportiere eine Aussage aus dem linken Konjunkt]{%
  ((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S\vdash P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S)
}{expl}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exp31}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impd}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Vertauschung einer Konjunktion im Antezedens]{%
  ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)\vdash ((Q \land P) \rightarrow R)
}{ancoms}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{expcom}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{((Q \land P) \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*3.22$}{*3.22} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  ((P \land Q) \rightarrow (Q \land P))
}{pm3.22}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \land P) \rightarrow (Q \land P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \rightarrow (Q \land P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancoms}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Kommutativgesetz der Konjunktion]{%
  ((P \land Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \land P))
}{ancom}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \rightarrow (Q \land P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm3.22}}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \land P) \rightarrow (P \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm3.22}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \land P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{ancom} macht sichtbar, dass die Reihenfolge der Konjunkte inhaltlich keine Rolle spielt. Viele spätere Umformungen mit \FormulaRefAuto{ancomd}, \FormulaRefAuto{ancomst} und \FormulaRefAuto{biancomi} sind nur verpackte Anwendungen genau dieses Grundsatzes.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Vertauschung der Konjunkte im Konsequens]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \land R)\vdash P \rightarrow (R \land Q)
}{ancomd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \land R) \leftrightarrow (R \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancom}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (R \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylib}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Vertauschung einer Konjunktion innerhalb einer Äquivalenz]{%
  P \leftrightarrow (R \land Q)\vdash P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)
}{biancomi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow (R \land Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \land R) \leftrightarrow (R \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancom}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr4i}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Vertauschung einer Konjunktion innerhalb einer Äquivalenz, Deduktionsform]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (S \land R))\vdash P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (R \land S))
}{biancomd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (S \land R))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(S \land R) \leftrightarrow (R \land S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancom}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (R \land S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitrdi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Geschlossene Form von \FormulaRefAuto{ancoms}]{%
  (((P \land Q) \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow ((Q \land P) \rightarrow R))
}{ancomst}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \land P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancom}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \land Q) \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow ((Q \land P) \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi1i}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktive Vertauschung einer Konjunktion im Antezedens]{%
  P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S)\vdash P \rightarrow ((R \land Q) \rightarrow S)
}{ancomsd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{expcomd}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((R \land Q) \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impd}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Assoziativgesetz der Konjunktion im Antezedens]{%
  ((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S\vdash (P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S
}{anasss}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exp31}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp32}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Umkehrung des Assoziativgesetzes der Konjunktion im Antezedens]{%
  (P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S\vdash ((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S
}{anassrs}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exp32}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp31}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Assoziativgesetz der Konjunktion]{%
  (((P \land Q) \land R) \leftrightarrow (P \land (Q \land R)))
}{anass}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow (P \land (Q \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow (P \land (Q \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anassrs}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow ((P \land Q) \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow ((P \land Q) \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{anasss}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \land Q) \land R) \leftrightarrow (P \land (Q \land R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{anass} erlaubt es, längere Konjunktionen ohne inhaltlichen Verlust anders zu klammern. Zusammen mit \FormulaRefAuto{ancom} liefert das die übliche Freiheit im Umgang mit endlichen Konjunktionen.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Konjunktionseinführung]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \land Q))
}{pm3.2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow (P \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inferenz zu \FormulaRefAuto{pm3.2}]{%
  P \dsep Q\vdash P \land Q
}{pm3.2i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm3.2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \land Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{mp2}{1,2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{pm3.2} und \FormulaRefAuto{pm3.2i} sind die grundlegenden Einführungsformen der Konjunktion. Zusammen mit \FormulaRefAuto{simpl} und \FormulaRefAuto{simpr} erhält man daraus genau das übliche Einführungs-/Eliminationsschema für \(\land\).
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*3.21$}{*3.21} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (Q \land P))
}{pm3.21}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \land P) \rightarrow (Q \land P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (Q \land P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{expcom}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Verschachtelte Konjunktion von Vordersätzen]{%
  ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \land R))))
}{pm3.43i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow (Q \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm3.2}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \land R))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imim3i}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*3.43$}{*3.43} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  (((P \rightarrow Q) \land (P \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \land R)))
}{pm3.43}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \land R))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm3.43i}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \land (P \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \land R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Äquivalenz als Konjunktion der beiden Richtungen]{%
  ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \land (Q \rightarrow P)))
}{dfbi2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{dfbi1}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \land (Q \rightarrow P)) \leftrightarrow \neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-an}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \land (Q \rightarrow P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr4i}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Abgekürzte Form der Definition der Äquivalenz]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &(((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \land (Q \rightarrow P)))\\
    &\quad \land (((P \rightarrow Q) \land (Q \rightarrow P)) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)))
  \end{aligned}
}{dfbi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \land (Q \rightarrow P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{dfbi2}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    \begin{aligned}[t]
      &((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \land (Q \rightarrow P)))\\
      &\leftrightarrow{}\\
      &\Bigl(((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \land (Q \rightarrow P)))\\
      &\quad \land (((P \rightarrow Q) \land (Q \rightarrow P)) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q))\Bigr)
    \end{aligned}
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{dfbi2}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    \begin{aligned}[t]
      &(((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \land (Q \rightarrow P)))\\
      &\quad \land (((P \rightarrow Q) \land (Q \rightarrow P)) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)))
    \end{aligned}
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{dfbi2} ist meist die anschaulichere Form: eine Äquivalenz ist genau dann erfüllt, wenn beide Implikationen gelten. \FormulaRefAuto{dfbi} zerlegt dieselbe Aussage noch einmal explizit in beide Richtungen.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Importations-Inferenz aus einer Äquivalenz]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\vdash (P \land Q) \rightarrow R
}{biimpa}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpd}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Importations-Inferenz aus einer Äquivalenz]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\vdash (P \land R) \rightarrow Q
}{biimpar}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimprd}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land R) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Importations-Inferenz aus einer Äquivalenz]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\vdash (Q \land P) \rightarrow R
}{biimpac}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpcd}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(Q \land P) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Importations-Inferenz aus einer Äquivalenz]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\vdash (R \land P) \rightarrow Q
}{biimparc}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{R \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimprcd}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(R \land P) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Füge rechts einen Konjunkt zum Antezedens hinzu]{%
  P \rightarrow Q\vdash (P \land R) \rightarrow Q
