}{adantr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1d}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land R) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Füge links einen Konjunkt zum Antezedens hinzu]{%
  P \rightarrow Q\vdash (R \land P) \rightarrow Q
}{adantl}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land R) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantr}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(R \land P) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancoms}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Elimination des linken Konjunkts]{%
  ((P \land Q) \rightarrow P)
}{simpl}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantr}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inferenz zur Elimination des linken Konjunkts]{%
  (P \land Q)\vdash P
}{simpli}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Elimination des rechten Konjunkts]{%
  ((P \land Q) \rightarrow Q)
}{simpr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{Q \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantl}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inferenz zur Elimination des rechten Konjunkts]{%
  (P \land Q)\vdash Q
}{simpri}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{1}{Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Führe innerhalb eines Widerspruchs einen linken Konjunkt ein]{%
  \neg P\vdash \neg(Q \land P)
}{intnan}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \land P) \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg(Q \land P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mto}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Führe innerhalb eines Widerspruchs einen rechten Konjunkt ein]{%
  \neg P\vdash \neg(P \land Q)
}{intnanr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg(P \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mto}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Führe unter einer Implikation einen linken Konjunkt in einen Widerspruch ein]{%
  P \rightarrow \neg Q\vdash P \rightarrow \neg(R \land Q)
}{intnand}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow \neg Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((R \land Q) \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow \neg(R \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nsyl}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Führe unter einer Implikation einen rechten Konjunkt in einen Widerspruch ein]{%
  P \rightarrow \neg Q\vdash P \rightarrow \neg(Q \land R)
}{intnanrd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow \neg Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \land R) \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow \neg(Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nsyl}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktiv füge links einen Konjunkt zum Antezedens hinzu]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)\vdash P \rightarrow ((S \land Q) \rightarrow R)
}{adantld}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((S \land Q) \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow ((S \land Q) \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl5}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktiv füge rechts einen Konjunkt zum Antezedens hinzu]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)\vdash P \rightarrow ((Q \land S) \rightarrow R)
}{adantrd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \land S) \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land S) \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl5}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*3.41$}{*3.41} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  ((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R))
}{pm3.41}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imim1i}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*3.42$}{*3.42} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  ((Q \rightarrow R) \rightarrow ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R))
}{pm3.42}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \rightarrow R) \rightarrow ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imim1i}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktive Elimination des linken Konjunkts]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \land R)\vdash P \rightarrow Q
}{simpld}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \land R) \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktive Elimination des rechten Konjunkts]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \land R)\vdash P \rightarrow R
}{simprd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (R \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancomd}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpld}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Elimination eines Konjunkts aus einer Äquivalenz]{%
  P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)\vdash P \rightarrow Q
}{simplbi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpld}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Elimination eines Konjunkts aus einer Äquivalenz]{%
  P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)\vdash P \rightarrow R
}{simprbi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simprd}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktive Elimination eines Konjunkts]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (R \land S))\vdash (P \land Q) \rightarrow R
}{simprbda}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (R \land S))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow (R \land S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpa}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpld}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktive Elimination eines Konjunkts]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (R \land S))\vdash (P \land Q) \rightarrow S
}{simplbda}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (R \land S))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow (R \land S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpa}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{simprd}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Elimination eines Konjunkts aus einer Äquivalenz]{%
  P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)\vdash Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow P)
}{simplbi2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Closed form of simplbi2com]{%
  ((P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)) \rightarrow (R \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow P)))
}{simplbi2comt}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simplbi2}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{R \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{com12}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)) \rightarrow (R \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow P)))}{\rRI{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A deduction eliminating a conjunct, similar to simplbi2]{%
  P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R) \vdash R \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow P)
}{simplbi2com}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simplbi2}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{R \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{com12}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Implication from an eliminated conjunct implied by the antecedent]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \land R) \dsep R \rightarrow S \vdash P \rightarrow S
}{simpl2im}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simprd}{1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{3,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Implication from an eliminated conjunct equivalent to the antecedent]{%
  P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R) \dsep R \rightarrow S \vdash P \rightarrow S
}{simplbiim}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simprbi}{1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{3,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[An inference for implication elimination]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \dsep S \rightarrow Q \vdash (P \land S) \rightarrow R
}{impel}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{S \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land S) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land S) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land S) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{3,1}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land S) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{4,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land S) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpd}{6,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Modus ponens conjoining dissimilar antecedents]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep R \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S) \vdash (P \land R) \rightarrow S
}{mpan9}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land R) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land R) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land R) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{3,1}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land R) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{4,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land R) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpd}{5,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Nested syllogism inference conjoining dissimilar antecedents]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \dsep S \rightarrow (R \rightarrow T) \vdash (P \land S) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow T)
}{sylan9}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{S \rightarrow (R \rightarrow T)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P \land S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{4}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpli}{3}}
  \proofstep{3}{S}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpri}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,3}{Q \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{5,1}}
  \proofstep{1,3,4}{R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{4,7}}
  \proofstep{2,3}{R \rightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{6,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3,4}{T}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{8,9}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{Q \rightarrow T}{\rRI{4,10}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land S) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow T)}{\rRI{3,11}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Nested syllogism inference conjoining dissimilar antecedents]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \dsep S \rightarrow (R \rightarrow T) \vdash (S \land P) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow T)
}{sylan9r}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{S \rightarrow (R \rightarrow T)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{S \land P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{4}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{S}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpli}{3}}
  \proofstep{3}{P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpri}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,3}{Q \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{6,1}}
  \proofstep{1,3,4}{R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{4,7}}
  \proofstep{2,3}{R \rightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{5,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3,4}{T}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{8,9}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{Q \rightarrow T}{\rRI{4,10}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(S \land P) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow T)}{\rRI{3,11}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Nested syllogism inference conjoining dissimilar antecedents]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R) \dsep S \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow T) \vdash (P \land S) \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow T)
}{sylan9bb}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{S \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow T)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpd}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{S \rightarrow (R \rightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land S) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan9}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimprd}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{S \rightarrow (T \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimprd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land S) \rightarrow (T \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan9r}{7,6}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land S) \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid}{5,8}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Nested syllogism inference conjoining dissimilar antecedents]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R) \dsep S \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow T) \vdash (S \land P) \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow T)
}{sylan9bbr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{S \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow T)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpd}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{S \rightarrow (R \rightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(S \land P) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan9r}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimprd}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{S \rightarrow (T \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimprd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(S \land P) \rightarrow (T \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan9}{7,6}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(S \land P) \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid}{5,8}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduce conjunction of the consequents of two implications (\enquote{join consequents with \enquote{and}})]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep P \rightarrow R \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \land R)
}{jca}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,3}{Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{3,1}}
  \proofstep{2,3}{R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{3,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{Q \land R}{\rAI{4,5}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\rRI{3,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{jca} ist genau die aus dem natürlichen Schließen bekannte Einführungsregel für die Konjunktion in deduktiver Form: aus zwei Folgerungen mit demselben Antezedens wird eine gemeinsame Konjunktion im Konsequens gebildet.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Deduction conjoining the consequents of two implications]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \dsep P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \land S))
}{jcad}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow (R \land S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \land S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inference conjoining the consequents of two implications]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \dsep Q \rightarrow S \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \land S))
}{jca2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \land S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jcad}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Join three consequents]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep P \rightarrow R \dsep P \rightarrow S \vdash P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \land S)
}{jca31}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \land S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{4,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Join three consequents]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep P \rightarrow R \dsep P \rightarrow S \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \land (R \land S))
}{jca32}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2,3}{P \rightarrow (R \land S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow (Q \land (R \land S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{1,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction replacing implication with conjunction]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \land R)
}{jcai}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpd}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Distributive law for implication over conjunction]{%
  ((P \rightarrow (Q \land R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \land (P \rightarrow R)))
}{jcab}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpld}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simprd}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow Q) \land (P \rightarrow R)}{\rAI{2,3}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow (Q \land R)) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \land (P \rightarrow R))}{\rRI{1,4}}
  \proofstep{6}{(P \rightarrow Q) \land (P \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{6}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpli}{6}}
  \proofstep{6}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpri}{6}}
  \proofstep{6}{P \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{7,8}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \land (P \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \land R))}{\rRI{6,9}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow (Q \land R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \land (P \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{5,10}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{jcab} ist die präzise Entfaltung dessen, was man beim Lesen von \(P \rightarrow (Q \land R)\) ohnehin erwartet: unter derselben Voraussetzung \(P\) müssen beide Teilfolgen \(Q\) und \(R\) gelten.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *4.76 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  (((P \rightarrow Q) \land (P \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \land R)))
}{pm4.76}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow (Q \land R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \land (P \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jcab}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \land (P \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \land R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inference conjoining a theorem to left of consequent in an implication]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep R \vdash P \rightarrow (R \land Q)
}{jctil}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (R \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{3,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inference conjoining a theorem to right of consequent in an implication]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep R \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \land R)
}{jctir}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inference conjoining a consequent of a consequent to the right of the consequent in an implication]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep Q \rightarrow R \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \land R)
}{jccir}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inference conjoining a consequent of a consequent to the left of the consequent in an implication]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep Q \rightarrow R \vdash P \rightarrow (R \land Q)
}{jccil}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (R \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{3,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inference conjoining a theorem to the left of a consequent]{%
  Q \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \land P)
}{jctl}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \land P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jctil}{2,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inference conjoining a theorem to the right of a consequent]{%
  Q \vdash P \rightarrow (P \land Q)
}{jctr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (P \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jctir}{2,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction conjoining a theorem to left of consequent in an implication]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \dsep P \rightarrow S \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (S \land R))
}{jctild}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1d}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (S \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jcad}{3,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction conjoining a theorem to right of consequent in an implication]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \dsep P \rightarrow S \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \land S))
}{jctird}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1d}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \land S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jcad}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Introduction of antecedent as conjunct. Theorem *4.73 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (Q \land P))
}{iba}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow (Q \land P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jctr}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \land P) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \leftrightarrow (Q \land P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (Q \land P))}{\rRI{1,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Introduction of antecedent as conjunct]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (P \land Q))
}{ibar}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow (P \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jctl}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \leftrightarrow (P \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (P \land Q))}{\rRI{1,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{iba} und \FormulaRefAuto{ibar} zeigen, dass eine bereits angenommene Aussage als zusätzlicher Konjunkt in das Konsequens aufgenommen werden kann, ohne den Wahrheitsgehalt zu ändern.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[A wff is equivalent to its conjunction with truth]{%
  P \vdash Q \leftrightarrow (Q \land P)
}{biantru}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (Q \land P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{iba}}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \leftrightarrow (Q \land P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A wff is equivalent to its conjunction with truth]{%
  P \vdash Q \leftrightarrow (P \land Q)
}{biantrur}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (P \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ibar}}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \leftrightarrow (P \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A wff is equivalent to its conjunction with truth]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \vdash P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow (R \land Q))
}{biantrud}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{Q \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow (R \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{iba}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow (R \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A wff is equivalent to its conjunction with truth]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \vdash P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow (Q \land R))
}{biantrurd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{Q \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow (Q \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ibar}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow (Q \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A wff conjoined with falsehood is false]{%
  \neg P \vdash P \leftrightarrow (Q \land P)
}{bianfi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.21i}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \land P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \land P) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow (Q \land P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{4,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A wff conjoined with falsehood is false]{%
  P \rightarrow \neg Q \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (Q \land R))
}{bianfd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow \neg Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{2,1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bianfi}{3}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (Q \land R))}{\rRI{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Move conjunction outside of biconditional]{%
  P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R) \vdash Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R)
}{baib}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simprbi}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simplbi2}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid}{4,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Move conjunction outside of biconditional]{%
  P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R) \vdash Q \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow P)
}{baibr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{baib}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomd}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Move conjunction outside of biconditional]{%
  P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R) \vdash R \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow P)
}{rbaibr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simplbi}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{R \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simplbi2com}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{R \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{R \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid}{3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Move conjunction outside of biconditional]{%
  P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R) \vdash R \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)
}{rbaib}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{R \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{rbaibr}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{R \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomd}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Move conjunction outside of biconditional]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (R \land S)) \vdash (P \land R) \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)
}{baibd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (R \land S))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \land R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpli}{2}}
  \proofstep{2}{R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpri}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \leftrightarrow (R \land S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{3,1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{simprbi}{5}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{R \rightarrow (S \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simplbi2com}{5}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{S \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{4,7}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \leftrightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{6,8}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land R) \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\rRI{2,9}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Move conjunction outside of biconditional]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (R \land S)) \vdash (P \land S) \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)
}{rbaibd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (R \land S))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \land S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpli}{2}}
  \proofstep{2}{S}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpri}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \leftrightarrow (R \land S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{3,1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simplbi}{5}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{S \rightarrow (R \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simplbi2com}{5}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{R \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{4,7}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \leftrightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{6,8}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land S) \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rRI{2,9}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Absorb a hypothesis into the second member of a biconditional]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (P \land R)) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)
}{bianabs}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (P \land R))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \leftrightarrow (P \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{2,1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simprbi}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simplbi2}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{R \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{2,5}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \leftrightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{4,6}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rRI{2,7}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *5.44 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \land R))))
}{pm5.44}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \land R))}{\rRI{2,3}}
  \proofstep{5}{P \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{5}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simprd}{5}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow (Q \land R)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)}{\rRI{5,6}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{4,7}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \land R))))}{\rRI{1,8}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *5.42 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  ((P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \land R))))
}{pm5.42}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jctild}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \land R)))}{\rRI{1,3}}
  \proofstep{5}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \land R))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{6}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{5,6}{Q \rightarrow (P \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{6,5}}
  \proofstep{8}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{5,6,8}{P \land R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{8,7}}
  \proofstep{5,6,8}{R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpri}{9}}
  \proofstep{5,6}{Q \rightarrow R}{\rRI{8,10}}
  \proofstep{5}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rRI{6,11}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \land R))) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R))}{\rRI{5,12}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \land R))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{4,13}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Conjoin antecedent to left of consequent]{%
  ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (P \land Q)))
}{ancl}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (P \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{2,1}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (P \land Q)))}{\rRI{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Conjoin antecedent to left of consequent. Theorem *4.7 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  ((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (P \land Q)))
}{anclb}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (P \land Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancl}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (P \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (P \land Q))}{\rRI{1,3}}
  \proofstep{5}{P \rightarrow (P \land Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{5}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simprd}{5}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow (P \land Q)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)}{\rRI{5,6}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (P \land Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{4,7}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Conjoin antecedent to right of consequent]{%
  ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \land P)))
}{ancr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \land P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \land P)))}{\rRI{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Conjoin antecedent to right of consequent]{%
  ((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \land P)))
}{ancrb}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \land P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancr}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \land P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \land P))}{\rRI{1,3}}
  \proofstep{5}{P \rightarrow (Q \land P)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{5}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpld}{5}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow (Q \land P)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)}{\rRI{5,6}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \land P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{4,7}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction conjoining antecedent to left of consequent]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \vdash P \rightarrow (P \land Q)
}{ancli}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (P \land Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancl}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (P \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction conjoining antecedent to right of consequent]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \land P)
}{ancri}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \land P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancr}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \land P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction conjoining antecedent to left of consequent in nested implication]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (Q \land R))
}{ancld}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{2,1}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{Q \land R}{\rAI{3,5}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\rRI{3,6}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (Q \land R))}{\rRI{2,7}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction conjoining antecedent to right of consequent in nested implication]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \land Q))
}{ancrd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{2,1}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{R \land Q}{\rAI{5,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \rightarrow (R \land Q)}{\rRI{3,6}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \land Q))}{\rRI{2,7}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Importation with conjunction in consequent]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \vdash (P \land Q) \rightarrow (R \land Q)
}{impac}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow (R \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Conjoin antecedent to left of consequent in nested implication]{%
  ((P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \land R))))
}{anc2l}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jctild}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \land R))))}{\rRI{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Conjoin antecedent to right of consequent in nested implication]{%
  ((P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \land P))))
}{anc2r}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \land P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jctird}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \land P))))}{\rRI{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction conjoining antecedent to left of consequent in nested implication]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \land R))
}{anc2li}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jctild}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction conjoining antecedent to right of consequent in nested implication]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \land P))
}{anc2ri}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \land P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jctird}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Implication in terms of biconditional and conjunction. Theorem *4.71 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  ((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow (P \land Q)))
}{pm4.71}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (P \land Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancl}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (P \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow (P \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow (P \land Q)))}{\rRI{1,5}}
  \proofstep{7}{P \leftrightarrow (P \land Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{7}{P \rightarrow (P \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{7}}
  \proofstep{7}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simprd}{8}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow (P \land Q)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))}{\rRI{7,9}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow (P \land Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{6,10}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Implication in terms of biconditional and conjunction. Theorem *4.71 of Whitehead-Russell (with conjunct reversed)]{%
  ((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow (Q \land P)))
}{pm4.71r}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \land P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancr}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \land P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \land P) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow (Q \land P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow (Q \land P)))}{\rRI{1,5}}
  \proofstep{7}{P \leftrightarrow (Q \land P)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{7}{P \rightarrow (Q \land P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{7}}
  \proofstep{7}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpld}{8}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow (Q \land P)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))}{\rRI{7,9}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow (Q \land P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{6,10}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{pm4.71} und \FormulaRefAuto{pm4.71r} sind konzeptionell wichtig: eine Implikation \(P \rightarrow Q\) lässt sich als Äquivalenz zwischen \(P\) und einer durch \(P\) verstärkten Konjunktion lesen.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Inference converting an implication to a biconditional with conjunction]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \vdash P \leftrightarrow (P \land Q)
}{pm4.71i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow (P \land Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm4.71}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow (P \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inference converting an implication to a biconditional with conjunction]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \vdash P \leftrightarrow (Q \land P)
}{pm4.71ri}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow (Q \land P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm4.71r}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow (Q \land P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction converting an implication to a biconditional with conjunction]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (Q \land R))
}{pm4.71d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{2,1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm4.71i}{3}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (Q \land R))}{\rRI{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction converting an implication to a biconditional with conjunction]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (R \land Q))
}{pm4.71rd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{2,1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \leftrightarrow (R \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm4.71ri}{3}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (R \land Q))}{\rRI{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *4.24 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  (P \leftrightarrow (P \land P))
}{pm4.24}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{P \leftrightarrow (P \land P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm4.71i}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Idempotent law for conjunction]{%
  ((P \land P) \leftrightarrow P)
}{anidm}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \leftrightarrow (P \land P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm4.24}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land P) \leftrightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{anidm} ist das Idempotenzgesetz der Konjunktion: dieselbe Aussage zweimal zu konjungieren fügt logisch nichts Neues hinzu.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Conjunction idempotence with antecedent]{%
  ((P \rightarrow (Q \land Q)) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))
}{anidmdbi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \land Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpld}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow (Q \land Q)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)}{\rRI{1,2}}
  \proofstep{4}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{4}{P \rightarrow (Q \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{4,4}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \land Q))}{\rRI{4,5}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow (Q \land Q)) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{3,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inference from idempotent law for conjunction]{%
  (P \land P) \rightarrow Q \vdash P \rightarrow Q
}{anidms}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land P) \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (P \land P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{2,2}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{3,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Distribution of implication with conjunction]{%
  ((P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \land Q) \rightarrow (P \land R)))
}{imdistan}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \land Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpli}{2}}
  \proofstep{2}{Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpri}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{3,1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{4,5}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \land R}{\rAI{3,6}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow (P \land R)}{\rRI{2,7}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow ((P \land Q) \rightarrow (P \land R))}{\rRI{1,8}}
  \proofstep{10}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow (P \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{11}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{12}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{10,11,12}{P \land Q}{\rAI{11,12}}
  \proofstep{10,11,12}{P \land R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{13,10}}
  \proofstep{10,11,12}{R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpri}{14}}
  \proofstep{10,11}{Q \rightarrow R}{\rRI{12,15}}
  \proofstep{10}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rRI{11,16}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \land Q) \rightarrow (P \land R)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)))}{\rRI{10,17}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \land Q) \rightarrow (P \land R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{9,18}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Distribution of implication with conjunction]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \vdash (P \land Q) \rightarrow (P \land R)
}{imdistani}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \land Q) \rightarrow (P \land R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imdistan}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow (P \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Distribution of implication with conjunction]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \vdash (Q \land P) \rightarrow (R \land P)
}{imdistanri}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \land P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpli}{2}}
  \proofstep{2}{P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpri}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{4,1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{3,5}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{R \land P}{\rAI{6,4}}
  \proofstep{1}{(Q \land P) \rightarrow (R \land P)}{\rRI{2,7}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Distribution of implication with conjunction (deduction form)]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)) \vdash P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow (Q \land S))
}{imdistand}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{2,1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow (Q \land S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{imdistani}{3}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow (Q \land S))}{\rRI{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Distribution of implication with conjunction (deduction version with conjoined antecedent)]{%
  (P \land Q) \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S) \vdash P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow (Q \land S))
}{imdistanda}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow (Q \land S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imdistand}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *5.3 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  (((P \land Q) \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow ((P \land Q) \rightarrow (P \land R)))
}{pm5.3}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \land Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{2,1}}
  \proofstep{2}{P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpli}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \land R}{\rAI{4,3}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow (P \land R)}{\rRI{2,5}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \rightarrow R) \rightarrow ((P \land Q) \rightarrow (P \land R))}{\rRI{1,6}}
  \proofstep{8}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow (P \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{8}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simprd}{8}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \land Q) \rightarrow (P \land R)) \rightarrow ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R))}{\rRI{8,9}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \land Q) \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow ((P \land Q) \rightarrow (P \land R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{7,10}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Distribution of implication over biconditional. Theorem *5.32 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  ((P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \land Q) \leftrightarrow (P \land R)))
}{pm5.32}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \land Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpli}{2}}
  \proofstep{2}{Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpri}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \leftrightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{3,1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{R}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbi}{4,5}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \land R}{\rAI{3,6}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow (P \land R)}{\rRI{2,7}}
  \proofstep{9}{P \land R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{9}{P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpli}{9}}
  \proofstep{9}{R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpri}{9}}
  \proofstep{1,9}{Q \leftrightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{10,1}}
  \proofstep{1,9}{Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbir}{11,12}}
  \proofstep{1,9}{P \land Q}{\rAI{10,13}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land R) \rightarrow (P \land Q)}{\rRI{9,14}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \leftrightarrow (P \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{8,15}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)) \rightarrow ((P \land Q) \leftrightarrow (P \land R))}{\rRI{1,16}}
  \proofstep{18}{(P \land Q) \leftrightarrow (P \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{19}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{20}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{18,19,20}{P \land Q}{\rAI{19,20}}
  \proofstep{18,19,20}{P \land R}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbi}{21,18}}
  \proofstep{18,19,20}{R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpri}{22}}
  \proofstep{18,19}{Q \rightarrow R}{\rRI{20,23}}
  \proofstep{25}{R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{18,19,25}{P \land R}{\rAI{19,25}}
  \proofstep{18,19,25}{P \land Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbir}{26,18}}
  \proofstep{18,19,25}{Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpri}{27}}
  \proofstep{18,19}{R \rightarrow Q}{\rRI{25,28}}
  \proofstep{18,19}{Q \leftrightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{24,29}}
  \proofstep{18}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rRI{19,30}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \land Q) \leftrightarrow (P \land R)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)))}{\rRI{18,31}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \land Q) \leftrightarrow (P \land R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{17,32}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.32} transportiert eine Äquivalenz unter festgehaltenem Antezedens \(P\) durch die Konjunktion. Das ist einer der zentralen Umformungssätze für spätere Äquivalenzrechnungen.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Distribution of implication over biconditional (inference form)]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R) \vdash (P \land Q) \leftrightarrow (P \land R)
}{pm5.32i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \land Q) \leftrightarrow (P \land R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.32}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \leftrightarrow (P \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Distribution of implication over biconditional (inference form)]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R) \vdash (Q \land P) \leftrightarrow (R \land P)
}{pm5.32ri}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \land P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpli}{2}}
  \proofstep{2}{P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpri}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \leftrightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{4,1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{R}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbi}{3,5}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{R \land P}{\rAI{6,4}}
  \proofstep{1}{(Q \land P) \rightarrow (R \land P)}{\rRI{2,7}}
  \proofstep{9}{R \land P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{9}{R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpli}{9}}
  \proofstep{9}{P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpri}{9}}
  \proofstep{1,9}{Q \leftrightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{11,1}}
  \proofstep{1,9}{Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbir}{10,12}}
  \proofstep{1,9}{Q \land P}{\rAI{13,11}}
  \proofstep{1}{(R \land P) \rightarrow (Q \land P)}{\rRI{9,14}}
  \proofstep{1}{(Q \land P) \leftrightarrow (R \land P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{8,15}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Exchanging conjunction in a biconditional]{%
  P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R) \dsep R \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S) \vdash P \leftrightarrow (S \land R)
}{bianim}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simprbi}{1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{3,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simplbi}{1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbid}{5,4}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (S \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{6,3}}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(S \land R) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{2}{(S \land R) \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{9,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(S \land R) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{2}{(S \land R) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbird}{11,10}}
  \proofstep{}{(S \land R) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{2}{(S \land R) \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{12,13}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(S \land R) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{14,8}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \leftrightarrow (S \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{7,15}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Distribution of implication over biconditional (deduction form)]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)) \vdash P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \leftrightarrow (Q \land S))
}{pm5.32d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{2,1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(Q \land R) \leftrightarrow (Q \land S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.32i}{3}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \leftrightarrow (Q \land S))}{\rRI{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Distribution of implication over biconditional (deduction form)]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)) \vdash P \rightarrow ((R \land Q) \leftrightarrow (S \land Q))
}{pm5.32rd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{2,1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(R \land Q) \leftrightarrow (S \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.32ri}{3}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((R \land Q) \leftrightarrow (S \land Q))}{\rRI{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Distribution of implication over biconditional (deduction form)]{%
  (P \land Q) \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S) \vdash P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \leftrightarrow (Q \land S))
}{pm5.32da}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \leftrightarrow (Q \land S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.32d}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Adding a superfluous conjunct in a biconditional]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (R \land S)) \vdash P \rightarrow ((R \land Q) \leftrightarrow (R \land S))
}{bian1d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (R \land S))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land R) \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{baibd}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((R \land Q) \leftrightarrow (R \land S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.32d}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism inference]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep (Q \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash (P \land R) \rightarrow S
}{sylan}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land R) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land R) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land R) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{3,1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land R) \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{5,4}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land R) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{6,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism inference]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q \dsep (Q \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash (P \land R) \rightarrow S
}{sylanb}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land R) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan}{3,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism inference]{%
  Q \leftrightarrow P \dsep (Q \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash (P \land R) \rightarrow S
}{sylanbr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \leftrightarrow P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}{1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land R) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan}{3,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Syllogism inference]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep P \rightarrow R \dsep T \leftrightarrow (Q \land R) \vdash P \rightarrow T
}{sylanbrc}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{T \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{3}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{4,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Syllogism inference combined with contraction]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep P \rightarrow R \dsep (Q \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash P \rightarrow S
}{syl2anc}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{4,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Double syllogism inference combined with contraction]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep Q \rightarrow R \dsep (Q \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash P \rightarrow S
}{syl2anc2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl2anc}{1,4,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Syllogism inference combined with modus ponens]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep R \dsep (Q \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash P \rightarrow S
}{sylancl}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jctir}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{4,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Syllogism inference combined with modus ponens]{%
  Q \dsep P \rightarrow R \dsep (Q \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash P \rightarrow S
}{sylancr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jctil}{2,1}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{4,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Syllogism inference with commutation of antecedents]{%
  (P \land Q) \rightarrow R \dsep (R \land Q) \rightarrow S \vdash (P \land Q) \rightarrow S
}{sylancom}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(R \land Q) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow (R \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{1,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{4,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Syllogism inference combined with a biconditional]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep R \dsep (Q \land R) \leftrightarrow S \vdash P \rightarrow S
}{sylanblc}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{(Q \land R) \leftrightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylancl}{1,2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Syllogism inference combined with a biconditional]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep R \dsep S \leftrightarrow (Q \land R) \vdash P \rightarrow S
}{sylanblrc}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{S \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylancl}{1,2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism deduction with conjoined antecedents]{%
  (P \land Q) \rightarrow R \dsep (P \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash (P \land Q) \rightarrow S
}{syldan}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow (P \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{3,1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{4,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism deduction]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R) \dsep (P \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash (P \land Q) \rightarrow S
}{sylbida}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \leftrightarrow (P \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.32i}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow (P \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{4,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism inference]{%
  P \rightarrow R \dsep (Q \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash (Q \land P) \rightarrow S
}{sylan2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \land P) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \land P) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{1}{(Q \land P) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{4,1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(Q \land P) \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{3,5}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(Q \land P) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{6,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism inference]{%
  P \leftrightarrow R \dsep (Q \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash (Q \land P) \rightarrow S
}{sylan2b}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(Q \land P) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan2}{3,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism inference]{%
  R \leftrightarrow P \dsep (Q \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash (Q \land P) \rightarrow S
}{sylan2br}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{R \leftrightarrow P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}{1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(Q \land P) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan2}{3,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A double syllogism inference]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep T \rightarrow R \dsep (Q \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash (P \land T) \rightarrow S
}{syl2an}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{T \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land T) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land T) \rightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land T) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{4,1}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land T) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{5,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land T) \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{6,7}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{(P \land T) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{8,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{syl2an} ist der saubere Konjunktionsgegenpart zu den bekannten Doppelsyllogismen für Implikationen: zwei getrennte Vorbedingungen werden erst unter einer gemeinsamen Konjunktion zusammengeführt und dann in einem einzigen Schluss weiterverarbeitet.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[A double syllogism inference]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep T \rightarrow R \dsep (Q \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash (T \land P) \rightarrow S
}{syl2anr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{T \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(T \land P) \rightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{}{(T \land P) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{1}{(T \land P) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{5,1}}
  \proofstep{2}{(T \land P) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{4,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(T \land P) \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{6,7}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{(T \land P) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{8,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A double syllogism inference]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q \dsep T \leftrightarrow R \dsep (Q \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash (P \land T) \rightarrow S
}{syl2anb}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{T \leftrightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{T \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{(P \land T) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl2an}{4,5,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A double syllogism inference]{%
  Q \leftrightarrow P \dsep R \leftrightarrow T \dsep (Q \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash (P \land T) \rightarrow S
}{syl2anbr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \leftrightarrow P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \leftrightarrow T}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{T \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{(P \land T) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl2an}{4,5,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism inference combined with contraction.]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q \dsep P \leftrightarrow R \dsep (Q \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash P \rightarrow S
}{sylancb}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \leftrightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{(P \land P) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl2anb}{1,2,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{anidms}{4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism inference combined with contraction.]{%
  Q \leftrightarrow P \dsep R \leftrightarrow P \dsep (Q \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash P \rightarrow S
}{sylancbr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \leftrightarrow P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \leftrightarrow P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{(P \land P) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl2anbr}{1,2,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{anidms}{4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism deduction with conjoined antecedents.]{%
  (P \land Q) \rightarrow R \dsep ((P \land R) \land S) \rightarrow T \vdash ((P \land Q) \land S) \rightarrow T
}{syldanl}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow (P \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{imdistani}{2}}
  \proofstep{2}{((P \land R) \land S) \rightarrow T}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{((P \land Q) \land S) \rightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan}{3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism deduction.]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \dsep P \rightarrow ((R \land S) \rightarrow T) \vdash P \rightarrow ((Q \land S) \rightarrow T)
}{syland}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow ((R \land S) \rightarrow T)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow (S \rightarrow T))}{\FormulaRefAuto{expd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (S \rightarrow T))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syld}{1,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land S) \rightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impd}{4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism inference.]{%
  P \rightarrow R \dsep Q \rightarrow ((R \land S) \rightarrow T) \vdash Q \rightarrow ((P \land S) \rightarrow T)
}{sylani}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \rightarrow ((R \land S) \rightarrow T)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \rightarrow ((P \land S) \rightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syland}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism deduction.]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \dsep P \rightarrow ((S \land R) \rightarrow T) \vdash P \rightarrow ((S \land Q) \rightarrow T)
}{sylan2d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow ((S \land R) \rightarrow T)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow ((R \land S) \rightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancomsd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land S) \rightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syland}{1,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow ((S \land Q) \rightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancomsd}{4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism inference.]{%
  P \rightarrow S \dsep Q \rightarrow ((R \land S) \rightarrow T) \vdash Q \rightarrow ((R \land P) \rightarrow T)
}{sylan2i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \rightarrow ((R \land S) \rightarrow T)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \rightarrow ((R \land P) \rightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan2d}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism inference.]{%
  P \rightarrow R \dsep S \rightarrow T \dsep Q \rightarrow ((R \land T) \rightarrow U) \vdash Q \rightarrow ((P \land S) \rightarrow U)
}{syl2ani}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T\dsep U}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{S \rightarrow T}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{Q \rightarrow ((R \land T) \rightarrow U)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2,3}{Q \rightarrow ((R \land S) \rightarrow U)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan2i}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{Q \rightarrow ((P \land S) \rightarrow U)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylani}{1,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism deduction.]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)\dsep
  P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow T)\dsep
  P \rightarrow ((R \land T) \rightarrow U)
  \vdash
  P \rightarrow ((Q \land S) \rightarrow U)
}{syl2and}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T\dsep U}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow T)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow ((R \land T) \rightarrow U)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2,3}{P \rightarrow ((R \land S) \rightarrow U)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan2d}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land S) \rightarrow U)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syland}{1,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Conjoin antecedents and consequents in a deduction.]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)\dsep
  P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow T)
  \vdash
  P \rightarrow ((Q \land S) \rightarrow (R \land T))
}{anim12d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow T)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow ((R \land T) \rightarrow (R \land T))}{\FormulaRefAuto{idd}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land S) \rightarrow (R \land T))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl2and}{1,2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{anim12d} ist einer der grundlegenden Konstruktionssätze für Konjunktionen im Deduktionsstil: zwei unter demselben Antezedens verfügbare Implikationen werden zu einer Implikation auf eine Konjunktion zusammengezogen.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Variant of anim12d where the second implication does not depend on the antecedent.]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \dsep S \rightarrow T \vdash P \rightarrow ((Q \land S) \rightarrow (R \land T))
}{anim12d1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{S \rightarrow T}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land S) \rightarrow (R \land T))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anim12d}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Add a conjunct to right of antecedent and consequent in a deduction.]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \vdash P \rightarrow ((Q \land S) \rightarrow (R \land S))
}{anim1d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{idd}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land S) \rightarrow (R \land S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anim12d}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}
