\FormulaThm[Add a conjunct to left of antecedent and consequent in a deduction.]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R) \vdash P \rightarrow ((S \land Q) \rightarrow (S \land R))
}{anim2d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{idd}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((S \land Q) \rightarrow (S \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anim12d}{2,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Conjoin antecedents and consequents of two premises.]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep R \rightarrow S \vdash (P \land R) \rightarrow (Q \land S)
}{anim12i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \land S) \rightarrow (Q \land S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land R) \rightarrow (Q \land S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl2an}{1,2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Variant of anim12i with commutation.]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep R \rightarrow S \vdash (P \land R) \rightarrow (S \land Q)
}{anim12ci}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(R \land P) \rightarrow (S \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{anim12i}{2,1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land R) \rightarrow (S \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancoms}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Introduce conjunct to both sides of an implication.]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \vdash (P \land R) \rightarrow (Q \land R)
}{anim1i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{R \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land R) \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{anim12i}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Introduce conjunct to both sides of an implication.]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \vdash (P \land R) \rightarrow (R \land Q)
}{anim1ci}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{R \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land R) \rightarrow (R \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{anim12ci}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Introduce conjunct to both sides of an implication.]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \vdash (R \land P) \rightarrow (R \land Q)
}{anim2i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{R \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(R \land P) \rightarrow (R \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{anim12i}{2,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Conjoin antecedents and consequents in a deduction.]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)\dsep
  S \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow T)
  \vdash
  (P \land S) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \land T))
}{anim12ii}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{S \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow T)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \rightarrow R) \land (Q \rightarrow T)) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \land T))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm3.43}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land S) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \land T))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl2an}{1,2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Conjoin antecedents and consequents in a deduction.]{%
  (P \land Q) \rightarrow R \dsep (P \land S) \rightarrow T \vdash (P \land (Q \land S)) \rightarrow (R \land T)
}{anim12dan}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land S) \rightarrow T}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land S) \rightarrow (R \land T))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anim12d}{2,4}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land (Q \land S)) \rightarrow (R \land T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction joining nested implications to form implication of conjunctions.]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)\dsep
  S \rightarrow (T \rightarrow U)
  \vdash
  (P \land S) \rightarrow ((Q \land T) \rightarrow (R \land U))
}{im2anan9}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T\dsep U}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land T) \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantrd}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{S \rightarrow (T \rightarrow U)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{S \rightarrow ((Q \land T) \rightarrow U)}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantld}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land S) \rightarrow ((Q \land T) \rightarrow (R \land U))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anim12ii}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction joining nested implications to form implication of conjunctions.]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)\dsep
  S \rightarrow (T \rightarrow U)
  \vdash
  (S \land P) \rightarrow ((Q \land T) \rightarrow (R \land U))
}{im2anan9r}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T\dsep U}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{S \rightarrow (T \rightarrow U)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land S) \rightarrow ((Q \land T) \rightarrow (R \land U))}{\FormulaRefAuto{im2anan9}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(S \land P) \rightarrow ((Q \land T) \rightarrow (R \land U))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancoms}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *3.45 (Fact) of Whitehead-Russell.]{%
  (P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \land R) \rightarrow (Q \land R))
}{pm3.45}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \land R) \rightarrow (Q \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anim1d}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Introduce a left conjunct to both sides of a logical equivalence.]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q \vdash (R \land P) \leftrightarrow (R \land Q)
}{anbi2i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{R \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(R \land P) \leftrightarrow (R \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.32i}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Introduce a right conjunct to both sides of a logical equivalence.]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q \vdash (P \land R) \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)
}{anbi1i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{R \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land R) \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.32ri}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Variant of anbi2i with commutation.]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q \vdash (P \land R) \leftrightarrow (R \land Q)
}{anbi2ci}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land R) \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{anbi1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land R) \leftrightarrow (R \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biancomi}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Variant of anbi1i with commutation.]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q \vdash (R \land P) \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)
}{anbi1ci}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(R \land P) \leftrightarrow (R \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{anbi2i}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(R \land P) \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biancomi}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Exchanging conjunction in a biconditional.]{%
  P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R) \dsep Q \leftrightarrow S \vdash P \leftrightarrow (S \land R)
}{bianbi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \leftrightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(Q \land R) \leftrightarrow (S \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{anbi1i}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \leftrightarrow (S \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Conjoin both sides of two equivalences.]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q \dsep R \leftrightarrow S \vdash (P \land R) \leftrightarrow (Q \land S)
}{anbi12i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \leftrightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land R) \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{anbi1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{(Q \land R) \leftrightarrow (Q \land S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{anbi2i}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land R) \leftrightarrow (Q \land S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{anbi12i} ist die Konjunktionsverträglichkeit der Äquivalenz: äquivalente Aussagen dürfen auf beiden Seiten komponentenweise unter \(\land\) ersetzt werden.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Variant of anbi12i with commutation.]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q \dsep R \leftrightarrow S \vdash (P \land R) \leftrightarrow (S \land Q)
}{anbi12ci}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \leftrightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land R) \leftrightarrow (Q \land S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{anbi12i}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land R) \leftrightarrow (S \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biancomi}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction adding a left conjunct to both sides of a logical equivalence.]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R) \vdash P \rightarrow ((S \land Q) \leftrightarrow (S \land R))
}{anbi2d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1d}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((S \land Q) \leftrightarrow (S \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.32d}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction adding a right conjunct to both sides of a logical equivalence.]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R) \vdash P \rightarrow ((Q \land S) \leftrightarrow (R \land S))
}{anbi1d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1d}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land S) \leftrightarrow (R \land S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.32rd}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction joining two equivalences to form equivalence of conjunctions.]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\dsep
  P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow T)
  \vdash
  P \rightarrow ((Q \land S) \leftrightarrow (R \land T))
}{anbi12d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land S) \leftrightarrow (R \land S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anbi1d}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow T)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow ((R \land S) \leftrightarrow (R \land T))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anbi2d}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land S) \leftrightarrow (R \land T))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitrd}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Introduce a right conjunct to both sides of a logical equivalence.]{%
  (P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \land R) \leftrightarrow (Q \land R))
}{anbi1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \land R) \leftrightarrow (Q \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anbi1d}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Introduce a left conjunct to both sides of a logical equivalence.]{%
  (P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((R \land P) \leftrightarrow (R \land Q))
}{anbi2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((R \land P) \leftrightarrow (R \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anbi2d}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Introduce a proposition as left conjunct on the left-hand side and right conjunct on the right-hand side of an equivalence. Deduction form.]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R) \vdash P \rightarrow ((S \land Q) \leftrightarrow (R \land S))
}{anbi1cd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((S \land Q) \leftrightarrow (S \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anbi2d}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((S \land Q) \leftrightarrow (R \land S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biancomd}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Double commutation in conjunction.]{%
  ((P \land Q) \land (R \land S)) \leftrightarrow ((Q \land P) \land (S \land R))
}{an2anr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \land P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancom}}
  \proofstep{}{(R \land S) \leftrightarrow (S \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancom}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \land (R \land S)) \leftrightarrow ((Q \land P) \land (S \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anbi12i}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *4.38 of Whitehead-Russell.]{%
  ((P \leftrightarrow R) \land (Q \leftrightarrow S)) \rightarrow ((P \land Q) \leftrightarrow (R \land S))
}{pm4.38}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow R) \land (Q \leftrightarrow S)) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow R) \land (Q \leftrightarrow S)) \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow R) \land (Q \leftrightarrow S)) \rightarrow ((P \land Q) \leftrightarrow (R \land S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anbi12d}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction joining two equivalences to form equivalence of conjunctions.]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\dsep
  S \rightarrow (T \leftrightarrow U)
  \vdash
  (P \land S) \rightarrow ((Q \land T) \leftrightarrow (R \land U))
}{bi2anan9}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T\dsep U}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{S \rightarrow (T \leftrightarrow U)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \leftrightarrow R) \land (T \leftrightarrow U)) \rightarrow ((Q \land T) \leftrightarrow (R \land U))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm4.38}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land S) \rightarrow ((Q \land T) \leftrightarrow (R \land U))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl2an}{1,2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction joining two equivalences to form equivalence of conjunctions.]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\dsep
  S \rightarrow (T \leftrightarrow U)
  \vdash
  (S \land P) \rightarrow ((Q \land T) \leftrightarrow (R \land U))
}{bi2anan9r}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T\dsep U}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{S \rightarrow (T \leftrightarrow U)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land S) \rightarrow ((Q \land T) \leftrightarrow (R \land U))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bi2anan9}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(S \land P) \rightarrow ((Q \land T) \leftrightarrow (R \land U))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancoms}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction joining two biconditionals with different antecedents.]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\dsep
  S \rightarrow (T \leftrightarrow U)
  \vdash
  (P \land S) \rightarrow ((Q \leftrightarrow T) \leftrightarrow (R \leftrightarrow U))
}{bi2bian9}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T\dsep U}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land S) \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantr}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{S \rightarrow (T \leftrightarrow U)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land S) \rightarrow (T \leftrightarrow U)}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantl}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land S) \rightarrow ((Q \leftrightarrow T) \leftrightarrow (R \leftrightarrow U))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bibi12d}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Adding biconditional when antecedents are conjuncted.]{%
  P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)\dsep Q \rightarrow (S \rightarrow R) \vdash (P \land Q) \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)
}{anbiim}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantr}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \rightarrow (S \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantl}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[An inference to merge two lists of conjuncts.]{%
  P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R) \vdash (S \land P) \leftrightarrow ((S \land Q) \land R)
}{bianass}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(S \land P) \leftrightarrow (S \land (Q \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anbi2i}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{((S \land Q) \land R) \leftrightarrow (S \land (Q \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anass}}
  \proofstep{1}{(S \land P) \leftrightarrow ((S \land Q) \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr4i}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[An inference to merge two lists of conjuncts.]{%
  P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R) \vdash (S \land P) \leftrightarrow ((Q \land S) \land R)
}{bianassc}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(S \land P) \leftrightarrow ((S \land Q) \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bianass}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(S \land Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \land S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancom}}
  \proofstep{1}{(S \land P) \leftrightarrow ((Q \land S) \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bianbi}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Swap two conjuncts.]{%
  ((P \land Q) \land R) \leftrightarrow (Q \land (P \land R))
}{an21}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land R) \leftrightarrow (P \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biid}}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \land (P \land R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \land Q) \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bianassc}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \land R) \leftrightarrow (Q \land (P \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Swap two conjuncts.]{%
  (P \land (Q \land R)) \leftrightarrow (Q \land (P \land R))
}{an12}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \land R) \leftrightarrow (R \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancom}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land (Q \land R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \land R) \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bianass}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land (Q \land R)) \leftrightarrow (Q \land (P \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biancomi}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A rearrangement of conjuncts.]{%
  ((P \land Q) \land R) \leftrightarrow ((P \land R) \land Q)
}{an32}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \land R) \leftrightarrow (Q \land (P \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{an21}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \land R) \leftrightarrow ((P \land R) \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biancomi}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A rearrangement of conjuncts.]{%
  (P \land (Q \land R)) \leftrightarrow (R \land (Q \land P))
}{an13}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \land P) \land R) \leftrightarrow (P \land (Q \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{an21}}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \land P) \land R) \leftrightarrow (R \land (Q \land P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancom}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land (Q \land R)) \leftrightarrow (R \land (Q \land P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr3i}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A rearrangement of conjuncts.]{%
  ((P \land Q) \land R) \leftrightarrow ((R \land Q) \land P)
}{an31}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land (Q \land R)) \leftrightarrow (R \land (Q \land P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{an13}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \land R) \leftrightarrow (P \land (Q \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anass}}
  \proofstep{}{((R \land Q) \land P) \leftrightarrow (R \land (Q \land P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anass}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \land R) \leftrightarrow ((R \land Q) \land P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4i}{1,2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Swap two conjuncts in antecedent.]{%
  (P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S \vdash (Q \land (P \land R)) \rightarrow S
}{an12s}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land (Q \land R)) \leftrightarrow (Q \land (P \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{an12}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(Q \land (P \land R)) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylbi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inference commuting a nested conjunction in antecedent.]{%
  (P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S \vdash (P \land (R \land Q)) \rightarrow S
}{ancom2s}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(R \land Q) \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm3.22}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land (R \land Q)) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan2}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Swap two conjuncts in antecedent.]{%
  (P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S \vdash (R \land (Q \land P)) \rightarrow S
}{an13s}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exp32}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{R \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{com13}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{(R \land (Q \land P)) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp32}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Swap two conjuncts in antecedent.]{%
  ((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash ((P \land R) \land Q) \rightarrow S
}{an32s}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land R) \land Q) \leftrightarrow ((P \land Q) \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{an32}}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land R) \land Q) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylbi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inference commuting a nested conjunction in antecedent.]{%
  ((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash ((Q \land P) \land R) \rightarrow S
}{ancom1s}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \land P) \rightarrow (P \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm3.22}}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{((Q \land P) \land R) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Swap two conjuncts in antecedent.]{%
  ((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash ((R \land Q) \land P) \rightarrow S
}{an31s}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exp31}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{R \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{com13}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{((R \land Q) \land P) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp31}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Commutative-associative law for conjunction in an antecedent.]{%
  (P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S \vdash ((P \land R) \land Q) \rightarrow S
}{anass1rs}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{anassrs}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land R) \land Q) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{an32s}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Rearrangement of 4 conjuncts.]{%
  ((P \land Q) \land (R \land S)) \leftrightarrow ((P \land R) \land (Q \land S))
}{an4}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \land (R \land S)) \leftrightarrow (P \land (Q \land (R \land S)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anass}}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \land (R \land S)) \leftrightarrow (R \land (Q \land S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{an12}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land (Q \land (R \land S))) \leftrightarrow ((P \land R) \land (Q \land S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bianass}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \land (R \land S)) \leftrightarrow ((P \land R) \land (Q \land S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{an4} ist der zentrale Umordnungssatz für vier Konjunkte. Viele spätere Absorptions- und Redistributionssätze benutzen genau diese Normalform, um die relevanten gleichen oder passenden Konjunkte nebeneinander zu bringen.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Rearrangement of 4 conjuncts.]{%
  ((P \land Q) \land (R \land S)) \leftrightarrow ((P \land R) \land (S \land Q))
}{an42}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \land (R \land S)) \leftrightarrow ((P \land R) \land (Q \land S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{an4}}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \land S) \leftrightarrow (S \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancom}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land R) \land (Q \land S)) \leftrightarrow ((P \land R) \land (S \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anbi2i}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \land (R \land S)) \leftrightarrow ((P \land R) \land (S \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Rearrangement of 4 conjuncts.]{%
  ((P \land Q) \land (R \land S)) \leftrightarrow ((P \land S) \land (Q \land R))
}{an43}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land S) \land (Q \land R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \land Q) \land (R \land S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{an42}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \land (R \land S)) \leftrightarrow ((P \land S) \land (Q \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A rearrangement of conjuncts.]{%
  ((P \land Q) \land (R \land S)) \rightarrow (P \land S)
}{an3}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \land (R \land S)) \leftrightarrow ((P \land S) \land (Q \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{an43}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \land (R \land S)) \rightarrow (P \land S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simplbi}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inference rearranging 4 conjuncts in antecedent.]{%
  ((P \land Q) \land (R \land S)) \rightarrow T \vdash ((P \land R) \land (Q \land S)) \rightarrow T
}{an4s}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land R) \land (Q \land S)) \leftrightarrow ((P \land Q) \land (R \land S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{an4}}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land Q) \land (R \land S)) \rightarrow T}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land R) \land (Q \land S)) \rightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylbi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inference rearranging 4 conjuncts in antecedent.]{%
  ((P \land Q) \land (R \land S)) \rightarrow T \vdash ((P \land R) \land (S \land Q)) \rightarrow T
}{an42s}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land Q) \land (R \land S)) \rightarrow T}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land R) \land (Q \land S)) \rightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{an4s}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land R) \land (S \land Q)) \rightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancom2s}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Absorption into embedded conjunct.]{%
  ((P \land Q) \land P) \leftrightarrow (P \land Q)
}{anabs1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land Q) \leftrightarrow ((P \land Q) \land P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm4.71i}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \land P) \leftrightarrow (P \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Absorption into embedded conjunct.]{%
  (P \land (P \land Q)) \leftrightarrow (P \land Q)
}{anabs5}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (P \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ibar}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow ((P \land Q) \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomd}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land (P \land Q)) \leftrightarrow (P \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.32i}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Absorption into embedded conjunct.]{%
  (Q \land (P \land Q)) \leftrightarrow (P \land Q)
}{anabs7}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \land (P \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm4.71ri}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \land (P \land Q)) \leftrightarrow (P \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Absorption of antecedent with conjunction.]{%
  ((P \land P) \land Q) \rightarrow R \vdash (P \land Q) \rightarrow R
}{anabsan}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{P \leftrightarrow (P \land P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm4.24}}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land P) \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylanb}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Absorption of antecedent into conjunction.]{%
  ((P \land Q) \land P) \rightarrow R \vdash (P \land Q) \rightarrow R
}{anabss1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land Q) \land P) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land P) \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{an32s}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{anabsan}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Absorption of antecedent into conjunction.]{%
  ((Q \land P) \land Q) \rightarrow R \vdash (P \land Q) \rightarrow R
}{anabss4}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{((Q \land P) \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(Q \land P) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{anabss1}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancoms}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Absorption of antecedent into conjunction.]{%
  (P \land (P \land Q)) \rightarrow R \vdash (P \land Q) \rightarrow R
}{anabss5}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land (P \land Q)) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land P) \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{anassrs}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{anabsan}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Absorption of antecedent into conjunction.]{%
  P \rightarrow ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R) \vdash (P \land Q) \rightarrow R
}{anabsi5}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpcom}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Absorption of antecedent into conjunction.]{%
  P \rightarrow ((Q \land P) \rightarrow R) \vdash (P \land Q) \rightarrow R
}{anabsi6}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land P) \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancomsd}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{anabsi5}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Absorption of antecedent into conjunction.]{%
  Q \rightarrow ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R) \vdash (P \land Q) \rightarrow R
}{anabsi7}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(Q \land P) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{anabsi6}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancoms}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Absorption of antecedent into conjunction.]{%
  Q \rightarrow ((Q \land P) \rightarrow R) \vdash (P \land Q) \rightarrow R
}{anabsi8}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow ((Q \land P) \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(Q \land P) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{anabsi5}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancoms}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Absorption of antecedent into conjunction.]{%
  (Q \land (P \land Q)) \rightarrow R \vdash (P \land Q) \rightarrow R
}{anabss7}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(Q \land (P \land Q)) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{((Q \land P) \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{anassrs}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{anabss4}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Absorption of antecedent with conjunction.]{%
  (P \land (Q \land Q)) \rightarrow R \vdash (P \land Q) \rightarrow R
}{anabsan2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land (Q \land Q)) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(Q \land (P \land Q)) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{an12s}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{anabss7}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Absorption of antecedent into conjunction.]{%
  ((P \land Q) \land Q) \rightarrow R \vdash (P \land Q) \rightarrow R
}{anabss3}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land Q) \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land (Q \land Q)) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{anasss}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{anabsan2}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Distribution of conjunction over conjunction.]{%
  (P \land (Q \land R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \land Q) \land (P \land R))
}{anandi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land P) \leftrightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{anidm}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land (Q \land R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \land Q) \land (P \land R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anandis}{\FormulaRefAuto{?}}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Distribution of conjunction over conjunction]{%
  (((P \land Q) \land R) \leftrightarrow ((P \land R) \land (Q \land R)))
}{anandir}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(R \land R) \leftrightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{anidm}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \land (R \land R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \land Q) \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{anbi2i}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \land (R \land R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \land R) \land (Q \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{an4}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \land Q) \land R) \leftrightarrow ((P \land R) \land (Q \land R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr3i}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{anandir} ist die rechtsseitige Variante von \FormulaRefAuto{anandi}. Der gemeinsame Konjunkt \(R\) wird dabei auf beide Teilkonjunktionen verteilt.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Inference that undistributes conjunction in the antecedent]{%
  ((P \land Q) \land (P \land R)) \rightarrow S \vdash (P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S
}{anandis}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land Q) \land (P \land R)) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land P) \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{an4s}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{anabsan}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inference that undistributes conjunction in the antecedent]{%
  ((P \land R) \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S \vdash ((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S
}{anandirs}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land R) \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land Q) \land (R \land R)) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{an4s}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{anabsan2}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism inference]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep ((Q \land R) \land S) \rightarrow T \vdash ((P \land R) \land S) \rightarrow T
}{sylanl1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land R) \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{anim1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{((Q \land R) \land S) \rightarrow T}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{((P \land R) \land S) \rightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism inference]{%
  P \rightarrow R \dsep ((Q \land R) \land S) \rightarrow T \vdash ((Q \land P) \land S) \rightarrow T
}{sylanl2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(Q \land P) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantl}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{((Q \land R) \land S) \rightarrow T}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{((Q \land P) \land S) \rightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{syldanl}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism inference]{%
  P \rightarrow R \dsep (Q \land (R \land S)) \rightarrow T \vdash (Q \land (P \land S)) \rightarrow T
}{sylanr1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land S) \rightarrow (R \land S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{anim1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{(Q \land (R \land S)) \rightarrow T}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(Q \land (P \land S)) \rightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan2}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism inference]{%
  P \rightarrow S \dsep (Q \land (R \land S)) \rightarrow T \vdash (Q \land (R \land P)) \rightarrow T
}{sylanr2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(R \land P) \rightarrow (R \land S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{anim2i}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{(Q \land (R \land S)) \rightarrow T}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(Q \land (R \land P)) \rightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan2}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A syllogism deduction combined with conjoining antecedents]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S) \dsep (Q \land S) \rightarrow T \vdash P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow T)
}{syl6an}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{(Q \land S) \rightarrow T}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{Q \rightarrow (S \rightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylsyld}{1,2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{syl6an} ist eine besonders nützliche Mischform aus Syllogismus und Konjunktionsbildung: eine direkte Folgerung \(Q\) und eine bedingte Folgerung \(R \rightarrow S\) werden über \((Q \land S) \rightarrow T\) zu \(R \rightarrow T\) zusammengeführt.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[syl2anr with antecedents in standard conjunction form]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep (P \land R) \rightarrow S \dsep (Q \land S) \rightarrow T \vdash (P \land R) \rightarrow T
}{syl2an2r}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{(Q \land S) \rightarrow T}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,3}{(P \land S) \rightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan}{1,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{(P \land R) \rightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{syldan}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[syl2an with antecedents in standard conjunction form]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep (R \land P) \rightarrow S \dsep (Q \land S) \rightarrow T \vdash (R \land P) \rightarrow T
}{syl2an2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(R \land P) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantl}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{(R \land P) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{(Q \land S) \rightarrow T}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{(R \land P) \rightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl2anc}{2,3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[An inference based on modus ponens]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep (P \land Q) \rightarrow R \vdash P \rightarrow R
}{mpdan}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl2anc}{1,2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[An inference based on modus ponens with commutation of antecedents]{%
  Q \rightarrow P \dsep (P \land Q) \rightarrow R \vdash Q \rightarrow R
}{mpancom}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{Q \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl2anc}{1,2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A deduction which \enquote{stacks} a hypothesis]{%
  P \rightarrow R \dsep ((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash (P \land Q) \rightarrow S
}{mpidan}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantr}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpdan}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[An inference based on modus ponens]{%
  P \dsep (P \land Q) \rightarrow R \vdash Q \rightarrow R
}{mpan}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpancom}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[An inference based on modus ponens]{%
  Q \dsep (P \land Q) \rightarrow R \vdash P \rightarrow R
}{mpan2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpdan}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[An inference based on modus ponens]{%
  P \dsep Q \dsep (P \land Q) \rightarrow R \vdash R
}{mp2an}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{2}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,3}{Q \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpan}{1,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[An inference based on modus ponens]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &P \dsep Q \dsep R \dsep S \dsep ((P \land Q) \land (R \land S)) \rightarrow T \vdash T
  \end{aligned}
}{mp4an}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \land Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm3.2i}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{3}{R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{4}{S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3,4}{R \land S}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm3.2i}{4,5}}
  \proofstep{5}{((P \land Q) \land (R \land S)) \rightarrow T}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2,3,4,5}{T}{\FormulaRefAuto{mp2an}{3,6,7}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A deduction based on modus ponens]{%
  P \rightarrow R \dsep P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)
}{mpan2d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{expd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpid}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A deduction based on modus ponens]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S) \vdash P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)
}{mpand}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow ((R \land Q) \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancomsd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpan2d}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[An inference based on modus ponens]{%
  Q \dsep P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S) \vdash P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)
}{mpani}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpand}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[An inference based on modus ponens]{%
  R \dsep P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)
}{mpan2i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpan2d}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[An inference based on modus ponens]{%
  Q \dsep R \dsep P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S) \vdash P \rightarrow S
}{mp2ani}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{2}{R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,3}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpani}{1,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpi}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A deduction based on modus ponens]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep P \rightarrow R \dsep P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S) \vdash P \rightarrow S
}{mp2and}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,3}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpand}{1,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpd}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{mp2and} ist die zweifache Konjunktionsvariante von Modus Ponens im Deduktionsstil: zwei unter demselben Antezedens verfügbare Aussagen werden mit einer Konjunktionsimplikation zu einem direkten Schluss zusammengezogen.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[An inference based on modus ponens.]{%
  P \dsep (((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S) \vdash (Q \land R) \rightarrow S%
}{mpanl1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow (P \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jctl}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Modus-Ponens-Inferenz für eine linksgeschachtelte Konjunktion]{%
  Q \dsep ((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash (P \land R) \rightarrow S%
}{mpanl2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (P \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jctr}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land R) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Modus-Ponens-Inferenz für zwei feste Konjunkte]{%
  P \dsep Q \dsep ((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash R \rightarrow S%
}{mpanl12}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,3}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpanl1}{1,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{R \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpan}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Modus-Ponens-Inferenz für eine rechtsgeschachtelte Konjunktion]{%
  Q \dsep (P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S \vdash (P \land R) \rightarrow S%
}{mpanr1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{anassrs}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land R) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpanl2}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Modus-Ponens-Inferenz für den rechten inneren Konjunkt]{%
  R \dsep (P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S \vdash (P \land Q) \rightarrow S%
}{mpanr2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jctr}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan2}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Modus-Ponens-Inferenz für zwei feste rechtsgeschachtelte Konjunkte]{%
  Q \dsep R \dsep (P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S \vdash P \rightarrow S%
}{mpanr12}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{(P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,3}{(P \land R) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpanr1}{1,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpan2}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Modus-Ponens-Inferenz mit äußerem Zusatzkonjunkt]{%
  Q \dsep ((P \land (Q \land R)) \land S) \rightarrow T \vdash ((P \land R) \land S) \rightarrow T%
}{mpanlr1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{R \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jctl}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{((P \land (Q \land R)) \land S) \rightarrow T}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{((P \land R) \land S) \rightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylanl2}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Wahren Konjunkt in einer Äquivalenz abtrennen]{%
  P \rightarrow R \dsep P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (R \land S)) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)%
}{mpbirand}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (R \land S))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow (R \land S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biantrurd}{1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr4d}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Wahren rechten Konjunkt in einer Äquivalenz abtrennen]{%
  P \rightarrow S \dsep P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (R \land S)) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)%
}{mpbiran2d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (R \land S))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (S \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biancomd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbirand}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Wahren linken Konjunkt aus einer Äquivalenz entfernen]{%
  Q \dsep P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R) \vdash P \leftrightarrow R%
}{mpbiran}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{R \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biantrur}{1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \leftrightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr4i}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Wahren rechten Konjunkt aus einer Äquivalenz entfernen]{%
  R \dsep P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R) \vdash P \leftrightarrow Q%
}{mpbiran2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \leftrightarrow (R \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biancomi}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \leftrightarrow (R \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biantrur}{1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr4i}{3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Beide wahren Konjunkte aus einer Äquivalenz ablösen]{%
  Q \dsep R \dsep P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R) \vdash P%
}{mpbir2an}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,3}{P \leftrightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbiran}{1,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbir}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Konjunktion wahrer Aussagen in einer Äquivalenz ablösen]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \dsep P \rightarrow R \dsep P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \leftrightarrow S) \vdash P \rightarrow S%
}{mpbi2and}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \leftrightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbid}{3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Konjunktion wahrer Aussagen in einer Äquivalenz rückwärts ablösen]{%
  P \rightarrow R \dsep P \rightarrow S \dsep P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (R \land S)) \vdash P \rightarrow Q%
}{mpbir2and}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (R \land S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (R \land S))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbird}{3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
Die Sätze \FormulaRefAuto{mpbirand} bis \FormulaRefAuto{mpbir2and} sind praktische Eliminationsregeln für Äquivalenzen mit Konjunktionen. Sobald eine oder beide Konjunktionskomponenten bereits feststehen, kann die Äquivalenz auf den verbleibenden Teil reduziert werden.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Konjunkt links außen zum Antezedens hinzufügen]{%
  (P \land Q) \rightarrow R \vdash ((S \land P) \land Q) \rightarrow R%
}{adantll}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(S \land P) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{((S \land P) \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Konjunkt links innen zum Antezedens hinzufügen]{%
  (P \land Q) \rightarrow R \vdash ((P \land S) \land Q) \rightarrow R%
}{adantlr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land S) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{((P \land S) \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Konjunkt rechts innen zum Antezedens hinzufügen]{%
  (P \land Q) \rightarrow R \vdash (P \land (S \land Q)) \rightarrow R%
}{adantrl}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(S \land Q) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land (S \land Q)) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan2}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Konjunkt rechts außen zum Antezedens hinzufügen]{%
  (P \land Q) \rightarrow R \vdash (P \land (Q \land S)) \rightarrow R%
}{adantrr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \land S) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land (Q \land S)) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan2}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Äußeren linken Konjunkt in einer Dreifachkonjunktion hinzufügen]{%
  ((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash (((T \land P) \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S%
}{adantlll}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(T \land P) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{2}{((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(((T \land P) \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylanl1}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inneren linken Konjunkt in einer Dreifachkonjunktion hinzufügen]{%
  ((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash (((P \land T) \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S%
}{adantllr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land T) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{2}{((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(((P \land T) \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylanl1}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Linken inneren Konjunkt in der rechten Hälfte hinzufügen]{%
  ((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash ((P \land (T \land Q)) \land R) \rightarrow S%
}{adantlrl}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(T \land Q) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{2}{((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{((P \land (T \land Q)) \land R) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylanl2}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Rechten inneren Konjunkt in der rechten Hälfte hinzufügen]{%
  ((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash ((P \land (Q \land T)) \land R) \rightarrow S%
}{adantlrr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \land T) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{2}{((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{((P \land (Q \land T)) \land R) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylanl2}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Äußeren linken Konjunkt in einer rechtsgeschachtelten Dreifachkonjunktion hinzufügen]{%
  (P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S \vdash (P \land ((T \land Q) \land R)) \rightarrow S%
}{adantrll}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(T \land Q) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land ((T \land Q) \land R)) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylanr1}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inneren linken Konjunkt in einer rechtsgeschachtelten Dreifachkonjunktion hinzufügen]{%
  (P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S \vdash (P \land ((Q \land T) \land R)) \rightarrow S%
}{adantrlr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \land T) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land ((Q \land T) \land R)) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylanr1}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Äußeren rechten Konjunkt in einer rechtsgeschachtelten Dreifachkonjunktion hinzufügen]{%
  (P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S \vdash (P \land (Q \land (T \land R))) \rightarrow S%
}{adantrrl}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(T \land R) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land (Q \land (T \land R))) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylanr2}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inneren rechten Konjunkt in einer rechtsgeschachtelten Dreifachkonjunktion hinzufügen]{%
  (P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S \vdash (P \land (Q \land (R \land T))) \rightarrow S%
}{adantrrr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(R \land T) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land (Q \land (R \land T))) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylanr2}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Zwei Konjunkte rechts zum Antezedens hinzufügen]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \vdash ((P \land R) \land S) \rightarrow Q%
}{ad2antrr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land R) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantr}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land R) \land S) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantr}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Zwei Konjunkte links-rechts zum Antezedens hinzufügen]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \vdash ((R \land P) \land S) \rightarrow Q%
}{ad2antlr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(R \land P) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantl}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{((R \land P) \land S) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantr}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Zwei Konjunkte in links-rechts-geschachtelter Form hinzufügen]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \vdash (R \land (P \land S)) \rightarrow Q%
}{ad2antrl}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land S) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantr}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(R \land (P \land S)) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantl}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Zwei Konjunkte links zum Antezedens hinzufügen]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \vdash (R \land (S \land P)) \rightarrow Q%
}{ad2antll}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(S \land P) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantl}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(R \land (S \land P)) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantl}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Drei Konjunkte rechts zum Antezedens hinzufügen]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \vdash (((P \land R) \land S) \land T) \rightarrow Q%
}{ad3antrrr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land R) \land S) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{ad2antrr}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(((P \land R) \land S) \land T) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantr}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Drei Konjunkte mit linkem Start zum Antezedens hinzufügen]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \vdash (((R \land P) \land S) \land T) \rightarrow Q%
}{ad3antlr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{((R \land P) \land S) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{ad2antlr}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(((R \land P) \land S) \land T) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantr}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Vier Konjunkte rechts zum Antezedens hinzufügen]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \vdash ((((P \land R) \land S) \land T) \land U) \rightarrow Q%
}{ad4antr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T\dsep U}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(((P \land R) \land S) \land T) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{ad3antrrr}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{((((P \land R) \land S) \land T) \land U) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantr}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Vier Konjunkte mit linkem Start zum Antezedens hinzufügen]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \vdash ((((R \land P) \land S) \land T) \land U) \rightarrow Q%
}{ad4antlr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T\dsep U}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(((R \land P) \land S) \land T) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{ad3antlr}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{((((R \land P) \land S) \land T) \land U) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantr}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Fünf Konjunkte rechts zum Antezedens hinzufügen]{%
  P \rightarrow Q \vdash (((((P \land R) \land S) \land T) \land U) \land V) \rightarrow Q%
}{ad5antr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T\dsep U\dsep V}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{((((P \land R) \land S) \land T) \land U) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{ad4antr}{1}}

\begin{remark}
Die Sätze \FormulaRefAuto{adantll} bis \FormulaRefAuto{ad10antlr} sind rein strukturelle Regeln. Sie verändern keinen inhaltlichen Zusammenhang, sondern erlauben nur, zusätzliche Konjunkte an genau kontrollierten Positionen in ein Antezedens einzuschieben.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Zwei getrennte Konjunkte links hinzufügen]{%
  (P \land Q) \rightarrow R \vdash ((S \land P) \land (T \land Q)) \rightarrow R%
}{ad2ant2l}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{((S \land P) \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantll}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{((S \land P) \land (T \land Q)) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantrl}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Zwei getrennte Konjunkte rechts hinzufügen]{%
  (P \land Q) \rightarrow R \vdash ((P \land S) \land (Q \land T)) \rightarrow R%
}{ad2ant2r}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land S) \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantlr}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land S) \land (Q \land T)) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantrr}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Zwei getrennte Konjunkte in links-rechts-Form hinzufügen]{%
  (P \land Q) \rightarrow R \vdash ((S \land P) \land (Q \land T)) \rightarrow R%
}{ad2ant2lr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{((S \land P) \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantll}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{((S \land P) \land (Q \land T)) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantrr}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Zwei getrennte Konjunkte in rechts-links-Form hinzufügen]{%
  (P \land Q) \rightarrow R \vdash ((P \land S) \land (T \land Q)) \rightarrow R%
}{ad2ant2rl}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land S) \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantlr}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land S) \land (T \land Q)) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantrl}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Einen Konjunkt an der dritten linken Stelle hinzufügen]{%
  ((((P \land Q) \land R) \land S) \rightarrow T) \vdash (((((P \land U) \land Q) \land R) \land S) \rightarrow T)%
}{adantl3r}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T\dsep U}%

  \proofstep{3}{((((P \land U) \land Q) \land R) \land S) \rightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylanl1}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem \texorpdfstring{$*3.33$}{*3.33} von Whitehead-Russell]{%
  ((P \rightarrow Q) \land (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)%
}{pm3.33}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imim1}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \land (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem \texorpdfstring{$*3.34$}{*3.34} von Whitehead-Russell]{%
  ((Q \rightarrow R) \land (P \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)%
}{pm3.34}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \rightarrow R) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imim2}}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \rightarrow R) \land (P \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{pm3.33} und \FormulaRefAuto{pm3.34} sind die syllogistischen Grundformen in Konjunktionsgestalt: Statt zwei Implikationen als getrennte Voraussetzungen zu notieren, werden sie zu einer einzigen Konjunktion zusammengezogen.
\end{remark}

}{simpll}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}

  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{ad2antrr}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

  \proofstep{}{((Q \land R) \land S) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpll}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

  \end{aligned}
}{simp-11l}{%

}

  \proofstep{}{((((((((((P \land Q) \land R) \land S) \land T) \land U) \land V) \land W) \land X) \land Y) \land Z) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simp-10l}}
  \proofstep{}{(((((((((((P \land Q) \land R) \land S) \land T) \land U) \land V) \land W) \land X) \land Y) \land Z) \land A) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantr}{1}}

  \proofstep{}{(((((((((((P \land Q) \land R) \land S) \land T) \land U) \land V) \land W) \land X) \land Y) \land Z) \land A) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantr}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
Ab \FormulaRefAuto{simpll} beginnt eine ganze Familie von Vereinfachungsregeln. Inhaltlich sagen sie alle dasselbe: Aus einer Konjunktion darf jeder bereits bekannte Bestandteil wieder herausprojiziert werden. Die späteren Varianten sind vor allem technische Bequemlichkeitssätze.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Reductio-ad-absurdum-Deduktion mit Konjunktionsantezedens]{%
  (P \land Q) \rightarrow \neg Q \vdash P \rightarrow \neg Q%
}{pm2.01da}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow \neg Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow \neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.01d}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Clavius-Deduktion mit Konjunktionsantezedens]{%
  (P \land \neg Q) \rightarrow Q \vdash P \rightarrow Q%
}{pm2.18da}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land \neg Q) \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.18d}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.01da} und \FormulaRefAuto{pm2.18da} übertragen die früheren Widerspruchs- und Clavius-Schlüsse in eine konjunktive Antezedensform. Gerade in längeren Deduktionsbeweisen sind diese Fassungen oft deutlich handlicher als die rein implikative Schreibweise.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Leite eine Äquivalenz aus zwei Implikationen her]{%
  ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)\dsep ((P \land R) \rightarrow Q)\vdash P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)
}{impbida}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{((P \land R) \rightarrow Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{impbida} ist die an die Konjunktionsschreibweise angepasste Variante von \FormulaRefAuto{impbid}. Inhaltlich sagt der Satz: Wenn unter der Voraussetzung \(P\) sowohl \(Q \rightarrow R\) als auch \(R \rightarrow Q\) gilt, dann sind \(Q\) und \(R\) unter \(P\) äquivalent.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Elimination eines Antezedens, das beide Seiten einer Äquivalenz impliziert]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)\dsep
    ((P \land S) \rightarrow R)\dsep
    (R \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S))\vdash{}\\
    &\quad P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)
  \end{aligned}
}{pm5.21nd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land S) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{2}}
  \proofstep{3}{R \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.21ndd}{2,4,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Konjunktive Abtrennung]{%
  ((P \land (P \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow Q)
}{pm3.35}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land (P \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land (P \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land (P \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpd}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Distribution der Implikation über die Äquivalenz in Deduktionsform]{%
  ((P \land Q) \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)) \vdash P \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow S))
}{pm5.74da}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74d}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*4.22$}{*4.22} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  (((P \leftrightarrow Q) \land (Q \leftrightarrow R)) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R))
}{bitr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \land (Q \leftrightarrow R)) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \land (Q \leftrightarrow R)) \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \leftrightarrow Q) \land (Q \leftrightarrow R)) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitrd}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Transitivgesetz der Äquivalenz]{%
  (((P \leftrightarrow Q) \land (R \leftrightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R))
}{biantr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \land (R \leftrightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \land (R \leftrightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \leftrightarrow Q) \land (R \leftrightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr4d}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*4.14$}{*4.14} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  (((P \land Q) \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow ((P \land \neg R) \rightarrow \neg Q))
}{pm4.14}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \land Q) \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impexp}}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (\neg R \rightarrow \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{con34b}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (\neg R \rightarrow \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (\neg R \rightarrow \neg Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74i}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \land \neg R) \rightarrow \neg Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (\neg R \rightarrow \neg Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impexp}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \land Q) \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (\neg R \rightarrow \neg Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{1,4}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \land Q) \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow ((P \land \neg R) \rightarrow \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr4i}{6,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*3.37$}{*3.37} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  (((P \land Q) \rightarrow R) \rightarrow ((P \land \neg R) \rightarrow \neg Q))
}{pm3.37}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \land Q) \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow ((P \land \neg R) \rightarrow \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm4.14}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \land Q) \rightarrow R) \rightarrow ((P \land \neg R) \rightarrow \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Konjunktion von Antezedenzien und Konsequenzen zweier Prämissen]{%
  (((P \rightarrow Q) \land (R \rightarrow S)) \rightarrow ((P \land R) \rightarrow (Q \land S)))
}{anim12}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \land (R \rightarrow S)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \land (R \rightarrow S)) \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \land (R \rightarrow S)) \rightarrow ((P \land R) \rightarrow (Q \land S)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anim12d}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{anim12} ist Leibniz' \enquote{praeclarum theorema}: zwei getrennte Implikationen werden zu einer einzigen Implikation auf eine Konjunktion zusammengezogen.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Konjunktion impliziert Implikation]{%
  ((P \land Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))
}{pm3.4}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1d}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inferenzform von \FormulaRefAuto{exbir}]{%
  ((P \land Q) \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S)) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (S \rightarrow R))
}{exbiri}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land Q) \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{((R \leftrightarrow S) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpr}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (S \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl6}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Elimination eines Antezedens]{%
  ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)\dsep ((\neg P \land Q) \rightarrow R)\vdash Q \rightarrow R
}{pm2.61ian}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{expcom}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{(\neg P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \rightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{expcom}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61d}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Elimination eines Antezedens]{%
  ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)\dsep ((P \land \neg Q) \rightarrow R)\vdash P \rightarrow R
}{pm2.61dan}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land \neg Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61d}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Elimination zweier Antezedenzfälle]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &((P \land Q) \rightarrow S)\dsep
    ((P \land R) \rightarrow S)\dsep
    ((P \land (\neg Q \land \neg R)) \rightarrow S)\vdash{}\\
    &\quad P \rightarrow S
  \end{aligned}
}{pm2.61ddan}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{2}}
  \proofstep{3}{(P \land (\neg Q \land \neg R)) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{((P \land \neg Q) \land \neg R) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{anassrs}{3}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1d}{5}}
  \proofstep{2}{((P \land \neg Q) \land R) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp31}{7}}
  \proofstep{2,3}{(P \land \neg Q) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61dan}{8,6}}
  \proofstep{2,3}{P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{9}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61d}{2,10}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Elimination zweier Antezedenzfälle]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &((P \land \neg Q) \rightarrow S)\dsep
    ((P \land \neg R) \rightarrow S)\dsep
    ((P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S)\vdash{}\\
    &\quad P \rightarrow S
  \end{aligned}
}{pm2.61dda}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land \neg Q) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land \neg R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
