  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (\neg R \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{2}}
  \proofstep{3}{(P \land (Q \land R)) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{((P \land Q) \land R) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{anassrs}{3}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (\neg R \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1d}{5}}
  \proofstep{2}{((P \land Q) \land \neg R) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp31}{7}}
  \proofstep{2,3}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61dan}{6,8}}
  \proofstep{2,3}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{9}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61d}{10,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Modus-tollens-Deduktion mit Konjunktionsantezedens]{%
  P \rightarrow \neg R\dsep ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)\vdash P \rightarrow \neg Q
}{mtand}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow \neg R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{2}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (\neg R \rightarrow \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con2d}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow \neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpd}{1,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Widerspruchsbeweis als Deduktion]{%
  ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)\dsep ((P \land Q) \rightarrow \neg R)\vdash P \rightarrow \neg Q
}{pm2.65da}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow \neg R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow \neg R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow \neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.65d}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Beweis durch Widerspruch]{%
  ((P \land \neg Q) \rightarrow R)\dsep ((P \land \neg Q) \rightarrow \neg R)\vdash P \rightarrow Q
}{condan}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land \neg Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land \neg Q) \rightarrow \neg R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow \neg R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow \neg \neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.65d}{2,4}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{notnotrd}{5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Eine Implikation als Äquivalenz zweier anderer Äquivalenzaussagen]{%
  ((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow ((Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R))))
}{biadan}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,3}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{1,4}}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimprd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{7}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{6,8}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{baib}}
  \proofstep{1}{(Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R)) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R))}{\rRI{2,9}}
  \proofstep{1}{((Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{11,10}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R))))}{\rRI{1,12}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inferenz zu \FormulaRefAuto{biadan}]{%
  (P \rightarrow Q) \vdash ((Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)))
}{biadani}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biadan}}
  \proofstep{1}{((Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Alternativer Beweis zu \FormulaRefAuto{biadani}]{%
  (P \rightarrow Q) \vdash ((Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)))
}{biadaniALT}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{baib}}
  \proofstep{3}{Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpd}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,3}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,4}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{1,3}{P \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{1,5}}
  \proofstep{3}{Q \rightarrow (R \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimprd}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,3}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{8}}
  \proofstep{1,3}{P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{7,9}}
  \proofstep{1}{(Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R)) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R))}{\rRI{3,10}}
  \proofstep{1}{((Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{11,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Füge einer Äquivalenz einen Konjunkt hinzu]{%
  (P \rightarrow Q)\dsep (Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R))\vdash P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)
}{biadanii}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{((Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow (P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biadani}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \leftrightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbi}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{biadan}, \FormulaRefAuto{biadani} und \FormulaRefAuto{biadanii} sind nützliche technische Werkzeuge, um eine bereits bekannte Implikation \(P \rightarrow Q\) in eine Äquivalenzaussage mit zusätzlicher Konjunktion einzubauen.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Deduktion zu \FormulaRefAuto{biadani}]{%
  ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)\dsep ((P \land R) \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (R \land S))
}{biadanid}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{((P \land R) \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,3}{Q \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{3,\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{1}}}
  \proofstep{2,3}{R \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{3,\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{2}}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{Q \leftrightarrow (R \land S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biadanii}{4,5}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (R \land S))}{\rRI{3,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Zwei Aussagen sind äquivalent, wenn beide wahr sind]{%
  ((P \land Q) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q))
}{pm5.1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.1im}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Zwei Aussagen sind äquivalent, wenn beide falsch sind]{%
  ((\neg P \land \neg Q) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q))
}{pm5.21}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.21im}}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg P \land \neg Q) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*5.35$}{*5.35} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  (((P \rightarrow Q) \land (P \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)))
}{pm5.35}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \land (P \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.1}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \land (P \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74rd}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Führe einen Konjunkt als Antezedens des anderen ein]{%
  ((P \land Q) \leftrightarrow (P \land (P \rightarrow Q)))
}{abai}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1d}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow (P \land (P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{1,3}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land (P \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land (P \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm3.35}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land (P \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (P \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{5,6}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \leftrightarrow (P \land (P \rightarrow Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{4,7}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Konjunktion mit einer Implikation]{%
  (P \leftrightarrow (P \land (Q \rightarrow P)))
}{pm4.45im}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-1}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (P \land (Q \rightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land (Q \rightarrow P)) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \leftrightarrow (P \land (Q \rightarrow P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Eine Implikation und ihre Umkehrung sind genau dann äquivalent, wenn die Operanden äquivalent sind]{%
  ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow P)))
}{impimprbi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{2th}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow P))}{\rRI{1,4}}
  \proofstep{6}{(P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow P)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{6}{(P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{6}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg (P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.521}}
  \proofstep{6}{Q \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61i}{7,8}}
  \proofstep{6}{(Q \rightarrow P) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}{6}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg (Q \rightarrow P) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.521}}
  \proofstep{6}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61i}{10,11}}
  \proofstep{6}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{12,9}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow P)) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q))}{\rRI{6,13}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{5,14}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Verschiebe einen Konjunkt in eine Negation hinein bzw. aus ihr heraus]{%
  ((P \rightarrow \neg (Q \land R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \land Q) \rightarrow \neg R))
}{nan}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \rightarrow \neg R) \leftrightarrow \neg (Q \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imnan}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow \neg R) \leftrightarrow \neg (Q \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow \neg R)) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow \neg (Q \land R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74i}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \land Q) \rightarrow \neg R) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow \neg R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impexp}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow \neg (Q \land R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \land Q) \rightarrow \neg R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr2i}{4,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*5.31$}{*5.31} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  ((R \land (P \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \land R)))
}{pm5.31}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(R \land (P \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{}{(R \land (P \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1d}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(R \land (P \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{}{((R \land (P \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \land R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jcad}{3,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Variante von \FormulaRefAuto{pm5.31}]{%
  ((R \land (P \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (R \land Q)))
}{pm5.31r}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(R \land (P \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{}{(R \land (P \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1d}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(R \land (P \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{}{((R \land (P \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (R \land Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jcad}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*4.15$}{*4.15} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  (((P \land Q) \rightarrow \neg R) \leftrightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow \neg P))
}{pm4.15}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(((Q \land R) \rightarrow \neg P) \leftrightarrow ((Q \land \neg \neg P) \rightarrow \neg R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm4.14}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \leftrightarrow \neg \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{notnotb}}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \land \neg \neg P) \leftrightarrow (Q \land P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anbi2i}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(((Q \land \neg \neg P) \rightarrow \neg R) \leftrightarrow ((Q \land P) \rightarrow \neg R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi1i}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{(((Q \land P) \rightarrow \neg R) \leftrightarrow ((P \land Q) \rightarrow \neg R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancomst}}
  \proofstep{}{(((Q \land R) \rightarrow \neg P) \leftrightarrow ((Q \land P) \rightarrow \neg R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{1,4}}
  \proofstep{}{(((Q \land R) \rightarrow \neg P) \leftrightarrow ((P \land Q) \rightarrow \neg R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{6,5}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \land Q) \rightarrow \neg R) \leftrightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow \neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicom}{7}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*5.36$}{*5.36} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  ((P \land (P \leftrightarrow Q)) \leftrightarrow (Q \land (P \leftrightarrow Q)))
}{pm5.36}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \land (P \leftrightarrow Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpli}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpri}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbi}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \land (P \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{\rAI{4,3}}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land (P \leftrightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (Q \land (P \leftrightarrow Q))}{\rRI{1,5}}
  \proofstep{7}{Q \land (P \leftrightarrow Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{7}{Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpli}{7}}
  \proofstep{7}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpri}{7}}
  \proofstep{7}{P}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbird}{8,9}}
  \proofstep{7}{P \land (P \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{\rAI{10,9}}}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \land (P \leftrightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (P \land (P \leftrightarrow Q))}{\rRI{7,11}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land (P \leftrightarrow Q)) \leftrightarrow (Q \land (P \leftrightarrow Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{6,12}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Eine Konjunktion mit einer negierten Konjunktion]{%
  ((P \land \neg (P \land Q)) \leftrightarrow (P \land \neg Q))
}{annotanannot}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land \neg (P \land Q)) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land \neg (P \land Q)) \rightarrow \neg (P \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land \neg (P \land Q)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylibr}{2,\FormulaRefAuto{imnan}}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land \neg (P \land Q)) \rightarrow \neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpd}{1,3}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land \neg (P \land Q)) \rightarrow (P \land \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{1,4}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land \neg Q) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land \neg Q) \rightarrow \neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land \neg Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1d}{7}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land \neg Q) \rightarrow \neg (P \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylib}{8,\FormulaRefAuto{imnan}}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land \neg Q) \rightarrow (P \land \neg (P \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{6,9}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land \neg (P \land Q)) \leftrightarrow (P \land \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{5,10}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*5.33$}{*5.33} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  ((P \land (Q \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow (P \land ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)))
}{pm5.33}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impd}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (P \land ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{1,3}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{6}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpd}{5,7}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (P \land (Q \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{5,8}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land (Q \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow (P \land ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{4,9}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Kombination von Syllogismus und Kontraktion]{%
  (P \rightarrow Q)\dsep (P \rightarrow R)\dsep (P \rightarrow S)\dsep ((Q \land (R \land S)) \rightarrow T)\vdash P \rightarrow T
}{syl12anc}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2,3}{P \rightarrow (R \land S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{4}{(Q \land (R \land S)) \rightarrow T}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2,3,4}{P \rightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl2anc}{1,4,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Kombination von Syllogismus und Kontraktion]{%
  (P \rightarrow Q)\dsep (P \rightarrow R)\dsep (P \rightarrow S)\dsep (((Q \land R) \land S) \rightarrow T)\vdash P \rightarrow T
}{syl21anc}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{4}{((Q \land R) \land S) \rightarrow T}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2,3,4}{P \rightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl2anc}{4,3,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Kombination von Syllogismus und Kontraktion]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &(P \rightarrow Q)\dsep (P \rightarrow R)\dsep (P \rightarrow S)\dsep (P \rightarrow T)\dsep{}\\
    &\quad (((Q \land R) \land (S \land T)) \rightarrow U)\vdash P \rightarrow U
  \end{aligned}
}{syl22anc}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T\dsep U}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{4}{P \rightarrow T}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{3,4}{P \rightarrow (S \land T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{5}{(((Q \land R) \land (S \land T)) \rightarrow U)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2,3,4,5}{P \rightarrow U}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl2anc}{5,6,7}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Elimination einer Hilfshypothese in einer Äquivalenz]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)\dsep
    ((P \land S) \rightarrow R)\dsep
    ((P \land R) \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S))\vdash{}\\
    &\quad P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)
  \end{aligned}
}{bibiad}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{((P \land S) \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{2}}
  \proofstep{3}{((P \land R) \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.21ndd}{2,4,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Vierfache Hypothesenelimination für ein einzelnes virtuelles Hypothesensystem]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &(P \rightarrow Q)\dsep (P \rightarrow R)\dsep (P \rightarrow S)\dsep (P \rightarrow T)\dsep{}\\
    &\quad ((((Q \land R) \land S) \land T) \rightarrow U)\vdash P \rightarrow U
  \end{aligned}
}{syl1111anc}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T\dsep U}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{4}{P \rightarrow T}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (Q \land R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{5}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \land S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{5,3}}
  \proofstep{6}{((((Q \land R) \land S) \land T) \rightarrow U)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2,3,4,6}{P \rightarrow U}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl2anc}{6,4,7}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Gestapelte Hypothesen implizieren das Ziel]{%
  ((P \land Q) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \vdash ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)
}{syldbl2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land Q) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpd}{2,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Eine Eliminationsdeduktion]{%
  P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep (S \rightarrow T)\dsep ((((P \land Q) \land R) \land T) \rightarrow U)\vdash S \rightarrow U
}{mpsyl4anc}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T\dsep U}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{4}{S \rightarrow T}{\rA}
  \proofstep{5}{((((P \land Q) \land R) \land T) \rightarrow U)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{S \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{S \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{2}}
  \proofstep{3}{S \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3,4,5}{S \rightarrow U}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl1111anc}{6,7,8,4,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$4.87$}{4.87} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &\Bigl(\bigl(((P \land Q) \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R))\bigr)\land{}\\
    &\qquad\bigl((P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R))\bigr)\Bigr)\land{}\\
    &\qquad\bigl((Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow ((Q \land P) \rightarrow R)\bigr)
  \end{aligned}
}{pm4.87}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{%
    (((P \land Q) \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)))
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{impexp}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    ((P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)))
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{bi2.04}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    (((Q \land P) \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)))
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{impexp}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    ((Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow ((Q \land P) \rightarrow R))
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{bicom}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    \begin{aligned}[t]
      &\Bigl(\bigl(((P \land Q) \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R))\bigr)\land{}\\
      &\qquad\bigl((P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R))\bigr)\Bigr)
    \end{aligned}
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    \begin{aligned}[t]
      &\Bigl(\bigl(((P \land Q) \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R))\bigr)\land{}\\
      &\qquad\bigl((P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R))\bigr)\Bigr)\land{}\\
      &\qquad\bigl((Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow ((Q \land P) \rightarrow R)\bigr)
    \end{aligned}
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{5,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Entfernung eines Konjunkts aus einer Seite einer Äquivalenz]{%
  (((P \rightarrow Q) \land (R \leftrightarrow (Q \land P))) \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow P))
}{bimsc1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \land (R \leftrightarrow (Q \land P))) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \land (R \leftrightarrow (Q \land P))) \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow (Q \land P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \land (R \leftrightarrow (Q \land P))) \land R) \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simplbda}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \land (R \leftrightarrow (Q \land P))) \rightarrow (R \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \land (R \leftrightarrow (Q \land P))) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{idd}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    ((P \rightarrow Q) \land (R \leftrightarrow (Q \land P))) \rightarrow
    (P \rightarrow (Q \land P))
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{jcad}{1,5}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \land (R \leftrightarrow (Q \land P))) \rightarrow ((Q \land P) \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimprd}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \land (R \leftrightarrow (Q \land P))) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl6}{6,7}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \land (R \leftrightarrow (Q \land P))) \rightarrow (R \leftrightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid}{8,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktion, die eine Konjunktion als eingebetteten Antezedens verteilt]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow T))\dsep
    P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow U)\vdash{}\\
    &\quad P \rightarrow (((Q \land U) \rightarrow S) \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow T))
  \end{aligned}
}{a2and}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T\dsep U}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow T))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow U)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{4}{((Q \land U) \rightarrow S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{5}{(Q \land R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{5}{Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpli}{5}}
  \proofstep{5}{R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpri}{5}}
  \proofstep{2,3}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow U}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{3,2}}
  \proofstep{2,3,5}{U}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{5,8}}
  \proofstep{3,5}{Q \land U}{\FormulaRefAuto{rAI}{6,9}}
  \proofstep{3,4,5}{S}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{10,4}}
  \proofstep{1,3}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{3,1}}
  \proofstep{1,3,5}{S \rightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{5,12}}
  \proofstep{1,3,4,5}{T}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{11,13}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3,4}{(Q \land R) \rightarrow T}{\rRI{5,14}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{((Q \land U) \rightarrow S) \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow T)}{\rRI{4,15}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow (((Q \land U) \rightarrow S) \rightarrow ((Q \land R) \rightarrow T))}{\rRI{3,16}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktion, die verschachtelte Implikationen aus Konjunktionen gewinnt]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &((Q \land P) \rightarrow (R \rightarrow (S \rightarrow T)))\dsep
    ((Q \land (P \land S)) \rightarrow (T \rightarrow U))\vdash{}\\
    &\quad P \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (S \rightarrow U)))
  \end{aligned}
}{animpimp2impd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T\dsep U}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{((Q \land P) \rightarrow (R \rightarrow (S \rightarrow T)))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{((Q \land (P \land S)) \rightarrow (T \rightarrow U))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{4}{(Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{5}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{6}{S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{4,5}{R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{5,4}}
  \proofstep{3,5}{Q \land P}{\FormulaRefAuto{rAI}{5,3}}
  \proofstep{1,3,5}{R \rightarrow (S \rightarrow T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{8,1}}
  \proofstep{1,3,4,5}{S \rightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{7,9}}
  \proofstep{1,3,4,5,6}{T}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{6,10}}
  \proofstep{3,6}{P \land S}{\FormulaRefAuto{rAI}{3,6}}
  \proofstep{3,5,6}{Q \land (P \land S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{rAI}{5,12}}
  \proofstep{2,3,5,6}{T \rightarrow U}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{13,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3,4,5,6}{U}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{11,14}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3,4,5}{S \rightarrow U}{\rRI{6,15}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3,4}{Q \rightarrow (S \rightarrow U)}{\rRI{5,16}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{(Q \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (S \rightarrow U))}{\rRI{4,17}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (S \rightarrow U)))}{\rRI{3,18}}
\end{tabproof}

\section{Logische Disjunktion}

\FormulaDef[Disjunktion als Bildungsregel]{%
  \text{Sind } P \text{ und } Q \text{ wohlgeformte Formeln, so ist auch } (P \lor Q) \text{ eine wohlgeformte Formel.}%
}{wo}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}

\FormulaDef[Definition der Disjunktion]{%
  ((P \lor Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow Q))
}{df-or}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{df-or} ist eine genuin klassische Definition: Die Disjunktion wird vollständig aus Negation und Implikation aufgebaut. Genau deshalb lässt sich der folgende Abschnitt noch ohne Rückgriff auf \FormulaRefAuto{df-an} entwickeln.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*4.64$}{*4.64} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  ((\neg P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \lor Q))
}{pm4.64}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-or}}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*4.66$}{*4.66} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  ((\neg P \rightarrow \neg Q) \leftrightarrow (P \lor \neg Q))
}{pm4.66}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg P \rightarrow \neg Q) \leftrightarrow (P \lor \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm4.64}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*2.53$}{*2.53} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  ((P \lor Q) \rightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow Q))
}{pm2.53}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-or}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor Q) \rightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimp}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*2.54$}{*2.54} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  ((\neg P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \lor Q))
}{pm2.54}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm4.64}}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Implikation in Form einer Disjunktion]{%
  ((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \lor Q))
}{imor}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg \neg P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm4.64}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \leftrightarrow \neg \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{notnotb}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg \neg P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi2i}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{3,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Schließe von einer Implikation auf eine Disjunktion]{%
  (P \rightarrow Q) \vdash (\neg P \lor Q)
}{imori}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imor}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\neg P \lor Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Schließe von einer Disjunktion auf eine Implikation]{%
  (\neg P \lor Q) \vdash (P \rightarrow Q)
}{imorri}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\neg P \lor Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imor}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbir}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*4.62$}{*4.62} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  ((P \rightarrow \neg Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \lor \neg Q))
}{pm4.62}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow \neg Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \lor \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imor}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inferenz, die die Antezedenzien zweier Implikationen disjungiert]{%
  (P \rightarrow Q)\dsep (R \rightarrow Q)\vdash ((P \lor R) \rightarrow Q)
}{jaoi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{(P \lor R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{\neg P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ori}{3}}
  \proofstep{2,3}{\neg P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{4,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61i}{1,5}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{((P \lor R) \rightarrow Q)}{\rRI{3,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Füge im Antezedens einer Implikation einen Disjunkt hinzu]{%
  (\psi \rightarrow (\chi \rightarrow \phi)) \vdash ((\phi \lor \psi) \rightarrow (\chi \rightarrow \phi))
}{jao1i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{\phi\dsep \psi\dsep \chi}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\psi \rightarrow (\chi \rightarrow \phi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow (\chi \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-1}}
  \proofstep{1}{((\phi \lor \psi) \rightarrow (\chi \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jaoi}{2,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktion, die die Antezedenzien zweier Implikationen disjungiert]{%
  (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R))\dsep (P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow R))\vdash P \rightarrow ((Q \lor S) \rightarrow R)
}{jaod}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,3}{Q \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{3,1}}
  \proofstep{2,3}{S \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{3,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{(Q \lor S) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{jaoi}{4,5}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow ((Q \lor S) \rightarrow R)}{\rRI{3,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Eliminiere eine Disjunktion in einer Deduktion]{%
  (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R))\dsep (P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow R))\dsep (P \rightarrow (Q \lor S))\vdash P \rightarrow R
}{mpjaod}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow (Q \lor S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow ((Q \lor S) \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jaod}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpd}{3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Schließe von einer Disjunktion auf eine Implikation]{%
  (P \lor Q) \vdash \neg P \rightarrow Q
}{ori}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \lor Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-or}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\neg P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Schließe von einer Implikation auf eine Disjunktion]{%
  (\neg P \rightarrow Q) \vdash (P \lor Q)
}{orri}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\neg P \rightarrow Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-or}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \lor Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbir}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduziere eine Disjunktion aus einer Implikation]{%
  (P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow R)) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \lor R)
}{orrd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg Q \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (Q \lor R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.54}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \lor R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduziere eine Implikation aus einer Disjunktion]{%
  (P \rightarrow (Q \lor R)) \vdash P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow R)
}{ord}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \lor R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \lor R) \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.53}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inferenz zur Einführung eines rechten Disjunkts]{%
  P \vdash (P \lor Q)
}{orci}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.21i}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \lor Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orri}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inferenz zur Einführung eines linken Disjunkts]{%
  P \vdash (Q \lor P)
}{olci}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg Q \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(Q \lor P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orri}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Einführung eines rechten Disjunkts]{%
  (P \rightarrow (P \lor Q))
}{orc}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.21i}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \lor Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orri}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow (P \lor Q))}{\rRI{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Einführung eines linken Disjunkts]{%
  (P \rightarrow (Q \lor P))
}{olc}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg Q \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(Q \lor P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orri}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow (Q \lor P))}{\rRI{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Axiom \texorpdfstring{$*1.4$}{*1.4} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  ((P \lor Q) \rightarrow (Q \lor P))
}{pm1.4}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow (Q \lor P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{olc}}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \rightarrow (Q \lor P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orc}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor Q) \rightarrow (Q \lor P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jaoi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Kommutativgesetz der Disjunktion]{%
  ((P \lor Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \lor P))
}{orcom}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor Q) \rightarrow (Q \lor P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm1.4}}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \lor P) \rightarrow (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm1.4}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \lor P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Vertauschung der Disjunkte im Konsequens]{%
  (P \rightarrow (Q \lor R)) \vdash P \rightarrow (R \lor Q)
}{orcomd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \lor R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \lor R) \rightarrow (R \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm1.4}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (R \lor Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Vertauschung der Disjunkte im Antezedens]{%
  ((P \lor Q) \rightarrow R) \vdash ((Q \lor P) \rightarrow R)
}{orcoms}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \lor Q) \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \lor P) \rightarrow (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm1.4}}
  \proofstep{1}{((Q \lor P) \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{2,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktion zur Einführung eines rechten Disjunkts]{%
  (P \rightarrow Q) \vdash P \rightarrow (Q \lor R)
}{orcd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \rightarrow (Q \lor R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orc}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \lor R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktion zur Einführung eines linken Disjunkts]{%
  (P \rightarrow Q) \vdash P \rightarrow (R \lor Q)
}{olcd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \rightarrow (R \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{olc}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (R \lor Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktion zur Elimination eines Disjunkts]{%
  ((P \lor Q) \rightarrow R) \vdash P \rightarrow R
}{orcs}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \lor Q) \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orc}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{2,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktion zur Elimination eines Disjunkts]{%
  ((P \lor Q) \rightarrow R) \vdash Q \rightarrow R
}{olcs}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \lor Q) \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \rightarrow (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{olc}}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{2,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Hilfssatz für Unit-Propagation in konjunktiver Normalform]{%
  (P \rightarrow (Q \lor R))\dsep (P \rightarrow \neg R)\vdash P \rightarrow Q
}{olcnd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \lor R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow \neg R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ord}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\neg R \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con4d}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpd}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Hilfssatz für Unit-Propagation in konjunktiver Normalform]{%
  (P \rightarrow (Q \lor R))\dsep (P \rightarrow \neg Q)\vdash P \rightarrow R
}{orcnd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \lor R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow \neg Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ord}{1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpd}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Modus-tollens-Deduktion mit Disjunktion]{%
  (P \rightarrow \neg R)\dsep (P \rightarrow \neg S)\dsep (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \lor S)))\vdash P \rightarrow \neg Q
}{mtord}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow \neg R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow \neg S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (R \lor S))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow (R \lor S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{3}}
  \proofstep{3}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow (\neg R \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ord}{4}}
  \proofstep{3}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (\neg R \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{5}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow \neg R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1d}{1}}
  \proofstep{1,3}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpdd}{7,6}}
  \proofstep{1,3}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow \neg \neg S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{notnotd}{8}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{P \rightarrow \neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{mt2d}{2,9}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Schließe auf eine negierte Disjunktion aus negierten Prämissen]{%
  \neg P\dsep \neg Q\vdash \neg (P \lor Q)
}{pm3.2ni}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\neg Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor Q) \rightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.53}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \lor Q) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpi}{1,3}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \lor Q) \rightarrow \neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\neg (P \lor Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.65i}{4,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*2.45$}{*2.45} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  (\neg (P \lor Q) \rightarrow \neg P)
}{pm2.45}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orc}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg (P \lor Q) \rightarrow \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con3i}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*2.46$}{*2.46} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  (\neg (P \lor Q) \rightarrow \neg Q)
}{pm2.46}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \rightarrow (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{olc}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg (P \lor Q) \rightarrow \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con3i}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*2.47$}{*2.47} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  (\neg (P \lor Q) \rightarrow (\neg P \lor Q))
}{pm2.47}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg (P \lor Q) \rightarrow \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.45}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg (P \lor Q) \rightarrow (\neg P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orcd}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*2.48$}{*2.48} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  (\neg (P \lor Q) \rightarrow (P \lor \neg Q))
}{pm2.48}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg (P \lor Q) \rightarrow \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.46}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg (P \lor Q) \rightarrow (P \lor \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{olcd}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*2.49$}{*2.49} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  (\neg (P \lor Q) \rightarrow (\neg P \lor \neg Q))
}{pm2.49}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg (P \lor Q) \rightarrow \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.46}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg (P \lor Q) \rightarrow (\neg P \lor \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{olcd}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Sind weder von zwei Aussagen wahr, so sind sie äquivalent]{%
  (\neg (P \lor Q) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q))
}{norbi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg (P \lor Q) \rightarrow \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.45}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg (P \lor Q) \rightarrow \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.46}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg (P \lor Q) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{2falsed}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Sind zwei Aussagen nicht äquivalent, so ist mindestens eine von beiden wahr]{%
  (\neg (P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \lor Q))
}{nbior}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg (P \lor Q) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{norbi}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg \neg (P \leftrightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{notnot}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg (P \lor Q) \rightarrow \neg \neg (P \leftrightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg (P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{con4i}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Elimination einer Disjunktion durch Verneinung eines Disjunkts]{%
  (\neg P \rightarrow ((P \lor Q) \rightarrow Q))
}{orel1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor Q) \rightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.53}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \rightarrow ((P \lor Q) \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{com12}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*2.25$}{*2.25} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  (P \lor ((P \lor Q) \rightarrow Q))
}{pm2.25}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \rightarrow ((P \lor Q) \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orel1}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \lor ((P \lor Q) \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orri}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Elimination einer Disjunktion durch Verneinung eines Disjunkts]{%
  (\neg P \rightarrow ((Q \lor P) \rightarrow Q))
}{orel2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \rightarrow ((P \lor Q) \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orel1}}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \lor P) \rightarrow (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm1.4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \rightarrow ((Q \lor P) \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl5}{2,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Leichte Verallgemeinerung von Satz \texorpdfstring{$*2.67$}{*2.67}]{%
  (((P \lor R) \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))
}{pm2.67-2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \lor R) \rightarrow Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow (P \lor R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orc}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{2,1}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \lor R) \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))}{\rRI{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*2.67$}{*2.67} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  (((P \lor Q) \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))
}{pm2.67}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \lor Q) \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.67-2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Curry-Axiom]{%
  (P \lor (P \rightarrow Q))
}{curryax}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow (P \lor (P \rightarrow Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orc}}
  \proofstep{2}{\neg P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.21i}{2}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \lor (P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{olci}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \rightarrow (P \lor (P \rightarrow Q)))}{\rRI{2,4}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor \neg P) \rightarrow (P \lor (P \rightarrow Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jaoi}{1,5}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \lor \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{exmid}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \lor (P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{7,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz vom ausgeschlossenen Dritten]{%
  (P \lor \neg P)
}{exmid}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \rightarrow \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \lor \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orri}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{exmid} ist der klassische Satz vom ausgeschlossenen Dritten. Zusammen mit \FormulaRefAuto{notnotr} markiert er besonders deutlich den Unterschied zwischen klassischer und intuitionistischer Logik.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Satz vom ausgeschlossenen Dritten im Kontext]{%
  (P \rightarrow (Q \lor \neg Q))
}{exmidd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \lor \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{exmid}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow (Q \lor \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*2.1$}{*2.1} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  (\neg P \lor P)
}{pm2.1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \lor \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{exmid}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \lor P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orcom}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*2.13$}{*2.13} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  (P \lor \neg \neg \neg P)
}{pm2.13}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \rightarrow \neg \neg \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{notnot}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \lor \neg \neg \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orri}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*2.621$}{*2.621} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \lor Q) \rightarrow Q))
}{pm2.621}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{Q \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \lor Q) \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jaoi}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \lor Q) \rightarrow Q))}{\rRI{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \texorpdfstring{$*2.62$}{*2.62} von Whitehead--Russell]{%
  ((P \lor Q) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow Q))
}{pm2.62}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \lor Q) \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.621}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor Q) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{com12}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *2.68 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  (((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \lor Q))
}{pm2.68}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jarl}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orrd}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Logical \enquote{or} expressed in terms of implication only]{%
  ((P \lor Q) \leftrightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow Q))
}{dfor2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \lor Q) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.62}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \lor Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.68}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor Q) \leftrightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}
\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{dfor2} ist für reine Implikationsbeweise besonders nützlich: Disjunktionen können damit vollständig durch \(\rightarrow\) beschrieben werden.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *2.07 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  (P \rightarrow (P \lor P))
}{pm2.07}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (P \lor P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{olc}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Axiom *1.2 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  ((P \lor P) \rightarrow P)
}{pm1.2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor P) \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jaoi}{1,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Idempotent law for disjunction]{%
  ((P \lor P) \leftrightarrow P)
}{oridm}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor P) \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm1.2}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (P \lor P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.07}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor P) \leftrightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *4.25 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  (P \leftrightarrow (P \lor P))
}{pm4.25}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor P) \leftrightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{oridm}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \leftrightarrow (P \lor P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *2.4 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  ((P \lor (P \lor Q)) \rightarrow (P \lor Q))
}{pm2.4}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (P \lor Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orc}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \lor Q) \rightarrow (P \lor Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor (P \lor Q)) \rightarrow (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jaoi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *2.41 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  ((Q \lor (P \lor Q)) \rightarrow (P \lor Q))
}{pm2.41}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{Q \rightarrow (P \lor Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{olc}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \lor Q) \rightarrow (P \lor Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \lor (P \lor Q)) \rightarrow (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jaoi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Disjoin antecedents and consequents of two premises]{%
  P \rightarrow Q\dsep R \rightarrow S\vdash (P \lor R) \rightarrow (Q \lor S)
}{orim12i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \lor S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orcd}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{R \rightarrow (Q \lor S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{olcd}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \lor R) \rightarrow (Q \lor S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jaoi}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Introduce disjunct to both sides of an implication]{%
  P \rightarrow Q\vdash (P \lor R) \rightarrow (Q \lor R)
}{orim1i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{R \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \lor R) \rightarrow (Q \lor R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orim12i}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Introduce disjunct to both sides of an implication]{%
  P \rightarrow Q\vdash (R \lor P) \rightarrow (R \lor Q)
}{orim2i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{R \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(R \lor P) \rightarrow (R \lor Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orim12i}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Alternate proof of orim12d which does not depend on df-an]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)\dsep P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow T)\vdash{}\\
    &\quad P \rightarrow ((Q \lor S) \rightarrow (R \lor T))
  \end{aligned}
}{orim12dALT}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \lor S) \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.53}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\neg R \rightarrow \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con3d}{2}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (S \rightarrow T)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow ((\neg Q \rightarrow S) \rightarrow (\neg R \rightarrow T))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imim12d}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg R \rightarrow T) \rightarrow (R \lor T)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.54}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow ((Q \lor S) \rightarrow (R \lor T))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl56}{1,5,6}}
\end{tabproof}
\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{orim12dALT} ist vor allem konzeptionell interessant: der Satz zeigt, dass \FormulaRefAuto{orim12d} auch ohne Rückgriff auf die Definitionsgleichung der Konjunktion bewiesen werden kann. Genau das illustriert die Konservativität bloßer Definitionen.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Inference adding a left disjunct to both sides of a logical equivalence]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q\vdash (R \lor P) \leftrightarrow (R \lor Q)
}{orbi2i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(R \lor P) \rightarrow (R \lor Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orim2i}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(R \lor Q) \rightarrow (R \lor P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orim2i}{4}}
  \proofstep{1}{(R \lor P) \leftrightarrow (R \lor Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{3,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inference adding a right disjunct to both sides of a logical equivalence]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q\vdash (P \lor R) \leftrightarrow (Q \lor R)
}{orbi1i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \lor R) \leftrightarrow (R \lor P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orcom}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(R \lor P) \leftrightarrow (R \lor Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orbi2i}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(R \lor Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \lor R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orcom}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \lor R) \leftrightarrow (Q \lor R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitri}{1,3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Infer the disjunction of two equivalences]{%
  P \leftrightarrow Q\dsep R \leftrightarrow S\vdash (P \lor R) \leftrightarrow (Q \lor S)
}{orbi12i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{2}{R \leftrightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \lor R) \leftrightarrow (P \lor S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orbi2i}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \leftrightarrow Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \lor S) \leftrightarrow (Q \lor S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orbi1i}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \lor R) \leftrightarrow (Q \lor S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction adding a left disjunct to both sides of a logical equivalence]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\vdash P \rightarrow ((S \lor Q) \leftrightarrow (S \lor R))
}{orbi2d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((\neg S \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg S \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi2d}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(S \lor Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg S \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-or}}
  \proofstep{}{(S \lor R) \leftrightarrow (\neg S \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-or}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((S \lor Q) \leftrightarrow (S \lor R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4g}{2,3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction adding a right disjunct to both sides of a logical equivalence]{%
  P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\vdash P \rightarrow ((Q \lor S) \leftrightarrow (R \lor S))
}{orbi1d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((S \lor Q) \leftrightarrow (S \lor R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orbi2d}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \lor S) \leftrightarrow (S \lor Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orcom}}
  \proofstep{}{(R \lor S) \leftrightarrow (S \lor R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orcom}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((Q \lor S) \leftrightarrow (R \lor S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4g}{2,3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *4.37 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \lor R) \leftrightarrow (Q \lor R)))
}{orbi1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((P \lor R) \leftrightarrow (Q \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orbi1d}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduction joining two equivalences to form equivalence of disjunctions]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)\dsep P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow T)\vdash{}\\
    &\quad P \rightarrow ((Q \lor S) \leftrightarrow (R \lor T))
  \end{aligned}
}{orbi12d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((Q \lor S) \leftrightarrow (R \lor S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orbi1d}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow (S \leftrightarrow T)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow ((R \lor S) \leftrightarrow (R \lor T))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orbi2d}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow ((Q \lor S) \leftrightarrow (R \lor T))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitrd}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Axiom *1.5 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  ((P \lor (Q \lor R)) \rightarrow (Q \lor (P \lor R)))
}{pm1.5}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (P \lor R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orc}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (Q \lor (P \lor R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{olcd}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{R \rightarrow (P \lor R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{olc}}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \lor R) \rightarrow (Q \lor (P \lor R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orim2i}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor (Q \lor R)) \rightarrow (Q \lor (P \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jaoi}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Swap two disjuncts]{%
  ((P \lor (Q \lor R)) \leftrightarrow (Q \lor (P \lor R)))
}{or12}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor (Q \lor R)) \rightarrow (Q \lor (P \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm1.5}}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \lor (P \lor R)) \rightarrow (P \lor (Q \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm1.5}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor (Q \lor R)) \leftrightarrow (Q \lor (P \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Associative law for disjunction]{%
  (((P \lor Q) \lor R) \leftrightarrow (P \lor (Q \lor R)))
}{orass}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor Q) \lor R) \leftrightarrow (R \lor (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orcom}}
  \proofstep{}{(R \lor (P \lor Q)) \leftrightarrow (P \lor (R \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{or12}}
  \proofstep{}{(R \lor Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \lor R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orcom}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \lor (R \lor Q)) \leftrightarrow (P \lor (Q \lor R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orbi2i}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \lor Q) \lor R) \leftrightarrow (P \lor (Q \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitri}{1,2,4}}
\end{tabproof}
\begin{remark}
Mit \FormulaRefAuto{orass} ist die Disjunktion nun bis auf Äquivalenz assoziativ. Dieser Satz wird in den nächsten Umordnungs- und Distributionssätzen ständig benutzt.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *2.31 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  ((P \lor (Q \lor R)) \rightarrow ((P \lor Q) \lor R))
}{pm2.31}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \lor Q) \lor R) \leftrightarrow (P \lor (Q \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orass}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor (Q \lor R)) \rightarrow ((P \lor Q) \lor R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *2.32 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  (((P \lor Q) \lor R) \rightarrow (P \lor (Q \lor R)))
}{pm2.32}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \lor Q) \lor R) \leftrightarrow (P \lor (Q \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orass}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \lor Q) \lor R) \rightarrow (P \lor (Q \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *2.3 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  ((P \lor (Q \lor R)) \rightarrow (P \lor (R \lor Q)))
}{pm2.3}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \lor R) \rightarrow (R \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm1.4}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor (Q \lor R)) \rightarrow (P \lor (R \lor Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orim2i}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A rearrangement of disjuncts]{%
  (((P \lor Q) \lor R) \leftrightarrow ((P \lor R) \lor Q))
}{or32}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \lor Q) \lor R) \leftrightarrow (P \lor (Q \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orass}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \lor (Q \lor R)) \leftrightarrow (Q \lor (P \lor R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{or12}}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \lor (P \lor R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \lor R) \lor Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orcom}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \lor Q) \lor R) \leftrightarrow ((P \lor R) \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitri}{1,2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Rearrangement of 4 disjuncts]{%
  (((P \lor Q) \lor (R \lor S)) \leftrightarrow ((P \lor R) \lor (Q \lor S)))
}{or4}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \lor (R \lor S)) \leftrightarrow (R \lor (Q \lor S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{or12}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \lor (Q \lor (R \lor S))) \leftrightarrow (P \lor (R \lor (Q \lor S)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orbi2i}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \lor Q) \lor (R \lor S)) \leftrightarrow (P \lor (Q \lor (R \lor S))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orass}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \lor R) \lor (Q \lor S)) \leftrightarrow (P \lor (R \lor (Q \lor S))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orass}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \lor Q) \lor (R \lor S)) \leftrightarrow ((P \lor R) \lor (Q \lor S)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4i}{2,3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Rearrangement of 4 disjuncts]{%
  (((P \lor Q) \lor (R \lor S)) \leftrightarrow ((P \lor R) \lor (S \lor Q)))
}{or42}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \lor Q) \lor (R \lor S)) \leftrightarrow ((P \lor R) \lor (Q \lor S)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{or4}}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \lor S) \leftrightarrow (S \lor Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orcom}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \lor R) \lor (Q \lor S)) \leftrightarrow ((P \lor R) \lor (S \lor Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orbi2i}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \lor Q) \lor (R \lor S)) \leftrightarrow ((P \lor R) \lor (S \lor Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Distribution of disjunction over disjunction]{%
  ((P \lor (Q \lor R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \lor Q) \lor (P \lor R)))
}{orordi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor P) \leftrightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{oridm}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \lor P) \lor (Q \lor R)) \leftrightarrow (P \lor (Q \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orbi1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \lor P) \lor (Q \lor R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \lor Q) \lor (P \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{or4}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor (Q \lor R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \lor Q) \lor (P \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr3i}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Distribution of disjunction over disjunction]{%
  (((P \lor Q) \lor R) \leftrightarrow ((P \lor R) \lor (Q \lor R)))
}{orordir}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((R \lor R) \leftrightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{oridm}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \lor Q) \lor (R \lor R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \lor Q) \lor R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orbi2i}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \lor Q) \lor (R \lor R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \lor R) \lor (Q \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{or4}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \lor Q) \lor R) \leftrightarrow ((P \lor R) \lor (Q \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr3i}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Disjunction distributes over implication]{%
  ((P \lor (Q \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \lor Q) \rightarrow (P \lor R)))
}{orimdi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow ((\neg P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imdi}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor (Q \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-or}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-or}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor R) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-or}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \lor Q) \rightarrow (P \lor R)) \leftrightarrow ((\neg P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi12i}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor (Q \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \lor Q) \rightarrow (P \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4i}{1,2,5}}
\end{tabproof}
\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{orimdi} und \FormulaRefAuto{pm4.78} sind die beiden komplementären Verteilungssätze zwischen Disjunktion und Implikation.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *2.76 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  ((P \lor (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow ((P \lor Q) \rightarrow (P \lor R)))
}{pm2.76}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor (Q \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \lor Q) \rightarrow (P \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orimdi}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow ((P \lor Q) \rightarrow (P \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *2.85 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  (((P \lor Q) \rightarrow (P \lor R)) \rightarrow (P \lor (Q \rightarrow R)))
}{pm2.85}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor (Q \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow ((P \lor Q) \rightarrow (P \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orimdi}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \lor Q) \rightarrow (P \lor R)) \rightarrow (P \lor (Q \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *2.75 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  ((P \lor Q) \rightarrow ((P \lor (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (P \lor R)))
}{pm2.75}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow ((P \lor Q) \rightarrow (P \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.76}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor Q) \rightarrow ((P \lor (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (P \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{com12}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Implication distributes over disjunction]{%
  (((P \rightarrow Q) \lor (P \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \lor R)))
}{pm4.78}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg P \lor (Q \lor R)) \leftrightarrow ((\neg P \lor Q) \lor (\neg P \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orordi}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow (Q \lor R)) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \lor (Q \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imor}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imor}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \lor R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imor}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \lor (P \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow ((\neg P \lor Q) \lor (\neg P \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orbi12i}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \lor (P \rightarrow R)) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow (Q \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4ri}{1,2,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A disjunction with a true formula is equivalent to that true formula]{%
  (P \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow (P \lor Q)))
}{biort}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow P}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (P \lor Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orc}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{2thd}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A wff is equivalent to its disjunction with falsehood]{%
  (\neg P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (P \lor Q)))
}{biorf}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{Q \rightarrow (P \lor Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{olc}}
  \proofstep{}{\neg P \rightarrow ((P \lor Q) \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orel1}}
  \proofstep{}{\neg P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid2}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A wff is equivalent to its negated disjunction with falsehood]{%
  (P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (\neg P \lor Q)))
}{biortn}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow \neg\neg P}{\FormulaRefAuto{notnot}}
  \proofstep{}{\neg\neg P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (\neg P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biorf}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (\neg P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A wff is equivalent to its disjunction with falsehood]{%
  \neg P\vdash Q \leftrightarrow (P \lor Q)
}{biorfi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (P \lor Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biorf}}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \leftrightarrow (P \lor Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A wff is equivalent to its disjunction with falsehood]{%
  \neg P\vdash Q \leftrightarrow (Q \lor P)
}{biorfri}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \leftrightarrow (P \lor Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biorfi}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \lor Q) \leftrightarrow (Q \lor P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orcom}}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \leftrightarrow (Q \lor P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Obsolete version of biorfri]{%
  \neg P\vdash Q \leftrightarrow (Q \lor P)
}{biorfriOLD}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{Q \rightarrow (Q \lor P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orc}}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \lor P) \rightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.53}}
  \proofstep{1}{(Q \lor P) \rightarrow Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{mt3i}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{1}{Q \leftrightarrow (Q \lor P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{1,4}}
\end{tabproof}
\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{biorfriOLD} ist in Metamath inzwischen als veraltete Variante markiert; für neue Verwendungen ist \FormulaRefAuto{biorfri} die passendere Referenz.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *2.26 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  (\neg P \lor ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow Q))
}{pm2.26}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.27}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \lor ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imori}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *2.63 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  ((P \lor Q) \rightarrow ((\neg P \lor Q) \rightarrow Q))
}{pm2.63}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \lor Q) \rightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.53}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \lor Q) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{idd}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \lor Q) \rightarrow ((\neg P \lor Q) \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jaod}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *2.64 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  ((P \lor Q) \rightarrow ((P \lor \neg Q) \rightarrow P))
}{pm2.64}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\neg Q \rightarrow ((P \lor Q) \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orel2}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \lor \neg Q) \rightarrow ((P \lor Q) \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jao1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor Q) \rightarrow ((P \lor \neg Q) \rightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{com12}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *2.42 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  ((\neg P \lor (P \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))
}{pm2.42}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.21}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg P \lor (P \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jaoi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[A general instance of Theorem *5.11 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  ((P \rightarrow Q) \lor (\neg P \rightarrow R))
}{pm5.11g}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\neg(P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.5g}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \lor (\neg P \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orri}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *5.11 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  ((P \rightarrow Q) \lor (\neg P \rightarrow Q))
}{pm5.11}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \lor (\neg P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.11g}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *5.12 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  ((P \rightarrow Q) \lor (P \rightarrow \neg Q))
}{pm5.12}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\neg(P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.51}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \lor (P \rightarrow \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orri}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *5.14 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  ((P \rightarrow Q) \lor (Q \rightarrow R))
}{pm5.14}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\neg(P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.521g}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \lor (Q \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orri}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *5.13 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  ((P \rightarrow Q) \lor (Q \rightarrow P))
}{pm5.13}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \lor (Q \rightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.14}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Theorem *5.55 of Whitehead-Russell]{%
  (((P \lor Q) \leftrightarrow P) \lor ((P \lor Q) \leftrightarrow Q))
}{pm5.55}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow (P \leftrightarrow (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biort}}
  \proofstep{}{P \rightarrow ((P \lor Q) \leftrightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomd}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{\neg P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biorf}}
  \proofstep{}{\neg P \rightarrow ((P \lor Q) \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomd}{3}}
