  \proofstep{2}{(Q \rightarrow P)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(Q \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl5}{2,1}}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{luklem6}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(Q \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Erstes Tarski--Bernays--Wajsberg-Axiom aus Merediths erstem CO-Axiom]{%
  ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)))
}{retbwax1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{tbw-ax1}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
Zusammen mit \FormulaRefAuto{retbwax2}, \FormulaRefAuto{retbwax3} und \FormulaRefAuto{retbwax4} zeigt \FormulaRefAuto{retbwax1}, dass bereits \FormulaRefAuto{merco1} als Einaxiomensystem ausreicht.
\end{remark}

\subsection{Merediths zweites CO-Axiom}

\FormulaThm[Merediths zweites CO-Axiom]{%
  (((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((\bot \rightarrow R) \rightarrow S)) \rightarrow ((S \rightarrow P) \rightarrow (T \rightarrow (U \rightarrow P))))
}{merco2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T\dsep U}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\bot \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{falim}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((\bot \rightarrow R) \rightarrow S)) \rightarrow ((\bot \rightarrow R) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow S)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.04}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((\bot \rightarrow R) \rightarrow S)) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpi}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow S) \rightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jarl}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow S) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{idd}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow S) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow S) \rightarrow S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jad}{4,5}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow S) \rightarrow S) \rightarrow ((S \rightarrow P) \rightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{looinv}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((\bot \rightarrow R) \rightarrow S)) \rightarrow ((S \rightarrow P) \rightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3syl}{3,6,7}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((\bot \rightarrow R) \rightarrow S)) \rightarrow ((S \rightarrow P) \rightarrow (T \rightarrow P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1dd}{8}}
  \proofstep{}{(U \rightarrow (((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((\bot \rightarrow R) \rightarrow S)) \rightarrow ((S \rightarrow P) \rightarrow (T \rightarrow P))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{9}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((\bot \rightarrow R) \rightarrow S)) \rightarrow ((S \rightarrow P) \rightarrow (T \rightarrow (U \rightarrow P))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{com4l}{10}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{merco2} ist ein zweites, zu \FormulaRefAuto{merco1} gleich kurzes Einaxiomensystem. Die beiden Formeln sind strukturell verschieden, leisten aber dieselbe vollständige Axiomatisierung.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Hilfssatz zu Merediths zweitem CO-Axiom]{%
  (((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (S \rightarrow R)))
}{mercolem1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{3,1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(S \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{4}}
  \proofstep{1}{(Q \rightarrow (S \rightarrow R))}{\rRI{2,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Hilfssatz zu Merediths zweitem CO-Axiom]{%
  (((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow P) \rightarrow (R \rightarrow (S \rightarrow P)))
}{mercolem2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow P)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow P) \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{peirce}}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1}{(S \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{3}}
  \proofstep{1}{(R \rightarrow (S \rightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Hilfssatz zu Merediths zweitem CO-Axiom]{%
  ((Q \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)))
}{mercolem3}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1d}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Hilfssatz zu Merediths zweitem CO-Axiom]{%
  ((S \rightarrow (U \rightarrow P)) \rightarrow (((S \rightarrow R) \rightarrow P) \rightarrow (T \rightarrow (U \rightarrow P))))
}{mercolem4}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T\dsep U}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(S \rightarrow (U \rightarrow P))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(T \rightarrow (U \rightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(((S \rightarrow R) \rightarrow P) \rightarrow (T \rightarrow (U \rightarrow P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Hilfssatz zu Merediths zweitem CO-Axiom]{%
  (S \rightarrow ((S \rightarrow P) \rightarrow (T \rightarrow (R \rightarrow P))))
}{mercolem5}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(S \rightarrow P)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(R \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(T \rightarrow (R \rightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{4}}
  \proofstep{1}{((S \rightarrow P) \rightarrow (T \rightarrow (R \rightarrow P)))}{\rRI{2,5}}
  \proofstep{}{(S \rightarrow ((S \rightarrow P) \rightarrow (T \rightarrow (R \rightarrow P))))}{\rRI{1,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Hilfssatz zu Merediths zweitem CO-Axiom]{%
  ((P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R))) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)))
}{mercolem6}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{2,1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{luklem6}{3}}
  \proofstep{1}{(Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R))}{\rRI{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Hilfssatz zu Merediths zweitem CO-Axiom]{%
  ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow Q)))
}{mercolem7}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow Q))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{mercolem3}}
  \proofstep{}{((((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow Q)))) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow Q))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{mercolem6}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow Q))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{mercolem5}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    ((P \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow Q))))
    \rightarrow
    ((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow ((((P \rightarrow P) \rightarrow ((\bot \rightarrow P) \rightarrow P)) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow P) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (P \rightarrow P)))) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow Q))))))
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{mercolem4}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    ((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow ((((P \rightarrow P) \rightarrow ((\bot \rightarrow P) \rightarrow P)) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow P) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (P \rightarrow P)))) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow Q)))))
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{4,5}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    ((((P \rightarrow P) \rightarrow ((\bot \rightarrow P) \rightarrow P)) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow P) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (P \rightarrow P))))
    \rightarrow
    ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow Q))))
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{3,6}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (((P \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow Q)) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{\FormulaRefAuto{merco2},7}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Hilfssatz zu Merediths zweitem CO-Axiom]{%
  ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (S \rightarrow (T \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)))))
}{mercolem8}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl5}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \rightarrow R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{luklem6}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(T \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{4}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(S \rightarrow (T \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Erstes Tarski--Bernays--Wajsberg-Axiom aus Merediths zweitem CO-Axiom]{%
  ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)))
}{re1tbw1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{tbw-ax1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Zweites Tarski--Bernays--Wajsberg-Axiom aus Merediths zweitem CO-Axiom]{%
  (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow P))
}{re1tbw2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{tbw-ax2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Drittes Tarski--Bernays--Wajsberg-Axiom aus Merediths zweitem CO-Axiom]{%
  (((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow P) \rightarrow P)
}{re1tbw3}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow P) \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{tbw-ax3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Viertes Tarski--Bernays--Wajsberg-Axiom aus Merediths zweitem CO-Axiom]{%
  (\bot \rightarrow P)
}{re1tbw4}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\bot \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{tbw-ax4}}
\end{tabproof}

\subsection{Russell--Bernays}

\FormulaThm[Rechtfertigung der Russell--Bernays-Form des Bikonditionals]{%
  ((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(\neg(\neg P \lor Q) \lor \neg(\neg Q \lor P)))
}{rb-bijust}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{dfbi1}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imor}}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \rightarrow P) \leftrightarrow (\neg Q \lor P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imor}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(Q \rightarrow P) \leftrightarrow \neg(\neg Q \lor P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{notbii}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P)) \leftrightarrow ((\neg P \lor Q) \rightarrow \neg(\neg Q \lor P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi12i}{2,4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow \neg(Q \rightarrow P)) \leftrightarrow \neg((\neg P \lor Q) \rightarrow \neg(\neg Q \lor P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{notbii}{5}}
  \proofstep{}{(((\neg P \lor Q) \rightarrow \neg(\neg Q \lor P)) \leftrightarrow (\neg(\neg P \lor Q) \lor \neg(\neg Q \lor P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm4.62}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg((\neg P \lor Q) \rightarrow \neg(\neg Q \lor P)) \leftrightarrow \neg(\neg(\neg P \lor Q) \lor \neg(\neg Q \lor P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{notbii}{7}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(\neg(\neg P \lor Q) \lor \neg(\neg Q \lor P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitri}{1,6,8}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Definition der Implikation in Russell--Bernays-Form]{%
  \neg(\neg(\neg(P \rightarrow Q) \lor (\neg P \lor Q)) \lor \neg(\neg(\neg P \lor Q) \lor (P \rightarrow Q)))
}{rb-imdf}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imor}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \lor Q)) \leftrightarrow \neg(\neg(\neg(P \rightarrow Q) \lor (\neg P \lor Q)) \lor \neg(\neg(\neg P \lor Q) \lor (P \rightarrow Q))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rb-bijust}}
  \proofstep{}{\neg(\neg(\neg(P \rightarrow Q) \lor (\neg P \lor Q)) \lor \neg(\neg(\neg P \lor Q) \lor (P \rightarrow Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Modus ponens für \texorpdfstring{$\lor,\neg$}{∨,¬}-Axiomatisierungen]{%
  P \dsep (\neg P \lor Q) \vdash Q
}{anmp}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(\neg P \lor Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{imorri}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Erstes Russell--Bernays-Axiom]{%
  (\neg(\neg Q \lor R) \lor (\neg(P \lor Q) \lor (P \lor R)))
}{rb-ax1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \rightarrow R) \rightarrow ((P \lor Q) \rightarrow (P \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orim2}}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (\neg Q \lor R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imor}}
  \proofstep{}{(((P \lor Q) \rightarrow (P \lor R)) \leftrightarrow (\neg(P \lor Q) \lor (P \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imor}}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg Q \lor R) \rightarrow (\neg(P \lor Q) \lor (P \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3imtr3i}{1,2,3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(\neg Q \lor R) \lor (\neg(P \lor Q) \lor (P \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imori}{4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Zweites Russell--Bernays-Axiom]{%
  (\neg(P \lor Q) \lor (Q \lor P))
}{rb-ax2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor Q) \rightarrow (Q \lor P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm1.4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(Q \lor P) \rightarrow \neg(P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{con3i}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg\neg(P \lor Q) \rightarrow (Q \lor P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{con1i}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(P \lor Q) \lor (Q \lor P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orri}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Drittes Russell--Bernays-Axiom]{%
  (\neg P \lor (Q \lor P))
}{rb-ax3}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(Q \lor P) \rightarrow \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.46}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg\neg P \rightarrow (Q \lor P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{con1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \lor (Q \lor P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orri}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Viertes Russell--Bernays-Axiom]{%
  (\neg(P \lor P) \lor P)
}{rb-ax4}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor P) \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm1.2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \rightarrow \neg(P \lor P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{con3i}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg\neg(P \lor P) \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con1i}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(P \lor P) \lor P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{orri}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Syllogismus im Russell--Bernays-Kalkül]{%
  (\neg Q \lor R)\dsep (P \lor Q)\vdash (P \lor R)
}{rbsyl}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \lor Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(\neg Q \lor R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(\neg Q \lor R) \lor (\neg(P \lor Q) \lor (P \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rb-ax1}}
  \proofstep{2}{(\neg(P \lor Q) \lor (P \lor R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anmp}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \lor R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{anmp}{1,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Hilfssatz zu Russell--Bernays]{%
  (\neg P \lor Q)\dsep (\neg R \lor S)\vdash (\neg(P \lor R) \lor (Q \lor S))
}{rblem1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\neg P \lor Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(\neg R \lor S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(\neg R \lor S) \lor (\neg(Q \lor R) \lor (Q \lor S)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rb-ax1}}
  \proofstep{2}{(\neg(Q \lor R) \lor (Q \lor S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anmp}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(R \lor Q) \lor (Q \lor R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rb-ax2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(\neg P \lor Q) \lor (\neg(R \lor P) \lor (R \lor Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rb-ax1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\neg(R \lor P) \lor (R \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anmp}{1,6}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(P \lor R) \lor (R \lor P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rb-ax2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(\neg(P \lor R) \lor (R \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rbsyl}{8,7}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(\neg(P \lor R) \lor (Q \lor R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rbsyl}{5,9}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(\neg(P \lor R) \lor (Q \lor S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rbsyl}{4,10}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Hilfssatz zu Russell--Bernays]{%
  (\neg(R \lor P) \lor (R \lor (P \lor Q)))
}{rblem2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(Q \lor P) \lor (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rb-ax2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \lor (Q \lor P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rb-ax3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \lor (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rbsyl}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(\neg P \lor (P \lor Q)) \lor (\neg(R \lor P) \lor (R \lor (P \lor Q))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rb-ax1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(R \lor P) \lor (R \lor (P \lor Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anmp}{3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Hilfssatz zu Russell--Bernays]{%
  (\neg(R \lor P) \lor ((R \lor Q) \lor P))
}{rblem3}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(P \lor (R \lor Q)) \lor ((R \lor Q) \lor P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rb-ax2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(P \lor R) \lor (P \lor (R \lor Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rblem2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(R \lor P) \lor (P \lor R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rb-ax2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(R \lor P) \lor (P \lor (R \lor Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rbsyl}{3,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(R \lor P) \lor ((R \lor Q) \lor P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rbsyl}{1,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Hilfssatz zu Russell--Bernays]{%
  (\neg P \lor S)\dsep (\neg Q \lor T)\dsep (\neg R \lor U)\vdash (\neg((P \lor Q) \lor R) \lor ((U \lor T) \lor S))
}{rblem4}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T\dsep U}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\neg P \lor S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(\neg Q \lor T)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{(\neg R \lor U)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2,3}{(\neg(R \lor Q) \lor (U \lor T))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rblem1}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{(\neg((R \lor Q) \lor P) \lor ((U \lor T) \lor S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rblem1}{1,4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(P \lor (R \lor Q)) \lor ((R \lor Q) \lor P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rb-ax2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(\neg(Q \lor R) \lor (R \lor Q)) \lor (\neg(P \lor (Q \lor R)) \lor (P \lor (R \lor Q))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rb-ax1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(P \lor (Q \lor R)) \lor (P \lor (R \lor Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anmp}{\FormulaRefAuto{rb-ax2},7}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg((Q \lor R) \lor P) \lor (P \lor (Q \lor R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rb-ax2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg((Q \lor R) \lor P) \lor (P \lor (R \lor Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rbsyl}{9,8}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(P \lor (Q \lor R)) \lor ((R \lor Q) \lor P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rbsyl}{6,10}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(((Q \lor R) \lor P) \lor ((Q \lor R) \lor P)) \lor ((Q \lor R) \lor P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rb-ax4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(P \lor (Q \lor R)) \lor ((Q \lor R) \lor P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rblem2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg\neg R \lor (Q \lor R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rb-ax3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(\neg\neg R \lor (Q \lor R)) \lor (\neg R \lor ((Q \lor R) \lor P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rblem2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg R \lor ((Q \lor R) \lor P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anmp}{14,15}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{(\neg((P \lor Q) \lor R) \lor (((Q \lor R) \lor P) \lor ((Q \lor R) \lor P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rblem1}{16,13}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{(\neg((P \lor Q) \lor R) \lor ((Q \lor R) \lor P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rbsyl}{12,17}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{(\neg((P \lor Q) \lor R) \lor ((R \lor Q) \lor P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rbsyl}{11,18}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{(\neg((P \lor Q) \lor R) \lor ((U \lor T) \lor S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rbsyl}{5,19}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Hilfssatz zu Russell--Bernays]{%
  (\neg(\neg\neg P \lor Q) \lor (\neg\neg Q \lor P))
}{rblem5}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(P \lor P) \lor P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{rb-ax4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \lor (P \lor P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rb-ax3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg P \lor P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{rbsyl}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(\neg\neg P \lor \neg\neg P) \lor \neg\neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{rb-ax4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg\neg\neg P \lor (\neg\neg P \lor \neg\neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rb-ax3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg\neg\neg P \lor \neg\neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{rbsyl}{4,5}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(\neg\neg\neg P \lor \neg\neg P) \lor (\neg\neg P \lor \neg\neg\neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rb-ax2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg\neg P \lor \neg\neg\neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{anmp}{6,7}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(\neg P \lor P) \lor (\neg\neg\neg P \lor P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rblem1}{8,3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg\neg\neg P \lor P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{anmp}{3,9}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(\neg Q \lor \neg Q) \lor \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{rb-ax4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg\neg Q \lor (\neg Q \lor \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rb-ax3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg\neg Q \lor \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{rbsyl}{11,12}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(\neg\neg Q \lor \neg Q) \lor (\neg Q \lor \neg\neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rb-ax2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg Q \lor \neg\neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{anmp}{13,14}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(\neg\neg P \lor Q) \lor (P \lor \neg\neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rblem1}{10,15}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(\neg Q \lor \neg\neg P) \lor (\neg\neg Q \lor P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rb-ax2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg(\neg\neg P \lor Q) \lor (\neg\neg Q \lor P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rbsyl}{17,16}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Hilfssatz zu Russell--Bernays]{%
  \neg(\neg(\neg P \lor Q) \lor \neg(\neg Q \lor P)) \vdash (\neg P \lor Q)
}{rblem6}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg(\neg(\neg P \lor Q) \lor \neg(\neg Q \lor P))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(\neg(\neg P \lor Q) \lor \neg(\neg Q \lor P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rb-bijust}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbir}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\neg P \lor Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{imori}{5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Hilfssatz zu Russell--Bernays]{%
  \neg(\neg(\neg P \lor Q) \lor \neg(\neg Q \lor P)) \vdash (\neg Q \lor P)
}{rblem7}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg(\neg(\neg P \lor Q) \lor \neg(\neg Q \lor P))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(\neg(\neg P \lor Q) \lor \neg(\neg Q \lor P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rb-bijust}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \leftrightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbir}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}}
  \proofstep{1}{(Q \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\neg Q \lor P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{imori}{5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Modus ponens aus Russell--Bernays]{%
  P \dsep (P \rightarrow Q)\vdash Q
}{re1axmp}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \rightarrow Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(\neg P \lor Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{imori}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{anmp}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Łukasiewicz-Axiom \FormulaRefAuto{luk-1} aus Russell--Bernays]{%
  ((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)))
}{re2luk1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{luk-1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Łukasiewicz-Axiom \FormulaRefAuto{luk-2} aus Russell--Bernays]{%
  ((\neg P \rightarrow P) \rightarrow P)
}{re2luk2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg P \rightarrow P) \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{luk-2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Łukasiewicz-Axiom \FormulaRefAuto{luk-3} aus Russell--Bernays]{%
  (P \rightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow Q))
}{re2luk3}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{luk-3}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
Die Russell--Bernays-Axiome formulieren dieselbe klassische Aussagenlogik vollständig nur mit \(\lor\) und \(\neg\). Damit wird besonders transparent, wie stark bereits diese beiden Junktoren sind.
\end{remark}

\subsection{Stoische Logik}

\FormulaThm[Modus ponendo tollens I]{%
  P \dsep \neg(P \land Q)\vdash \neg Q
}{mptnan}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\neg(P \land Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(P \rightarrow \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-an}}
  \proofstep{2}{\neg\neg(P \rightarrow \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{notnoti}{2}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \rightarrow \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbi}{4,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Modus ponendo tollens II]{%
  P \dsep (P \lxor Q)\vdash \neg Q
}{mptxor}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \lxor Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lxor Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(P \land Q) \land (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{xor2}}
  \proofstep{2}{(\neg(P \land Q) \land (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbi}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\neg(P \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpli}{4}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{mptnan}{1,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Modus tollendo ponens in der inklusiven Oder-Version]{%
  \neg P \dsep (P \lor Q)\vdash Q
}{mtpor}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \lor Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(\neg\neg P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.53i}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{mt4}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Modus tollendo ponens in der exklusiven Oder-Version]{%
  \neg P \dsep (P \lxor Q)\vdash Q
}{mtpxor}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \lxor Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lxor Q) \leftrightarrow \neg(P \land Q) \land (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{xor2}}
  \proofstep{2}{(\neg(P \land Q) \land (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbi}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \lor Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpri}{4}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{mtpor}{1,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Stoische Logik, Thema 1a]{%
  (P \land Q) \rightarrow R \vdash (P \land \neg R) \rightarrow \neg Q
}{stoic1a}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land \neg R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpli}{2}}
  \proofstep{2}{\neg R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpri}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{Q \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{1,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\neg Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{mto}{5,4}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land \neg R) \rightarrow \neg Q}{\rRI{2,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Stoische Logik, Thema 1b]{%
  (P \land Q) \rightarrow R \vdash (Q \land \neg R) \rightarrow \neg P
}{stoic1b}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(Q \land \neg R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpli}{2}}
  \proofstep{2}{\neg R}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpri}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{1,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\neg P}{\FormulaRefAuto{mto}{5,4}}
  \proofstep{1}{(Q \land \neg R) \rightarrow \neg P}{\rRI{2,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Stoische Logik, Thema 2a]{%
  (P \land Q) \rightarrow R \dsep (P \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash (P \land Q) \rightarrow S
}{stoic2a}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{syldan}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Stoische Logik, Thema 2b]{%
  (P \land Q) \rightarrow R \dsep (P \land Q \land R) \rightarrow S \vdash (P \land Q) \rightarrow S
}{stoic2b}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \land Q \land R) \rightarrow S}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow S}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpd3an3}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Stoische Logik, Thema 3]{%
  (P \land Q) \rightarrow R \dsep (R \land S) \rightarrow T \vdash (P \land Q \land S) \rightarrow T
}{stoic3}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(R \land S) \rightarrow T}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q \land S) \rightarrow (P \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3simpa}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q \land S) \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simp3}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q \land S) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{3,1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q \land S) \rightarrow (R \land S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{5,4}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land Q \land S) \rightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{6,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Stoische Logik, Thema 4a]{%
  (P \land Q) \rightarrow R \dsep (R \land P \land S) \rightarrow T \vdash (P \land Q \land S) \rightarrow T
}{stoic4a}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(R \land P \land S) \rightarrow T}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q \land S) \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simp1}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q \land S) \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simp3}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q \land S) \rightarrow (P \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3simpa}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q \land S) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{5,1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q \land S) \rightarrow (R \land P \land S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3anim123i}{6,3,4}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land Q \land S) \rightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{7,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Stoische Logik, Thema 4b]{%
  (P \land Q) \rightarrow R \dsep ((R \land P \land Q) \land S) \rightarrow T \vdash (P \land Q \land S) \rightarrow T
}{stoic4b}{%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S\dsep T}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q) \rightarrow R}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{((R \land P \land Q) \land S) \rightarrow T}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q \land S) \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simp1}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q \land S) \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simp2}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q \land S) \rightarrow S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simp3}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q \land S) \rightarrow (P \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3simpa}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q \land S) \rightarrow R}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{6,1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q \land S) \rightarrow (R \land P \land Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3anim123i}{7,3,4}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \land Q \land S) \rightarrow ((R \land P \land Q) \land S)}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{8,5}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \land Q \land S) \rightarrow T}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{9,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\section{Prädikatenlogik mit Gleichheit}

\subsection{Existenzquantor und Nichtfreiheitsprädikat}

\FormulaDef[Existenzquantor als Bildungsregel]{%
  \text{Ist } P \text{ eine wohlgeformte Formel und } x \text{ eine Mengenvariable, dann ist auch } \exists x\, P \text{ eine wohlgeformte Formel.}%
}{wex}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}

\FormulaDef[Definition des Existenzquantors]{%
  (\exists x\, P \leftrightarrow \neg \forall x\, \neg P)
}{df-ex}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{df-ex} ist die klassische Dualitätsdefinition von \(\exists\) durch \(\forall\) und \(\neg\). Dadurch bleibt der Existenzquantor zunächst rein definitorisch.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Negierte Allquantifikation als Negation einer Existenzquantifikation]{%
  (\forall x\, \neg P \leftrightarrow \neg \exists x\, P)
}{alnex}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, P \leftrightarrow \neg \forall x\, \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-ex}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, \neg P \leftrightarrow \neg \exists x\, P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con2bii}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Äquivalenz zwischen existentiellem Antezedens und universellem Konsequens]{%
  ((\exists x\, P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow \forall x\, \neg P))
}{eximal}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, P \leftrightarrow \neg \forall x\, \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-ex}}
  \proofstep{}{((\exists x\, P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg \forall x\, \neg P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg \forall x\, \neg P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow \forall x\, \neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{con1b}}
  \proofstep{}{((\exists x\, P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow \forall x\, \neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaDef[Nichtfreiheitsprädikat als Bildungsregel]{%
  \text{Ist } P \text{ eine wohlgeformte Formel und } x \text{ eine Mengenvariable, dann ist auch } \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P \text{ eine wohlgeformte Formel.}%
}{wnf}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}

\FormulaDef[Definition des Nichtfreiheitsprädikats]{%
  (\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P \leftrightarrow (\exists x\, P \rightarrow \forall x\, P))
}{df-nf}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}

\begin{remark}
Mit \FormulaRefAuto{df-nf} wird Nichtfreiheit nicht metalogisch, sondern innerhalb des Kalküls selbst formuliert. Das ist für spätere Ableitungen besonders praktisch.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Alternative Form des Nichtfreiheitsprädikats]{%
  (\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, P \lor \neg \exists x\, P))
}{nf2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P \leftrightarrow (\exists x\, P \rightarrow \forall x\, P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-nf}}
  \proofstep{}{((\exists x\, P \rightarrow \forall x\, P) \leftrightarrow (\neg \exists x\, P \lor \forall x\, P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imor}}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg \exists x\, P \lor \forall x\, P) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, P \lor \neg \exists x\, P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orcom}}
  \proofstep{}{(\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, P \lor \neg \exists x\, P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitri}{1,2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Weitere alternative Form des Nichtfreiheitsprädikats]{%
  (\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, P \lor \forall x\, \neg P))
}{nf3}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, P \lor \neg \exists x\, P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nf2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, \neg P \leftrightarrow \neg \exists x\, P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alnex}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall x\, P \lor \forall x\, \neg P) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, P \lor \neg \exists x\, P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orbi2i}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, P \lor \forall x\, \neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr4i}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Primitive Form des Nichtfreiheitsprädikats]{%
  (\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P \leftrightarrow (\neg \forall x\, P \rightarrow \forall x\, \neg P))
}{nf4}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, P \lor \forall x\, \neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nf3}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall x\, P \lor \forall x\, \neg P) \leftrightarrow (\neg \forall x\, P \rightarrow \forall x\, \neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-or}}
  \proofstep{}{(\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P \leftrightarrow (\neg \forall x\, P \rightarrow \forall x\, \neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Schließe auf Nichtfreiheit aus der Definition]{%
  (\exists x\, P \rightarrow \forall x\, P)\vdash \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P
}{nfi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\exists x\, P \rightarrow \forall x\, P)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P \leftrightarrow (\exists x\, P \rightarrow \forall x\, P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-nf}}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbir}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Folgerung aus der Definition der Nichtfreiheit]{%
  \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P \vdash (\exists x\, P \rightarrow \forall x\, P)
}{nfri}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P \leftrightarrow (\exists x\, P \rightarrow \forall x\, P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-nf}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\exists x\, P \rightarrow \forall x\, P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Nichtfreiheit in einem Kontext]{%
  P \rightarrow (\exists x\, Q \rightarrow \forall x\, Q)\vdash P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, Q
}{nfd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\exists x\, Q \rightarrow \forall x\, Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, Q \leftrightarrow (\exists x\, Q \rightarrow \forall x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-nf}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylibr}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Folgerung der Nichtfreiheit in einem Kontext]{%
  P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, Q \vdash P \rightarrow (\exists x\, Q \rightarrow \forall x\, Q)
}{nfrd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, Q \leftrightarrow (\exists x\, Q \rightarrow \forall x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-nf}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\exists x\, Q \rightarrow \forall x\, Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylib}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Geschlossene Form von \FormulaRefAuto{nfth}]{%
  (\forall x\, P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P)
}{nftht}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, P \rightarrow (\exists x\, P \rightarrow \forall x\, P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfd}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Geschlossene Form von \FormulaRefAuto{nfnth}]{%
  (\neg \exists x\, P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P)
}{nfntht}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \exists x\, P \rightarrow (\exists x\, P \rightarrow \forall x\, P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.21}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \exists x\, P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfd}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Closed form of nfnth]{%
  (\forall x \neg \varphi \rightarrow \text{Ⅎ} x \varphi)
}{nfntht2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Wffs}{\varphi}%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{%
    (\forall x \neg \varphi \leftrightarrow \neg \exists x \varphi)
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{alnex}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    (\neg \exists x \varphi \rightarrow \text{Ⅎ} x \varphi)
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{nfntht}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    (\forall x \neg \varphi \rightarrow \text{Ⅎ} x \varphi)
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{sylbi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{nfntht2} ist die zu \FormulaRefAuto{nfntht} äquivalente Variante, in der die Negation bereits in den Allquantor hineingezogen wurde. Der Beweis reduziert den Satz sofort mit \FormulaRefAuto{alnex} auf die vorherige Form.
\end{remark}

\section{Regelschema ax-gen (Verallgemeinerung)}

\FormulaAxiom[Rule of universal generalization]{%
  \varphi \vdash \forall x \varphi
}{ax-gen}{%
  \DeltaRow{Wffs}{\varphi}%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
}

\begin{remark}
Mit \FormulaRefAuto{ax-gen} beginnt der eigentliche quantorenlogische Teil des Kalküls: Neben \FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp} tritt nun eine zweite primitive Schlussregel auf. Sie erlaubt, einen bereits theorematisch gewonnenen Satz unter einen Allquantor zu stellen.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Generalization applied twice]{%
  \varphi \vdash \forall x \forall y \varphi
}{gen2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Wffs}{\varphi}%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\varphi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\forall y \varphi}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-gen}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{\forall x \forall y \varphi}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-gen}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Modus ponens combined with generalization]{%
  \forall x \varphi \rightarrow \psi \dsep \varphi \vdash \psi
}{mpg}{%
  \DeltaRow{Wffs}{\varphi\dsep \psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\forall x \varphi \rightarrow \psi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\varphi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\forall x \varphi}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-gen}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\psi}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{3,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Modus ponens on biconditional combined with generalization]{%
  \forall x \varphi \leftrightarrow \psi \dsep \varphi \vdash \psi
}{mpgbi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Wffs}{\varphi\dsep \psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\forall x \varphi \leftrightarrow \psi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\varphi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\forall x \varphi}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-gen}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\psi}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbi}{3,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{mpgbi} ist die bikonditionale Variante von \FormulaRefAuto{mpg}. Beide Sätze sind für den weiteren Aufbau sehr nützlich, weil sie eine Verallgemeinerung direkt mit einer anschließenden Auswertung durch Implikation bzw. Äquivalenz verbinden.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Modus ponens für eine Äquivalenz kombiniert mit Generalisierung]{%
  (\,P \leftrightarrow \forall x\, Q\,)\dsep Q \vdash P
}{mpgbir}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \leftrightarrow \forall x\, Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\forall x\, Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-gen}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbir}{3,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Generalisierungsregel für eine negierte Formel]{%
  \neg P \vdash \neg \exists x\, P
}{nex}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, \neg P \leftrightarrow \neg \exists x\, P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alnex}}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg \exists x\, P}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpgbi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[In einem Theorem ist keine Variable effektiv frei]{%
  P \vdash \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P
}{nfth}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nftht}}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpg}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Auch in einer negierten Formel ist keine Variable effektiv frei]{%
  \neg P \vdash \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P
}{nfnth}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, \neg P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfntht2}}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpg}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Hypothesenaufbau für Nichtfreiheit in Theoremen]{%
  P \vdash (P \rightarrow \forall x\, P)
}{hbth}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\forall x\, P}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-gen}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow \forall x\, P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{hbth} gehört noch zur älteren hb-Sprache für Hypothesen vom Typ \(P \rightarrow \forall x\, P\). Inhaltlich wird dieselbe Idee später meist mit dem Nichtfreiheitsprädikat \(\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P\) ausgedrückt.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Die Wahrheitskonstante hat keine freien Variablen]{%
  \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \top
}{nftru}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\top}{\FormulaRefAuto{tru}}
  \proofstep{}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \top}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfth}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Die Falschheitskonstante hat keine freien Variablen]{%
  \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \bot
}{nffal}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\neg \bot}{\FormulaRefAuto{fal}}
  \proofstep{}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \bot}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfnth}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Spezialisierung im trivialen Fall]{%
  P \vdash (\forall x\, P \rightarrow P)
}{sptruw}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, P \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Für alle Mengen gilt \(\top\)]{%
  \forall x\, \top
}{altru}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\top}{\FormulaRefAuto{tru}}
  \proofstep{}{\forall x\, \top}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-gen}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Für alle Mengen gilt \(\neg \bot\)]{%
  \forall x\, \neg \bot
}{alfal}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\neg \bot}{\FormulaRefAuto{fal}}
  \proofstep{}{\forall x\, \neg \bot}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-gen}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\section{Axiomenschema ax-4 (Quantifizierte Implikation)}

\FormulaAxiom[Axiom der quantifizierten Implikation]{%
  (\forall x\, (P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\forall x\, P \rightarrow \forall x\, Q))
}{ax-4}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}

\FormulaThm[Neuformulierung von ax-4]{%
  (\forall x\, (P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\forall x\, P \rightarrow \forall x\, Q))
}{alim}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\forall x\, P \rightarrow \forall x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Allquantifikation von Antezedens und Konsequens]{%
  (P \rightarrow Q)\vdash (\forall x\, P \rightarrow \forall x\, Q)
}{alimi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\forall x\, P \rightarrow \forall x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alim}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, P \rightarrow \forall x\, Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpg}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Doppelte Allquantifikation von Antezedens und Konsequens]{%
  (P \rightarrow Q)\vdash (\forall x\, \forall y\, P \rightarrow \forall x\, \forall y\, Q)
}{2alimi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x\dsep y}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall y\, P \rightarrow \forall y\, Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alimi}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, \forall y\, P \rightarrow \forall x\, \forall y\, Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alimi}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Füge ein Antezedens in eine allquantifizierte Formel ein]{%
  (\forall x\, P \rightarrow \forall x\, (Q \rightarrow P))
}{ala1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, P \rightarrow \forall x\, (Q \rightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alimi}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Geschlossene Form von \FormulaRefAuto{al2imi}]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &(\forall x\, (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow {}\\
    &\quad (\forall x\, P \rightarrow (\forall x\, Q \rightarrow \forall x\, R)))
  \end{aligned}
}{al2im}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (\forall x\, P \rightarrow \forall x\, (Q \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alim}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (Q \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (\forall x\, Q \rightarrow \forall x\, R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alim}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (\forall x\, P \rightarrow (\forall x\, Q \rightarrow \forall x\, R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl6}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Allquantifikation von äußerem Antezedens, innerem Antezedens und Konsequens]{%
  (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R))\vdash (\forall x\, P \rightarrow (\forall x\, Q \rightarrow \forall x\, R))
}{al2imi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow (\forall x\, P \rightarrow (\forall x\, Q \rightarrow \forall x\, R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{al2im}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, P \rightarrow (\forall x\, Q \rightarrow \forall x\, R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpg}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Variante von \FormulaRefAuto{al2imi} mit konjunktivem Antezedens]{%
  ((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)\vdash ((\forall x\, P \land \forall x\, Q) \rightarrow \forall x\, R)
}{alanimi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{((P \land Q) \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, P \rightarrow (\forall x\, Q \rightarrow \forall x\, R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{al2imi}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{((\forall x\, P \land \forall x\, Q) \rightarrow \forall x\, R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktionsform von Satz 19.20]{%
  P \rightarrow \forall x\, P \dsep P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R)\vdash P \rightarrow (\forall x\, Q \rightarrow \forall x\, R)
}{alimdh}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow \forall x\, P)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(\forall x\, P \rightarrow (\forall x\, Q \rightarrow \forall x\, R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{al2imi}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \rightarrow (\forall x\, Q \rightarrow \forall x\, R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Allquantifikation über einer Äquivalenz]{%
  (\forall x\, (P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\forall x\, P \leftrightarrow \forall x\, Q))
}{albi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimp}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\forall x\, P \rightarrow \forall x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{al2imi}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpr}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\forall x\, Q \rightarrow \forall x\, P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{al2imi}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\forall x\, P \leftrightarrow \forall x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inferenzform zu \FormulaRefAuto{albi}]{%
  (P \leftrightarrow Q)\vdash (\forall x\, P \leftrightarrow \forall x\, Q)
}{albii}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \leftrightarrow Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \leftrightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\forall x\, P \leftrightarrow \forall x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albi}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, P \leftrightarrow \forall x\, Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpg}{2,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Doppelte Inferenzform zu \FormulaRefAuto{albi}]{%
  (P \leftrightarrow Q)\vdash (\forall x\, \forall y\, P \leftrightarrow \forall x\, \forall y\, Q)
}{2albii}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x\dsep y}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \leftrightarrow Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall y\, P \leftrightarrow \forall y\, Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, \forall y\, P \leftrightarrow \forall x\, \forall y\, Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Dreifache Inferenzform zu \FormulaRefAuto{albi}]{%
  (P \leftrightarrow Q)\vdash (\forall x\, \forall y\, \forall z\, P \leftrightarrow \forall x\, \forall y\, \forall z\, Q)
}{3albii}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \leftrightarrow Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall y\, \forall z\, P \leftrightarrow \forall y\, \forall z\, Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{2albii}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, \forall y\, \forall z\, P \leftrightarrow \forall x\, \forall y\, \forall z\, Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Geschlossene Form von \FormulaRefAuto{sylg}]{%
  (\forall x\, (Q \rightarrow R) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow \forall x\, Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow \forall x\, R)))
}{sylgt}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (Q \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (\forall x\, Q \rightarrow \forall x\, R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alim}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (Q \rightarrow R) \rightarrow ((P \rightarrow \forall x\, Q) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow \forall x\, R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imim2d}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Syllogismus kombiniert mit Generalisierung]{%
  (P \rightarrow \forall x\, Q)\dsep (Q \rightarrow R)\vdash P \rightarrow \forall x\, R
}{sylg}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow \forall x\, Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(Q \rightarrow R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(\forall x\, Q \rightarrow \forall x\, R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alimi}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \rightarrow \forall x\, R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Spezialfall von \FormulaRefAuto{sylg}]{%
  (P \rightarrow \forall x\, P)\dsep (P \rightarrow Q)\vdash P \rightarrow \forall x\, Q
}{alrimih}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow \forall x\, P)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \rightarrow Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \rightarrow \forall x\, Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylg}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Übertrage eine Hypothese der gebundenen Variablen entlang einer Äquivalenz]{%
  (P \leftrightarrow Q)\dsep (Q \rightarrow \forall x\, Q)\vdash (P \rightarrow \forall x\, P)
}{hbxfrbi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \leftrightarrow Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(Q \rightarrow \forall x\, Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, P \leftrightarrow \forall x\, Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \rightarrow \forall x\, P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3imtr4i}{1,2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Allquantor durch Existenzquantor und Negation]{%
  (\forall x\, P \leftrightarrow \neg \exists x\, \neg P)
}{alex}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \leftrightarrow \neg \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{notnotb}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, P \leftrightarrow \forall x\, \neg \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, \neg \neg P \leftrightarrow \neg \exists x\, \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alnex}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, P \leftrightarrow \neg \exists x\, \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{alex} ist das Dualstück zu \FormulaRefAuto{df-ex}. Zusammen beschreiben beide Sätze die klassische Dualität von \(\forall\) und \(\exists\) über der Negation.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Existenzquantifikation einer Negation als Negation einer Allquantifikation]{%
  (\exists x\, \neg P \leftrightarrow \neg \forall x\, P)
}{exnal}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, P \leftrightarrow \neg \exists x\, \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alex}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, \neg P \leftrightarrow \neg \forall x\, P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con2bii}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Doppelte Negation einer doppelten Allquantifikation]{%
  (\neg \forall x\, \forall y\, P \leftrightarrow \exists x\, \exists y\, \neg P)
}{2nalexn}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x\dsep y}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, \exists y\, P \leftrightarrow \neg \forall x\, \forall y\, \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-ex}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, \forall y\, P \leftrightarrow \neg \exists x\, \exists y\, \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alex}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x\, \forall y\, P \leftrightarrow \exists x\, \exists y\, \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x\, \forall y\, P \leftrightarrow \exists x\, \exists y\, \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{xchbinxr}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x\, \forall y\, P \leftrightarrow \exists x\, \exists y\, \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Doppelte Existenzquantifikation als negierte doppelte Allquantifikation]{%
  (\exists x\, \exists y\, P \leftrightarrow \neg \forall x\, \forall y\, \neg P)
}{2exnaln}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x\dsep y}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, P \leftrightarrow \neg \forall x\, \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-ex}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y\, \exists x\, P \leftrightarrow \forall y\, \neg \forall x\, \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, \exists y\, P \leftrightarrow \neg \forall x\, \forall y\, \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{xchbinxr}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Negation einer doppelten Existenzquantifikation]{%
  (\neg \exists x\, \exists y\, P \leftrightarrow \forall x\, \forall y\, \neg P)
}{2nexaln}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x\dsep y}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, \exists y\, P \leftrightarrow \neg \forall x\, \forall y\, \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{2exnaln}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x\, \forall y\, \neg P \leftrightarrow \exists x\, \exists y\, P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \exists x\, \exists y\, P \leftrightarrow \forall x\, \forall y\, \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con1bii}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Äquivalenz zwischen universell quantifiziertem Konsequens und existentiell quantifiziertem Antezedens]{%
  ((P \rightarrow \forall x\, Q) \leftrightarrow (\exists x\, \neg Q \rightarrow \neg P))
}{alimex}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, Q \leftrightarrow \neg \exists x\, \neg Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alex}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow \forall x\, Q) \leftrightarrow (P \rightarrow \neg \exists x\, \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi2i}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow \neg \exists x\, \neg Q) \leftrightarrow (\exists x\, \neg Q \rightarrow \neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{con2b}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow \forall x\, Q) \leftrightarrow (\exists x\, \neg Q \rightarrow \neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Variante von \FormulaRefAuto{al2imi} mit Existenzquantoren]{%
  (P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R))\vdash (\forall x\, P \rightarrow (\exists x\, Q \rightarrow \exists x\, R))
}{aleximi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\neg R \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow \neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{con3d}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, \neg R \rightarrow (\forall x\, Q \rightarrow \forall x\, \neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{al2imi}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\neg \exists x\, R \rightarrow (\forall x\, Q \rightarrow \forall x\, \neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alnex}{3}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\neg \exists x\, R \rightarrow (\exists x\, Q \rightarrow \neg \forall x\, P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alnex}{4}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, P \rightarrow (\exists x\, Q \rightarrow \exists x\, R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3imtr3g}{1,4,5}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, P \rightarrow (\exists x\, Q \rightarrow \exists x\, R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{con4d}{6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Bikonditionale Form von \FormulaRefAuto{aleximi}]{%
  (P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R))\vdash (\forall x\, P \rightarrow (\exists x\, Q \leftrightarrow \exists x\, R))
}{alexbii}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow (Q \leftrightarrow R))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpd}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, P \rightarrow (\exists x\, Q \rightarrow \exists x\, R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{aleximi}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow (R \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimprd}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, P \rightarrow (\exists x\, R \rightarrow \exists x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{aleximi}{4}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, P \rightarrow (\exists x\, Q \leftrightarrow \exists x\, R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid}{3,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz 19.22 von Margaris]{%
  (\forall x\, (P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\exists x\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, Q))
}{exim}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\exists x\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{aleximi}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Existenzquantifikation von Antezedens und Konsequens]{%
  (P \rightarrow Q)\vdash (\exists x\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, Q)
}{eximi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\exists x\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exim}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\exists x\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpg}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Doppelte Existenzquantifikation von Antezedens und Konsequens]{%
  (P \rightarrow Q)\vdash (\exists x\, \exists y\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, \exists y\, Q)
}{2eximi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x\dsep y}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\exists y\, P \rightarrow \exists y\, Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{eximi}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\exists x\, \exists y\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, \exists y\, Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{eximi}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inferenzform zu \FormulaRefAuto{eximi}]{%
  \exists x\, P \dsep (P \rightarrow Q)\vdash \exists x\, Q
}{eximii}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\exists x\, P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \rightarrow Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(\exists x\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{eximi}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\exists x\, Q}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Füge ein Antezedens in eine existenzquantifizierte Formel ein]{%
  (\exists x\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, (Q \rightarrow P))
}{exa1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, (Q \rightarrow P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{eximi}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz 19.38 von Margaris]{%
  ((\exists x\, P \rightarrow \forall x\, Q) \rightarrow \forall x\, (P \rightarrow Q))
}{19.38}{%

\FormulaThm[Satz 19.26 von Margaris]{%
  (\forall x\, (P \land Q) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, P \land \forall x\, Q))
}{19.26}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
