  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \land Q) \rightarrow \forall x\, Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alimi}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \land Q) \rightarrow (\forall x\, P \land \forall x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{2,4}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \rightarrow (P \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall x\, P \land \forall x\, Q) \rightarrow \forall x\, (P \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alanimi}{6}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \land Q) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, P \land \forall x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{5,7}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{19.26} ist die grundlegende Distributionsregel des Allquantors über die Konjunktion. Später steht ihr mit \FormulaRefAuto{19.40} nur noch eine einseitige Aussage für den Existenzquantor gegenüber.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Satz \FormulaRefAuto{19.26} mit zwei Quantoren]{%
  (\forall x\, \forall y\, (P \land Q) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, \forall y\, P \land \forall x\, \forall y\, Q))
}{19.26-2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x\dsep y}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \land Q) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, P \land \forall x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.26}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, \forall y\, (P \land Q) \leftrightarrow \forall x\, (\forall y\, P \land \forall y\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (\forall y\, P \land \forall y\, Q) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, \forall y\, P \land \forall x\, \forall y\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.26}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, \forall y\, (P \land Q) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, \forall y\, P \land \forall x\, \forall y\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz \FormulaRefAuto{19.26} mit dreifacher Konjunktion]{%
  (\forall x\, (P \land Q \land R) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, P \land \forall x\, Q \land \forall x\, R))
}{19.26-3an}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q \land R) \leftrightarrow ((P \land Q) \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-3an}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \land Q \land R) \leftrightarrow \forall x\, ((P \land Q) \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, ((P \land Q) \land R) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, (P \land Q) \land \forall x\, R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.26}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall x\, (P \land Q) \land \forall x\, R) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, P \land \forall x\, Q \land \forall x\, R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anbi1i}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.26}}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall x\, (P \land Q) \land \forall x\, R) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, P \land \forall x\, Q \land \forall x\, R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-3an}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \land Q \land R) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, P \land \forall x\, Q \land \forall x\, R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4i}{2,3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz 19.29 von Margaris]{%
  ((\forall x\, P \land \exists x\, Q) \rightarrow \exists x\, (P \land Q))
}{19.29}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \land Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm3.2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, P \rightarrow (\exists x\, Q \rightarrow \exists x\, (P \land Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{aleximi}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall x\, P \land \exists x\, Q) \rightarrow \exists x\, (P \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Variante von \FormulaRefAuto{19.29}]{%
  ((\exists x\, P \land \forall x\, Q) \rightarrow \exists x\, (P \land Q))
}{19.29r}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (P \land Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm3.21}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, Q \rightarrow (\exists x\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, (P \land Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{aleximi}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{((\exists x\, P \land \forall x\, Q) \rightarrow \exists x\, (P \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impcom}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Variante von \FormulaRefAuto{19.29r} mit doppelter Quantifikation]{%
  ((\exists x\, \exists y\, P \land \forall x\, \forall y\, Q) \rightarrow \exists x\, \exists y\, (P \land Q))
}{19.29r2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x\dsep y}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((\exists x\, \exists y\, P \land \forall x\, \forall y\, Q) \rightarrow \exists x\, (\exists y\, P \land \forall y\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.29r}}
  \proofstep{}{((\exists y\, P \land \forall y\, Q) \rightarrow \exists y\, (P \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.29r}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, (\exists y\, P \land \forall y\, Q) \rightarrow \exists x\, \exists y\, (P \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{eximi}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{((\exists x\, \exists y\, P \land \forall x\, \forall y\, Q) \rightarrow \exists x\, \exists y\, (P \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Variante von \FormulaRefAuto{19.29} mit gemischter Quantifikation]{%
  ((\exists x\, \forall y\, P \land \forall x\, \exists y\, Q) \rightarrow \exists x\, \exists y\, (P \land Q))
}{19.29x}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x\dsep y}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((\exists x\, \forall y\, P \land \forall x\, \exists y\, Q) \rightarrow \exists x\, (\forall y\, P \land \exists y\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.29r}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall y\, P \land \exists y\, Q) \rightarrow \exists y\, (P \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.29}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, (\forall y\, P \land \exists y\, Q) \rightarrow \exists x\, \exists y\, (P \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{eximi}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{((\exists x\, \forall y\, P \land \forall x\, \exists y\, Q) \rightarrow \exists x\, \exists y\, (P \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz 19.35 von Margaris]{%
  (\exists x\, (P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, Q))
}{19.35}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg Q \rightarrow \neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.27}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, (P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \exists x\, (\neg Q \rightarrow \neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exbii}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, (\neg Q \rightarrow \neg P) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, \neg Q \rightarrow \exists x\, \neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.35}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, \neg Q \leftrightarrow \neg \exists x\, Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alnex}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, \neg P \rightarrow (\forall x\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, \neg P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.21}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, \neg P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{eximi}{5}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylbir}{4,6}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, (P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ja}{2,7}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{19.35} ist besonders nützlich, wenn eine Implikation unter einen einzelnen Existenzquantor hineingezogen oder daraus herausgezogen werden soll.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Inferenzform zu \FormulaRefAuto{19.35}]{%
  \exists x\, (P \rightarrow Q)\vdash (\forall x\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, Q)
}{19.35i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\exists x\, (P \rightarrow Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, (P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.35}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Umkehrinferenz zu \FormulaRefAuto{19.35}]{%
  (\forall x\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, Q)\vdash \exists x\, (P \rightarrow Q)
}{19.35ri}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, (P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.35}}
  \proofstep{1}{\exists x\, (P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbir}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz 19.25 von Margaris]{%
  (\forall y\, \exists x\, (P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\exists y\, \forall x\, P \rightarrow \exists y\, \exists x\, Q))
}{19.25}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x\dsep y}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, (P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.35}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, (P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\forall x\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y\, \exists x\, (P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\exists y\, \forall x\, P \rightarrow \exists y\, \exists x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{aleximi}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz 19.30 von Margaris]{%
  (\forall x\, (P \lor Q) \rightarrow (\forall x\, P \lor \exists x\, Q))
}{19.30}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, \neg P \leftrightarrow \neg \forall x\, P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{exnal}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor Q) \rightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.53}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \lor Q) \rightarrow (\exists x\, \neg P \rightarrow \exists x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{aleximi}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \lor Q) \rightarrow (\neg \forall x\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimtrrid}{1,3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \lor Q) \rightarrow (\forall x\, P \lor \exists x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orrd}{4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz 19.43 von Margaris]{%
  (\exists x\, (P \lor Q) \leftrightarrow (\exists x\, P \lor \exists x\, Q))
}{19.43}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-or}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, (P \lor Q) \leftrightarrow \exists x\, (\neg P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exbii}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, (\neg P \rightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, \neg P \rightarrow \exists x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.35}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, \neg P \leftrightarrow \neg \exists x\, P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alnex}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall x\, \neg P \rightarrow \exists x\, Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg \exists x\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi1i}{4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, (P \lor Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg \exists x\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitri}{2,3,5}}
  \proofstep{}{((\exists x\, P \lor \exists x\, Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg \exists x\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-or}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, (P \lor Q) \leftrightarrow (\exists x\, P \lor \exists x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr4i}{6,7}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{19.43} ist die Distributionsregel des Existenzquantors über die Disjunktion. Sie ist das disjunktive Gegenstück zu \FormulaRefAuto{19.26}.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Veralteter Beweis von \FormulaRefAuto{19.43}]{%
  (\exists x\, (P \lor Q) \leftrightarrow (\exists x\, P \lor \exists x\, Q))
}{19.43OLD}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg (P \lor Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg P \land \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ioran}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, \neg (P \lor Q) \leftrightarrow \forall x\, (\neg P \land \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (\neg P \land \neg Q) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, \neg P \land \forall x\, \neg Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.26}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, \neg P \leftrightarrow \neg \exists x\, P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alnex}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, \neg Q \leftrightarrow \neg \exists x\, Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alnex}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall x\, \neg P \land \forall x\, \neg Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg \exists x\, P \land \neg \exists x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anbi12i}{4,5}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, \neg (P \lor Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg \exists x\, P \land \neg \exists x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitri}{2,3,6}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x\, \neg (P \lor Q) \leftrightarrow \neg (\neg \exists x\, P \land \neg \exists x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{notbii}{7}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, (P \lor Q) \leftrightarrow \neg \forall x\, \neg (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-ex}}
  \proofstep{}{((\exists x\, P \lor \exists x\, Q) \leftrightarrow \neg (\neg \exists x\, P \land \neg \exists x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{oran}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, (P \lor Q) \leftrightarrow (\exists x\, P \lor \exists x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4i}{8,9,10}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz 19.33 von Margaris]{%
  ((\forall x\, P \lor \forall x\, Q) \rightarrow \forall x\, (P \lor Q))
}{19.33}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, P \rightarrow \forall x\, (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orc}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, Q \rightarrow \forall x\, (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{olc}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall x\, P \lor \forall x\, Q) \rightarrow \forall x\, (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jaoi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Bedingte Umkehrung von \FormulaRefAuto{19.33}]{%
  (\neg (\exists x\, P \land \exists x\, Q) \rightarrow (\forall x\, (P \lor Q) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, P \lor \forall x\, Q)))
}{19.33b}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg (\exists x\, P \land \exists x\, Q) \leftrightarrow (\neg \exists x\, P \lor \neg \exists x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ianor}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, \neg P \leftrightarrow \neg \exists x\, P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alnex}}
  \proofstep{}{((P \lor Q) \rightarrow (\neg P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.53}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \lor Q) \rightarrow (\forall x\, \neg P \rightarrow \forall x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{al2imi}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \lor Q) \rightarrow (\neg \exists x\, P \rightarrow \forall x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimtrrid}{2,4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, Q \rightarrow (\forall x\, P \lor \forall x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{olc}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \exists x\, P \rightarrow (\forall x\, (P \lor Q) \rightarrow (\forall x\, P \lor \forall x\, Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl6com}{5,6}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \lor Q) \rightarrow (\forall x\, P \lor \exists x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.30}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \lor Q) \rightarrow (\exists x\, Q \lor \forall x\, P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orcomd}{8}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \lor Q) \rightarrow (\neg \exists x\, Q \rightarrow \forall x\, P))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ord}{9}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, P \rightarrow (\forall x\, P \lor \forall x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orc}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \exists x\, Q \rightarrow (\forall x\, (P \lor Q) \rightarrow (\forall x\, P \lor \forall x\, Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl6com}{10,11}}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg \exists x\, P \lor \neg \exists x\, Q) \rightarrow (\forall x\, (P \lor Q) \rightarrow (\forall x\, P \lor \forall x\, Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jaoi}{7,12}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg (\exists x\, P \land \exists x\, Q) \rightarrow (\forall x\, (P \lor Q) \rightarrow (\forall x\, P \lor \forall x\, Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylbi}{1,13}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall x\, P \lor \forall x\, Q) \rightarrow \forall x\, (P \lor Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.33}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg (\exists x\, P \land \exists x\, Q) \rightarrow (\forall x\, (P \lor Q) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, P \lor \forall x\, Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid1}{14,15}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz 19.40 von Margaris]{%
  (\exists x\, (P \land Q) \rightarrow (\exists x\, P \land \exists x\, Q))
}{19.40}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, (P \land Q) \rightarrow \exists x\, P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{exsimpl}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, (P \land Q) \rightarrow \exists x\, Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{exsimpr}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, (P \land Q) \rightarrow (\exists x\, P \land \exists x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz 19.40 mit zwei Quantoren]{%
  (\exists x\, \exists y\, (P \land Q) \rightarrow (\exists x\, \exists y\, P \land \exists x\, \exists y\, Q))
}{19.40-2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x\dsep y}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, (P \land Q) \rightarrow (\exists x\, P \land \exists x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.40}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists y\, \exists x\, (P \land Q) \rightarrow \exists y\, (\exists x\, P \land \exists x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{eximi}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists y\, (\exists x\, P \land \exists x\, Q) \rightarrow (\exists y\, \exists x\, P \land \exists y\, \exists x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.40}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, \exists y\, (P \land Q) \rightarrow (\exists x\, \exists y\, P \land \exists x\, \exists y\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Bedingte Umkehrung von \FormulaRefAuto{19.40}]{%
  ((\forall x\, P \lor \forall x\, Q) \rightarrow ((\exists x\, P \land \exists x\, Q) \leftrightarrow \exists x\, (P \land Q)))
}{19.40b}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(Q \rightarrow (P \rightarrow (P \land Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm3.21}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, Q \rightarrow (\exists x\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, (P \land Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{aleximi}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow (Q \rightarrow (P \land Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm3.2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, P \rightarrow (\exists x\, Q \rightarrow \exists x\, (P \land Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{aleximi}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall x\, Q \lor \forall x\, P) \rightarrow ((\exists x\, P \land \exists x\, Q) \rightarrow \exists x\, (P \land Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jaoa}{2,4}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall x\, P \lor \forall x\, Q) \rightarrow ((\exists x\, P \land \exists x\, Q) \rightarrow \exists x\, (P \land Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orcoms}{5}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, (P \land Q) \rightarrow (\exists x\, P \land \exists x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.40}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall x\, P \lor \forall x\, Q) \rightarrow ((\exists x\, P \land \exists x\, Q) \leftrightarrow \exists x\, (P \land Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid1}{6,7}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Zerlege eine Äquivalenz und verteile den Quantor]{%
  (\forall x\, (P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, (P \rightarrow Q) \land \forall x\, (Q \rightarrow P)))
}{albiim}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow ((P \rightarrow Q) \land (Q \rightarrow P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{dfbi2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \forall x\, ((P \rightarrow Q) \land (Q \rightarrow P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, ((P \rightarrow Q) \land (Q \rightarrow P)) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, (P \rightarrow Q) \land \forall x\, (Q \rightarrow P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.26}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, (P \rightarrow Q) \land \forall x\, (Q \rightarrow P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Wie \FormulaRefAuto{albiim}, aber mit zwei Quantoren]{%
  (\forall x\, \forall y\, (P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, \forall y\, (P \rightarrow Q) \land \forall x\, \forall y\, (Q \rightarrow P)))
}{2albiim}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x\dsep y}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, (P \rightarrow Q) \land \forall x\, (Q \rightarrow P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albiim}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, \forall y\, (P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow \forall x\, (\forall y\, (P \rightarrow Q) \land \forall y\, (Q \rightarrow P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (\forall y\, (P \rightarrow Q) \land \forall y\, (Q \rightarrow P)) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, \forall y\, (P \rightarrow Q) \land \forall x\, \forall y\, (Q \rightarrow P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.26}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, \forall y\, (P \leftrightarrow Q) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, \forall y\, (P \rightarrow Q) \land \forall x\, \forall y\, (Q \rightarrow P)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Füge im Bereich eines Existenzquantors einen Konjunkt hinzu oder entferne ihn]{%
  (\forall x\, (P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\exists x\, P \leftrightarrow \exists x\, (P \land Q)))
}{exintrbi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \leftrightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{abai}}
  \proofstep{}{(P \leftrightarrow (P \land Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{rbaibr}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\exists x\, P \leftrightarrow \exists x\, (P \land Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alexbii}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Führe im Bereich eines Existenzquantors einen Konjunkt ein]{%
  (\forall x\, (P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\exists x\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, (P \land Q)))
}{exintr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \land Q) \rightarrow P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancl}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow (\exists x\, P \rightarrow \exists x\, (P \land Q)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{aleximi}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Universell quantifizierte und entkrümmte Form des Syllogismus]{%
  ((\forall x\, (P \rightarrow Q) \land \forall x\, (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow \forall x\, (P \rightarrow R))
}{alsyl}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((P \rightarrow Q) \rightarrow ((Q \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (P \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm3.33}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall x\, (P \rightarrow Q) \land \forall x\, (Q \rightarrow R)) \rightarrow \forall x\, (P \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alanimi}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Nichtfreiheit der Implikation in einem Kontext]{%
  (P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, Q)\dsep (P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, R)\vdash (P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, (Q \rightarrow R))
}{nfimd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, (Q \rightarrow R) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, Q \rightarrow \exists x\, R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.35}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, (Q \rightarrow R) \rightarrow (\forall x\, Q \rightarrow \exists x\, R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow (\exists x\, Q \rightarrow \forall x\, Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfrd}{3}}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \rightarrow (\exists x\, R \rightarrow \forall x\, R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfrd}{5}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \rightarrow ((\forall x\, Q \rightarrow \exists x\, R) \rightarrow (\exists x\, Q \rightarrow \forall x\, R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imim12d}{4,6}}
  \proofstep{}{((\exists x\, Q \rightarrow \forall x\, R) \rightarrow \forall x\, (Q \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.38}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \rightarrow (\exists x\, (Q \rightarrow R) \rightarrow \forall x\, (Q \rightarrow R)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl56}{2,7,8}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, (Q \rightarrow R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfd}{9}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Geschlossene Form von \FormulaRefAuto{nfim} und \FormulaRefAuto{nfimd}]{%
  ((\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P \land \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, Q) \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, (P \rightarrow Q))
}{nfimt}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P \land \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, Q) \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{}{((\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P \land \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, Q) \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{}{((\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P \land \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, Q) \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, (P \rightarrow Q))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfimd}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Nichtfreiheit bleibt unter Implikation erhalten]{%
  \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P \dsep \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, Q \vdash \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, (P \rightarrow Q)
}{nfim}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, Q}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, P \land \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mp2an}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, (P \rightarrow Q)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{3,\FormulaRefAuto{nfimt}}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Nichtfreiheit der Konjunktion in einem Kontext]{%
  (P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, Q)\dsep (P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, R)\vdash (P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, (Q \land R))
}{nfand}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \land R) \leftrightarrow \neg (Q \rightarrow \neg R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-an}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \neg R)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfnd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, (Q \rightarrow \neg R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfimd}{1,4}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \neg (Q \rightarrow \neg R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfnd}{5}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, (Q \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfxfrd}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-an},6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Nichtfreiheit der dreifachen Konjunktion in einem Kontext]{%
  (P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, Q)\dsep (P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, R)\dsep (P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, S)\vdash (P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, (Q \land R \land S))
}{nf3and}{%
  \DeltaRow{Set variables}{x}%
  \DeltaRow{Propositions}{P\dsep Q\dsep R\dsep S}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((Q \land R \land S) \leftrightarrow ((Q \land R) \land S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-3an}}
  \proofstep{1}{(P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, Q)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, R)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, (Q \land R))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfand}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{3}{(P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, S)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{(P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, ((Q \land R) \land S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfand}{4,5}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{(P \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, (Q \land R \land S))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfxfrd}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-3an},6}}
\end{tabproof}

\section{Weitere Sätze zur Nichtfreiheitsrelation}

\FormulaThm[Nichtfreiheit bleibt unter Konjunktion erhalten]{%
  \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi \dsep \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi \vdash \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,(\phi \land \psi)
}{nfan}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\top \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\top \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\top \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,(\phi \land \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfand}{2,4}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,(\phi \land \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mptru}{5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Nichtfreiheit bleibt unter NAND erhalten]{%
  \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi \dsep \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi \vdash \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,(\phi \nand \psi)
}{nfnan}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((\phi \nand \psi) \leftrightarrow \neg(\phi \land \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-nan}}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,(\phi \land \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfan}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\neg(\phi \land \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfn}{4}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,(\phi \nand \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfxfr}{1,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Nichtfreiheit bei dreifacher Konjunktion]{%
  \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi \dsep \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi \dsep \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\chi \vdash \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,(\phi \land \psi \land \chi)
}{nf3an}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi\dsep\chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((\phi \land \psi \land \chi) \leftrightarrow ((\phi \land \psi) \land \chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-3an}}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,(\phi \land \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfan}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{3}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\chi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,((\phi \land \psi) \land \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfan}{4,5}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,(\phi \land \psi \land \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfxfr}{1,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Nichtfreiheit bleibt unter Äquivalenz im Kontext erhalten]{%
  P \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi \dsep P \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\chi \vdash P \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,(\psi \leftrightarrow \chi)
}{nfbid}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{P\dsep\psi\dsep\chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((\psi \leftrightarrow \chi) \leftrightarrow ((\psi \rightarrow \chi) \land (\chi \rightarrow \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{dfbi2}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{P \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\chi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,(\psi \rightarrow \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfimd}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,(\chi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfimd}{3,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,((\psi \rightarrow \chi) \land (\chi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfand}{4,5}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{P \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,(\psi \leftrightarrow \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfxfrd}{1,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Nichtfreiheit bleibt unter Äquivalenz erhalten]{%
  \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi \dsep \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi \vdash \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,(\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)
}{nfbi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\top \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\top \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\top \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,(\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfbid}{2,4}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,(\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mptru}{5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Nichtfreiheit bleibt unter Disjunktion erhalten]{%
  \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi \dsep \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi \vdash \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,(\phi \lor \psi)
}{nfor}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((\phi \lor \psi) \leftrightarrow (\neg \phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-or}}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\neg \phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfn}{2}}
  \proofstep{2}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,(\neg \phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfim}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,(\phi \lor \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfxfr}{1,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Nichtfreiheit bei dreifacher Disjunktion]{%
  \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi \dsep \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi \dsep \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\chi \vdash \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,(\phi \lor \psi \lor \chi)
}{nf3or}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi\dsep\chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((\phi \lor \psi \lor \chi) \leftrightarrow ((\phi \lor \psi) \lor \chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-3or}}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,(\phi \lor \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfor}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{3}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\chi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,((\phi \lor \psi) \lor \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfor}{4,5}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,(\phi \lor \psi \lor \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfxfr}{1,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\section{Die leere Domäne}

\FormulaThm[Charakterisierung der leeren Domäne]{%
  (\neg \exists x \top \leftrightarrow \forall x \bot)
}{empty}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\bot \leftrightarrow \neg \top)}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-fal}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \bot \leftrightarrow \forall x \neg \top)}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \neg \top \leftrightarrow \neg \exists x \top)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alnex}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \exists x \top \leftrightarrow \forall x \bot)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr2i}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Auf der leeren Domäne ist jede Existenzaussage falsch]{%
  \neg \exists x \top \vdash \neg \exists x \phi
}{emptyex}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow \top)}{\FormulaRefAuto{trud}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \phi \rightarrow \exists x \top)}{\FormulaRefAuto{eximi}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg \exists x \top}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg \exists x \phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{con3i}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Auf der leeren Domäne ist jede Allaussage wahr]{%
  \neg \exists x \top \vdash \forall x \phi
}{emptyal}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \exists x \top \rightarrow \neg \exists x \neg \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{emptyex}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \phi \leftrightarrow \neg \exists x \neg \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alex}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \exists x \top \rightarrow \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylibr}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Auf der leeren Domäne ist jede Variable effektiv nicht frei]{%
  \neg \exists x \top \vdash \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi
}{emptynf}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \exists x \top \rightarrow \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{emptyal}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nftht}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \exists x \top \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
Diese kleine Gruppe von Sätzen beschreibt präzise, was in einer leeren Domäne geschieht: Existenzaussagen schlagen fehl, Allaussagen werden trivial wahr, und die Nichtfreiheitsbedingung wird automatisch erfüllt.
\end{remark}

\section{Axiomenschema \texttt{ax-5} und Folgerungen}

\begin{remark}
Bei den im Folgenden mit dem Suffix \enquote{v} versehenen Sätzen ist jeweils die zugehörige Metamath-Verschiedenheitsbedingung mitzudenken. Inhaltlich bedeutet dies hier, dass die betreffende Quantorvariable in den jeweils \enquote{transportierten} Formeln nicht vorkommt.
\end{remark}

\FormulaAxiom[Axiom der Verschiedenheit]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi)
}{ax-5}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}

\FormulaThm[Version von \FormulaRefAuto{ax-5} mit zusätzlichem Antezedens]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \forall x \psi))
}{ax5d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\psi \rightarrow \forall x \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-5}}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \forall x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Existenzielle Umformulierung von \FormulaRefAuto{ax-5}]{%
  (\exists x \phi \rightarrow \phi)
}{ax5e}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \phi \rightarrow \forall x \neg \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-5}}
  \proofstep{}{((\exists x \phi \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow (\neg \phi \rightarrow \forall x \neg \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{eximal}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \phi \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbir}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Aus Gültigkeit für ein solches \(x\) folgt Gültigkeit für alle \(x\)]{%
  (\exists x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi)
}{ax5ea}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \phi \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax5e}}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-5}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Wenn \(x\) nicht vorkommt, ist \(x\) nicht frei]{%
  \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi
}{nfv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax5ea}}
  \proofstep{}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfi}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Version von \FormulaRefAuto{nfv} mit Antezedens]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi)
}{nfvd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfv}}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktionsform von Margaris 19.20]{%
  P \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \chi) \vdash P \rightarrow (\forall x \psi \rightarrow \forall x \chi)
}{alimdv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{P\dsep\psi\dsep\chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow \forall x P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-5}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \chi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\forall x \psi \rightarrow \forall x \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alimdh}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktionsform von Margaris 19.22]{%
  P \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \chi) \vdash P \rightarrow (\exists x \psi \rightarrow \exists x \chi)
}{eximdv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{P\dsep\psi\dsep\chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow \forall x P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-5}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \chi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\exists x \psi \rightarrow \exists x \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{eximdh}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Margaris 19.20 mit zwei Allquantoren in Deduktionsform]{%
  P \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \chi) \vdash P \rightarrow (\forall x \forall y \psi \rightarrow \forall x \forall y \chi)
}{2alimdv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{P\dsep\psi\dsep\chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \chi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\forall y \psi \rightarrow \forall y \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alimdv}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\forall x \forall y \psi \rightarrow \forall x \forall y \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alimdv}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Margaris 19.22 mit zwei Existenzquantoren in Deduktionsform]{%
  P \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \chi) \vdash P \rightarrow (\exists x \exists y \psi \rightarrow \exists x \exists y \chi)
}{2eximdv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{P\dsep\psi\dsep\chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \chi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\exists y \psi \rightarrow \exists y \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{eximdv}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\exists x \exists y \psi \rightarrow \exists x \exists y \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{eximdv}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Äquivalenzregel für den Allquantor in Deduktionsform]{%
  P \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi) \vdash P \rightarrow (\forall x \psi \leftrightarrow \forall x \chi)
}{albidv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{P\dsep\psi\dsep\chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow \forall x P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-5}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\forall x \psi \leftrightarrow \forall x \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{albidh}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Äquivalenzregel für den Existenzquantor in Deduktionsform]{%
  P \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi) \vdash P \rightarrow (\exists x \psi \leftrightarrow \exists x \chi)
}{exbidv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{P\dsep\psi\dsep\chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(P \rightarrow \forall x P)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-5}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\exists x \psi \leftrightarrow \exists x \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{exbidh}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Nichtfreiheitsäquivalenz in Deduktionsform]{%
  P \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi) \vdash P \rightarrow (\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi \leftrightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\chi)
}{nfbidv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{P\dsep\psi\dsep\chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\exists x \psi \leftrightarrow \exists x \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{exbidv}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\forall x \psi \leftrightarrow \forall x \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{albidv}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow ((\exists x \psi \rightarrow \forall x \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \chi \rightarrow \forall x \chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi12d}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi \leftrightarrow (\exists x \psi \rightarrow \forall x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-nf}}
  \proofstep{}{(\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\chi \leftrightarrow (\exists x \chi \rightarrow \forall x \chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-nf}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi \leftrightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4g}{5,4,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Zwei Allquantoren in der Äquivalenzregel]{%
  P \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi) \vdash P \rightarrow (\forall x \forall y \psi \leftrightarrow \forall x \forall y \chi)
}{2albidv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{P\dsep\psi\dsep\chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\forall y \psi \leftrightarrow \forall y \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{albidv}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\forall x \forall y \psi \leftrightarrow \forall x \forall y \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{albidv}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Zwei Existenzquantoren in der Äquivalenzregel]{%
  P \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi) \vdash P \rightarrow (\exists x \exists y \psi \leftrightarrow \exists x \exists y \chi)
}{2exbidv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{P\dsep\psi\dsep\chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\exists y \psi \leftrightarrow \exists y \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{exbidv}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\exists x \exists y \psi \leftrightarrow \exists x \exists y \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{exbidv}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Drei Existenzquantoren in der Äquivalenzregel]{%
  P \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi) \vdash P \rightarrow (\exists x \exists y \exists z \psi \leftrightarrow \exists x \exists y \exists z \chi)
}{3exbidv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{P\dsep\psi\dsep\chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\exists z \psi \leftrightarrow \exists z \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{exbidv}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\exists x \exists y \exists z \psi \leftrightarrow \exists x \exists y \exists z \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{2exbidv}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Vier Existenzquantoren in der Äquivalenzregel]{%
  P \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi) \vdash P \rightarrow (\exists x \exists y \exists z \exists w \psi \leftrightarrow \exists x \exists y \exists z \exists w \chi)
}{4exbidv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{P\dsep\psi\dsep\chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep w}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\exists w \psi \leftrightarrow \exists w \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{exbidv}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{P \rightarrow (\exists x \exists y \exists z \exists w \psi \leftrightarrow \exists x \exists y \exists z \exists w \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3exbidv}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inferenzform von Margaris 19.21]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow \psi) \vdash \phi \rightarrow \forall x \psi
}{alrimiv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-5}}
  \proofstep{1}{\phi \rightarrow \psi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\phi \rightarrow \forall x \psi}{\FormulaRefAuto{alrimih}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inferenzform von Margaris 19.21 mit zwei Allquantoren]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow \psi) \vdash \phi \rightarrow \forall x \forall y \psi
}{alrimivv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\phi \rightarrow \psi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\phi \rightarrow \forall y \psi}{\FormulaRefAuto{alrimiv}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{\phi \rightarrow \forall x \forall y \psi}{\FormulaRefAuto{alrimiv}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktionsform von Margaris 19.21]{%
  \phi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \chi) \vdash \phi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \forall x \chi)
}{alrimdv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi\dsep\chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-5}}
  \proofstep{}{(\psi \rightarrow \forall x \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-5}}
  \proofstep{1}{\phi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \chi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\phi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \forall x \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alrimdh}{1,2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inferenzform von Margaris 19.23]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow \psi) \vdash \exists x \phi \rightarrow \psi
}{exlimiv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\phi \rightarrow \psi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\exists x \phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi}{\FormulaRefAuto{eximi}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \psi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax5e}}
  \proofstep{1}{\exists x \phi \rightarrow \psi}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Regel \enquote{Rule C} in Inferenzform]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow \psi) \dsep \exists x \phi \vdash \psi
}{exlimiiv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\phi \rightarrow \psi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\exists x \phi \rightarrow \psi}{\FormulaRefAuto{exlimiv}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{\exists x \phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\psi}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{3,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inferenzform von Margaris 19.23 mit zwei Existenzquantoren]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow \psi) \vdash \exists x \exists y \phi \rightarrow \psi
}{exlimivv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\phi \rightarrow \psi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\exists y \phi \rightarrow \psi}{\FormulaRefAuto{exlimiv}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{\exists x \exists y \phi \rightarrow \psi}{\FormulaRefAuto{exlimiv}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktionsform von Margaris 19.23]{%
  \phi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \chi) \vdash \phi \rightarrow (\exists x \psi \rightarrow \chi)
}{exlimdv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi\dsep\chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\phi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \chi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\phi \rightarrow (\exists x \psi \rightarrow \exists x \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{eximdv}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \chi \rightarrow \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax5e}}
  \proofstep{1}{\phi \rightarrow (\exists x \psi \rightarrow \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl6}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktionsform von Margaris 19.23 mit zwei Existenzquantoren]{%
  \phi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \chi) \vdash \phi \rightarrow (\exists x \exists y \psi \rightarrow \chi)
}{exlimdvv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi\dsep\chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\phi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \chi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\phi \rightarrow (\exists y \psi \rightarrow \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{exlimdv}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{\phi \rightarrow (\exists x \exists y \psi \rightarrow \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{exlimdv}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Existenzelimination im Stil der natürlichen Deduktion]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi) \dsep ((\phi \land \psi) \rightarrow \chi) \vdash \phi \rightarrow \chi
}{exlimddv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi\dsep\chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(\phi \land \psi) \rightarrow \chi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\phi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{2}}
  \proofstep{2}{\phi \rightarrow (\exists x \psi \rightarrow \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{exlimdv}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\phi \rightarrow \chi}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpd}{1,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{exlimiv}, \FormulaRefAuto{exlimiiv} und \FormulaRefAuto{exlimddv} bilden zusammen den präzisen Hilbertstil-Kern dessen, was in informellen Beweisen meist als \enquote{Rule C} verwendet wird.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Negierte Existenz in Deduktionsform]{%
  \phi \rightarrow \neg \psi \vdash \phi \rightarrow \neg \exists x \psi
}{nexdv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-5}}
  \proofstep{1}{\phi \rightarrow \neg \psi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\phi \rightarrow \neg \exists x \psi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nexdh}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Zweifache Quantifikation einer unabhängigen Formel]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow \forall x \forall y \phi)
}{2ax5}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall x \forall y \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alrimivv}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Verschiebung eines Allquantors in den Konsequenten]{%
  (\forall x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x \psi))
}{stdpc5v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}

  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-5}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \rightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alim}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl5}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Margaris 19.21 unter Verschiedenheitsbedingung]{%
  (\forall x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x \psi))
}{19.21v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{stdpc5v}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \phi \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax5e}}
  \proofstep{}{((\phi \rightarrow \forall x \psi) \rightarrow (\exists x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imim1i}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{((\exists x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \psi) \rightarrow \forall x(\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.38}}
  \proofstep{}{((\phi \rightarrow \forall x \psi) \rightarrow \forall x(\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{1,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Margaris 19.32 unter Verschiedenheitsbedingung]{%
  (\forall x(\phi \lor \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \lor \forall x \psi))
}{19.32v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\neg \phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\neg \phi \rightarrow \forall x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.21v}}
  \proofstep{}{((\phi \lor \psi) \leftrightarrow (\neg \phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-or}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \lor \psi) \leftrightarrow \forall x(\neg \phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{((\phi \lor \forall x \psi) \leftrightarrow (\neg \phi \rightarrow \forall x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-or}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \lor \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \lor \forall x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4i}{1,3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Margaris 19.31 unter Verschiedenheitsbedingung]{%
  (\forall x(\phi \lor \psi) \leftrightarrow (\forall x \phi \lor \psi))
}{19.31v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\psi \lor \phi) \leftrightarrow (\psi \lor \forall x \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.32v}}
  \proofstep{}{((\phi \lor \psi) \leftrightarrow (\psi \lor \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orcom}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \lor \psi) \leftrightarrow \forall x(\psi \lor \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall x \phi \lor \psi) \leftrightarrow (\psi \lor \forall x \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orcom}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \lor \psi) \leftrightarrow (\forall x \phi \lor \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4i}{1,3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Margaris 19.23 unter Verschiedenheitsbedingung]{%
  (\forall x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \phi \rightarrow \psi))
}{19.23v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \rightarrow (\exists x \phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exim}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \psi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax5e}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \rightarrow (\exists x \phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl6}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\psi \rightarrow \forall x \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-5}}
  \proofstep{}{((\exists x \phi \rightarrow \psi) \rightarrow (\exists x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imim2i}{4}}
  \proofstep{}{((\exists x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \psi) \rightarrow \forall x(\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.38}}
  \proofstep{}{((\exists x \phi \rightarrow \psi) \rightarrow \forall x(\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{5,6}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{3,7}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Margaris 19.23 mit zwei Variablen]{%
  (\forall x \forall y(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \exists y \phi \rightarrow \psi))
}{19.23vv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists y \phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.23v}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \forall y(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow \forall x(\exists y \phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\exists y \phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \exists y \phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.23v}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \forall y(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \exists y \phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Whitehead--Russell 11.53 unter Verschiedenheitsbedingung]{%
  (\forall x \forall y(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \phi \rightarrow \forall y \psi))
}{pm11.53v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall y \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.21v}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \forall y(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow \forall x(\phi \rightarrow \forall y \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \rightarrow \forall y \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \phi \rightarrow \forall y \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.23v}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \forall y(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \phi \rightarrow \forall y \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Eine Richtung von \FormulaRefAuto{19.36v}]{%
  (\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \rightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi))
}{19.36imv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow ((\phi \rightarrow \psi) \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.27}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow (\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \rightarrow \exists x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{aleximi}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \psi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax5e}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \rightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl6com}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inferenz zu \FormulaRefAuto{19.36v}]{%
  \exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \vdash \forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi
}{19.36iv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \rightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.36imv}}
  \proofstep{1}{\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Eine Richtung von \FormulaRefAuto{19.37v}]{%
  (\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi))
}{19.37imv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%

}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-5}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.35}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \rightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl5}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inferenz zu \FormulaRefAuto{19.37v}]{%
  \exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \vdash \phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi

  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%

}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.37imv}}
  \proofstep{1}{\phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
