\FormulaThm[Margaris 19.41 unter Verschiedenheitsbedingung]{%
  (\exists x(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \phi \land \psi))
}{19.41v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\phi \land \psi) \rightarrow (\exists x \phi \land \exists x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.40}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \psi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax5e}}
  \proofstep{}{((\exists x \phi \land \exists x \psi) \rightarrow (\exists x \phi \land \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anim2i}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\phi \land \psi) \rightarrow (\exists x \phi \land \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\psi \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow (\phi \land \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm3.21}}
  \proofstep{}{(\psi \rightarrow (\exists x \phi \rightarrow \exists x(\phi \land \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{eximdv}{5}}
  \proofstep{}{((\exists x \phi \land \psi) \rightarrow \exists x(\phi \land \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impcom}{6}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \phi \land \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{4,7}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Margaris 19.41 mit zwei Existenzquantoren]{%
  (\exists x \exists y(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \exists y \phi \land \psi))
}{19.41vv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists y(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists y \phi \land \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.41v}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow \exists x(\exists y \phi \land \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exbii}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\exists y \phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \exists y \phi \land \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.41v}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \exists y \phi \land \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Margaris 19.41 mit drei Existenzquantoren]{%
  (\exists x \exists y \exists z(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \exists y \exists z \phi \land \psi))
}{19.41vvv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists y \exists z(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists y \exists z \phi \land \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.41vv}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y \exists z(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow \exists x(\exists y \exists z \phi \land \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exbii}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\exists y \exists z \phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \exists y \exists z \phi \land \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.41v}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y \exists z(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \exists y \exists z \phi \land \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Margaris 19.41 mit vier Existenzquantoren]{%
  (\exists w \exists x \exists y \exists z(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists w \exists x \exists y \exists z \phi \land \psi))
}{19.41vvvv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{w\dsep x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y \exists z(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \exists y \exists z \phi \land \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.41vvv}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists w \exists x \exists y \exists z(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow \exists w(\exists x \exists y \exists z \phi \land \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exbii}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists w(\exists x \exists y \exists z \phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists w \exists x \exists y \exists z \phi \land \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.41v}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists w \exists x \exists y \exists z(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists w \exists x \exists y \exists z \phi \land \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Margaris 19.42 unter Verschiedenheitsbedingung]{%
  (\exists x(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \land \exists x \psi))
}{19.42v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\psi \land \phi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \psi \land \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.41v}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow \exists x(\psi \land \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exancom}}
  \proofstep{}{((\phi \land \exists x \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \psi \land \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancom}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \land \exists x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4i}{1,2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Verteilung von Existenzquantoren]{%
  (\exists x \exists y(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow \exists x(\phi \land \exists y \psi))
}{exdistr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists y(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \land \exists y \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.42v}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow \exists x(\phi \land \exists y \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exbii}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Distributivität zweier Existenzquantoren über eine Konjunktion]{%
  (\exists x \exists y(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \phi \land \exists y \psi))
}{exdistrv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow \exists x(\phi \land \exists y \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exdistr}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\phi \land \exists y \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \phi \land \exists y \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.41v}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \phi \land \exists y \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Distributivität zweier Paare von Existenzquantoren]{%
  (\exists x \exists z \exists y \exists w(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \exists y \phi \land \exists z \exists w \psi))
}{4exdistrv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep w}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists y \exists w(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists y \phi \land \exists w \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exdistrv}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists z \exists y \exists w(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow \exists x \exists z(\exists y \phi \land \exists w \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{2exbii}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists z(\exists y \phi \land \exists w \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \exists y \phi \land \exists z \exists w \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exdistrv}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists z \exists y \exists w(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \exists y \phi \land \exists z \exists w \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Margaris 19.42 mit zwei Existenzquantoren]{%
  (\exists x \exists y(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \land \exists x \exists y \psi))
}{19.42vv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow \exists x(\phi \land \exists y \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exdistr}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\phi \land \exists y \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \land \exists x \exists y \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.42v}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \land \exists x \exists y \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Verteilung von drei Existenzquantoren]{%
  (\exists x \exists y \exists z(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow \exists x(\phi \land \exists y \exists z \psi))
}{exdistr2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists y \exists z(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \land \exists y \exists z \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.42vv}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y \exists z(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow \exists x(\phi \land \exists y \exists z \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exbii}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Margaris 19.42 mit drei Existenzquantoren]{%
  (\exists x \exists y \exists z(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \land \exists x \exists y \exists z \psi))
}{19.42vvv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y \exists z(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow \exists x(\phi \land \exists y \exists z \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exdistr2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\phi \land \exists y \exists z \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \land \exists x \exists y \exists z \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.42v}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y \exists z(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \land \exists x \exists y \exists z \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Verteilung von Existenzquantoren in einer dreifachen Konjunktion]{%
  (\exists x \exists y \exists z(\phi \land \psi \land \chi) \leftrightarrow \exists x(\phi \land \exists y(\psi \land \exists z \chi)))
}{3exdistr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi\dsep\chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((\phi \land \psi \land \chi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \land (\psi \land \chi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3anass}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists y \exists z(\phi \land \psi \land \chi) \leftrightarrow \exists y \exists z(\phi \land (\psi \land \chi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{2exbii}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists y \exists z(\phi \land (\psi \land \chi)) \leftrightarrow (\phi \land \exists y \exists z(\psi \land \chi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.42vv}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists y \exists z(\psi \land \chi) \leftrightarrow \exists y(\psi \land \exists z \chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exdistr}}
  \proofstep{}{((\phi \land \exists y \exists z(\psi \land \chi)) \leftrightarrow (\phi \land \exists y(\psi \land \exists z \chi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anbi2i}{4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists y \exists z(\phi \land \psi \land \chi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \land \exists y(\psi \land \exists z \chi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitri}{2,3,5}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y \exists z(\phi \land \psi \land \chi) \leftrightarrow \exists x(\phi \land \exists y(\psi \land \exists z \chi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exbii}{6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Verteilung von Existenzquantoren in einer vierfachen Konjunktion]{%
  (\exists x \exists y \exists z \exists w((\phi \land \psi) \land (\chi \land \theta)) \leftrightarrow \exists x(\phi \land \exists y(\psi \land \exists z(\chi \land \exists w \theta))))
}{4exdistr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi\dsep\chi\dsep\theta}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep w}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists w((\phi \land \psi) \land (\chi \land \theta)) \leftrightarrow ((\phi \land \psi) \land \exists w(\chi \land \theta)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.42v}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists w(\chi \land \theta) \leftrightarrow (\chi \land \exists w \theta))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.42v}}
  \proofstep{}{(((\phi \land \psi) \land \exists w(\chi \land \theta)) \leftrightarrow ((\phi \land \psi) \land (\chi \land \exists w \theta)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anbi2i}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \land \psi \land (\chi \land \exists w \theta) \leftrightarrow ((\phi \land \psi) \land (\chi \land \exists w \theta)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-3an}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists w((\phi \land \psi) \land (\chi \land \theta)) \leftrightarrow (\phi \land \psi \land (\chi \land \exists w \theta)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4i}{1,3,4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y \exists z \exists w((\phi \land \psi) \land (\chi \land \theta)) \leftrightarrow \exists x \exists y \exists z(\phi \land \psi \land (\chi \land \exists w \theta)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3exbii}{5}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y \exists z(\phi \land \psi \land (\chi \land \exists w \theta)) \leftrightarrow \exists x(\phi \land \exists y(\psi \land \exists z(\chi \land \exists w \theta))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3exdistr}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y \exists z \exists w((\phi \land \psi) \land (\chi \land \theta)) \leftrightarrow \exists x(\phi \land \exists y(\psi \land \exists z(\chi \land \exists w \theta))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{6,7}}
\end{tabproof}

\section{Gleichheitspredikat (Fortsetzung)}

\FormulaDef[Gleichheit als atomare Formel]{%
  \text{Wenn } x \text{ und } y \text{ Variablen sind, dann ist } (x = y) \text{ eine wff.}%
}{weq}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}

\FormulaThm[Spezialisierung mit zusätzlicher Abschwächung]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi)) \vdash (\neg \forall x \neg x = y \rightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi))
}{speimfw}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, x = y \leftrightarrow \neg \forall x \neg x = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-ex}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x \neg x = y \rightarrow \exists x\, x = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{com12}{3}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow (\exists x\, x = y \rightarrow \exists x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{aleximi}{4}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\neg \forall x \neg x = y \rightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl5com}{2,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Alternativer Beweis von \FormulaRefAuto{speimfw}]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi)) \vdash (\neg \forall x \neg x = y \rightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi))
}{speimfwALT}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\neg \forall x \neg x = y \rightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{speimfw}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Spezialisierung mit zusätzlicher Abschwächung und schwacher Zielaussage]{%
  (\neg \psi \rightarrow \forall x \neg \psi) \dsep (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi)) \vdash (\neg \forall x \neg x = y \rightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi))
}{spimfw}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{2}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(\neg \forall x \neg x = y \rightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{speimfw}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \psi \leftrightarrow \neg \forall x \neg \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-ex}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\neg \psi \rightarrow \forall x \neg \psi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\neg \forall x \neg \psi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con1i}{4}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\exists x \psi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylbi}{3,5}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(\neg \forall x \neg x = y \rightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl6}{2,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Hilfssatz zu \FormulaRefAuto{ax-12}]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)) \dsep (\psi \rightarrow \forall x \psi) \vdash (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))
}{ax12i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(\psi \rightarrow \forall x \psi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\psi \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimprcd}{1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(\psi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alrimih}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimtrdi}{1,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\section{Axiomenschema \texttt{ax-6} und Existenz}

\FormulaAxiom[Axiom der Existenz]{%
  \neg \forall x \neg x = y
}{ax-6}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}

\FormulaThm[Geschwächte Form von \FormulaRefAuto{ax-6}]{%
  \neg \forall x \neg x = y
}{ax6v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\neg \forall x \neg x = y}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Es existiert mindestens ein Individuum]{%
  \exists x\, x = y
}{ax6ev}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\neg \forall x \neg x = y}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax6v}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, x = y \leftrightarrow \neg \forall x \neg x = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-ex}}
  \proofstep{}{\exists x\, x = y}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbir}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Spezialisierung]{%
  (\neg \psi \rightarrow \forall x \neg \psi) \dsep (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi)) \vdash \forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi
}{spimw}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{ \neg \forall x \neg x = y}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax6v}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\neg \psi \rightarrow \forall x \neg \psi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(\neg \forall x \neg x = y \rightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{spimfw}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Existenzeinführung mittels impliziter Substitution]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi) \dsep (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi)) \vdash \phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi
}{spimew}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{ \neg \forall x \neg x = y}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax6v}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(\neg \forall x \neg x = y \rightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{speimfw}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(\phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpsyl}{1,3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inferenz aus existenzieller Spezialisierung]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \phi)) \dsep \psi \vdash \exists x \phi
}{speiv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{2}{\psi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(\psi \rightarrow \forall x \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{hbth}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \phi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(\psi \rightarrow \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{spimew}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\exists x \phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Version von \FormulaRefAuto{spei} unter Verschiedenheitsbedingung]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)) \dsep \psi \vdash \exists x \phi
}{speivw}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimprd}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{\psi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\exists x \phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{speiv}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Regel der Existenzverallgemeinerung]{%
  \phi \vdash \exists x \phi
}{exgen}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{idd}}
  \proofstep{1}{\phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\exists x \phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{speiv}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Es gibt ein \(x\), für das \(\top\) gilt]{%
  \exists x \top
}{extru}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\top}{\FormulaRefAuto{tru}}
  \proofstep{}{\exists x \top}{\FormulaRefAuto{exgen}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Margaris 19.2]{%
  (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \exists x \phi)
}{19.2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exgen}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.35i}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktionsform zu Margaris 19.2]{%
  \phi \rightarrow \forall x \psi \vdash \phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi
}{19.2d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall x \psi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \psi \rightarrow \exists x \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.2}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Schwache Form von Margaris 19.8]{%
  \phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi \vdash \phi \rightarrow \exists x \phi
}{19.8w}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.2d}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Schwache Form der Spezialisierung]{%
  (\neg \phi \rightarrow \forall x \neg \phi) \vdash \forall x \phi \rightarrow \phi
}{spnfw}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\neg \phi \rightarrow \forall x \neg \phi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{idd}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{spimw}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Spezialisierung im falschen Fall]{%
  \neg \phi \vdash \forall x \phi \rightarrow \phi
}{spfalw}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg \phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\neg \phi \rightarrow \forall x \neg \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{hbth}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{spnfw}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Spezialisierung bei nicht vorkommender Variablen]{%
  (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \phi)
}{spvw}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \phi \rightarrow \forall x \neg \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-5}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{spnfw}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Margaris 19.3 unter Verschiedenheitsbedingung]{%
  (\forall x \phi \leftrightarrow \phi)
}{19.3v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{spvw}}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-5}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \phi \leftrightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Margaris 19.8 unter Verschiedenheitsbedingung]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow \exists x \phi)
}{19.8v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-5}}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.8w}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Margaris 19.9 unter Verschiedenheitsbedingung]{%
  (\exists x \phi \leftrightarrow \phi)
}{19.9v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \phi \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax5e}}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.8v}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \phi \leftrightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{19.3v} und \FormulaRefAuto{19.9v} beschreiben die vakue Quantifikation: wenn \(x\) in \(\phi\) nicht vorkommt, ändern weder \(\forall x\) noch \(\exists x\) den Wahrheitswert der Formel.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Existenzeinführung mit Verschiedenheitsbedingung]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi)) \vdash \phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi
}{spimevw}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-5}}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{spimew}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Schwache Spezialisierung mit Verschiedenheitsbedingung]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi)) \vdash \forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi
}{spimvw}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \psi \rightarrow \forall x \neg \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-5}}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{spimw}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Verallgemeinerung des Antezedens unter Verschiedenheitsbedingung]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow \psi) \vdash \forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi
}{spsv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{spimvw}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Spezialisierung durch implizite Substitution unter Verschiedenheitsbedingung]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)) \vdash \forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi
}{spvv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpd}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{spimvw}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Implizite Ersetzung von \(y\) für \(x\) unter Verschiedenheitsbedingung]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)) \dsep \phi \vdash \psi
}{chvarvv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{spvv}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{\phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\psi}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpg}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Margaris 19.39]{%
  ((\exists x \phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi) \rightarrow \exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi))
}{19.39}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exgen}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{((\exists x \phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi) \rightarrow \exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imim1i}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{((\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \phi) \rightarrow \exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi)) \leftrightarrow ((\exists x \phi \rightarrow \exists x \phi) \rightarrow (\exists x \phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.35}}
  \proofstep{}{((\exists x \phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi) \rightarrow \exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylibr}{3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Margaris 19.24]{%
  ((\forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \psi) \rightarrow \exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi))
}{19.24}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exgen}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \psi) \rightarrow \exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imim2i}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{((\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \phi) \rightarrow \exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi)) \leftrightarrow ((\forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi) \rightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.35}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \psi) \rightarrow \exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylibr}{3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Margaris 19.34]{%
  ((\forall x \phi \lor \exists x \psi) \rightarrow \exists x(\phi \lor \psi))
}{19.34}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exgen}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall x \phi \lor \exists x \psi) \rightarrow \exists x(\phi \lor \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orim1i}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{((\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \phi) \lor \exists x \psi) \leftrightarrow \exists x((\phi \rightarrow \phi) \lor \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.43}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall x \phi \lor \exists x \psi) \rightarrow \exists x(\phi \lor \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylibr}{3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Margaris 19.36 unter Verschiedenheitsbedingung]{%
  (\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi))
}{19.36v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.35}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \psi \leftrightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.9v}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall x \phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi) \leftrightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi2i}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Vertauschung von \(\exists\forall\) in einem Implikationszusammenhang]{%
  (\exists x \forall y(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow \forall y \exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi))
}{19.12vvv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y(\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi)) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \forall y \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.21v}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \forall y(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow \exists x(\phi \rightarrow \forall y \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exbii}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \forall y \psi) \leftrightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall y \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.36v}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall y \psi) \leftrightarrow \forall y(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.36v}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y((\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y(\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi)) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \forall y(\phi \rightarrow \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.21v}}
  \proofstep{}{((\exists x \forall y(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow \forall y(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr2i}{2,4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \forall y(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow \forall y \exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitri}{7,5,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Margaris 19.27 unter Verschiedenheitsbedingung]{%
  (\forall x(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\forall x \phi \land \psi))
}{19.27v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\forall x \phi \land \forall x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.26}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \psi \leftrightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.3v}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall x \phi \land \forall x \psi) \leftrightarrow (\forall x \phi \land \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anbi2i}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\forall x \phi \land \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Margaris 19.28 unter Verschiedenheitsbedingung]{%
  (\forall x(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \land \forall x \psi))
}{19.28v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\forall x \phi \land \forall x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.26}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \phi \leftrightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.3v}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \land \forall x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bianbi}{2,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Margaris 19.37 unter Verschiedenheitsbedingung]{%
  (\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi))
}{19.37v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.35}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \phi \leftrightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.3v}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall x \phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi1i}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Margaris 19.44 unter Verschiedenheitsbedingung]{%
  (\exists x(\phi \lor \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \phi \lor \psi))
}{19.44v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\phi \lor \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \phi \lor \exists x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.43}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \psi \leftrightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.9v}}
  \proofstep{}{((\exists x \phi \lor \exists x \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \phi \lor \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{orbi2i}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\phi \lor \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \phi \lor \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
Mit \FormulaRefAuto{19.21v}, \FormulaRefAuto{19.23v}, \FormulaRefAuto{19.36v}, \FormulaRefAuto{19.37v}, \FormulaRefAuto{19.41v} und \FormulaRefAuto{19.44v} steht nun das übliche Arsenal für das kontrollierte Verschieben von Quantoren durch Implikation, Konjunktion und Disjunktion zur Verfügung.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Existenzquantor über einer Disjunktion bei variablefreiem linken Disjunkt]{%
  (\exists x(\phi \lor \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \lor \exists x \psi))
}{19.45v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\phi \lor \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \phi \lor \exists x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.43}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \phi \leftrightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.9v}}
  \proofstep{}{((\exists x \phi \lor \exists x \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \lor \exists x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{2orbi1i}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\phi \lor \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \lor \exists x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{19.45v} ist die Existenz-Analogie zur Verschiebung eines variablefreien Disjunktionsgliedes vor den Quantor: Der linke Disjunkt \(\phi\) bleibt unverändert stehen, weil \(x\) in \(\phi\) nicht frei vorkommt.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Existenz aus einer gleichheitsgebundenen Allformel]{%
  (\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi) \rightarrow \exists x(x = y \land \phi))
}{equs4v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\exists x\, x = y}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax6ev}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi) \rightarrow (\exists x\, x = y \rightarrow \exists x(x = y \land \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exintr}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi) \rightarrow \exists x(x = y \land \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Existenz aus einer gleichheitsgebundenen Allformel]{%
  (\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi) \rightarrow \exists x \phi)
}{alequexv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\exists x\, x = y}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax6ev}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi) \rightarrow (\exists x\, x = y \rightarrow \exists x \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exim}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi) \rightarrow \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Existenz aus einem impliziten Substitutionsprototyp]{%
  (\exists y\, \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi) \rightarrow \exists x \phi)
}{exsbim}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi) \rightarrow \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alequexv}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists y\, \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi) \rightarrow \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{exlimiv}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Substitutionsprototyp für eine variablefreie Formel]{%
  (\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow \phi)
}{equsv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x\, x = y \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.23v}}
  \proofstep{}{\exists x\, x = y}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax6ev}}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \leftrightarrow (\exists x\, x = y \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1bi}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr4i}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Allquantorform eines Substitutionsprototyps]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)) \vdash \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow \psi
}{equsalvw}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{((x = y \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow (x = y \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74i}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{equsv}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Existenzform eines Substitutionsprototyps]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)) \vdash \exists x(x = y \land \phi) \leftrightarrow \psi
}{equsexvw}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \neg \phi) \leftrightarrow \neg \exists x(x = y \land \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alinexa}}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\neg \phi \leftrightarrow \neg \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{notbid}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \neg \phi) \leftrightarrow \neg \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{equsalvw}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\neg \exists x(x = y \land \phi) \leftrightarrow \neg \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr3i}{1,4}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\exists x(x = y \land \phi) \leftrightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con4bii}{5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Änderung gebundener Variablen]{%
  (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall y \forall x \phi) \dsep (\neg \psi \rightarrow \forall x \neg \psi) \dsep (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi)) \vdash \forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall y \psi
}{cbvaliw}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall y \forall x \phi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(\neg \psi \rightarrow \forall x \neg \psi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2,3}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{spimw}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall y \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alrimih}{1,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Änderung gebundener Variablen unter Verschiedenheitsbedingungen]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi)) \vdash \forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall y \psi
}{cbvalivw}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{spimvw}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall y \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alrimiv}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\section{Axiomenschema \texttt{ax-7} und Gleichheit}

\FormulaAxiom[Axiom der Gleichheit]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (x = z \rightarrow y = z))
}{ax-7}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}

\FormulaThm[Geschwächte Form von \FormulaRefAuto{ax-7}]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (x = z \rightarrow y = z))
}{ax7v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (x = z \rightarrow y = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-7}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Weitere geschwächte Form von \FormulaRefAuto{ax7v}]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (x = z \rightarrow y = z))
}{ax7v1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (x = z \rightarrow y = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax7v}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Weitere geschwächte Form von \FormulaRefAuto{ax7v}]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (x = z \rightarrow y = z))
}{ax7v2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (x = z \rightarrow y = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax7v}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Reflexivität der Gleichheit]{%
  x = x
}{equid}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(y = x \rightarrow (y = x \rightarrow x = x))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax7v1}}
  \proofstep{}{(y = x \rightarrow x = x)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.43i}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{\exists y\, y = x}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax6ev}}
  \proofstep{}{x = x}{\FormulaRefAuto{exlimiiv}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Nichtfreiheitsaussage zur Reflexivität]{%
  \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} y\, x = x
}{nfequid}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{x = x}{\FormulaRefAuto{equid}}
  \proofstep{}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} y\, x = x}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfth}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Symmetrie der Gleichheit]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow y = x)
}{equcomiv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{x = x}{\FormulaRefAuto{equid}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (x = x \rightarrow y = x))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax7v2}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow y = x)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Existenz in umgekehrter Gleichheitsrichtung]{%
  \exists x\, y = x
}{ax6evr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\exists x\, x = y}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax6ev}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow y = x)}{\FormulaRefAuto{equcomiv}}
  \proofstep{}{\exists x\, y = x}{\FormulaRefAuto{eximii}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Herleitung von \FormulaRefAuto{ax-7} aus den geschwächten Formen]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (x = z \rightarrow y = z))
}{ax7}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep t}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = t \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow t = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax7v2}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = t \rightarrow (x = z \rightarrow t = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax7v2}}
  \proofstep{}{(t = y \rightarrow (t = z \rightarrow y = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax7v1}}
  \proofstep{}{((t = y \land t = z) \rightarrow y = z)}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = t \rightarrow ((t = y \land t = z) \rightarrow y = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{4}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = t \rightarrow ((x = y \land x = z) \rightarrow y = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl2and}{1,2,5}}
  \proofstep{}{\exists t\, x = t}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax6evr}}
  \proofstep{}{((x = y \land x = z) \rightarrow y = z)}{\FormulaRefAuto{exlimiiv}{6,7}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (x = z \rightarrow y = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{8}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{ax7} zeigt, dass die beiden geschwächten Fassungen \FormulaRefAuto{ax7v1} und \FormulaRefAuto{ax7v2} zusammen bereits genügen, um das volle Gleichheitsaxiom wiederzugewinnen.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Symmetrie der Gleichheit]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow y = x)
}{equcomi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{x = x}{\FormulaRefAuto{equid}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (x = x \rightarrow y = x))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax7}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow y = x)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Symmetrie der Gleichheit als Äquivalenz]{%
  (x = y \leftrightarrow y = x)
}{equcom}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow y = x)}{\FormulaRefAuto{equcomi}}
  \proofstep{}{(y = x \rightarrow x = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{equcomi}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \leftrightarrow y = x)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktionsform der Symmetrie]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow x = y) \vdash \phi \rightarrow y = x
}{equcomd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow x = y)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \leftrightarrow y = x)}{\FormulaRefAuto{equcom}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow y = x)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylib}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Syllogismus mit symmetrisch gewendeter Gleichheit]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow \phi) \vdash y = x \rightarrow \phi
}{equcoms}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(y = x \rightarrow x = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{equcomi}}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow \phi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(y = x \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Transitivität der Gleichheit]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (y = z \rightarrow x = z))
}{equtr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(y = x \rightarrow (y = z \rightarrow x = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax7}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (y = z \rightarrow x = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equcoms}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Transitivität mit vertauschter linker Gleichheit]{%

  \proofstep{}{(\forall y \psi \rightarrow \forall x \forall y \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-5}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \phi \rightarrow \forall y \neg \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-5}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall y \psi \rightarrow \forall x \neg \forall y \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-5}}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\neg \forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \neg \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{hbn1fw}{1,2,3,4,5,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Schwache Form von \FormulaRefAuto{hba1}]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)) \vdash \forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \forall x \phi
}{hba1w}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \phi \leftrightarrow \forall y \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{cbvalvw}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\neg \forall x \phi \leftrightarrow \neg \forall y \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{notbii}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\neg \forall x \phi \leftrightarrow \neg \forall y \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{3}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \neg \forall x \phi \rightarrow \neg \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{spw}{4}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \neg \forall x \neg \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con2i}{5}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\neg \forall x \neg \forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \neg \forall x \neg \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{hbn1w}{4}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\neg \forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \neg \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{hbn1w}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\neg \forall x \neg \forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con1i}{8}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \neg \forall x \neg \forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alimi}{9}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3syl}{6,7,10}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Schwache Form von \FormulaRefAuto{hbe1}]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)) \vdash \exists x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \exists x \phi
}{hbe1w}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \phi \leftrightarrow \neg \forall x \neg \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-ex}}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\neg \phi \leftrightarrow \neg \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{notbid}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\neg \forall x \neg \phi \rightarrow \forall x \neg \forall x \neg \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{hbn1w}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\exists x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{hbxfrbi}{1,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Schwache Form von \FormulaRefAuto{hbal}]{%
  (x = z \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)) \dsep (\phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi) \vdash \forall y \phi \rightarrow \forall x \forall y \phi
}{hbalw}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{2}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(\forall y \phi \rightarrow \forall y \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alimi}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = z \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall y \forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \forall y \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alcomimw}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(\forall y \phi \rightarrow \forall x \forall y \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Schwache Form von Margaris 19.8a]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)) \vdash \phi \rightarrow \exists x \phi
}{19.8aw}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \neg \phi \leftrightarrow \neg \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alnex}}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\neg \phi \leftrightarrow \neg \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{notbid}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \neg \phi \rightarrow \neg \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{spw}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\neg \exists x \phi \rightarrow \neg \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylbir}{1,4}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con4i}{5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Idempotenz der Existenz unter schwachen Bedingungen]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)) \vdash \exists x \phi \leftrightarrow \exists x \exists x \phi
}{exexw}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\neg \phi \leftrightarrow \neg \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{notbid}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \neg \phi \rightarrow \forall x \forall x \neg \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{hba1w}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \neg \phi \rightarrow \neg \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{spw}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \forall x \neg \phi \rightarrow \forall x \neg \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alimi}{4}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \neg \phi \leftrightarrow \forall x \forall x \neg \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{3,5}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\neg \forall x \neg \phi \leftrightarrow \neg \forall x \forall x \neg \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{notbii}{6}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \phi \leftrightarrow \neg \forall x \neg \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-ex}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists x \phi \leftrightarrow \neg \forall x \forall x \neg \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{2exnaln}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\exists x \phi \leftrightarrow \exists x \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4i}{7,8,9}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Spezialisierung der allgemeinen Reflexivität]{%
  (\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow x = y)
}{spaev}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = z \rightarrow (x = y \leftrightarrow z = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equequ1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow x = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{spw}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Änderung gebundener Variablen in einer Gleichheit]{%
  (\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \forall z\, z = y)
}{cbvaev}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep t}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = t \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow t = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax7}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \forall t\, t = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{cbvalivw}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(t = z \rightarrow (t = y \rightarrow z = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax7}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall t\, t = y \rightarrow \forall z\, z = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{cbvalivw}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \forall z\, z = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Hilfssatz zu \FormulaRefAuto{aevlem}]{%
  (\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \forall z\, z = x)
}{aevlem0}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow x = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{spaev}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \forall z\, x = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alrimiv}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \forall z\, z = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{cbvaev}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (z = y \rightarrow z = x))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equeuclr}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall z\, x = y \rightarrow (\forall z\, z = y \rightarrow \forall z\, z = x))}{\FormulaRefAuto{al2imi}{4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \forall z\, z = x)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylc}{2,3,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Änderung freier und gebundener Variablen]{%
  (\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \forall z\, z = t)
}{aevlem}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep t\dsep u}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \forall u\, u = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{cbvaev}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall u\, u = y \rightarrow \forall x\, x = u)}{\FormulaRefAuto{aevlem0}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, x = u \rightarrow \forall t\, t = u)}{\FormulaRefAuto{cbvaev}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall t\, t = u \rightarrow \forall z\, z = t)}{\FormulaRefAuto{aevlem0}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \forall z\, z = t)}{\FormulaRefAuto{4syl}{1,2,3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Ein-Objekt-Universum erzwingt Gleichheit aller Objekte]{%
  (\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow z = t)
}{aeveq}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep t\dsep u}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \forall u\, u = z)}{\FormulaRefAuto{aevlem}}
  \proofstep{}{\exists u\, u = t}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax6ev}}
  \proofstep{}{(u = z \rightarrow (u = t \rightarrow z = t))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax7}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall u\, u = z \rightarrow (\exists u\, u = t \rightarrow \exists u\, z = t))}{\FormulaRefAuto{aleximi}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall u\, u = z \rightarrow \exists u\, z = t)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpi}{2,4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists u\, z = t \rightarrow z = t)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax5e}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow z = t)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3syl}{1,5,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Distinktor-Elimination]{%
  (\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \forall z\, t = u)
}{aev}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep t\dsep u\dsep v\dsep w}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \forall v\, v = w)}{\FormulaRefAuto{aevlem}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall v\, v = w \rightarrow t = u)}{\FormulaRefAuto{aeveq}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall v\, v = w \rightarrow \forall z\, t = u)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alrimiv}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \forall z\, t = u)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Zweifache Distinktor-Elimination]{%
  (\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \forall z \forall t\, u = v)
}{aev2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep t\dsep u\dsep v\dsep w\dsep s}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \forall w\, w = s)}{\FormulaRefAuto{aev}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall w\, w = s \rightarrow \forall t\, u = v)}{\FormulaRefAuto{aev}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall w\, w = s \rightarrow \forall z \forall t\, u = v)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alrimiv}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \forall z \forall t\, u = v)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Alle Variablen sind in einem Identitätsspezifizierer effektiv gebunden]{%
  (\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \forall z \forall x\, x = y)
}{hbaev}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \forall z \forall x\, x = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{aev2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Nichtidentität eines Ein-Objekt-Universums ist unmöglich]{%
  (\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \neg \forall u\, u = v)
}{naev}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep u\dsep v}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall u\, u = v \rightarrow \forall x\, x = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{aev}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \neg \forall u\, u = v)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con3i}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Verallgemeinerte Negationsform der Distinktor-Elimination]{%
  (\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \forall z\, \neg \forall t\, t = u)
}{naev2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep t\dsep u\dsep v\dsep w}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \neg \forall v\, v = w)}{\FormulaRefAuto{naev}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall v\, v = w \rightarrow \forall z\, \neg \forall v\, v = w)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-5}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall v\, v = w \rightarrow \neg \forall t\, t = u)}{\FormulaRefAuto{naev}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall z\, \neg \forall v\, v = w \rightarrow \forall z\, \neg \forall t\, t = u)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alimi}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \forall z\, \neg \forall t\, t = u)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3syl}{1,2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Jede Variable ist in \(\neg \forall x\, x = y\) frei]{%
  (\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \forall z\, \neg \forall x\, x = y)
}{hbnaev}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \forall z\, \neg \forall x\, x = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{naev2}}
\end{tabproof}

\section{Eigentliche Substitution}

\FormulaThm[Rechtfertigung der Definition der eigentlichen Substitution]{%
  (\forall y(y = t \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)) \leftrightarrow \forall z(z = t \rightarrow \forall x(x = z \rightarrow \phi)))
}{sbjust}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep t}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(y = z \rightarrow (y = t \leftrightarrow z = t))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equequ1}}
  \proofstep{}{(y = z \rightarrow (x = y \leftrightarrow x = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equequ2}}
  \proofstep{}{(y = z \rightarrow ((x = y \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow (x = z \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi1d}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(y = z \rightarrow (\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow \forall x(x = z \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albidv}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{(y = z \rightarrow ((y = t \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)) \leftrightarrow (z = t \rightarrow \forall x(x = z \rightarrow \phi))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi12d}{1,4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y(y = t \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)) \leftrightarrow \forall z(z = t \rightarrow \forall x(x = z \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{cbvalvw}{5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaDef[Eigentliche Substitution]{%
  ([t / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall y(y = t \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))
}{df-sb}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep t}%
}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{sbjust} zeigt, dass in der Definition \FormulaRefAuto{df-sb} die Wahl der Hilfsvariablen \(y\) keine Rolle spielt. Genau dies ist der inhaltliche Kern der Metamath-Rechtfertigung von \FormulaRefAuto{df-sb}.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Vereinfachte Form von \FormulaRefAuto{df-sb}]{%
  ([t / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall y(y = t \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))
}{dfsb}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep t\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y(y = t \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)) \leftrightarrow \forall z(z = t \rightarrow \forall x(x = z \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbjust}}
  \proofstep{}{([t / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall y(y = t \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-sb}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaDef[Eigentliche Substitution als Bildungsregel]{%
  \text{Ist } \phi \text{ eine wohlgeformte Formel und sind } x,t \text{ Variablen, dann ist auch } [t / x]\phi \text{ eine wohlgeformte Formel.}%
}{wsb}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep t}%
}

\FormulaThm[Hilfssatz zu \FormulaRefAuto{sbt}]{%
  \phi \vdash \forall y(y = t \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi))
}{sbtlem}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep t}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-gen}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{(y = t \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{3}}
  \proofstep{1}{\forall y(y = t \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-gen}{4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Substitution in einen Satz liefert wieder einen Satz]{%
  \phi \vdash [t / x]\phi
}{sbt}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep t}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\forall y(y = t \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbtlem}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{\forall z(z = t \rightarrow \forall x(x = z \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbtlem}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall y(y = t \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)) \leftrightarrow \forall z(z = t \rightarrow \forall x(x = z \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{2th}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{1}{([t / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall y(y = t \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-sb}}
  \proofstep{1}{[t / x]\phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbir}{2,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Substitution in \(\top\)]{%
  [y / x]\top
}{sbtru}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\top}{\FormulaRefAuto{tru}}
  \proofstep{}{[y / x]\top}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbt}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Spezialisierungsaxiom des Standardprädikatenkalküls]{%
  (\forall x \phi \rightarrow [t / x]\phi)
}{stdpc4}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep t}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ala1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow (y = t \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1d}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall y(y = t \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alrimiv}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{([t / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall y(y = t \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-sb}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow [t / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylibr}{3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{stdpc4} ist die explizite Substitutionsfassung des klassischen Spezialisierungsaxioms: aus \(\forall x\,\phi\) folgt der durch Einsetzen von \(t\) gewonnene Spezialfall.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Alternative Herleitung von \FormulaRefAuto{sbt}]{%
  \phi \vdash [y / x]\phi
}{sbtALT}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow [y / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{stdpc4}}
  \proofstep{1}{\phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{[y / x]\phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpg}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Doppelte Spezialisierung mittels expliziter Substitution]{%
  (\forall x \forall y \phi \rightarrow [z / x][w / y]\phi)
}{2stdpc4}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep w}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y \phi \rightarrow [w / y]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{stdpc4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \forall y \phi \rightarrow \forall x [w / y]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alimi}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x [w / y]\phi \rightarrow [z / x][w / y]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{stdpc4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \forall y \phi \rightarrow [z / x][w / y]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Substitution verteilt sich über Implikation]{%
  ([y / x](\phi \rightarrow \psi) \rightarrow ([y / x]\phi \rightarrow [y / x]\psi))
}{sbi1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x](\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow \forall z(z = y \rightarrow \forall x(x = z \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{dfsb}}
  \proofstep{}{((x = z \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi)) \rightarrow ((x = z \rightarrow \phi) \rightarrow (x = z \rightarrow \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(x = z \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi)) \rightarrow (\forall x(x = z \rightarrow \phi) \rightarrow \forall x(x = z \rightarrow \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{al2imi}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{((z = y \rightarrow \forall x(x = z \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))) \rightarrow ((z = y \rightarrow \forall x(x = z \rightarrow \phi)) \rightarrow (z = y \rightarrow \forall x(x = z \rightarrow \psi))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imim3i}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall z(z = y \rightarrow \forall x(x = z \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))) \rightarrow (\forall z(z = y \rightarrow \forall x(x = z \rightarrow \phi)) \rightarrow \forall z(z = y \rightarrow \forall x(x = z \rightarrow \psi))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{al2imi}{4}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall z(z = y \rightarrow \forall x(x = z \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{dfsb}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x]\psi \leftrightarrow \forall z(z = y \rightarrow \forall x(x = z \rightarrow \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{dfsb}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall z(z = y \rightarrow \forall x(x = z \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))) \rightarrow ([y / x]\phi \rightarrow [y / x]\psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3imtr4g}{5,6,7}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x](\phi \rightarrow \psi) \rightarrow ([y / x]\phi \rightarrow [y / x]\psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylbi}{1,8}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Spezialisierung einer Implikation]{%
  (\forall x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \rightarrow ([t / x]\phi \rightarrow [t / x]\psi))
}{spsbim}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep t}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \rightarrow [t / x](\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{stdpc4}}
  \proofstep{}{([t / x](\phi \rightarrow \psi) \rightarrow ([t / x]\phi \rightarrow [t / x]\psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbi1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \rightarrow ([t / x]\phi \rightarrow [t / x]\psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Spezialisierung einer Äquivalenz]{%
  (\forall x(\phi \leftrightarrow \psi) \rightarrow ([t / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [t / x]\psi))
}{spsbbi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep t}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((\phi \leftrightarrow \psi) \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimp}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \leftrightarrow \psi) \rightarrow \forall x(\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alimi}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \rightarrow ([t / x]\phi \rightarrow [t / x]\psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{spsbim}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \leftrightarrow \psi) \rightarrow ([t / x]\phi \rightarrow [t / x]\psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{}{((\phi \leftrightarrow \psi) \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpr}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \leftrightarrow \psi) \rightarrow \forall x(\psi \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alimi}{5}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\psi \rightarrow \phi) \rightarrow ([t / x]\psi \rightarrow [t / x]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{spsbim}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \leftrightarrow \psi) \rightarrow ([t / x]\psi \rightarrow [t / x]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{6,7}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \leftrightarrow \psi) \rightarrow ([t / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [t / x]\psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid}{4,8}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Substitution verteilt sich über Implikation]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow \psi) \vdash [t / x]\phi \rightarrow [t / x]\psi
}{sbimi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep t}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{[t / x](\phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbt}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{([t / x](\phi \rightarrow \psi) \rightarrow ([t / x]\phi \rightarrow [t / x]\psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbi1}}
  \proofstep{1}{([t / x]\phi \rightarrow [t / x]\psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Substitution verteilt sich über verschachtelte Implikation]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \chi)) \vdash [t / x]\phi \rightarrow ([t / x]\psi \rightarrow [t / x]\chi)
}{sb2imi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi\dsep\chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep t}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \chi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{([t / x]\phi \rightarrow [t / x](\psi \rightarrow \chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbimi}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{([t / x](\psi \rightarrow \chi) \rightarrow ([t / x]\psi \rightarrow [t / x]\chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbi1}}
  \proofstep{1}{([t / x]\phi \rightarrow ([t / x]\psi \rightarrow [t / x]\chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Substitution in beide Seiten einer Äquivalenz]{%
  (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi) \vdash [t / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [t / x]\psi
}{sbbii}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep t}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{([t / x]\phi \rightarrow [t / x]\psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbimi}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\psi \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpri}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{([t / x]\psi \rightarrow [t / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbimi}{4}}
  \proofstep{1}{([t / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [t / x]\psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{3,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Doppelte Substitution in beide Seiten einer Äquivalenz]{%
  (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi) \vdash [t / x][u / y]\phi \leftrightarrow [t / x][u / y]\psi
}{2sbbii}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep t\dsep u}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{([u / y]\phi \leftrightarrow [u / y]\psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbbii}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{([t / x][u / y]\phi \leftrightarrow [t / x][u / y]\psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbbii}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktionsform für Substitution in beide Seiten einer Implikation]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \chi)) \vdash \phi \rightarrow ([t / x]\psi \rightarrow [t / x]\chi)
}{sbimdv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi\dsep\chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep t}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \chi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall x(\psi \rightarrow \chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alrimiv}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\psi \rightarrow \chi) \rightarrow ([t / x]\psi \rightarrow [t / x]\chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{spsbim}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow ([t / x]\psi \rightarrow [t / x]\chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktionsform für Substitution in beide Seiten einer Äquivalenz]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi)) \vdash \phi \rightarrow ([t / x]\psi \leftrightarrow [t / x]\chi)
}{sbbidv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi\dsep\chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep t}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall x(\psi \leftrightarrow \chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alrimiv}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\psi \leftrightarrow \chi) \rightarrow ([t / x]\psi \leftrightarrow [t / x]\chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{spsbbi}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow ([t / x]\psi \leftrightarrow [t / x]\chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Substitution verteilt sich über Konjunktion]{%
  ([y / x](\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow ([y / x]\phi \land [y / x]\psi))
}{sban}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((\phi \land \psi) \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpl}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x](\phi \land \psi) \rightarrow [y / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbimi}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{((\phi \land \psi) \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{simpr}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x](\phi \land \psi) \rightarrow [y / x]\psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbimi}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x](\phi \land \psi) \rightarrow ([y / x]\phi \land [y / x]\psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{jca}{2,4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow (\phi \land \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm3.2}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x]\phi \rightarrow ([y / x]\psi \rightarrow [y / x](\phi \land \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sb2imi}{6}}
  \proofstep{}{(([y / x]\phi \land [y / x]\psi) \rightarrow [y / x](\phi \land \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{7}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x](\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow ([y / x]\phi \land [y / x]\psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{5,8}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Substitution verteilt sich über dreifache Konjunktion]{%
  ([y / x](\phi \land \psi \land \chi) \leftrightarrow ([y / x]\phi \land [y / x]\psi \land [y / x]\chi))
}{sb3an}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi\dsep\chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x](\phi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow ([y / x]\phi \land [y / x]\psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sban}}
  \proofstep{}{(([y / x](\phi \land \psi) \land [y / x]\chi) \leftrightarrow (([y / x]\phi \land [y / x]\psi) \land [y / x]\chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anbi1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{((\phi \land \psi \land \chi) \leftrightarrow ((\phi \land \psi) \land \chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-3an}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x](\phi \land \psi \land \chi) \leftrightarrow [y / x]((\phi \land \psi) \land \chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbbii}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x]((\phi \land \psi) \land \chi) \leftrightarrow ([y / x](\phi \land \psi) \land [y / x]\chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sban}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x](\phi \land \psi \land \chi) \leftrightarrow ([y / x](\phi \land \psi) \land [y / x]\chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{4,5}}
  \proofstep{}{(([y / x]\phi \land [y / x]\psi \land [y / x]\chi) \leftrightarrow (([y / x]\phi \land [y / x]\psi) \land [y / x]\chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-3an}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x](\phi \land \psi \land \chi) \leftrightarrow ([y / x]\phi \land [y / x]\psi \land [y / x]\chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4i}{2,6,7}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Existenzeinführung aus einer substituierten Instanz]{%
  ([t / x]\phi \rightarrow \exists x \phi)
}{spsbe}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep t}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{([t / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall y(y = t \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{dfsb}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y(y = t \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)) \rightarrow \exists y \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alequexv}}
  \proofstep{}{([t / x]\phi \rightarrow \exists y \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylbi}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists y \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi) \rightarrow \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{exsbim}}
  \proofstep{}{([t / x]\phi \rightarrow \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Gleichheitseigenschaft der expliziten Substitution]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow ([x / z]\phi \leftrightarrow [y / z]\phi))
}{sbequ}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep u}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (u = x \leftrightarrow u = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equequ2}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow ((u = x \rightarrow \forall z(z = u \rightarrow \phi)) \leftrightarrow (u = y \rightarrow \forall z(z = u \rightarrow \phi))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi1d}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (\forall u(u = x \rightarrow \forall z(z = u \rightarrow \phi)) \leftrightarrow \forall u(u = y \rightarrow \forall z(z = u \rightarrow \phi))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albidv}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{([x / z]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall u(u = x \rightarrow \forall z(z = u \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{dfsb}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / z]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall u(u = y \rightarrow \forall z(z = u \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{dfsb}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow ([x / z]\phi \leftrightarrow [y / z]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4g}{3,4,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Einseitige Gleichheitseigenschaft der expliziten Substitution]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow ([x / z]\phi \rightarrow [y / z]\phi))
}{sbequi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow ([x / z]\phi \leftrightarrow [y / z]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbequ}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow ([x / z]\phi \rightarrow [y / z]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpd}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Alternative Definition der Substitution für disjunkte Variablen]{%
  ([t / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall x(x = t \rightarrow \phi))
}{sb6}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep t}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{([t / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall y(y = t \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-sb}}
  \proofstep{}{(y = t \rightarrow (x = y \leftrightarrow x = t))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equequ2}}
  \proofstep{}{(y = t \rightarrow ((x = y \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow (x = t \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi1d}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(y = t \rightarrow (\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow \forall x(x = t \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albidv}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y(y = t \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)) \leftrightarrow \forall x(x = t \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equsalvw}{4}}
  \proofstep{}{([t / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall x(x = t \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{1,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{sb6} ist die gebräuchlichste Kurzform der expliziten Substitution: bei disjunkten Variablen reduziert sich die allgemeine Definition auf einen einzigen Allquantor mit Gleichheitsvordersatz.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Doppelte Substitution]{%
  ([z / x][w / y]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall x \forall y((x = z \land y = w) \rightarrow \phi))
}{2sb6}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep w}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{([z / x][w / y]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall x(x = z \rightarrow [w / y]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sb6}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y(x = z \rightarrow (y = w \rightarrow \phi)) \leftrightarrow (x = z \rightarrow \forall y(y = w \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.21v}}
  \proofstep{}{(((x = z \land y = w) \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow (x = z \rightarrow (y = w \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impexp}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y((x = z \land y = w) \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow \forall y(x = z \rightarrow (y = w \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{([w / y]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall y(y = w \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sb6}}
  \proofstep{}{((x = z \rightarrow [w / y]\phi) \leftrightarrow (x = z \rightarrow \forall y(y = w \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi2i}{5}}
  \proofstep{}{((x = z \rightarrow [w / y]\phi) \leftrightarrow \forall y((x = z \land y = w) \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4ri}{2,4,6}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(x = z \rightarrow [w / y]\phi) \leftrightarrow \forall x \forall y((x = z \land y = w) \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{7}}
  \proofstep{}{([z / x][w / y]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall x \forall y((x = z \land y = w) \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{1,8}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Einseitige Form von \FormulaRefAuto{sb5}]{%
  ([y / x]\phi \rightarrow \exists x(x = y \land \phi))
}{sb1v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sb6}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi) \rightarrow \exists x(x = y \land \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equs4v}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x]\phi \rightarrow \exists x(x = y \land \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylbi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Substitution in variablenfreier Formel]{%
  ([t / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \phi)
}{sbv}{%
