  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep t}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{([t / x]\phi \rightarrow \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{spsbe}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \phi \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax5e}}
  \proofstep{}{([t / x]\phi \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-5}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow [t / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{stdpc4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow [t / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{4,5}}
  \proofstep{}{([t / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{3,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Vertauschungsgesetz für Substitution]{%
  ([w / x][y / z]\phi \leftrightarrow [y / x][w / z]\phi)
}{sbcom4}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{w\dsep x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{([w / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbv}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / z]\phi \leftrightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbv}}
  \proofstep{}{([w / x][y / z]\phi \leftrightarrow [w / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbbii}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{([w / z]\phi \leftrightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbv}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x][w / z]\phi \leftrightarrow [y / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbbii}{4}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbv}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x][w / z]\phi \leftrightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{5,6}}
  \proofstep{}{([w / x][y / z]\phi \leftrightarrow [y / x][w / z]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4i}{1,3,7}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Axiom *11.07 von Whitehead-Russell]{%
  \phi \vdash \phi
}{pm11.07}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\phi}{\rA}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Substitution trifft bei variablenfreiem Antezedens nur das Konsequens]{%
  ([y / x](\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \rightarrow [y / x]\psi))
}{sbrimvw}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x](\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sb6}}
  \proofstep{}{((\phi \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow \psi)) \leftrightarrow (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bi2.04}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow \psi)) \leftrightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow \psi)) \leftrightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.21v}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x](\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr2i}{1,3,4}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x]\psi \leftrightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sb6}}
  \proofstep{}{((\phi \rightarrow [y / x]\psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi2i}{6}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x](\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \rightarrow [y / x]\psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr4i}{5,7}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Äquivalenz zweier substituierter Formeln unter der Zusatzannahme \(x=t\)]{%
  (x = t \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)) \vdash [t / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [t / x]\psi
}{sbbiiev}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep t}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = t \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{((x = t \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow (x = t \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74i}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x(x = t \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow \forall x(x = t \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{([t / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall x(x = t \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sb6}}
  \proofstep{}{([t / x]\psi \leftrightarrow \forall x(x = t \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sb6}}
  \proofstep{1}{([t / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [t / x]\psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4i}{3,4,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Übergang von impliziter zu expliziter Substitution]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)) \vdash [y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \psi
}{sbievw}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [y / x]\psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbbiiev}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x]\psi \leftrightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbv}}
  \proofstep{1}{([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Veraltete Fassung von \FormulaRefAuto{sbievw}]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)) \vdash [y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \psi
}{sbievwOLD}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sb6}}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{equsalvw}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Umwandlung impliziter in explizite Substitution in Deduktionsform]{%
  ((\phi \land x = y) \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi))
  \vdash
  (\phi \rightarrow ([y / x]\psi \leftrightarrow \chi))
}{sbiedvw}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi\dsep\chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x](\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \rightarrow [y / x]\psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbrimvw}}
  \proofstep{1}{((\phi \land x = y) \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{expcom}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow ((\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \chi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74d}{3}}
  \proofstep{1}{([y / x](\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbievw}{4}}
  \proofstep{1}{((\phi \rightarrow [y / x]\psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr3i}{1,5}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow ([y / x]\psi \leftrightarrow \chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74ri}{6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Doppelte implizite Substitution als explizite Substitution]{%
  ((x = t \land y = u) \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))
  \vdash
  ([t / x][u / y]\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)
}{2sbievw}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep t\dsep u}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{((x = t \land y = u) \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = t \rightarrow ([u / y]\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbiedvw}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{([t / x][u / y]\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbievw}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Substitution von \(y\) für \(x\) und danach von \(z\) für \(y\)]{%
  ([z / y][y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [z / y][z / x]\phi)
}{sbcom3vv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(y = z \rightarrow ([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [z / x]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbequ}}
  \proofstep{}{([z / y][y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [z / y][z / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbbiiev}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Zweimalige Anwendung von \FormulaRefAuto{sbievw}]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &(x = w \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \chi))
    \dsep
    (w = y \rightarrow (\chi \leftrightarrow \psi))
    \vdash{}\\
    &\quad ([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)
  \end{aligned}
}{sbievw2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi\dsep\chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep w}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{([y / w][w / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [y / w][y / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbcom3vv}}
  \proofstep{2}{(x = w \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \chi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{([w / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbievw}{2}}
  \proofstep{2}{([y / w][w / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [y / w]\chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbbii}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / w][y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [y / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbv}}
  \proofstep{2}{([y / w]\chi \leftrightarrow [y / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr3i}{1,4,5}}
  \proofstep{1}{(w = y \rightarrow (\chi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{([y / w]\chi \leftrightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbievw}{7}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [y / w]\chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{6}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{9,8}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Kompositionsgesetz für Substitution]{%
  ([y / z][z / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [y / x]\phi)
}{sbco2vv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep w}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(z = w \rightarrow ([z / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [w / x]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbequ}}
  \proofstep{}{(w = y \rightarrow ([w / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [y / x]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbequ}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / z][z / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [y / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbievw2}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Änderung der ersetzten Variablen in implizit verknüpften Formeln]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))
  \vdash
  ([z / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [z / y]\psi)
}{cbvsbv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{([z / y][y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [z / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbco2vv}}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbievw}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{([z / y][y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [z / y]\psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbbii}{3}}
  \proofstep{1}{([z / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [z / y]\psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr3i}{1,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Hilfssatz zum Austausch zweier Variablen]{%
  ([x / v][y / x][v / y]\phi \leftrightarrow [x / w][y / x][w / y]\phi)
}{sbco4lem}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{v\dsep w\dsep x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(v = w \rightarrow ([v / y]\phi \leftrightarrow [w / y]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbequ}}
  \proofstep{}{(v = w \rightarrow ([y / x][v / y]\phi \leftrightarrow [y / x][w / y]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbbidv}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{([x / v][y / x][v / y]\phi \leftrightarrow [x / w][y / x][w / y]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{cbvsbv}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Zwei Arten, zwei Variablen auszutauschen]{%
  ([y / u][x / v][u / x][v / y]\phi \leftrightarrow [x / w][y / x][w / y]\phi)
}{sbco4}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{u\dsep v\dsep w\dsep x\dsep y\dsep t}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(u = y \rightarrow ([u / x][v / y]\phi \leftrightarrow [y / x][v / y]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbequ}}
  \proofstep{}{(u = y \rightarrow ([x / v][u / x][v / y]\phi \leftrightarrow [x / v][y / x][v / y]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbbidv}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / u][x / v][u / x][v / y]\phi \leftrightarrow [x / v][y / x][v / y]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbievw}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{([x / v][y / x][v / y]\phi \leftrightarrow [x / t][y / x][t / y]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbco4lem}}
  \proofstep{}{([x / t][y / x][t / y]\phi \leftrightarrow [x / w][y / x][w / y]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbco4lem}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / u][x / v][u / x][v / y]\phi \leftrightarrow [x / w][y / x][w / y]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitri}{3,4,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Substitution in einer Gleichung]{%
  ([y / x]x = z \leftrightarrow y = z)
}{equsb3}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep w}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = w \rightarrow (x = z \leftrightarrow w = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equequ1}}
  \proofstep{}{(w = y \rightarrow (w = z \leftrightarrow y = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equequ1}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x]x = z \leftrightarrow y = z)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbievw2}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Substitution in einer Gleichung, rechte Seite]{%
  ([y / x]z = x \leftrightarrow z = y)
}{equsb3r}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep w}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = w \rightarrow (z = x \leftrightarrow z = w))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equequ2}}
  \proofstep{}{(w = y \rightarrow (z = w \leftrightarrow z = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equequ2}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x]z = x \leftrightarrow z = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbievw2}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Substitution in einer atomaren Gleichungsformel]{%
  [y / x]x = y
}{equsb1v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{y = y}{\FormulaRefAuto{equid}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x]x = y \leftrightarrow y = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{equsb3}}
  \proofstep{}{[y / x]x = y}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbir}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Jede Substitution in einer stets falschen Formel bleibt falsch]{%
  (\forall x \neg \phi \rightarrow \neg [t / x]\phi)
}{nsb}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep t}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \neg \phi \leftrightarrow \neg \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alnex}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \neg \phi \rightarrow \neg \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{([t / x]\phi \rightarrow \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{spsbe}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \neg \phi \rightarrow \neg [t / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nsyl}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Eine Richtung von \FormulaRefAuto{sbn}]{%
  ([t / x]\neg \phi \rightarrow \neg [t / x]\phi)
}{sbn1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep t}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \neg \bot \rightarrow \neg [t / x]\bot)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nsb}}
  \proofstep{}{\neg \bot}{\FormulaRefAuto{fal}}
  \proofstep{}{\neg [t / x]\bot}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpg}{2,1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \phi \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \bot))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.21}}
  \proofstep{}{([t / x]\neg \phi \rightarrow ([t / x]\phi \rightarrow [t / x]\bot))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sb2imi}{4}}
  \proofstep{}{([t / x]\neg \phi \rightarrow \neg [t / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mtoi}{3,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\section{Mitgliedschaftsprädikat}

\FormulaDef[Mitgliedschaftsverknüpfung für Klassen]{%
  \text{Wenn } A \text{ und } B \text{ Klassen sind, dann ist } A \in B \text{ eine wff.}%
}{wcel}{%
  \DeltaRow{Klassen}{A\dsep B}%
}

\FormulaDef[Atomare Formel mit dem Mitgliedschaftsprädikat]{%
  \text{Wenn } x \text{ und } y \text{ Variablen sind, dann ist } x \in y \text{ eine wff.}%
}{wel}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}

\section{Axiomenschema \texttt{ax-8} (Linke Gleichheit für ein binäres Prädikat)}

\FormulaAxiom[Axiom der linken Gleichheit für ein binäres Prädikat]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (x \in z \rightarrow y \in z))
}{ax-8}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}

\FormulaThm[Geschwächte Form von \FormulaRefAuto{ax-8}]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (x \in z \rightarrow y \in z))
}{ax8v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (x \in z \rightarrow y \in z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-8}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Erste weiter abgeschwächte Form von \FormulaRefAuto{ax8v}]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (x \in z \rightarrow y \in z))
}{ax8v1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (x \in z \rightarrow y \in z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax8v}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Zweite weiter abgeschwächte Form von \FormulaRefAuto{ax8v}]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (x \in z \rightarrow y \in z))
}{ax8v2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (x \in z \rightarrow y \in z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax8v}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Herleitung von \FormulaRefAuto{ax-8} aus den beiden weiter abgeschwächten Formen]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (x \in z \rightarrow y \in z))
}{ax8}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep t}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \leftrightarrow \exists t(t = x \land t = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equvinv}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = t \rightarrow (x \in z \rightarrow t \in z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax8v2}}
  \proofstep{}{(t = x \rightarrow (x \in z \rightarrow t \in z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equcoms}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(t = y \rightarrow (t \in z \rightarrow y \in z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax8v1}}
  \proofstep{}{((t = x \land t = y) \rightarrow (x \in z \rightarrow y \in z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan9}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists t(t = x \land t = y) \rightarrow (x \in z \rightarrow y \in z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exlimiv}{5}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (x \in z \rightarrow y \in z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylbi}{1,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Identitätsgesetz für das erste Argument des Mitgliedschaftsprädikats]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (x \in z \leftrightarrow y \in z))
}{elequ1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (x \in z \rightarrow y \in z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax8}}
  \proofstep{}{(y = x \rightarrow (y \in z \rightarrow x \in z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax8}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (y \in z \rightarrow x \in z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equcoms}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (x \in z \leftrightarrow y \in z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Substitution im ersten Argument des Mitgliedschaftsprädikats]{%
  ([y / x]x \in z \leftrightarrow y \in z)
}{elsb1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep w}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = w \rightarrow (x \in z \leftrightarrow w \in z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{elequ1}}
  \proofstep{}{(w = y \rightarrow (w \in z \leftrightarrow y \in z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{elequ1}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x]x \in z \leftrightarrow y \in z)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbievw2}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Rechtfertigung der auf Variablen eingeschränkten Klassenmitgliedschaft]{%
  (x \in y \leftrightarrow \exists z(z = x \land z \in y))
}{cleljust}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(z = x \rightarrow (z \in y \leftrightarrow x \in y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{elequ1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists z(z = x \land z \in y) \leftrightarrow x \in y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{equsexvw}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(x \in y \leftrightarrow \exists z(z = x \land z \in y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\section{Axiomenschema \texttt{ax-9} (Rechte Gleichheit für ein binäres Prädikat)}

\FormulaAxiom[Axiom der rechten Gleichheit für ein binäres Prädikat]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (z \in x \rightarrow z \in y))
}{ax-9}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}

\FormulaThm[Geschwächte Form von \FormulaRefAuto{ax-9}]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (z \in x \rightarrow z \in y))
}{ax9v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (z \in x \rightarrow z \in y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-9}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Erste weiter abgeschwächte Form von \FormulaRefAuto{ax9v}]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (z \in x \rightarrow z \in y))
}{ax9v1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (z \in x \rightarrow z \in y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax9v}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Zweite weiter abgeschwächte Form von \FormulaRefAuto{ax9v}]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (z \in x \rightarrow z \in y))
}{ax9v2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (z \in x \rightarrow z \in y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax9v}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Herleitung von \FormulaRefAuto{ax-9} aus den beiden weiter abgeschwächten Formen]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (z \in x \rightarrow z \in y))
}{ax9}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep t}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \leftrightarrow \exists t(t = x \land t = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equvinv}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = t \rightarrow (z \in x \rightarrow z \in t))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax9v2}}
  \proofstep{}{(t = x \rightarrow (z \in x \rightarrow z \in t))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equcoms}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(t = y \rightarrow (z \in t \rightarrow z \in y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax9v1}}
  \proofstep{}{((t = x \land t = y) \rightarrow (z \in x \rightarrow z \in y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan9}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists t(t = x \land t = y) \rightarrow (z \in x \rightarrow z \in y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exlimiv}{5}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (z \in x \rightarrow z \in y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylbi}{1,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Identitätsgesetz für das zweite Argument des Mitgliedschaftsprädikats]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (z \in x \leftrightarrow z \in y))
}{elequ2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (z \in x \rightarrow z \in y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax9}}
  \proofstep{}{(y = x \rightarrow (z \in y \rightarrow z \in x))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax9}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (z \in y \rightarrow z \in x))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equcoms}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (z \in x \leftrightarrow z \in y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Universell quantifizierte Form von \FormulaRefAuto{elequ2}]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow \forall z(z \in x \leftrightarrow z \in y))
}{elequ2g}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (z \in x \leftrightarrow z \in y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{elequ2}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow \forall z(z \in x \leftrightarrow z \in y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alrimiv}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Substitution im zweiten Argument des Mitgliedschaftsprädikats]{%
  ([y / x]z \in x \leftrightarrow z \in y)
}{elsb2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep w}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = w \rightarrow (z \in x \leftrightarrow z \in w))}{\FormulaRefAuto{elequ2}}
  \proofstep{}{(w = y \rightarrow (z \in w \leftrightarrow z \in y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{elequ2}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x]z \in x \leftrightarrow z \in y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbievw2}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Gemeinsames Identitätsgesetz für beide Argumente des Mitgliedschaftsprädikats]{%
  ((x = y \land z = t) \rightarrow (x \in z \leftrightarrow y \in t))
}{elequ12}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep t}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (x \in z \leftrightarrow y \in z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{elequ1}}
  \proofstep{}{(z = t \rightarrow (y \in z \leftrightarrow y \in t))}{\FormulaRefAuto{elequ2}}
  \proofstep{}{((x = y \land z = t) \rightarrow (x \in z \leftrightarrow y \in t))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan9bb}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Aussagenlogischer Kern von Russells Paradox]{%
  \neg \forall x(x \in y \leftrightarrow \neg x \in x)
}{ru0}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\neg (y \in y \leftrightarrow \neg y \in y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.19}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (x \in y \leftrightarrow y \in y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{elequ1}}
  \proofstep{}{((x = y \land x = y) \rightarrow (x \in x \leftrightarrow y \in y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{elequ12}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (x \in x \leftrightarrow y \in y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anidms}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (\neg x \in x \leftrightarrow \neg y \in y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{notbid}{4}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow ((x \in y \leftrightarrow \neg x \in x) \leftrightarrow (y \in y \leftrightarrow \neg y \in y)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bibi12d}{2,5}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(x \in y \leftrightarrow \neg x \in x) \rightarrow (y \in y \leftrightarrow \neg y \in y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{spvv}{6}}
  \proofstep{}{\neg \forall x(x \in y \leftrightarrow \neg x \in x)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mto}{1,7}}
\end{tabproof}

\section{Logische Redundanz von \texttt{ax-10}, \texttt{ax-11}, \texttt{ax-12}, \texttt{ax-13}}

\FormulaThm[Degenerierter Fall von \FormulaRefAuto{ax-6}]{%
  \neg \forall x \neg x = x
}{ax6dgen}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{x = x}{\FormulaRefAuto{equid}}
  \proofstep{}{\neg \neg x = x}{\FormulaRefAuto{notnoti}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \neg x = x \rightarrow \neg x = x)}{\FormulaRefAuto{spfalw}}
  \proofstep{}{\neg \forall x \neg x = x}{\FormulaRefAuto{mt2}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Schwache Form von \FormulaRefAuto{ax-10}]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))
  \vdash
  (\neg \forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \neg \forall x \phi)
}{ax10w}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\neg \forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \neg \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{hbn1w}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Schwache Form von \FormulaRefAuto{ax-11}]{%
  (y = z \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))
  \vdash
  (\forall x \forall y \phi \rightarrow \forall y \forall x \phi)
}{ax11w}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(y = z \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \forall y \phi \rightarrow \forall y \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alcomimw}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Degenerierter Fall von \FormulaRefAuto{ax-11}]{%
  (\forall x \forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \forall x \phi)
}{ax11dgen}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Hilfssatz zur schwachen Form von \FormulaRefAuto{ax-12}]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))
  \vdash
  (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))
}{ax12wlem}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(\psi \rightarrow \forall x \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-5}}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax12i}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Schwache Form von \FormulaRefAuto{ax-12}]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    &(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))
    \dsep
    (y = z \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \chi))
    \vdash{}\\
    &\quad (x = y \rightarrow (\forall y \phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))
  \end{aligned}
}{ax12w}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi\dsep\chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{2}{(y = z \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \chi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(\forall y \phi \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{spw}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax12wlem}{3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(x = y \rightarrow (\forall y \phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl5}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Degenerierter Fall von \FormulaRefAuto{ax-12}]{%
  (x = x \rightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = x \rightarrow \phi)))
}{ax12dgen}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = x \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ala1}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = x \rightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = x \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Beispiel einer Anwendung von \FormulaRefAuto{ax12w}]{%
  \begin{aligned}[t]
    x = y \rightarrow \bigl(&\forall y(x \in y \land \forall x\, z \in x \land \forall y \forall z\, y \in x) \rightarrow{}\\
    &\forall x(x = y \rightarrow (x \in y \land \forall x\, z \in x \land \forall y \forall z\, y \in x))\bigr)
  \end{aligned}
}{ax12wdemo}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep w\dsep v}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (x \in y \leftrightarrow y \in y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{elequ1}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = w \rightarrow (z \in x \leftrightarrow z \in w))}{\FormulaRefAuto{elequ2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, z \in x \leftrightarrow \forall w\, z \in w)}{\FormulaRefAuto{cbvalvw}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (\forall x\, z \in x \leftrightarrow \forall w\, z \in w))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{(y = v \rightarrow (y \in x \leftrightarrow v \in x))}{\FormulaRefAuto{elequ1}}
  \proofstep{}{(y = v \rightarrow (\forall z\, y \in x \leftrightarrow \forall z\, v \in x))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albidv}{5}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y \forall z\, y \in x \leftrightarrow \forall v \forall z\, v \in x)}{\FormulaRefAuto{cbvalvw}{6}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (v \in x \leftrightarrow v \in y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{elequ2}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (\forall z\, v \in x \leftrightarrow \forall z\, v \in y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albidv}{8}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (\forall v \forall z\, v \in x \leftrightarrow \forall v \forall z\, v \in y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albidv}{9}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (\forall y \forall z\, y \in x \leftrightarrow \forall v \forall z\, v \in y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitrid}{7,10}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    \begin{aligned}[t]
      x = y \rightarrow \bigl(&
      (x \in y \land \forall x\, z \in x \land \forall y \forall z\, y \in x) \leftrightarrow{}\\
      &(y \in y \land \forall w\, z \in w \land \forall v \forall z\, v \in y)
      \bigr)
    \end{aligned}
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{3anbi123d}{1,4,11}}
  \proofstep{}{(y = v \rightarrow (x \in y \leftrightarrow x \in v))}{\FormulaRefAuto{elequ2}}
  \proofstep{}{(y = v \rightarrow (\forall y \forall z\, y \in x \leftrightarrow \forall v \forall z\, v \in x))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{7}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    \begin{aligned}[t]
      y = v \rightarrow \bigl(&
      (x \in y \land \forall x\, z \in x \land \forall y \forall z\, y \in x) \leftrightarrow{}\\
      &(x \in v \land \forall x\, z \in x \land \forall v \forall z\, v \in x)
      \bigr)
    \end{aligned}
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{3anbi13d}{13,14}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    \begin{aligned}[t]
      x = y \rightarrow \bigl(&\forall y(x \in y \land \forall x\, z \in x \land \forall y \forall z\, y \in x) \rightarrow{}\\
      &\forall x(x = y \rightarrow (x \in y \land \forall x\, z \in x \land \forall y \forall z\, y \in x))\bigr)
    \end{aligned}
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{ax12w}{12,15}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Schwache Form von \FormulaRefAuto{ax-13}]{%
  (\neg x = y \rightarrow (y = z \rightarrow \forall x\, y = z))
}{ax13w}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg x = y \rightarrow (y = z \rightarrow \forall x\, y = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax5d}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Erster degenerierter Fall von \FormulaRefAuto{ax-13}]{%
  (\neg x = x \rightarrow (x = z \rightarrow \forall x\, x = z))
}{ax13dgen1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{x = x}{\FormulaRefAuto{equid}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg x = x \rightarrow (x = z \rightarrow \forall x\, x = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.24i}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Zweiter degenerierter Fall von \FormulaRefAuto{ax-13}]{%
  (\neg x = y \rightarrow (y = x \rightarrow \forall x\, y = x))
}{ax13dgen2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(y = x \rightarrow x = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{equcomi}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg x = y \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow \forall x\, y = x))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.21}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg x = y \rightarrow (y = x \rightarrow \forall x\, y = x))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl5}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Dritter degenerierter Fall von \FormulaRefAuto{ax-13}]{%
  (\neg x = y \rightarrow (y = y \rightarrow \forall x\, y = y))
}{ax13dgen3}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{y = y}{\FormulaRefAuto{equid}}
  \proofstep{}{\forall x\, y = y}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-gen}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg x = y \rightarrow (y = y \rightarrow \forall x\, y = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{2a1i}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Vierter degenerierter Fall von \FormulaRefAuto{ax-13}]{%
  (\neg x = x \rightarrow (x = x \rightarrow \forall x\, x = x))
}{ax13dgen4}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg x = x \rightarrow (x = x \rightarrow \forall x\, x = x))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.21}}
\end{tabproof}

\section{Prädikatenkalkül mit Gleichheit: Hilfsaxiomenschemata}

\subsection{Axiomenschema \texttt{ax-10} (Quantifizierte Negation)}

\FormulaAxiom[Axiom der quantifizierten Negation]{%
  (\neg \forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \neg \forall x \phi)
}{ax-10}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}

\FormulaThm[Alias für \FormulaRefAuto{ax-10}]{%
  (\neg \forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \neg \forall x \phi)
}{hbn1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \neg \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-10}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Die Variable \(x\) ist in \(\exists x \phi\) nicht frei]{%
  (\exists x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \exists x \phi)
}{hbe1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \phi \leftrightarrow \neg \forall x \neg \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-ex}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x \neg \phi \rightarrow \forall x \neg \forall x \neg \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{hbn1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{hbxfrbi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Duale Form von \FormulaRefAuto{hbe1}]{%
  (\exists x \forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi)
}{hbe1a}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \forall x \phi \leftrightarrow \neg \forall x \neg \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-ex}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x \neg \forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con1i}{hbn1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylbi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Eine Richtung von \FormulaRefAuto{nf5} mit kleinerem Axiomen-Fußabdruck]{%
  (\forall x(\phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi) \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \phi)
}{nf5-1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi) \rightarrow (\exists x \phi \rightarrow \exists x \forall x \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exim}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{hbe1a}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi) \rightarrow (\exists x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl6}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi) \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfd}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Ableitung der Nichtfreiheit aus der Definition]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi) \vdash \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \phi
}{nf5i}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi) \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nf5-1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpg}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Nichtfreiheit in einem Kontext]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi) \dsep (\phi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \forall x \psi))
  \vdash
  (\phi \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \psi)
}{nf5dh}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(\phi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \forall x \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall x(\psi \rightarrow \forall x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alrimih}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(\psi \rightarrow \forall x \psi) \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nf5-1}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(\phi \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Anwendung der Nichtfreiheitsdefinition in einem Kontext]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \forall x \psi))
  \vdash
  (\phi \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \psi)
}{nf5dv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-5}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \forall x \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nf5dh}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Alle Variablen sind in einer Verschiedenheitsangabe effektiv gebunden]{%
  \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} z\, \neg \forall x\, x = y
}{nfnaew}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \forall z \neg \forall x\, x = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{hbnaev}}
  \proofstep{}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} z\, \neg \forall x\, x = y}{\FormulaRefAuto{nf5i}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Die Variable \(x\) ist in \(\exists x \phi\) nicht frei]{%
  \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \exists x \phi
}{nfe1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{hbe1}}
  \proofstep{}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \exists x \phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nf5i}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Die Variable \(x\) ist in \(\forall x \phi\) nicht frei]{%
  \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \forall x \phi
}{nfa1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \phi \leftrightarrow \neg \exists x \neg \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alex}}
  \proofstep{}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \exists x \neg \phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfe1}}
  \proofstep{}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \neg \exists x \neg \phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfn}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \forall x \phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfxfr}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Bequeme Form zur Vermeidung von \FormulaRefAuto{ax-11}]{%
  \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \neg \forall x \phi
}{nfna1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \forall x \phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfa1}}
  \proofstep{}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \neg \forall x \phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfn}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Die innere Variable eines Allquantors ist gebunden]{%
  \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \psi)
}{nfia1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \forall x \phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfa1}}
  \proofstep{}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \forall x \psi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfa1}}
  \proofstep{}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfim}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Die Variable \(x\) ist in \(\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \phi\) nicht frei]{%
  \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \phi
}{nfnf1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \phi \leftrightarrow (\exists x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-nf}}
  \proofstep{}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \exists x \phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfe1}}
  \proofstep{}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \forall x \phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfa1}}
  \proofstep{}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, (\exists x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfim}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfxfr}{1,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Das Analogon zu Axiom (5) der Modallogik S5]{%
  (\neg \forall x \neg \phi \rightarrow \forall x \neg \forall x \neg \phi)
}{modal5}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x \neg \phi \rightarrow \forall x \neg \forall x \neg \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{hbn1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Die Variable \(x\) ist in der Substitution von \(y\) für \(x\) in \(\phi\) nicht frei]{%
  \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, [y / x]\phi
}{nfs1v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sb6}}
  \proofstep{}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfa1}}
  \proofstep{}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, [y / x]\phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfxfr}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\subsection{Axiomenschema \texttt{ax-11} (Vertauschung von Quantoren)}

\FormulaAxiom[Axiom der Vertauschung von Quantoren]{%
  (\forall x \forall y \phi \rightarrow \forall y \forall x \phi)
}{ax-11}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}

\FormulaThm[Vertauschung von Quantoren im Antezedens]{%
  (\forall x \forall y \phi \rightarrow \psi)
  \vdash
  (\forall y \forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi)
}{alcoms}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y \forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \forall y \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-11}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \forall y \phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall y \forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Vertauschung zweier Allquantoren]{%
  (\forall x \forall y \phi \leftrightarrow \forall y \forall x \phi)
}{alcom}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \forall y \phi \rightarrow \forall y \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-11}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y \forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \forall y \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-11}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \forall y \phi \leftrightarrow \forall y \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Rotation von drei Allquantoren]{%
  (\forall x \forall y \forall z \phi \leftrightarrow \forall y \forall z \forall x \phi)
}{alrot3}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \forall y \forall z \phi \leftrightarrow \forall y \forall x \forall z \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alcom}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \forall z \phi \leftrightarrow \forall z \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alcom}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y \forall x \forall z \phi \leftrightarrow \forall y \forall z \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \forall y \forall z \phi \leftrightarrow \forall y \forall z \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Rotation von vier Allquantoren um zwei Stellen]{%
  (\forall x \forall y \forall z \forall w \phi \leftrightarrow \forall z \forall w \forall x \forall y \phi)
}{alrot4}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep w}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y \forall z \forall w \phi \leftrightarrow \forall z \forall w \forall y \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alrot3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \forall y \forall z \forall w \phi \leftrightarrow \forall x \forall z \forall w \forall y \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \forall z \forall w \forall y \phi \leftrightarrow \forall z \forall w \forall x \forall y \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alrot3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \forall y \forall z \forall w \phi \leftrightarrow \forall z \forall w \forall x \forall y \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Vertauschung zweier Existenzquantoren]{%
  (\exists x \exists y \phi \leftrightarrow \exists y \exists x \phi)
}{excom}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \forall y \neg \phi \leftrightarrow \forall y \forall x \neg \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alcom}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x \forall y \neg \phi \leftrightarrow \neg \forall y \forall x \neg \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{notbii}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y \phi \leftrightarrow \neg \forall x \forall y \neg \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{2exnaln}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists y \exists x \phi \leftrightarrow \neg \forall y \forall x \neg \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{2exnaln}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y \phi \leftrightarrow \exists y \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4i}{2,3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Eine Richtung von \FormulaRefAuto{excom}]{%
  (\exists x \exists y \phi \rightarrow \exists y \exists x \phi)
}{excomim}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y \phi \leftrightarrow \exists y \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{excom}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y \phi \rightarrow \exists y \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Vertauschung des ersten und dritten Existenzquantors]{%
  (\exists x \exists y \exists z \phi \leftrightarrow \exists z \exists y \exists x \phi)
}{excom13}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y \exists z \phi \leftrightarrow \exists y \exists x \exists z \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{excom}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists z \phi \leftrightarrow \exists z \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{excom}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists y \exists x \exists z \phi \leftrightarrow \exists y \exists z \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{exbii}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists y \exists z \exists x \phi \leftrightarrow \exists z \exists y \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{excom}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y \exists z \phi \leftrightarrow \exists z \exists y \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitri}{1,3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Rotation von drei Existenzquantoren]{%
  (\exists x \exists y \exists z \phi \leftrightarrow \exists y \exists z \exists x \phi)
}{exrot3}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y \exists z \phi \leftrightarrow \exists z \exists y \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{excom13}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists z \exists y \exists x \phi \leftrightarrow \exists y \exists z \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{excom}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y \exists z \phi \leftrightarrow \exists y \exists z \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Rotation von vier Existenzquantoren um zwei Stellen]{%
  (\exists x \exists y \exists z \exists w \phi \leftrightarrow \exists z \exists w \exists x \exists y \phi)
}{exrot4}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep w}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists y \exists z \exists w \phi \leftrightarrow \exists w \exists z \exists y \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{excom13}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y \exists z \exists w \phi \leftrightarrow \exists x \exists w \exists z \exists y \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{exbii}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists w \exists z \exists y \phi \leftrightarrow \exists z \exists w \exists x \exists y \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{excom13}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y \exists z \exists w \phi \leftrightarrow \exists z \exists w \exists x \exists y \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Aus Nichtfreiheit folgt Nichtfreiheit nach zusätzlicher Allquantifizierung]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi) \vdash (\forall y \phi \rightarrow \forall x \forall y \phi)
}{hbal}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall y \phi \rightarrow \forall y \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alimi}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y \forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \forall y \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-11}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall y \phi \rightarrow \forall x \forall y \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktionsform von \FormulaRefAuto{hbal}]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow \forall y \phi) \dsep (\phi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \forall x \psi))
  \vdash
  (\phi \rightarrow (\forall y \psi \rightarrow \forall x \forall y \psi))
}{hbald}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall y \phi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(\phi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \forall x \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(\phi \rightarrow (\forall y \psi \rightarrow \forall y \forall x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alimdh}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y \forall x \psi \rightarrow \forall x \forall y \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-11}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(\phi \rightarrow (\forall y \psi \rightarrow \forall x \forall y \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl6}{3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Allquantor in und aus einer Substitution herausziehen]{%
  ([z / y]\forall x \phi \leftrightarrow \forall x[y / z]\phi)
}{sbal}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep w}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall w \forall x(w = z \rightarrow \forall y(y = w \rightarrow \phi)) \leftrightarrow \forall x \forall w(w = z \rightarrow \forall y(y = w \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alcom}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(y = w \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow (y = w \rightarrow \forall x \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.21v}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y \forall x(y = w \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow \forall y(y = w \rightarrow \forall x \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y \forall x(y = w \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow \forall x \forall y(y = w \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alcom}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y(y = w \rightarrow \forall x \phi) \leftrightarrow \forall x \forall y(y = w \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr3i}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{}{((w = z \rightarrow \forall y(y = w \rightarrow \forall x \phi)) \leftrightarrow (w = z \rightarrow \forall x \forall y(y = w \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi2i}{5}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall w(w = z \rightarrow \forall y(y = w \rightarrow \forall x \phi)) \leftrightarrow \forall w(w = z \rightarrow \forall x \forall y(y = w \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{6}}
  \proofstep{}{([z / y]\forall x \phi \leftrightarrow \forall w(w = z \rightarrow \forall y(y = w \rightarrow \forall x \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{dfsb}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(w = z \rightarrow \forall y(y = w \rightarrow \phi)) \leftrightarrow (w = z \rightarrow \forall x \forall y(y = w \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.21v}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall w \forall x(w = z \rightarrow \forall y(y = w \rightarrow \phi)) \leftrightarrow \forall w(w = z \rightarrow \forall x \forall y(y = w \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{9}}
  \proofstep{}{([z / y]\forall x \phi \leftrightarrow \forall w \forall x(w = z \rightarrow \forall y(y = w \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4i}{7,8,10}}
  \proofstep{}{([z / y]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall w(w = z \rightarrow \forall y(y = w \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{dfsb}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x[z / y]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall x \forall w(w = z \rightarrow \forall y(y = w \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{12}}
  \proofstep{}{([z / y]\forall x \phi \leftrightarrow \forall x[z / y]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4i}{1,11,13}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Mit neuer Variablen im Inneren der Substitution quantifizieren]{%
  ([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \psi) \vdash ([y / x]\forall z \phi \leftrightarrow \forall z \psi)
}{sbalv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x]\forall z \phi \leftrightarrow \forall z[y / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbal}}
  \proofstep{1}{([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall z[y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall z \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{([y / x]\forall z \phi \leftrightarrow \forall z \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Nichtfreiheit bleibt unter Substitution erhalten]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow \forall z \phi) \vdash ([y / x]\phi \rightarrow \forall z[y / x]\phi)
}{hbsbw}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall z \phi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{([y / x]\phi \rightarrow [y / x]\forall z \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbimi}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x]\forall z \phi \leftrightarrow \forall z[y / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbal}}
  \proofstep{1}{([y / x]\phi \rightarrow \forall z[y / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylib}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Veraltete Fassung von \FormulaRefAuto{hbsbw}]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow \forall z \phi) \vdash ([y / x]\phi \rightarrow \forall z[y / x]\phi)
}{hbsbwOLD}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall z \phi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{([y / x]\phi \rightarrow \forall z[y / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{hbsbw}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Vertauschungsgesetz für Substitution]{%
  ([w / z][y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [y / x][w / z]\phi)
}{sbcom2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep w\dsep v\dsep u}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{([v / z][u / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall z \forall x((z = v \land x = u) \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{2sb6}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall z \forall x((z = v \land x = u) \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow \forall x \forall z((z = v \land x = u) \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alcom}}
  \proofstep{}{(((z = v \land x = u) \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow ((x = u \land z = v) \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ancomst}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \forall z((z = v \land x = u) \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow \forall x \forall z((x = u \land z = v) \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{2albii}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{([v / z][u / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall x \forall z((x = u \land z = v) \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitri}{1,2,4}}
  \proofstep{}{([u / x][v / z]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall x \forall z((x = u \land z = v) \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{2sb6}}
  \proofstep{}{([v / z][u / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [u / x][v / z]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr4i}{5,6}}
  \proofstep{}{(u = y \rightarrow ([u / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [y / x]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbequ}}
  \proofstep{}{(u = y \rightarrow ([v / z][u / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [v / z][y / x]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbbidv}{8}}
  \proofstep{}{(u = y \rightarrow ([u / x][v / z]\phi \leftrightarrow [v / z][y / x]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr3id}{7,9}}
  \proofstep{}{(v = w \rightarrow ([v / z][y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [w / z][y / x]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbequ}}
  \proofstep{}{((u = y \land v = w) \rightarrow ([u / x][v / z]\phi \leftrightarrow [w / z][y / x]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan9bb}{10,11}}
  \proofstep{}{(v = w \rightarrow ([v / z]\phi \leftrightarrow [w / z]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbequ}}
  \proofstep{}{(v = w \rightarrow ([u / x][v / z]\phi \leftrightarrow [u / x][w / z]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbbidv}{13}}
  \proofstep{}{(u = y \rightarrow ([u / x][w / z]\phi \leftrightarrow [y / x][w / z]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbequ}}
  \proofstep{}{((u = y \land v = w) \rightarrow ([u / x][v / z]\phi \leftrightarrow [y / x][w / z]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan9bbr}{14,15}}
  \proofstep{}{((u = y \land v = w) \rightarrow ([w / z][y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [y / x][w / z]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr3d}{12,16}}
  \proofstep{}{(u = y \rightarrow (v = w \rightarrow ([w / z][y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [y / x][w / z]\phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{17}}
  \proofstep{}{\exists u\, u = y}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax6ev}}
  \proofstep{}{(v = w \rightarrow ([w / z][y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [y / x][w / z]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exlimiiv}{18,19}}
  \proofstep{}{\exists v\, v = w}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax6ev}}
  \proofstep{}{([w / z][y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [y / x][w / z]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{exlimiiv}{20,21}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Veraltete Fassung von \FormulaRefAuto{sbco4lem}]{%
  ([x / v][y / x][v / y]\phi \leftrightarrow [x / w][y / x][w / y]\phi)
}{sbco4lemOLD}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{v\dsep w\dsep x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{([x / v][y / x][v / y]\phi \leftrightarrow [x / w][y / x][w / y]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbco4lem}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Veraltete Fassung von \FormulaRefAuto{sbco4}]{%
  ([y / u][x / v][u / x][v / y]\phi \leftrightarrow [x / w][y / x][w / y]\phi)
}{sbco4OLD}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{u\dsep v\dsep w\dsep x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{([y / u][x / v][u / x][v / y]\phi \leftrightarrow [x / w][y / x][w / y]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbco4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Die innere Variable eines Allquantors ist gebunden]{%
  \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \forall y \forall x \phi
}{nfa2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y \forall x \phi \leftrightarrow \forall x \forall y \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alcom}}
  \proofstep{}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \forall x \forall y \phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfa1}}
  \proofstep{}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \forall y \forall x \phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfxfr}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Existenzielle Form einer Nichtfreiheitsvoraussetzung]{%
  (\exists x \phi \rightarrow \phi) \vdash \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \exists y \phi
}{nfexhe}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y \phi \rightarrow \forall x \exists x \exists y \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{hbe1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y \phi \rightarrow \exists y \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{excomim}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\exists x \phi \rightarrow \phi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\exists y \exists x \phi \rightarrow \exists y \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{eximi}{3}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\exists x \exists y \phi \rightarrow \exists y \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{2,4}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\exists x \exists y \phi \rightarrow \forall x \exists y \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alrimih}{1,5}}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \exists y \phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfi}{6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Die innere Variable eines Allquantors unter einem Existenzquantor ist gebunden]{%
  \mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \exists y \forall x \phi
}{nfexa2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{hbe1a}}
  \proofstep{}{\mathord{\text{Ⅎ}} x\, \exists y \forall x \phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfexhe}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\subsection{Axiomenschema \texttt{ax-12} (Substitution)}

\FormulaAxiom[Axiom der Substitution]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\forall y \phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))
}{ax-12}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}

\FormulaThm[Abgeschwächte Form von \FormulaRefAuto{ax-12}]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))
}{ax12v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall y \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-5}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (\forall y \phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-12}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl5}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Variante von \FormulaRefAuto{ax12v} ohne Einschränkung an \(\phi\)]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))
}{ax12v2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(y = z \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow x = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equtrr}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = z \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = z \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax12v}}
  \proofstep{}{(y = z \rightarrow ((x = z \rightarrow \phi) \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imim1d}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(y = z \rightarrow (\forall x(x = z \rightarrow \phi) \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alimdv}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{(y = z \rightarrow (x = z \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl9r}{2,4}}
  \proofstep{}{(y = z \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syld}{1,5}}
  \proofstep{}{\exists z\, y = z}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax6evr}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exlimiiv}{6,7}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Existenzielle Form von \FormulaRefAuto{ax12v2}]{%
  (\exists x(x = y \land \phi) \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow \phi))
}{ax12ev2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(x = y \land \phi) \leftrightarrow \neg \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \neg \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exnalimn}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (\neg \phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \neg \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax12v2}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (\neg \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \neg \phi) \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{con1d}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (\exists x(x = y \land \phi) \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimtrid}{1,3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(x = y \land \phi) \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{com12}{4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Wenn eine Formel wahr ist, ist sie für wenigstens eine Instanz wahr]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow \exists x \phi)
}{19.8a}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax12v}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi) \rightarrow \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alequexv}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \exists x \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl6}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{\exists y\, x = y}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax6evr}}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{exlimiiv}{3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktionsform von \FormulaRefAuto{19.8a}]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow \psi) \vdash (\phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi)
}{19.8ad}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(\psi \rightarrow \exists x \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.8a}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Spezialisierung]{%
  (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \phi)
}{sp}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \phi \leftrightarrow \neg \exists x \neg \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alex}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \phi \rightarrow \exists x \neg \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.8a}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \exists x \neg \phi \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{con1i}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylbi}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inferenzform der Spezialisierung]{%
  \forall x \phi \vdash \phi
}{spi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\forall x \phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sp}}
  \proofstep{1}{\phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Verallgemeinerung des Antezedens]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow \psi) \vdash (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi)
}{sps}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sp}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{2,1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Doppelte Spezialisierung]{%
  (\forall x \forall y \phi \rightarrow \phi)
}{2sp}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y \phi \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sp}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \forall y \phi \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sps}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktive Verallgemeinerung des Antezedens]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \chi)) \vdash (\phi \rightarrow (\forall x \psi \rightarrow \chi))
}{spsd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi\dsep\chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \psi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sp}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \chi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow (\forall x \psi \rightarrow \chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl5}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Verallgemeinerte Form von \FormulaRefAuto{19.2}]{%
  (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \exists y \phi)
}{19.2g}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow \exists y \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.8a}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \exists y \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sps}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inferenzform von \FormulaRefAuto{19.21} und zugleich Deduktionsform von \FormulaRefAuto{sp}]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow \forall x \psi) \vdash (\phi \rightarrow \psi)
}{19.21bi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall x \psi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \psi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sp}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inferenz zum Entfernen von zwei Allquantoren]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow \forall x \forall y \psi) \vdash (\phi \rightarrow \psi)
}{19.21bbi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall x \forall y \psi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall y \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.21bi}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.21bi}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inferenzform von \FormulaRefAuto{19.23}]{%
  (\exists x \phi \rightarrow \psi) \vdash (\phi \rightarrow \psi)
}{19.23bi}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.8a}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\exists x \phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Inferenz zur Kontraposition von \FormulaRefAuto{19.8a}]{%
  \neg \exists x \phi \vdash \neg \phi
}{nexr}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg \exists x \phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.8a}}
  \proofstep{1}{\neg \phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{mto}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\section{Nichtfreiheitskriterien und Quantoren}

\FormulaThm[Quantifizierte Form des ausgeschlossenen Dritten]{%
  \exists x(\varphi \rightarrow \forall x \varphi)
}{qexmid}{%
  \DeltaRow{Wffs}{\varphi}%
  \DeltaRow{Setvars}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \varphi \rightarrow \exists x \forall x \varphi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.8a}}
  \proofstep{}{%
    \exists x(\varphi \rightarrow \forall x \varphi)
  }{\FormulaRefAuto{19.35ri}{1}}
\end{tabproof}
\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{qexmid} ist auch als \enquote{Trinkerparadoxon} bekannt: Es gibt ein \(x\), so dass aus \(\varphi(x)\) schon \(\forall x\,\varphi(x)\) folgt.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Folgerung aus der Definition von \enquote{nicht frei}]{%
  (\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi \rightarrow (\varphi \rightarrow \forall x \varphi))
}{nf5r}{%
  \DeltaRow{Wffs}{\varphi}%
  \DeltaRow{Setvars}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\varphi \rightarrow \exists x \varphi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.8a}}
  \proofstep{}{(\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi \rightarrow \operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{(\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi \rightarrow (\exists x \varphi \rightarrow \forall x \varphi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfrd}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi \rightarrow (\varphi \rightarrow \forall x \varphi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl5}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Folgerung aus der Definition von \enquote{nicht frei} (Inferenzform)]{%
  \operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi \vdash \varphi \rightarrow \forall x \varphi
}{nf5ri}{%
  \DeltaRow{Wffs}{\varphi}%
  \DeltaRow{Setvars}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\exists x \varphi \rightarrow \forall x \varphi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfri}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\varphi \rightarrow \forall x \varphi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.23bi}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Folgerung aus der Definition von \enquote{nicht frei} im Kontext]{%
  (\varphi \rightarrow \operatorname{Ⅎ} x \psi) \vdash \varphi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \forall x \psi)
}{nf5rd}{%
  \DeltaRow{Wffs}{\varphi\dsep \psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Setvars}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\varphi \rightarrow \operatorname{Ⅎ} x \psi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \psi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \forall x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nf5r}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\varphi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \forall x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Spezialisierung mit impliziter Substitution im Kontext]{%
  \chi \rightarrow \operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi \dsep x = y \rightarrow (\varphi \rightarrow \psi)
  \vdash
  \chi \rightarrow (\varphi \rightarrow \exists x \psi)
}{spimedv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Wffs}{\chi\dsep \varphi\dsep \psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Setvars}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\chi \rightarrow \operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\chi \rightarrow (\varphi \rightarrow \forall x \varphi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nf5rd}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, x = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax6ev}}
  \proofstep{2}{(x = y \rightarrow (\varphi \rightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\exists x(\varphi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{eximii}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{2}{(\forall x \varphi \rightarrow \exists x \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.35i}{5}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(\chi \rightarrow (\varphi \rightarrow \exists x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl6}{2,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Spezialisierung mit impliziter Substitution]{%
  \operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi \dsep x = y \rightarrow (\varphi \rightarrow \psi)
  \vdash
  \varphi \rightarrow \exists x \psi
}{spimefv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Wffs}{\varphi\dsep \psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Setvars}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\top \rightarrow \operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{(x = y \rightarrow (\varphi \rightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(\top \rightarrow (\varphi \rightarrow \exists x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{spimedv}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(\varphi \rightarrow \exists x \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mptru}{4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Geschlossene Form von \FormulaRefAuto{nfim}]{%
  \operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi \dsep (\varphi \rightarrow \operatorname{Ⅎ} x \psi)
  \vdash
  \operatorname{Ⅎ} x(\varphi \rightarrow \psi)
}{nfim1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Wffs}{\varphi\dsep \psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Setvars}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(\varphi \rightarrow \operatorname{Ⅎ} x \psi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\operatorname{Ⅎ} x(\varphi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfim}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Geschlossene Form von \FormulaRefAuto{nfan}]{%
  \operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi \dsep (\varphi \rightarrow \operatorname{Ⅎ} x \psi)
  \vdash
  \operatorname{Ⅎ} x(\varphi \land \psi)
}{nfan1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Wffs}{\varphi\dsep \psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Setvars}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{((\varphi \land \psi) \leftrightarrow \neg(\varphi \rightarrow \neg \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-an}}
  \proofstep{1}{\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(\varphi \rightarrow \operatorname{Ⅎ} x \psi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(\varphi \rightarrow \operatorname{Ⅎ} x \neg \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfnd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\operatorname{Ⅎ} x(\varphi \rightarrow \neg \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfim1}{1,4}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\operatorname{Ⅎ} x\neg(\varphi \rightarrow \neg \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfn}{5}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\operatorname{Ⅎ} x(\varphi \land \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfxfr}{1,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Geschlossene Form von Satz 19.3]{%
  (\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi \rightarrow (\forall x \varphi \leftrightarrow \varphi))
}{19.3t}{%
  \DeltaRow{Wffs}{\varphi}%
  \DeltaRow{Setvars}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \varphi \rightarrow \varphi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sp}}
  \proofstep{}{(\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi \rightarrow (\varphi \rightarrow \forall x \varphi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nf5r}}
  \proofstep{}{(\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi \rightarrow (\forall x \varphi \leftrightarrow \varphi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid2}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Eine Formel darf mit einer in ihr nicht freien Variablen allquantifiziert werden]{%
  \operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi \vdash \forall x \varphi \leftrightarrow \varphi
}{19.3}{%
  \DeltaRow{Wffs}{\varphi}%
  \DeltaRow{Setvars}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \varphi \rightarrow \varphi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sp}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\varphi \rightarrow \forall x \varphi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nf5ri}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \varphi \leftrightarrow \varphi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktive Form einer Richtung von Satz 19.9]{%
  (\psi \rightarrow \operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi) \vdash \psi \rightarrow (\exists x \varphi \rightarrow \varphi)
}{19.9d}{%
  \DeltaRow{Wffs}{\psi\dsep \varphi}%
  \DeltaRow{Setvars}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\psi \rightarrow \operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi \rightarrow (\exists x \varphi \rightarrow \varphi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfrd}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \varphi \rightarrow \varphi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sp}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\psi \rightarrow (\exists x \varphi \rightarrow \varphi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl6}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Geschlossene Form von Satz 19.9]{%
  (\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi \rightarrow (\exists x \varphi \leftrightarrow \varphi))
}{19.9t}{%
  \DeltaRow{Wffs}{\varphi}%
  \DeltaRow{Setvars}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi \rightarrow \operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{(\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi \rightarrow (\exists x \varphi \rightarrow \varphi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.9d}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\varphi \rightarrow \exists x \varphi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.8a}}
  \proofstep{}{(\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi \rightarrow (\exists x \varphi \leftrightarrow \varphi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid1}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Eine Formel darf mit einer in ihr nicht freien Variablen existenzquantifiziert werden]{%
  \operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi \vdash \exists x \varphi \leftrightarrow \varphi
}{19.9}{%
  \DeltaRow{Wffs}{\varphi}%
  \DeltaRow{Setvars}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi \rightarrow (\exists x \varphi \leftrightarrow \varphi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.9t}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\exists x \varphi \leftrightarrow \varphi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Geschlossene Form von Satz 19.21]{%
  (\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi \rightarrow (\forall x(\varphi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\varphi \rightarrow \forall x \psi)))
}{19.21t}{%
  \DeltaRow{Wffs}{\varphi\dsep \psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Setvars}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi \rightarrow ((\exists x \varphi \rightarrow \forall x \psi) \leftrightarrow \forall x(\varphi \rightarrow \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.38a}}
  \proofstep{}{(\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi \rightarrow (\exists x \varphi \leftrightarrow \varphi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.9t}}
  \proofstep{}{(\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi \rightarrow ((\exists x \varphi \rightarrow \forall x \psi) \leftrightarrow (\varphi \rightarrow \forall x \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi1d}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi \rightarrow (\forall x(\varphi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\varphi \rightarrow \forall x \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr3d}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz 19.21]{%
  \operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi \vdash \forall x(\varphi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\varphi \rightarrow \forall x \psi)
}{19.21}{%
  \DeltaRow{Wffs}{\varphi\dsep \psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Setvars}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi \rightarrow (\forall x(\varphi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\varphi \rightarrow \forall x \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.21t}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x(\varphi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\varphi \rightarrow \forall x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Standardaxiom der Prädikatenlogik zu Axiom 5]{%
  \operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi \vdash \forall x(\varphi \rightarrow \psi) \rightarrow (\varphi \rightarrow \forall x \psi)
}{stdpc5}{%
  \DeltaRow{Wffs}{\varphi\dsep \psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Setvars}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x(\varphi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\varphi \rightarrow \forall x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.21}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x(\varphi \rightarrow \psi) \rightarrow (\varphi \rightarrow \forall x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpi}{2}}
\end{tabproof}
\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{stdpc5} ist die hier zentrale Form des üblichen Allquantor-Axioms aus Standarddarstellungen der Prädikatenlogik.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Version von Satz 19.21 mit zwei Quantoren]{%
  \operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi \dsep \operatorname{Ⅎ} y \varphi
  \vdash
  \forall x \forall y(\varphi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\varphi \rightarrow \forall x \forall y \psi)
}{19.21-2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Wffs}{\varphi\dsep \psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Setvars}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{2}{\operatorname{Ⅎ} y \varphi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(\forall y(\varphi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\varphi \rightarrow \forall y \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.21}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{(\forall x \forall y(\varphi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow \forall x(\varphi \rightarrow \forall y \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \varphi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x(\varphi \rightarrow \forall y \psi) \leftrightarrow (\varphi \rightarrow \forall x \forall y \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.21}{4}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(\forall x \forall y(\varphi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\varphi \rightarrow \forall x \forall y \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{3,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Geschlossene Form von Satz 19.23]{%
  (\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \psi \rightarrow (\forall x(\varphi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \varphi \rightarrow \psi)))
}{19.23t}{%
  \DeltaRow{Wffs}{\varphi\dsep \psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Setvars}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \psi \rightarrow ((\exists x \varphi \rightarrow \forall x \psi) \leftrightarrow \forall x(\varphi \rightarrow \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.38b}}
  \proofstep{}{(\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \psi \rightarrow (\forall x \psi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.3t}}
  \proofstep{}{(\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \psi \rightarrow ((\exists x \varphi \rightarrow \forall x \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \varphi \rightarrow \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi2d}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \psi \rightarrow (\forall x(\varphi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \varphi \rightarrow \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr3d}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Satz 19.23]{%
  \operatorname{Ⅎ} x \psi \vdash \forall x(\varphi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \varphi \rightarrow \psi)
}{19.23}{%
  \DeltaRow{Wffs}{\varphi\dsep \psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Setvars}{x}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \psi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(\operatorname{Ⅎ} x \psi \rightarrow (\forall x(\varphi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \varphi \rightarrow \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.23t}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x(\varphi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\exists x \varphi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktive Form von Satz 19.20]{%
