  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow \forall x(y = x \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{5}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equsv}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y\,[y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall y \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{6,7}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \phi \leftrightarrow \forall y\,[y / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitrri}{2,3,8}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Substitution einer Variablen im Allquantor bei nichtfreier Zielvariablen]{%
  \mathrm{F}\!/\;y\,\phi \vdash (\forall x \phi \leftrightarrow \forall y\,[y / x]\phi)
}{sb8f}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x]\forall x \phi \leftrightarrow \forall x\,[y / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sb6}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y\,[y / x]\forall x \phi \leftrightarrow \forall y \forall x\,[y / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y \forall x\,[y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall x \forall y\,[y / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alcom}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \phi \leftrightarrow \forall y\,[y / x]\forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sb6}}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;y\,\phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \leftrightarrow [y / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbf}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \leftrightarrow y = x)}{\FormulaRefAuto{equcom}}
  \proofstep{}{((x = y \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow (y = x \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi1i}{7}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow \forall x(y = x \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{8}}
  \proofstep{1}{([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall x(y = x \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr3ri}{6,7,9}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall y\,[y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall x \forall y\,[y / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{10}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \phi \leftrightarrow \forall y\,[y / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitrri}{4,2,11}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Substitution einer Variablen im Existenzquantor]{%
  \mathrm{F}\!/\;y\,\phi \vdash (\exists x \phi \leftrightarrow \exists y\,[y / x]\phi)
}{sb8ef}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;y\,\phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;y\,x = y}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfs1v}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow [y / x]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbequ12}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\exists x \phi \leftrightarrow \exists y\,[y / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{cbvexv1}{2,3,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Äquivalente Form doppelter Existenz]{%
  \mathrm{F}\!/\;w\,\phi \dsep \mathrm{F}\!/\;z\,\phi \vdash (\exists x \exists y \phi \leftrightarrow \exists z \exists w\,[z / x][w / y]\phi)
}{2sb8ef}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep w}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;w\,\phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\exists y \phi \leftrightarrow \exists w\,[w / y]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sb8ef}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y \phi \leftrightarrow \exists x \exists w\,[w / y]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{exbii}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists w\,[w / y]\phi \leftrightarrow \exists w \exists x\,[w / y]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{excom}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\exists x \exists y \phi \leftrightarrow \exists w \exists x\,[w / y]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{2}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;z\,\phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;z\,[w / y]\phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfsbv}{2}}
  \proofstep{2}{(\exists x\,[w / y]\phi \leftrightarrow \exists z\,[z / x][w / y]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sb8ef}{7}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists w \exists x\,[w / y]\phi \leftrightarrow \exists w \exists z\,[z / x][w / y]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{exbii}{8}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists w \exists z\,[z / x][w / y]\phi \leftrightarrow \exists z \exists w\,[z / x][w / y]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{excom}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(\exists x \exists y \phi \leftrightarrow \exists z \exists w\,[z / x][w / y]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitri}{5,9,10}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Umgekehrte Substitution]{%
  \mathrm{F}\!/\;y\,\phi \vdash (\phi \leftrightarrow \forall y(y = x \rightarrow [y / x]\phi))
}{sb6rfv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;y\,\phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow [y / x]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbequ12r}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall y(y = x \rightarrow [y / x]\phi) \leftrightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{equsalv}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \leftrightarrow \forall y(y = x \rightarrow [y / x]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Zwei Charakterisierungen von Nichtfreiheit]{%
  (\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi \leftrightarrow \forall y \forall z([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [z / x]\phi))
}{sbnf2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;y\,\phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfv}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \phi \leftrightarrow \exists y\,[y / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sb8ef}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \phi \leftrightarrow \forall y\,[y / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sb8v}}
  \proofstep{}{((\exists x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi) \leftrightarrow (\exists y\,[y / x]\phi \rightarrow \forall y\,[y / x]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi12i}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi \leftrightarrow (\exists x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-nf}}
  \proofstep{}{(\mathrm{F}\!/\;y\,[y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow (\exists y\,[y / x]\phi \rightarrow \forall y\,[y / x]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm11.53v}}
  \proofstep{}{(\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi \leftrightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;y\,[y / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4i}{5,4,6}}
  \proofstep{}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;z\,\phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfv}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \phi \leftrightarrow \exists z\,[z / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sb8ef}{8}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \phi \leftrightarrow \forall z\,[z / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sb8v}}
  \proofstep{}{((\exists x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi) \leftrightarrow (\exists z\,[z / x]\phi \rightarrow \forall z\,[z / x]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi12i}{9,10}}
  \proofstep{}{(\mathrm{F}\!/\;z\,[z / x]\phi \leftrightarrow (\exists z\,[z / x]\phi \rightarrow \forall z\,[z / x]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm11.53v}}
  \proofstep{}{(\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi \leftrightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;z\,[z / x]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4i}{5,11,12}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y \mathrm{F}\!/\;z([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [z / x]\phi) \leftrightarrow \forall z \mathrm{F}\!/\;y([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [z / x]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alcom}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y \forall z([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [z / x]\phi) \leftrightarrow \forall y \mathrm{F}\!/\;z([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [z / x]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{13,14}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y \mathrm{F}\!/\;z([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [z / x]\phi) \leftrightarrow (\forall y\,[y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall z\,[z / x]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anbi12i}{7,13}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall y\,[y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall z\,[z / x]\phi) \leftrightarrow \forall y \forall z([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [z / x]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm4.24}}
  \proofstep{}{(\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi \leftrightarrow \forall y \forall z([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [z / x]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4i}{15,16,17}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Äquivalente Darstellung des Existenzquantors]{%
  (\exists x \phi \leftrightarrow \exists y \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi))
}{exsb}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;y\,\phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfv}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfa1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists y \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow \exists y \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax12v}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists y \phi \leftrightarrow \exists x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sp}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \phi \leftrightarrow \exists y \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid}{4,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Äquivalente Darstellung doppelter Existenz]{%
  (\exists x \exists y \phi \leftrightarrow \exists z \exists w \forall x \forall y((x = z \land y = w) \rightarrow \phi))
}{2exsb}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z\dsep w}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;z\,\phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfv}}
  \proofstep{}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;w\,\phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfv}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y \phi \leftrightarrow \exists z \exists w\,[z / x][w / y]\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{2sb8ef}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{([z / x][w / y]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall x \forall y((x = z \land y = w) \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{2sb6}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists z \exists w\,[z / x][w / y]\phi \leftrightarrow \exists z \exists w \forall x \forall y((x = z \land y = w) \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{2exbii}{4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \exists y \phi \leftrightarrow \exists z \exists w \forall x \forall y((x = z \land y = w) \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{3,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Umkehrung einer Substitution]{%
  \mathrm{F}\!/\;y\,\phi \dsep \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi \vdash (\forall y([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow \forall x(\phi \leftrightarrow [x / y]\psi))
}{sbbib}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\mathrm{F}\!/\;y\,x = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfs1v}}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;y\,\phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;y\,(\phi \leftrightarrow [x / y]\psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfbi}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{2}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,y = x)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfs1v}}
  \proofstep{2}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfbi}{5,4}}
  \proofstep{}{(y = x \rightarrow ([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow [x / y]\psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbequ12r}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow [x / y]\psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbequ12}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (\forall y([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow \forall x(\phi \leftrightarrow [x / y]\psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bibi12d}{7,8}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(\forall y([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow \forall x(\phi \leftrightarrow [x / y]\psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{cbvalv1}{3,6,9}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Umkehrung einer Substitution ohne Zusatzhypothesen]{%
  (\forall y([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow \forall x(\phi \leftrightarrow [x / y]\psi))
}{sbbibvv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;y\,\phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfv}}
  \proofstep{}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfv}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow \forall x(\phi \leftrightarrow [x / y]\psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbbib}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Änderung der substituierten Variablen bei impliziter Substitution]{%
  \mathrm{F}\!/\;y\,\phi \dsep \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi \dsep (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)) \vdash ([z / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [z / y]\psi)
}{cbvsbvf}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;y\,x = z}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfv}}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;y\,\phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;y\,(x = z \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfim}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,y = z}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfv}}
  \proofstep{2}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,(y = z \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfim}{4,5}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow ((x = z \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow (y = z \rightarrow \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equequ1}}
  \proofstep{3}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{(x = y \rightarrow ((x = z \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow (y = z \rightarrow \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi12d}{7,8}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{(\forall x(x = z \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow \forall y(y = z \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{cbvalv1}{3,6,9}}
  \proofstep{}{([z / x]\phi \leftrightarrow \forall x(x = z \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sb6}}
  \proofstep{}{([z / y]\psi \leftrightarrow \forall y(y = z \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sb6}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{([z / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [z / y]\psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4i}{11,10,12}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Alternative Herleitung von \FormulaRefAuto{cleljust}]{%
  (x \in y \leftrightarrow \exists z(z = x \land z \in y))
}{cleljustALT}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep z}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x \in y \rightarrow \forall z\, x \in y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-5}}
  \proofstep{}{(z = x \rightarrow (z \in y \leftrightarrow x \in y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{elequ1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists z(z = x \land z \in y) \leftrightarrow x \in y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{equsexhv}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(x \in y \leftrightarrow \exists z(z = x \land z \in y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Alternative Herleitung von \FormulaRefAuto{cleljust} im Stil der Nichtfreiheit]{%
  (x \in y \leftrightarrow \exists z(z = x \land z \in y))
}{cleljustALT2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep z}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;z\,x \in y}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfv}}
  \proofstep{}{(z = x \rightarrow (z \in y \leftrightarrow x \in y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{elequ1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists z(z = x \land z \in y) \leftrightarrow x \in y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{equsexv}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(x \in y \leftrightarrow \exists z(z = x \land z \in y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bicomi}{3}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{cleljustALT2} ist inhaltlich gleichwertig zu \FormulaRefAuto{cleljustALT}, benutzt aber die spätere und meist angenehmere Formulierung über \(\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\) statt über einen expliziten Gebundenheitsaufbau.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Alternative Herleitung von \FormulaRefAuto{equs5a}]{%
  (\exists x(x = y \land \forall y \phi) \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi))
}{equs5aALT}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfa1}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (\forall y \phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-12}}
  \proofstep{}{((x = y \land \forall y \phi) \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(x = y \land \forall y \phi) \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exlimi}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Alternative Herleitung von \FormulaRefAuto{equs5e}]{%
  (\exists x(x = y \land \phi) \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \exists y \phi))
}{equs5eALT}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \exists y \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfa1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists y \phi \rightarrow \forall y \exists y \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{hbe1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall y \exists y \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.23bi}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (\forall y \exists y \phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \exists y \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-12}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \exists y \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl5}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{}{((x = y \land \phi) \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \exists y \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{5}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(x = y \land \phi) \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \exists y \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exlimi}{1,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\section{Axiomenschema \texttt{ax-13} und quantifizierte Gleichheit}

\FormulaThm[Umgekehrte Form von \FormulaRefAuto{axc11}]{%
  (\forall y\, y = x \rightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall y \phi))
}{axc11r}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(y = x \rightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall y(y = x \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-12}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y\, y = x \rightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall y(y = x \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sps}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(y = x \rightarrow ((y = x \rightarrow \phi) \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.27}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y\, y = x \rightarrow (\forall y(y = x \rightarrow \phi) \rightarrow \forall y \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{al2imi}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y\, y = x \rightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall y \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syld}{2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Hilfssatz für den Distinktorabbau bei Allquantoren]{%
  (\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)) \vdash (\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (\forall x \phi \leftrightarrow \forall y \psi))
}{dral1v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \forall x \forall x\, x = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{hbaev}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (\forall x \phi \leftrightarrow \forall x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albidh}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (\forall x \psi \rightarrow \forall y \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{axc11v}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (\forall y \psi \rightarrow \forall x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{axc11rv}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (\forall x \psi \leftrightarrow \forall y \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid}{4,5}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (\forall x \phi \leftrightarrow \forall y \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitrd}{3,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Hilfssatz für den Distinktorabbau bei Existenzquantoren]{%
  (\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)) \vdash (\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (\exists x \phi \leftrightarrow \exists y \psi))
}{drex1v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (\neg \phi \leftrightarrow \neg \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{notbid}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (\forall x \neg \phi \leftrightarrow \forall y \neg \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{dral1v}{2}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (\neg \forall x \neg \phi \leftrightarrow \neg \forall y \neg \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{notbid}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \phi \leftrightarrow \neg \forall x \neg \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-ex}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists y \psi \leftrightarrow \neg \forall y \neg \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-ex}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (\exists x \phi \leftrightarrow \exists y \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4g}{4,5,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Hilfssatz für den Distinktorabbau bei Nichtfreiheit]{%
  (\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)) \vdash (\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi \leftrightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;y\,\psi))
}{drnf1v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (\exists x \phi \leftrightarrow \exists y \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{drex1v}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (\forall x \phi \leftrightarrow \forall y \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{dral1v}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow ((\exists x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi) \leftrightarrow (\exists y \psi \rightarrow \forall y \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi12d}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi \leftrightarrow (\exists x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-nf}}
  \proofstep{}{(\mathrm{F}\!/\;y\,\psi \leftrightarrow (\exists y \psi \rightarrow \forall y \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-nf}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi \leftrightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;y\,\psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4g}{4,5,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaAxiom[Axiom der quantifizierten Gleichheit]{%
  (\neg x = y \rightarrow (y = z \rightarrow \forall x\, y = z))
}{ax-13}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{ax-13} ist technisch sehr nützlich, sollte aber sparsam eingesetzt werden. In vielen Situationen reichen die schwächeren Varianten mit expliziten Verschiedenheitsbedingungen aus und halten spätere Abhängigkeiten deutlich kleiner.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Alias für \FormulaRefAuto{ax-13}]{%
  (\neg x = y \rightarrow (y = z \rightarrow \forall x\, y = z))
}{ax13v}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg x = y \rightarrow (y = z \rightarrow \forall x\, y = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-13}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Hilfssatz zu \FormulaRefAuto{ax-13}]{%
  (\neg x = y \rightarrow (z = y \rightarrow \forall x\, z = y))
}{ax13lem1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(z = y \rightarrow \exists w(z = w \land y = w))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equvinva}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg x = y \rightarrow (y = w \rightarrow \forall x\, y = w))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax13v}}
  \proofstep{}{(z = w \rightarrow (y = w \rightarrow z = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equeucl}}
  \proofstep{}{(z = w \rightarrow (\forall x\, y = w \rightarrow \forall x\, z = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alimdv}{3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg x = y \rightarrow (z = w \rightarrow (y = w \rightarrow \forall x\, z = y)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl9}{2,4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg x = y \rightarrow ((z = w \land y = w) \rightarrow \forall x\, z = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impd}{5}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg x = y \rightarrow (\exists w(z = w \land y = w) \rightarrow \forall x\, z = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exlimdv}{6}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg x = y \rightarrow (z = y \rightarrow \forall x\, z = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl5}{1,7}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Herleitung von \FormulaRefAuto{ax-13} aus \FormulaRefAuto{ax13v}]{%
  (\neg x = y \rightarrow (y = z \rightarrow \forall x\, y = z))
}{ax13}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(y = z \leftrightarrow \exists w(w = y \land w = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equvinv}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg x = y \rightarrow (w = y \rightarrow \forall x\, w = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax13lem1}}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg x = y \land w = y) \rightarrow \forall x\, w = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg x = z \rightarrow (w = z \rightarrow \forall x\, w = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax13lem1}}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg x = z \land w = z) \rightarrow \forall x\, w = z)}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{4}}
  \proofstep{}{(w = y \rightarrow (w = z \rightarrow y = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax7v1}}
  \proofstep{}{((w = y \land w = z) \rightarrow y = z)}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{6}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall x\, w = y \land \forall x\, w = z) \rightarrow \forall x\, y = z)}{\FormulaRefAuto{alanimi}{7}}
  \proofstep{}{(((\neg x = y \land w = y) \land (\neg x = z \land w = z)) \rightarrow \forall x\, y = z)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl2an}{3,5,8}}
  \proofstep{}{(((\neg x = y \land \neg x = z) \land (w = y \land w = z)) \rightarrow \forall x\, y = z)}{\FormulaRefAuto{an4s}{9}}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg x = y \land \neg x = z) \rightarrow ((w = y \land w = z) \rightarrow \forall x\, y = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{10}}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg x = y \land \neg x = z) \rightarrow (\exists w(w = y \land w = z) \rightarrow \forall x\, y = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exlimdv}{11}}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg x = y \land \neg x = z) \rightarrow (y = z \rightarrow \forall x\, y = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimtrid}{1,12}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg x = y \rightarrow (\neg x = z \rightarrow (y = z \rightarrow \forall x\, y = z)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{13}}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg x = y \rightarrow (y = z \rightarrow \forall x\, y = z)) \leftrightarrow (\neg x = y \rightarrow (\neg x = z \rightarrow (y = z \rightarrow \forall x\, y = z))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax13b}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg x = y \rightarrow (y = z \rightarrow \forall x\, y = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbir}{14,15}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Zweiter Hilfssatz zu \FormulaRefAuto{ax-13}]{%
  (\neg x = y \rightarrow (\exists x\, z = y \rightarrow z = y))
}{ax13lem2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg x = y \rightarrow (w = y \rightarrow \forall x\, w = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax13lem1}}
  \proofstep{}{(z = y \rightarrow (w = y \rightarrow z = w))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equeucl}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, z = y \rightarrow \exists x(w = y \rightarrow z = w))}{\FormulaRefAuto{eximi}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(w = y \rightarrow z = w) \leftrightarrow (\forall x\, w = y \rightarrow z = w))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.36v}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, z = y \rightarrow (\forall x\, w = y \rightarrow z = w))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylib}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg x = y \rightarrow (\exists x\, z = y \rightarrow (w = y \rightarrow z = w)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl9}{1,5}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg x = y \rightarrow (\exists x\, z = y \rightarrow \forall w(w = y \rightarrow z = w)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alrimdv}{6}}
  \proofstep{}{(w = y \rightarrow (z = w \leftrightarrow z = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equequ2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall w(w = y \rightarrow z = w) \leftrightarrow z = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{equsalvw}{8}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg x = y \rightarrow (\exists x\, z = y \rightarrow z = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbitrdi}{7,9}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Gleichungen sind von jeder anderen Variablen frei]{%
  (\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,z = y)
}{nfeqf2}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \neg x = y \leftrightarrow \neg \forall x\, x = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{exnal}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, z = y \rightarrow \forall x \exists x\, z = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{hbe1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg x = y \rightarrow (\exists x\, z = y \rightarrow z = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax13lem2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg x = y \rightarrow (z = y \rightarrow \forall x\, z = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax13lem1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, z = y \rightarrow (\neg x = y \rightarrow \forall x\, z = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syldc}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, z = y \rightarrow (\exists x \neg x = y \rightarrow \exists x \forall x\, z = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{eximdh}{2,5}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \forall x\, z = y \rightarrow \forall x\, z = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{hbe1a}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \neg x = y \rightarrow (\exists x\, z = y \rightarrow \forall x\, z = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl6com}{6,7}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x \neg x = y \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,z = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfd}{8}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,z = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylbir}{1,9}}
\end{tabproof}

  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,z = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfeqf2}}

\FormulaThm[Symmetrische Form von \FormulaRefAuto{nfeqf2}]{%
  (\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,y = z)
}{nfeqf1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep z}%

  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,z = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfeqf2}}
  \proofstep{}{(z = y \leftrightarrow y = z)}{\FormulaRefAuto{equcom}}
  \proofstep{}{(\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,z = y \leftrightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,y = z)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfbii}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,y = z)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylib}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktionsform von \FormulaRefAuto{nfeqf1}]{%
  (\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (y = z \rightarrow \forall x\, y = z))
}{dveeq1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,y = z)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfeqf1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (y = z \rightarrow \forall x\, y = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nf5rd}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Jede Gleichung ist unter dem Distinktor effektiv variablenfrei]{%
  (\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,y = z)
}{nfeqf}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\neg \forall x\, x = y}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfna1}}
  \proofstep{}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\neg \forall x\, x = z}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfna1}}
  \proofstep{}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,((\neg \forall x\, x = y \land \neg \forall x\, x = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfan}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(y = z \leftrightarrow z = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{equvinva}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (y = z \rightarrow \forall x\, y = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{dveeq1}}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg \forall x\, x = y \land y = z) \rightarrow \forall x\, y = z)}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{5}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x\, x = z \rightarrow (z = y \rightarrow \forall x\, z = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{dveeq1}}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg \forall x\, x = z \land z = y) \rightarrow \forall x\, z = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{imp}{7}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, z = y \rightarrow \forall x\, y = z)}{\FormulaRefAuto{equtr2}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall x\, y = z \land \forall x\, z = y) \rightarrow \forall x(y = z \leftrightarrow z = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alanimi}{4}}
  \proofstep{}{(((\neg \forall x\, x = y \land y = z) \land (\neg \forall x\, x = z \land z = y)) \rightarrow \forall x(y = z \leftrightarrow z = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl2an}{6,8,10}}
  \proofstep{}{(((\neg \forall x\, x = y \land \neg \forall x\, x = z) \land (y = z \land z = y)) \rightarrow \forall x(y = z \leftrightarrow z = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{an4s}{11}}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg \forall x\, x = y \land \neg \forall x\, x = z) \rightarrow ((y = z \land z = y) \rightarrow \forall x(y = z \leftrightarrow z = y)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{12}}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg \forall x\, x = y \land \neg \forall x\, x = z) \rightarrow (y = z \rightarrow \forall x(y = z \leftrightarrow z = y)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exlimdv}{13}}
  \proofstep{}{((\neg \forall x\, x = y \land \neg \forall x\, x = z) \rightarrow (y = z \rightarrow \forall x\, y = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl5}{14,9}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,y = z)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nf5d}{3,15}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Herleitung von \FormulaRefAuto{ax-c9}]{%
  (\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (y = z \rightarrow \forall x\, y = z))
}{axc9}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,y = z)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfeqf}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (y = z \rightarrow \forall x\, y = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nf5rd}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Es existiert mindestens ein Individuum]{%
  \exists x\, x = y
}{ax6e}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow \exists x\, x = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.8a}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg x = y \rightarrow (w = y \rightarrow \forall x\, w = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax13lem1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, x = w)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax6ev}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = w \rightarrow (w = y \rightarrow x = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equtr}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(w = y \rightarrow x = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{eximii}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, w = y \rightarrow \exists x\, x = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.35i}{5}}
  \proofstep{}{(w = y \rightarrow (\neg x = y \rightarrow \exists x\, x = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl6com}{2,6}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists w\, w = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax6ev}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg x = y \rightarrow \exists x\, x = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{exlimiiv}{7,8}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, x = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61i}{1,9}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Herleitung von \FormulaRefAuto{ax-6} aus \FormulaRefAuto{ax6v}]{%
  \neg \forall x \neg x = y
}{ax6}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, x = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax6e}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, x = y \leftrightarrow \neg \forall x \neg x = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-ex}}
  \proofstep{}{\neg \forall x \neg x = y}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbi}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Herleitung von \FormulaRefAuto{ax-c10}]{%
  (\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \forall x \phi) \rightarrow \phi)
}{axc10}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\neg \forall x \neg x = y}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax6}}
  \proofstep{}{((x = y \rightarrow \forall x \phi) \rightarrow (\neg \forall x \phi \rightarrow \neg x = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{con3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \forall x \phi) \rightarrow (\forall x \neg \forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall x \neg x = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{al2imi}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \forall x \phi) \rightarrow \neg \forall x \neg \forall x \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mtoi}{1,3}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x \neg \forall x \phi \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{axc7}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \forall x \phi) \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{4,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\section{Implizite Spezialisierung und Variablenwechsel}

\FormulaThm[Abgeschlossene Form von \FormulaRefAuto{spim}]{%
  ((\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi \land \forall x(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))) \rightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi))
}{spimt}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, x = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax6e}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi)) \rightarrow (\exists x\, x = y \rightarrow \exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exim}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi)) \rightarrow \exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpi}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.35}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi)) \rightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylib}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{id}}
  \proofstep{}{(\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi \rightarrow (\exists x \psi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.9d}{6}}
  \proofstep{}{((\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi \land \forall x(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))) \rightarrow (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sylan9r}{5,7}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Spezialisierung mittels impliziter Substitution]{%
  \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi \dsep (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi)) \vdash (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi)
}{spim}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, x = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax6e}}
  \proofstep{2}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{eximii}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.36i}{1,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktionsform von \FormulaRefAuto{spime}]{%
  (\chi \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi) \dsep (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi)) \vdash (\chi \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi))
}{spimed}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\chi\dsep\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(\chi \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\chi \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nf5rd}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, x = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax6e}}
  \proofstep{2}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\exists x(\phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{eximii}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{2}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{19.35i}{5}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(\chi \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl6}{2,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Existenzeinführung mittels impliziter Substitution]{%
  \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi \dsep (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi)) \vdash (\phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi)
}{spime}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\top \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{2}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(\top \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{spimed}{2,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(\phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mptru}{4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Spezialisierung mit Verschiedenheitsbedingung]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi)) \vdash (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi)
}{spimv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfv}}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{spim}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Alternative Fassung von \FormulaRefAuto{spimv}]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi)) \vdash (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi)
}{spimvALT}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{spimv}{1}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Existenzeinführung mit Verschiedenheitsbedingung]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi)) \vdash (\phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi)
}{spimev}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfv}}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \exists x \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{spime}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Spezialisierung über eine Äquivalenzhypothese]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)) \vdash (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi)
}{spv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpd}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{spimv}{2}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Spezialisierung einer Äquivalenz]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)) \dsep \phi \vdash \psi
}{spei}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists x\, x = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax6e}}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(\phi \leftrightarrow \exists x \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbiri}{1,3}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\psi}{\FormulaRefAuto{eximii}{4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Implizite Substitution einer Variablen in einen Satz]{%
  \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi \dsep (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)) \dsep \phi \vdash \psi
}{chvar}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpd}{2}}
  \proofstep{1,2}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{spim}{1,3}}
  \proofstep{3}{\phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{\psi}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpg}{4,5}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{spim}, \FormulaRefAuto{spime} und \FormulaRefAuto{chvar} bilden zusammen einen zentralen kleinen Werkzeugkasten für implizite Substitution: Spezialisierung, Existenzeinführung und schließlich den direkten Variablenwechsel in einem bereits bewiesenen Satz.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Implizite Substitution einer Variablen in einen Satz mit Verschiedenheitsbedingung]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)) \dsep \phi \vdash \psi
}{chvarv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfv}}
  \proofstep{1}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2}{\psi}{\FormulaRefAuto{chvar}{1,2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Wechsel gebundener Variablen über eine Implikation]{%
  \mathrm{F}\!/\;y\,\phi \dsep \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi \dsep (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))
  \vdash
  (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall y \psi)
}{cbv3}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;y\,\phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall y \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{cbv3v}{1,2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Wechsel gebundener Variablen bei Allquantoren]{%

  \vdash

  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%

  \proofstep{1,2,3}{(\forall x \phi \leftrightarrow \forall y \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{cbvalv1}{1,2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktiver Wechsel gebundener Variablen für Allquantoren]{%
  \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi \dsep \mathrm{F}\!/\;y\,\phi \dsep (\phi \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;y\,\psi) \dsep (\phi \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\chi)

  \vdash

  \proofstep{5}{(\phi \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \chi)))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2,3,4,5}{(\phi \rightarrow (\forall x \psi \rightarrow \forall y \chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{cbv1v}{1,2,3,4,5}}
\end{tabproof}

  \vdash

\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Wechsel gebundener Variablen aus gebundenen Hypothesen]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow \forall y \phi) \dsep (\psi \rightarrow \forall x \psi) \dsep (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))
  \vdash
  (\forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall y \psi)
}{cbv3h}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}

  \proofstep{2}{(\psi \rightarrow \forall x \psi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{(\forall x \phi \rightarrow \forall y \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{cbv3hv}{1,2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Wechsel gebundener Variablen unter doppelt universeller Voraussetzung]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \forall y \psi)) \dsep (\phi \rightarrow (\chi \rightarrow \forall x \chi))
  \dsep
  (\phi \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \chi)))
  \vdash
  (\forall x \forall y \phi \rightarrow (\forall x \psi \rightarrow \forall y \chi))
}{cbv1h}{%

  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\forall x \forall y \phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfa1}}
  \proofstep{}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;y\,\forall x \forall y \phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfa2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x \forall y \phi \rightarrow \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{2sp}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \forall y \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \forall y \phi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \forall y \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\forall x \forall y \phi \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;y\,\psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nf5d}{1,5}}
  \proofstep{2}{(\phi \rightarrow (\chi \rightarrow \forall x \chi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(\forall x \forall y \phi \rightarrow (\chi \rightarrow \forall x \chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{3,7}}
  \proofstep{2}{(\forall x \forall y \phi \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nf5d}{2,8}}
  \proofstep{3}{(\phi \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \chi)))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{(\forall x \forall y \phi \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \chi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{3,10}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{(\forall x \forall y \phi \rightarrow (\forall x \psi \rightarrow \forall y \chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{cbv1}{1,2,6,9,11}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Wechsel gebundener Variablen unter doppelt universeller Voraussetzung über Äquivalenz]{%
  (\phi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \forall y \psi)) \dsep (\phi \rightarrow (\chi \rightarrow \forall x \chi))
  \dsep
  (\phi \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi)))
  \vdash
  (\forall x \forall y \phi \rightarrow (\forall x \psi \leftrightarrow \forall y \chi))

  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}

  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \forall y \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(\phi \rightarrow (\chi \rightarrow \forall x \chi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{(\phi \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi)))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{}{((\psi \leftrightarrow \chi) \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimp}}
  \proofstep{3}{(\phi \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow (\psi \rightarrow \chi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl6}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{(\forall x \forall y \phi \rightarrow (\forall x \psi \rightarrow \forall y \chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{cbv1h}{1,2,5}}
  \proofstep{}{(y = x \leftrightarrow x = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{equcomi}}
  \proofstep{}{((\psi \leftrightarrow \chi) \rightarrow (\chi \rightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{biimpr}}
  \proofstep{3}{(\phi \rightarrow (y = x \rightarrow (\chi \rightarrow \psi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl56}{7,3,8}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{(\forall y \forall x \phi \rightarrow (\forall y \chi \rightarrow \forall x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{cbv1h}{2,1,9}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{(\forall x \forall y \phi \rightarrow (\forall y \chi \rightarrow \forall x \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{alcoms}{10}}
  \proofstep{1,2,3}{(\forall x \forall y \phi \rightarrow (\forall x \psi \leftrightarrow \forall y \chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbid}{6,11}}
\end{tabproof}

  \proofstep{1,2}{(\exists x \exists y \exists z \exists w \phi \leftrightarrow \exists v \exists u \exists f \exists g \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{cbvex4vw}{1,2}}

\FormulaThm[Eine Richtung der impliziten Substitution]{%
  (\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi) \rightarrow \exists x(x = y \land \phi))

}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi) \rightarrow \exists x(x = y \land \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equs4v}}
\end{tabproof}

  \vdash

\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Herleitung der älteren Distinktorform von Axiom \enquote{ax-12}]{%
  (\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi))))
}{axc15}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\exists z\, z = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax6ev}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (z = y \rightarrow \forall x\, z = y))}{\FormulaRefAuto{dveeq2}}
  \proofstep{}{(x = z \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = z \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax12v}}
  \proofstep{}{(z = y \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow x = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equeuclr}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, z = y \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow x = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sps}{4}}
  \proofstep{}{(z = y \rightarrow ((x = z \rightarrow \phi) \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imim1d}{4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, z = y \rightarrow (\forall x(x = z \rightarrow \phi) \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{al2imi}{6}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, z = y \rightarrow ((\phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = z \rightarrow \phi)) \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imim2d}{7}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, z = y \rightarrow ((x = z \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = z \rightarrow \phi))) \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imim12d}{5,8}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (z = y \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl6mpi}{2,3,9}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (\exists z\, z = y \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{exlimdv}{10}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpi}{1,11}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{axc15} und \FormulaRefAuto{ax12} bilden zusammen die klassische Rückübersetzung zwischen der älteren Distinktorform und der heute bevorzugten Form von \enquote{ax-12}.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Herleitung von \FormulaRefAuto{ax-12} aus \FormulaRefAuto{axc15}]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow (\forall y \phi \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))
}{ax12}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}

  \proofstep{1,2,3}{(\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfxfrd}{4,11}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Deduktionsform von \FormulaRefAuto{dvelimf}]{%
  \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\phi \dsep \mathrm{F}\!/\;z\,\phi \dsep (\phi \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\psi) \dsep (\phi \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;z\,\chi)
  \dsep
  (\phi \rightarrow (z = y \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi)))
  \vdash
  (\phi \rightarrow (\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\chi))
}{dvelimdf}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep\psi\dsep\chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}

  \proofstep{1,2}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,(\phi \rightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfim1}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{3}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;z\,\phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{4}{(\phi \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;z\,\chi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3,4}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;z\,(\phi \rightarrow \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfim1}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{5}{(\phi \rightarrow (z = y \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi)))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{5}{(z = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{com12}{5}}
  \proofstep{5}{(z = y \rightarrow ((\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \chi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74d}{8}}
  \proofstep{1,3,5}{(\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,(\phi \rightarrow \chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{dvelimf}{3,6,9}}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow ((\phi \rightarrow \chi) \leftrightarrow \chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.5}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow (\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,(\phi \rightarrow \chi) \leftrightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfbidf}{1,11}}
  \proofstep{1,3,5}{(\phi \rightarrow (\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\,\chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbitrid}{10,12}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Variableneinführung für Gleichheit]{%
  (x = y \rightarrow \exists z(x = z \land z = y))
}{equvini}{%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%

\FormulaThm[Alternative Definition der Substitution nur mit primitiven Junktoren]{%
  ([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow ((x = y \rightarrow \neg \phi) \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))
}{dfsb3}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(((x = y \land \phi) \lor \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)) \leftrightarrow (\neg (x = y \land \phi) \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{df-or}}

  \proofstep{}{([y / x]\phi \leftrightarrow ((x = y \rightarrow \neg \phi) \rightarrow \forall x(x = y \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4i}{1,2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Aufbaulemma für Substitution im Distinktor-Reduktions-Theorem]{%
  (\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow ([z / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [z / y]\phi))
}{drsb1}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(x = y \rightarrow (x = z \leftrightarrow y = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equequ1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (x = z \leftrightarrow y = z))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sps}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow ((x = z \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow (y = z \rightarrow \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{imbi1d}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow ((x = z \land \phi) \leftrightarrow (y = z \land \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anbi1d}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (\exists x(x = z \land \phi) \leftrightarrow \exists y(y = z \land \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{drex1}{4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow (((x = z \rightarrow \phi) \land \exists x(x = z \land \phi)) \leftrightarrow ((y = z \rightarrow \phi) \land \exists y(y = z \land \phi))))}{\FormulaRefAuto{anbi12d}{3,5}}
  \proofstep{}{([z / x]\phi \leftrightarrow ((x = z \rightarrow \phi) \land \exists x(x = z \land \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{dfsb1}}
  \proofstep{}{([z / y]\phi \leftrightarrow ((y = z \rightarrow \phi) \land \exists y(y = z \land \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{dfsb1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow ([z / x]\phi \leftrightarrow [z / y]\phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr4g}{6,7,8}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{dfsb1}, \FormulaRefAuto{dfsb2} und \FormulaRefAuto{dfsb3} geben drei äquivalente Lesarten der Substitution: als Konjunktion, als Disjunktion und schließlich in einer Fassung, die rechts nur primitive Junktoren verwendet.
\end{remark}

  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep u\dsep v}%
}

  \proofstep{}{([u / y] [v / y] \phi \leftrightarrow [v / y] \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbf}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow ([u / x] [v / y] \phi \leftrightarrow [v / y] \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitrdi}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Substitution als Allquantorform bei Nichtfreiheit]{%
  \mathrm{F}\!/\;y\, \phi \vdash
  ([y / x] \phi \leftrightarrow \forall x\, (x = y \rightarrow \phi))
}{sb6f}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;y\, \phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{(\phi \rightarrow \forall y\, \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nf5ri}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{([y / x] \phi \rightarrow [y / x] \forall y\, \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbimi}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x] \forall y\, \phi \rightarrow \forall x\, (x = y \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sb4a}}
  \proofstep{1}{([y / x] \phi \rightarrow \forall x\, (x = y \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{syl}{3,4}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, (x = y \rightarrow \phi) \rightarrow [y / x] \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sb2}}
  \proofstep{1}{([y / x] \phi \leftrightarrow \forall x\, (x = y \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{impbii}{5,6}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Substitution als Existenzform bei Nichtfreiheit]{%
  \mathrm{F}\!/\;y\, \phi \vdash
  ([y / x] \phi \leftrightarrow \exists x\, (x = y \land \phi))
}{sb5f}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;y\, \phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{([y / x] \phi \leftrightarrow \forall x\, (x = y \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sb6f}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{(\exists x\, (x = y \land \phi) \leftrightarrow \forall x\, (x = y \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{equs45f}{1}}
  \proofstep{1}{([y / x] \phi \leftrightarrow \exists x\, (x = y \land \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr4i}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{sb6f} und \FormulaRefAuto{sb5f} geben unter einer Nichtfreiheitsvoraussetzung zwei besonders handliche Normalformen der Substitution: einmal über einen Allquantor und einmal über einen Existenzquantor mit Gleichheitsbedingung.
\end{remark}

  \proofstep{}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;z\, \mathrm{F}\!/\;z\, \phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfnf1}}

  \proofstep{}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\, (\forall x\, \mathrm{F}\!/\;z\, \phi \land \neg \forall x\, x = y)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfan}{12,13}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, \mathrm{F}\!/\;z\, \phi \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;z\, \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sp}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall x\, \mathrm{F}\!/\;z\, \phi \land \neg \forall x\, x = y) \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;z\, \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantr}{15}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\, [y / x] \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfsb2}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall x\, \mathrm{F}\!/\;z\, \phi \land \neg \forall x\, x = y) \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\, [y / x] \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{adantl}{17}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall x\, \mathrm{F}\!/\;z\, \phi \land \neg \forall x\, x = y) \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow [y / x] \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1i}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{((\forall x\, \mathrm{F}\!/\;z\, \phi \land \neg \forall x\, x = y) \rightarrow (\neg \forall z\, z = y \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;z\, [y / x] \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{dvelimdf}{11,14,16,18,19}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, \mathrm{F}\!/\;z\, \phi \rightarrow (\neg \forall z\, z = y \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;z\, [y / x] \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61dan}{7,20}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Nichtfreiheit bleibt unter Substitution mit Distinktor erhalten]{%
  \mathrm{F}\!/\;z\, \phi \vdash
  (\neg \forall z\, z = y \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;z\, [y / x] \phi)
}{nfsb4}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, \mathrm{F}\!/\;z\, \phi \rightarrow (\neg \forall z\, z = y \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;z\, [y / x] \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfsb4t}}
  \proofstep{2}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;z\, \phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(\neg \forall z\, z = y \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;z\, [y / x] \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpg}{1,2}}
\end{tabproof}

\begin{remark}
\FormulaRefAuto{nfsb4t} und \FormulaRefAuto{nfsb4} sind technische, aber wichtige Hilfssätze: sie sichern, dass Nichtfreiheitsbedingungen beim Übergang zu Substitutionsausdrücken kontrolliert erhalten bleiben.
\end{remark}

\FormulaThm[Beseitigung der Gleichheit im Antezedens nach Substitution]{%
  ([y / x] \phi \leftrightarrow [y / x] (x = y \rightarrow \phi))
}{sbequ8}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{[y / x] x = y}{\FormulaRefAuto{equsb1}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x] \phi \leftrightarrow ([y / x] x = y \rightarrow [y / x] \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{a1bi}{1}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x] (x = y \rightarrow \phi) \leftrightarrow ([y / x] x = y \rightarrow [y / x] \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbim}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x] \phi \leftrightarrow [y / x] (x = y \rightarrow \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr4i}{2,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Von impliziter zu expliziter Substitution]{%
  \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\, \psi \dsep (x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)) \vdash
  ([y / x] \phi \leftrightarrow \psi)
}{sbie}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep \psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{[y / x] x = y}{\FormulaRefAuto{equsb1}}
  \proofstep{2}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{([y / x] x = y \rightarrow [y / x] (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbimi}{2}}
  \proofstep{2}{[y / x] (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{ax-mp}{1,3}}
  \proofstep{5}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\, \psi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{5}{([y / x] \psi \leftrightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbf}{5}}
  \proofstep{5}{([y / x] (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow ([y / x] \phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sblbis}{6}}
  \proofstep{2,5}{([y / x] \phi \leftrightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{mpbi}{4,7}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Von impliziter zu expliziter Substitution, deduktive Form]{%
  \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\, \phi \dsep (\phi \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\, \chi) \dsep (\phi \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi))) \vdash
  (\phi \rightarrow ([y / x] \psi \leftrightarrow \chi))
}{sbied}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep \psi\dsep \chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{1}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\, \phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1}{([y / x] (\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \rightarrow [y / x] \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbrim}{1}}
  \proofstep{3}{(\phi \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\, \chi)}{\rA}
  \proofstep{1,3}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\, (\phi \rightarrow \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfim1}{1,3}}
  \proofstep{5}{(\phi \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi)))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{5}{(x = y \rightarrow (\phi \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{com12}{5}}
  \proofstep{5}{(x = y \rightarrow ((\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \chi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74d}{6}}
  \proofstep{1,3,5}{([y / x] (\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbie}{4,7}}
  \proofstep{1,3,5}{((\phi \rightarrow [y / x] \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \rightarrow \chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr3i}{2,8}}
  \proofstep{1,3,5}{(\phi \rightarrow ([y / x] \psi \leftrightarrow \chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74ri}{9}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Von impliziter zu expliziter Substitution, deduktive Form]{%
  ((\phi \land x = y) \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi)) \vdash
  (\phi \rightarrow ([y / x] \psi \leftrightarrow \chi))
}{sbiedv}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep \psi\dsep \chi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\, \phi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfv}}
  \proofstep{}{(\phi \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\, \chi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfvd}}
  \proofstep{3}{((\phi \land x = y) \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{3}{(\phi \rightarrow (x = y \rightarrow (\psi \leftrightarrow \chi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{ex}{3}}
  \proofstep{3}{(\phi \rightarrow ([y / x] \psi \leftrightarrow \chi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbied}{1,2,4}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Doppelte implizite Substitution als explizite Substitution]{%
  ((x = t \land y = u) \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi)) \vdash
  ([t / x] [u / y] \phi \leftrightarrow \psi)
}{2sbiev}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi\dsep \psi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep u\dsep t}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\, \psi}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfv}}
  \proofstep{2}{((x = t \land y = u) \rightarrow (\phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\rA}
  \proofstep{2}{(x = t \rightarrow ([u / y] \phi \leftrightarrow \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbiedv}{2}}
  \proofstep{2}{([t / x] [u / y] \phi \leftrightarrow \psi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbie}{1,3}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Zusammensetzen zweier Variablensubstitutionen]{%
  ([z / y] [y / x] \phi \leftrightarrow [z / y] [z / x] \phi)
}{sbcom3}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x\dsep y\dsep z}%
}
\begin{tabproof}
  \proofstep{}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;y\, \forall y\, y = z}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfa1}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y\, y = z \rightarrow ([y / x] \phi \leftrightarrow [z / x] \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{drsb2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y\, y = z \rightarrow ([z / y] [y / x] \phi \leftrightarrow [z / y] [z / x] \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbbid}{1,2}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall y\, y = z \rightarrow ([z / y] [y / x] \phi \leftrightarrow \forall y\, (y = z \rightarrow [y / x] \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sb4b}}
  \proofstep{}{(y = z \rightarrow ([y / x] \phi \leftrightarrow [z / x] \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbequ}}
  \proofstep{}{((y = z \rightarrow [y / x] \phi) \leftrightarrow (y = z \rightarrow [z / x] \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74i}{5}}
  \proofstep{}{(\forall y\, (y = z \rightarrow [y / x] \phi) \leftrightarrow \forall y\, (y = z \rightarrow [z / x] \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{albii}{6}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall y\, y = z \rightarrow ([z / y] [y / x] \phi \leftrightarrow \forall y\, (y = z \rightarrow [z / x] \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitrdi}{4,7}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall y\, y = z \rightarrow ([z / y] [z / x] \phi \leftrightarrow \forall y\, (y = z \rightarrow [z / x] \phi)))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sb4b}}
  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall y\, y = z \rightarrow ([z / y] [y / x] \phi \leftrightarrow [z / y] [z / x] \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr4d}{8,9}}
  \proofstep{}{([z / y] [y / x] \phi \leftrightarrow [z / y] [z / x] \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61i}{3,10}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Kompositionsgesetz der Substitution]{%

  \proofstep{}{([y / x] [y / y] \phi \leftrightarrow [y / x] \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbbii}{2}}
  \proofstep{}{([y / x] [x / y] \phi \leftrightarrow [y / x] \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{1,3}}

\FormulaThm[Kompositionsgesetz der Substitution]{%

  ([y / z] [z / x] \phi \leftrightarrow [y / x] \phi)

  \proofstep{}{(z = y \rightarrow ([y / z] [z / x] \phi \leftrightarrow [y / x] \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitr3d}{1,2}}

  \proofstep{6}{(\neg \forall z\, z = y \rightarrow ([y / z] [z / x] \phi \leftrightarrow [y / x] \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbied}{5,7,8}}
  \proofstep{6}{([y / z] [z / x] \phi \leftrightarrow [y / x] \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm2.61i}{4,9}}
\end{tabproof}

  \proofstep{2}{(\phi \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;z\, \psi)}{\rA}

  \proofstep{5}{\mathrm{F}\!/\;x\, \phi}{\rA}
  \proofstep{5}{([z / x] (\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \rightarrow [z / x] \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbrim}{5}}

  \proofstep{1,5}{([y / z] [z / x] (\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \rightarrow [y / z] [z / x] \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{bitri}{7,8}}
  \proofstep{5}{([y / x] (\phi \rightarrow \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \rightarrow [y / x] \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{sbrim}{5}}
  \proofstep{1,2,5}{((\phi \rightarrow [y / z] [z / x] \psi) \leftrightarrow (\phi \rightarrow [y / x] \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{3bitr3i}{4,9,10}}
  \proofstep{1,2,5}{(\phi \rightarrow ([y / z] [z / x] \psi \leftrightarrow [y / x] \psi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{pm5.74ri}{11}}
\end{tabproof}

\FormulaThm[Kompositionsgesetz der Substitution]{%
  ([z / y] [y / x] \phi \leftrightarrow [z / x] [x / y] \phi)

}

  \proofstep{}{(\forall x\, x = y \rightarrow ([z / x] [y / x] \phi \leftrightarrow [z / y] [y / x] \phi))}{\FormulaRefAuto{drsb1}}

  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall y\, y = x \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;y\, [x / y] \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfsb2}}

  \proofstep{}{(\neg \forall x\, x = y \rightarrow \mathrm{F}\!/\;x\, [y / x] \phi)}{\FormulaRefAuto{nfsb2}}

\section{Eindeutigkeit und eindeutige Existenz}

\subsection{Eindeutigkeit}

\FormulaDef[Formationsregel für den Quantor \(\exists^{\le 1}\)]{%
  \text{Ist }\phi\text{ eine Formel, so ist auch }\exists^{\le 1} x\, \phi\text{ eine Formel.}%
}{wmo}{%
  \DeltaRow{Formeln}{\phi}%
  \DeltaRow{Variablen}{x}%
}
